blocks:
Ayahs

الٓمٓصٓ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Mim, Suad. (1)
كِتَـٰبٌ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ فَلَا يَكُن فِى صَدْرِكَ حَرَجٌ مِّنْهُ لِتُنذِرَ بِهِۦ وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۲)
[This is] a Book that has been sent down to you—so let there be no disquiet in your heart on its account that you may warn thereby—and as an admonition for the faithful. (2)

Key Points

| Out of the total 29 Surahs that begin with the disjointed letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah), some begin with the letters "الم" (Alif Lam Mim) and some with the letter "ص" (Sad). However, this Surah begins with the combination "المص" (Alif Lam Mim Sad), which perhaps indicates that whatever is contained in all those Surahs is also present in this Surah. (1)

1) Tafsir Al-Mizan

Messages

1- The Quran is a very great Book. (The word "كتاب" [Kitab] is used in the indefinite form).

2- Paying attention to the Quran and its concepts leads to broad-mindedness (Saa'at al-Sadr). «كتاب... فلایكن فى صدرك حرج منه»

3- The condition for prophethood (Risalah) and propagation (Tabligh) is broad-mindedness. «انزل الیك فلایكن فى صدرك حرج منه لتنذر به»

4- Do not worry about the obstinacy of the disbelievers; your duty is to warn (Inthar), not to compel. «لتنذر»

After the revelation of the Quran, the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله was concerned about people not accepting it and opposing it, so Allah consoles the Prophet with this Ayah.

5- The warnings of the Prophets are for the general public, but only the believers benefit from them and take heed. «لتنذر به وذكرى للمؤمنین»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Al-A'raf This Surah has 206 Ayahs and is among the Makkan Surahs. In this Surah, the Companions of Al-A'raf and the story of Al-A'raf (Ayahs 46 and 48) are mentioned, hence it is named "Al-A'raf." This Surah is the third Surah that begins with the disjointed letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah) and is the first of the prostration (Sajdah) Surahs of the Quran; a recommended prostration (Sajdah Mustahabb) is mentioned in its last Ayah. Out of the one hundred and fourteen Surahs of the Quran, eighty-six were revealed in Mecca, which usually discuss the fundamentals of belief (Usul al-Aqa'id), the struggle against polytheism (Shirk), drawing attention to the status of humanity, and similar topics. In addition to those topics, this Surah refers to the incident of Adam عليه السلام with Iblis, the stories of Prophets Nuh, Hud, Salih, Lut, Shu'ayb, and Musa علیهم السلام, the explanation of the principles and foundations of the call of the Prophet of Islam, a description of the events of the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), repentance and self-reform, the subject of the Throne (Arsh), the Scale (Mizan), the World of Particles (Alam al-Dhar), Allah's covenant with humanity, the explanation of the Quran and its greatness, and Al-A'raf and the Companions of Al-A'raf.

Ayah

ٱتَّبِعُوا۟ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۗ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۳)
Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and do not follow any masters besides Him. Little is the admonition that you take! (3)

Key Points

The previous Ayah stated the duty of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله regarding warning and reminding, and this Ayah states the duty of the Ummah regarding obedience and following. That Ayah requested "broadness of chest" (patience/tolerance) from the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله; here, it demands "following" from the Ummah. The previous Ayah contained "أنزل الیك" (was revealed to you [singular]), while this one contains "اُنزل الیكم" (was revealed to you [plural]). The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «فاذا التَبَست علیكم الفِتَن كقطع اللیل المظلِم فعلیكم بالقرآن... مَن جعله اَمامه قاده الى الجنّة و مَن جَعَله خلفه ساقه الى النّار» [2], whenever tribulations engulf you like pieces of the dark night, then hold fast to the Quran... whoever places it in front of him, it will lead him to Paradise, and whoever places it behind him, it will drive him to the Fire. Just as Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «ففى اتّباع ماجائكم من اللّه الفوزالعظیم و فى تركه الخطاء المبین» [3], the great salvation lies in following what has come to you from Allah, and in abandoning it lies manifest error and deviation.

2) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 3) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- Following the Divine verses is the cause of human growth and nurturing. «اتّبعوا... ربّكم»

2- The prerequisite of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah) and nurturing is the revelation of commands, guidance, and reminders. «ما اُنزل الیكم من ربّكم»

3- The result of following revelation is being placed under Divine guardianship (Wilayah), and abandoning it means being placed under the guardianship of others. «ولا تتّبعوا من دونه أولیاء»

4- Obedience and following others is, in reality, accepting their guardianship (Wilayah). «ولا تتّبعوا من دونه أولیاء» even though He could have said: «لاتتّبعوا من دونى احدا» (Do not follow anyone besides Me).

5- Whoever does not accept the guardianship (Wilayah) of the One God, «ربّكم» (your Lord), must satisfy several "guardians" (Wali). «أولیاء»

6- Man's acceptance of advice is scarce. «قلیلاً ما تذكّرون»

Ayahs

وَكَم مِّن قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهَا فَجَآءَهَا بَأْسُنَا بَيَـٰتًا أَوْ هُمْ قَآئِلُونَ (٤)
How many a town We have destroyed! Our punishment came to it at night, or while they were taking a midday nap. (4)
فَمَا كَانَ دَعْوَىٰهُمْ إِذْ جَآءَهُم بَأْسُنَآ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ (٥)
Then their cry, when Our punishment overtook them, was only that they said, ‘We have indeed been wrongdoers!’ (5)

Key Points

Qaryah (قَریة) does not mean 'village,' but rather the center where people gather, whether it is a city or a village. Bayāt (بیات) means nighttime or during the night, and qā'ilūn (قائلون) is derived from qaylūlah (قیلولة), meaning sleep or rest during the midday. Iqālah (اقاله), meaning the cancellation or retraction of a sale, comes from the same root, because the buyer is relieved of the anxiety of the transaction. Every oppressor will one day confess to his mistake when facing Allah's wrath: «انّا كنّا ظالمین» (Indeed, we were wrongdoers), but these confessions will bear no fruit. As we read in another Ayah: When they saw Our punishment, they said, "We believe in Allah, the One, and we disbelieve in that which we used to associate [with Him]," but faith at the time of punishment was of no benefit to them. «فلمّا رأوا بأسنا قالوا آمنّا باللّه و كفرنا بما كنّا مشركین فلم یك ینفعهم ایمانهم لمّا رأوا بأسنا...» (4)

4) Mu'min, 84 to 85

Messages

1- The regions destroyed by Divine wrath are numerous. «وكم»

2- We must learn lessons from the bitter experiences of others. «وكم من قریة اهلكناها»

3- The Quran expresses the magnitude of the punishment by mentioning the destruction of the settlements (qurā) instead of the destruction of the people. «و كم من قریة اهلكناها»

4- Divine punishments are not exclusive to the Day of Judgment; we may see examples of them in this world as well. «و كم من قریة أهلكناها»

5- Whoever takes guardians besides Allah should await Divine wrath. «ولا تتّبعوا من دونه اولیاء... و كم من قریة اهلكناها»

6- It is among the Divine traditions to destroy nations due to disobedience and following others [besides Allah]. «و لا تتّبعوا من دونه اولیاء... وكم من قریة اهلكناها»

7- The Will of Allah transforms everything. «اهلكناها»

8- Divine wrath is sometimes sudden, having no specific day or hour, and allowing no opportunity for thought or remedy. «بیاتاً أو هم قائلون»

9- Punishment during rest is more surprising and bitter. «بیاتاً أو هم قائلون»

10- Many slogans are uttered during times of ease; when danger strikes, no one has anything to say. «فما كان دعواهم...»

11- Events and dangers break arrogance, remove the veils of heedlessness, and awaken consciences. «الاّ أن قالوا انّا كنّا ظالمین»

12- If you do not submit willingly today, you will be forced to bow down one day. «قالوا انّا كنّا ظالمین»

13- Turning to others besides Allah and failing to follow the Prophets (which was mentioned in the two preceding verses) is oppression (ẓulm). «كنّا ظالمین»

Ayahs

فَلَنَسْـَٔلَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَنَسْـَٔلَنَّ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (٦)
We will surely question those to whom the apostles were sent, and We will surely question the apostles. (6)
فَلَنَقُصَّنَّ عَلَيْهِم بِعِلْمٍ ۖ وَمَا كُنَّا غَآئِبِينَ (۷)
Then We will surely recount to them with knowledge, for We had not been absent. (7)

Key Points

The previous Ayah discussed worldly punishment, and this Ayah addresses the retribution and reckoning of the Hereafter. With several emphatic statements, it confirms the certainty of questioning on the Day of Judgment, and that this questioning and accountability are not exclusive to people; rather, the Prophets will also be questioned. «یوم یجمع اللّه الرّسل فیقول ماذا اجبتم قالوا لا علم لنا انّك انت علاّم الغیوب» [5]

Question: What will be asked about on the Day of Judgment?

A: About blessings (favors). «ثمّ لتسئلنّ یومئذ عن النّعیم» [6] In numerous narrations, leadership (Imamate) and Wilayah have also been counted among the instances of the blessings mentioned in the Ayah that will be questioned. [7]

B: About the Quran and the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: People will be questioned about how they acted regarding the Quran and my Ahl al-Bayt: «ثمّ اسألهم ما فعلتم بكتاب اللّه و باهل بیتى» [8]

C: About behavior and deeds. «لنسئلنّهم اجمعین . عمّا كانوا یعملون» [9]

D: About the limbs and organs. «اِنّ السّمع والبَصر والفؤاد كلّ اولئك كان عنه مسئولاً» [10]

E: About the acceptance or rejection of the Messengers. «یا معشر الجنّ والانس ألم یأتكم رسل منكم...» [11] Just as elsewhere, religious leaders will also be questioned regarding how people treated them. «یوم یَجمع اللّه الرُّسل فیقول ماذا اُجِبتُم...» [12]

F: About one's lifespan and youth, how they were spent.

G: About earnings and income. As we read in narrations: On the Day of Judgment, man will be specifically questioned and held accountable for four things: «شبابك فیما أبلیته و عمرك فیما أفنیتَه و مالك ممّا اكتسبته و فیما أنفقتَه», [13] concerning your lifespan and youth, how you spent them, and concerning your wealth and possessions, how you acquired them and in what way you spent them.

Question: These Ayahs state that questioning everyone is certain, but in some Ayahs, including Ayah 39 of Surah Ar-Rahman, it is stated that on that Day, no human or jinn will be questioned about their sins: «فیؤمئذ لا یسئل عن ذنبه انس و لاجانّ» How can these Ayahs be reconciled?

Answer: On the Day of Judgment, there are multiple stations (Mawaqif) and stages, and each station has a specific scene. In one station, seals are placed upon the lips, and they are unable to speak. In another station, the seals are removed, and everyone engages in lamentation, seeking help, and confession. In one station, everyone is questioned, while in another, silence prevails, and no questioning occurs.

The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «كُلّكم راع و كُلّكم مسئول عن رَعیّته فالامام یسأل عن النّاس و الرّجل یسأل عن أهله و المرأة تسأل عن بیت زوجها و العبد یسأل عن مال سیّده» All of you are responsible for those under your care and must be accountable; the Imam for his people, the man for his wife, the woman for her conduct in her husband's house, and the servant for the wealth and possessions of his master. He also said: Allah will also question Me whether I conveyed the message or not. [14]

5) مائده، ۱۰۹ 6) تكاثر، ۸ 7) وسائل، ج ۲۴، ص ۲۹۹ 8) تفسیر فرقان 9) حجر، ۹۲ تا ۹۳ 10) اسراء، ۳۶ 11) انعام، ۱۳۰ 12) مائده، ۱۰۹ 13) كافى، ج ۲، ص ۱۳۵، بحار، ج ۷، ص ۲۵۹ 14) تفسیر درّالمنثور

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, both the leaders and the nations (jinn and humans), both the righteous and the wicked, both the scholars and their followers will be questioned. «فلنسئلنّ الّذین ارسل الیهم ولنسئلنّ المرسلین»

2- Questioning on the Day of Judgment is a form of seeking testimony, extracting confession, and reprimand, and sometimes it is for appreciation and thanks. Otherwise, nothing is hidden from Allah that needs clarification through questioning. «فلنسئلنّ... فلنقُصّنّ...»

3- The knowledge of Allah is precise. «بعلم» (The indefinite form indicates greatness and precision.)

4- The knowledge of Allah is based on His own presence and supervision, and there is no intermediary involved. «...بعلم و ما كنّا غائبین»

Ayah

وَٱلْوَزْنُ يَوْمَئِذٍ ٱلْحَقُّ ۚ فَمَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۸)
The weighing [of deeds] on that Day is a truth. As for those whose deeds weigh heavy in the scales—it is they who are the felicitous. (8)

Key Points

The Mizān (Scale/Balance) is a means of measurement, and everything has its specific measuring tool. For example, a wall is measured with a plumb line, the heat and cold of the air with a thermometer, fruit with kilograms, and fabric with meters. Similarly, the means of measuring ordinary people are the perfect human beings. The standard (Mizān) on the Day of Judgment is truth and reality (Haqq). On that Day, sovereignty belongs to the Truth, «هنالك الولایة للّه الحقّ» (15), it is the Day of Truth, «ذلك الیوم الحقّ» (16), and the standard of measurement is also Truth. «و الوزن یومئذ الحقّ» Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in interpreting the Ayah «ونضع الموازین القسط» (17), said: The Mizān (Scale) consists of the Prophets and the Successors (of the Prophets). (18) Just as we read in the general Ziyarat (visitation prayer) of Hadrat Amir عليه السلام: «السّلام على میزان الاعمال» (Peace be upon the Scale of Deeds). This means that they are the criterion and standard for measuring the deeds of others. Hadrat Ali عليه السلام said: Good deeds (Hasanāt) cause the Scale to be heavy, while evil deeds (Sayyi'āt) and sins cause it to be light. (19)

(15) Kahf, 44 (16) Naba', 39 (17) Anbiya, 47 (18) Tafsir al-Mizan (19) Bihar, Vol. 90, p. 141

Messages

1- The structure of the Resurrection is calculated and lawful; accountability, judgment, sentencing, reward, and punishment are all based on truth (Haqq). «والوزن یومئذ الحقّ»

2- Allah desires humanity to attain truth (Haqq) and achieve worthy and correct beliefs and deeds. «والوزن یومئذ الحقّ»

3- For every person, there are several means and standards of measurement and calculation at work. «موازینه»

4- Expecting happiness and reward without action is futile. «فمَن ثَقُلت ... المفلحون»

Ayah

وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَٰزِينُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَظْلِمُونَ (۹)
As for those whose deeds weigh light in the scales—it is they who have ruined their souls, because they used to wrong Our signs. (9)

Key Points

| "Khisārat" (loss) means losing both capital and profit, but "ḍarar" (harm) means losing profit, not capital.

Messages

1- For a human being, several means of measurement are employed. «موازینه»

2- Having few righteous deeds on the Day of Judgment is a loss, let alone having none. «و مَن خَفّت... خسروا»

3- The world is like a market where faith is its "profit" and denial and disbelief are its "loss." Indeed, ignoring the arguments and invitation of the prophets is, in reality, erasing the innate nature (fitrah) and the essence of humanity, and oppression against oneself. «خسروا أنفسهم»

4- Indifference and denial of the divine signs (Ayat) is oppression against them. «كانوا بآیاتنا یظلمون» Indeed, failing to fulfill the right of anything and failing to observe its rights and limits is oppression against it, and the result of oppression is loss and destruction.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّـٰكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَجَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَعَـٰيِشَ ۗ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ (۱٠)
Certainly We have established you on the earth, and made in it [various] means of livelihood for you. Little do you thank. (10)

Messages

1- Remembering and paying attention to divine blessings is the basis for the flourishing of gnosis (ma'rifah), love, and submission to Allah. «و لقد مكّناكم»

2- The right to utilize the resources of the earth belongs to all human beings and is not specific to a particular group. «مكّناكم فى الارض»

3- The creation of the earth and its conditions (in terms of rotation, heat, light, absorption and discharge of water and plants, acceptance of waste, and delivery of greens and fruits, etc.) are such that man can fulfill his needs therein. «مكّناكم فى الارض و جعلنا لكم فیها معایش»

4- The laws governing nature are such that man can dominate it and take control of it. If Allah had not made it subservient, mankind alone would not have the power to control and utilize it. «مكّناكم»

5- The earth has been placed at the disposal and made subservient to man so that he may achieve growth and perfection and secure his livelihood. «مكّناكم...» Sa'di says:

The cloud, the wind, the moon, the sun, and the heavens are all working So that you may obtain a loaf of bread and not eat it heedlessly.

6- Blessings must be the basis for gratitude, not a cause for heedlessness and indulgence. «تشكرون»

7- Man is ungrateful, and the Quran has repeatedly spoken of the lack of gratitude, heedlessness, and lack of faith among the majority of people. «قلیلاً ما تشكرون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ ثُمَّ صَوَّرْنَـٰكُمْ ثُمَّ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِـَٔادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ لَمْ يَكُن مِّنَ ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ (۱۱)
Certainly We created you, then We formed you, then We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate before Adam.’ So they [all] prostrated, but not Iblis: he was not among those who prostrated. (11)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the material power and dominion of man over the earth were discussed, but in this Ayah, reference is made to the spiritual status of man, for whom all the angels prostrated. When all the angels are obedient to Allah and prostrated before Adam, it is regrettable that man should not obey and should not prostrate before Allah.

All are bewildered and obedient for your sake; it would not be fair that you should not obey.

In this Ayah, all human beings are addressed twice, "و لقد خلقناكم... صوّرناكم" (We have certainly created you... We have fashioned you), but the prostration is mentioned for Adam عليه السلام. Perhaps this indicates that all human beings have the potential to become the object of prostration for the angels. From this Ayah up to 14 Ayahs later, the narrative relates to the story of Adam عليه السلام.

A Jewish man asked Ali عليه السلام: Allah commanded the angels to prostrate before Adam; has He shown such respect to the Prophet of Islam as well? He replied: Allah granted the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله a virtue higher than this. Allah, with all His Greatness, sends blessings (Salawat) upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله along with all the angels, and He has called the Salawat of the believers upon the Prophet an act of worship. [20]

20) Ihtijaj Tabarsi, Vol. 1, p. 204

Messages

1- The creation of man was realized in several stages. "ولقد خلقناكم ثمّ صوّرناكم"

2- The prostration was for Adam, not for all human beings. "قلنا للملائكة اسجدوا لآدم" (We said to the angels, 'Prostrate before Adam'); otherwise, He would have said, "اسجدوا لكم" (Prostrate before you), just as He said, "خلقناكم ثمّ صوّرناكم" (We created you, then We fashioned you).

3- Man possesses the potential and merit to reach a status where he becomes the object of prostration for the angels. "اُسجدوا لآدم"

4- Prostration to other than Allah is not polytheism (Shirk) if it is based on Allah's command. "قلنا... اُسجدوا لآدم"

5- Iblis was on the same level as the angels, and the address directed to the angels included him as well. "فسجدوا الاّ ابلیس"

6- Iblis's refusal to prostrate was not accidental; rather, it was done knowingly and deliberately. "الاّ ابلیس لم یكن من السّاجدین"

Ayah

قَالَ مَا مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ ۖ قَالَ أَنَا۠ خَيْرٌ مِّنْهُ خَلَقْتَنِى مِن نَّارٍ وَخَلَقْتَهُۥ مِن طِينٍ (۱۲)
Said He, ‘What prevented you from prostrating, when I commanded you? ’‘I am better than him,’ he said. ‘You created me from fire and You created him from clay.’ (12)

Key Points

The reason for the angels' prostration to Adam was the command of Allah and the inherent worthiness of man, not his gender or physical composition. Satan, in justifying his disobedience, used sophistry and an inappropriate analogy (qiyās) to claim: I am superior to Adam, because I am made of fire and Adam is made of clay. Although each is the source of a specific effect, in reality, with this false claim, instead of obeying the command of the Wise Allah and paying attention to the status of man before Allah, "إِنِّي جَاعِلٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةً" [21], and ignoring the Divine Spirit in man, "وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِي" [22], he rebelled against the command of Allah. Just as many of us, in many instances, weigh religious rulings and commands against our own intellect without paying attention to their wisdom and philosophy, and since we do not find an obvious reason for them, we do not accept them and become indifferent to them. Or, in some cases, by comparing similar subjects, we issue the same ruling for all of them. Therefore, analogy (qiyās) is not permissible in deriving rulings, and Imam Sadiq عليه السلام always severely criticized the analogies used by Abu Hanifa. [23]

21) Baqarah, 30 22) Hijr, 29 23) Tafsir-e Nemooneh

Messages

  1. Before issuing a verdict, trial and interrogation are necessary. "قَالَ مَا مَنَعَكَ"
  2. Satan is the founder of disobedience and sin. "مَا مَنَعَكَ... إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ" because the first act of disobedience was carried out by him.
  3. Punishment without prior notification is ugly (qabīh). One cannot hold someone accountable or punish them until a ruling or matter has been stated. "قَالَ مَا مَنَعَكَ... إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ"
  4. Satan stood against Allah, not against Adam. "إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ" As we read in another Ayah: "فَفَسَقَ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِ" [24]
  5. Satan, like man, possesses obligation (taklīf) and free will. "مَا مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ قَالَ..."
  6. The criterion is obedience to Allah's command. Age, race, background, etc., are none of them criteria. "إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ قَالَ أَنَا خَيْرٌ..."
  7. In judgment, we should also give criminals the opportunity to express their views. "قَالَ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِّنْهُ"
  8. Ignoring the celestial dimension of man and focusing only on his physical body, which is the doctrine and path of materialists, is a satanic trait. "قَالَ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِّنْهُ"
  9. Self-superiority can lead the creature to take a stand against the Creator. "أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِّنْهُ"
  10. Accepting Allah's Lordship (Khāliqiyyah) alone is not enough; obedience and submission are also necessary. "خَلَقْتَنِي... خَلَقْتَهُ" Satan had accepted Allah's Lordship but did not obey Him.
  11. Racism is among the satanic values. "خَلَقْتَنِي مِن نَّارٍ"
  12. Satan acted according to his personal preference in the face of Allah's clear and explicit command, and, in terminology, performed Ijtihād (independent reasoning) against the Nass (explicit text). "إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ قَالَ... خَلَقْتَنِي مِن نَّارٍ..."
  13. Satan used analogy (qiyās), which lacks a scientific basis and is founded upon conjecture. "خَلَقْتَنِي مِن نَّارٍ وَخَلَقْتَهُ مِن طِينٍ"

24) Kahf, 50

Ayah

قَالَ فَٱهْبِطْ مِنْهَا فَمَا يَكُونُ لَكَ أَن تَتَكَبَّرَ فِيهَا فَٱخْرُجْ إِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰغِرِينَ (۱۳)
‘Get down from it!’ He said. ‘It is not for you to be arrogant therein. Begone! You are indeed among the degraded ones.’ (13)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Take a lesson from the fate of Iblis, who, despite a history of six thousand years of worship—it is unknown whether these were worldly years (365 days) or otherworldly years (where one day equals fifty thousand worldly years)—fell due to a single moment of arrogance (takabbur) and boasting. [25] The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: On the Day of Resurrection, the arrogant will be resurrected in the most humiliating form and will be trampled under the feet of the people of the gathering. [26] In another narration, the Prophet said: Whoever shows humility and modesty, Allah will raise him, and whoever shows arrogance and haughtiness, Allah will disgrace and abase him. [27] It is also stated in a narration: The roots of disbelief (kufr) are three things: greed (ḥirṣ), envy (ḥasad), and arrogance (takabbur). [28]

25) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 192 26) Tafsir Maraghi 27) Maḥajjat al-Bayḍā’, Vol. 6, p. 215 28) Kafi, Vol. 2, Chapter on the Roots of Kufr

Messages

1- Arrogance is dangerous not only for ordinary individuals but also for those who hold high status, associate with angels in the highest assembly (al-Malā’ al-A‘lā), and have a history of worship spanning years and centuries. «فاهبط، فاخرج» (So descend, so get out).

2- Neither Satan’s knowledge and recognition of Allah is salvific, nor are his long periods of worship; rather, the path to salvation is submission to Allah. «تتكبّر فیها فاخرج» (You show arrogance therein, so get out).

3- Arrogance causes the nullification and destruction (ḥabṭ) of deeds. «فاهبط... فاخرج» (So descend... so get out).

4- Sometimes a single moment of arrogance leads to permanent downfall. «تتكبّر فیها فاخرج» (You show arrogance therein, so get out).

5- The result of arrogance and self-aggrandizement is smallness and disgrace. Indeed, the consequence of the slogan "I am better," «أنا خیر», is hearing the response, «فاخرج انّك من الصاغرین» (So get out, for you are among the disgraced).

Ayahs

قَالَ أَنظِرْنِىٓ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ (۱٤)
He said, ‘Respite me till the day they will be resurrected.’ (14)
قَالَ إِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنظَرِينَ (۱٥)
Said He, ‘You are indeed among the reprieved.’ (15)

Key Points

Iblis's request was a respite until the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). This Ayah does not specify until when He granted him respite, but it is understood from Ayah 38 of Surah Al-Hijr and Ayahs 80 and 81 of Surah Sad that he was granted respite only for a long period, not until the Day of Resurrection: «اِنّك من المنظرین. الى یوم الوَقت المَعلوم» Some say: Satan will remain alive until Allah deems it appropriate. [29] Fayd Kashani says: Iblis is alive until the first blast (of the Trumpet) or until the rising of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, and after that, death will come to him as well. [30]

Question: Why did Allah grant Iblis respite? Answer: Granting respite to evildoers is among the Divine traditions and is part of the process of testing and trying humanity. Furthermore, the means for good and evil must be available so that man can choose a path by his own free will. This is because Iblis does not force man into deviation; he only whispers (tempts), as stated in Ayah 22 of Surah Ibrahim: «و ما كان لى علیكم من سلطان الاّ اَن دعَوتُكم فاستجبتم لى»

He said, "Grant me respite until the Day of Recompense." If only he had said, "Repent, O our Lord!"

29) Tafsir Ayyashi 30) Tafsir Safi

Messages

1- Even Iblis was not despairing of the acceptance of his prayer (request). «قال أنظِرنى»

2- Satan also knows that life (duration) is by the will and in the hand of Allah. «قال أنظرنى»

3- Not every long life is valuable; Satan also has a long life. «أنظِرنى الى یوم یبعثون»

4- Iblis knew the Creator, «خلقتنى», and he also accepted the Resurrection, «یوم یبعثون», but what good was that when he did not obey the command of Allah?

5- Sometimes the request of disbelievers is also accepted. «قال انّك من المنظرین»

Ayahs

قَالَ فَبِمَآ أَغْوَيْتَنِى لَأَقْعُدَنَّ لَهُمْ صِرَٰطَكَ ٱلْمُسْتَقِيمَ (۱٦)
‘As You have consigned me to perversity,’ he said, ‘I will surely lie in wait for them on Your straight path. (16)
ثُمَّ لَـَٔاتِيَنَّهُم مِّنۢ بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ وَعَنْ أَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ وَعَن شَمَآئِلِهِمْ ۖ وَلَا تَجِدُ أَكْثَرَهُمْ شَـٰكِرِينَ (۱۷)
Then I will come at them from their front and from their rear, and from their right and their left, and You will not find most of them to be grateful.’ (17)

Key Points

We read in a hadith: When Satan swore that he would lie in wait for man from four directions to mislead or halt him, the angels, out of compassion, said: "Our Lord! How will this human being be saved?" Allah said: "Two paths are open, from above the head and from below, and whenever a person raises his hands in prayer, or places his face upon the earth (in prostration), I will forgive his seventy years of sins." [31] As soon as Adam (عليه السلام) became aware of Satan's dominance over humanity, he turned to Allah's court and cried out. The address came: "Do not be distressed, for I count a sin as one and a reward as ten times, and the path of repentance is also open." [32] Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) said: The entry of Iblis upon man from the front is in the form of making the affairs of the Hereafter seem simple and light to him; from behind, it is by instilling the accumulation of wealth, stinginess, and attention to children and heirs; from the right side, it is by creating doubt, thereby destabilizing and corrupting religion; and from the left side, it is by making pleasures, desires, and forbidden acts dominant. [33] If Satan can, he prevents man's faith; if he cannot, he opens the paths of hypocrisy and apostasy; and if he is unsuccessful, by creating doubt, skepticism, and whispers (waswasa), he drives man toward sin so that he does not enjoy his faith and worship, and good deeds appear heavy and repulsive to him. In numerous narrations, the Straight Path (Sirat al-Mustaqim) has been interpreted as the Ahl al-Bayt (عليهم السلام) and the Wilayah (Guardianship) of Ali (عليه السلام). [34]

31) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi 32) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 33) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq 34) Tafsir Furqan

Messages

1- Attributing one's disobedience and sin to divine decree (qada wa qadar) is a Satanic act. «قال فبما اغویتنى»

2- Iblis asked for respite to take revenge, not to repent. «أنظرنى، لاقعدنّ...»

3- Instead of thanking Allah, Iblis showed insolence. «انّك من المنظرین، لاقعدنّ لهم...»

4- Satan considers man to be a passive and easily seduced creature. «لاقعدنّ لهم...»

5- Satan is man's sworn enemy. «لاقعدنّ...» Just as he says elsewhere: «فبعزّتك لاغویّنّهم اجمعین» [35]

6- Man is instinctively a traveler on the path of truth and the Straight Path. «لاقعدنّ لهم صراطك المستقیم»

7- Iblis also admits that the Straight Path is the path of Allah. «صراطك المستقیم»

8- The methods of Iblis's deception are numerous and varied. «من بین ایدیهم ومن خلفهم و...»

9- Satan knows the Straight Path, «صراطك المستقیم», the ways of temptation, attack, and dominance, «لآتینّهم من بین...», and also the grateful and the ungrateful. «و لاتجد اكثرهم شاكرین»

10- Satan's goal and desire is man's ingratitude. «ولاتجد اكثرهم شاكرین» Therefore, following Satan is ingratitude toward Allah, and the grateful person is on the Straight Path.

35) Sad, 82

Ayah

قَالَ ٱخْرُجْ مِنْهَا مَذْءُومًا مَّدْحُورًا ۖ لَّمَن تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱۸)
Said He, ‘Begone hence, blameful and banished! Whoever of them follows you, I will surely fill hell with you all.’ (18)

Key Points

How astonishing are the consequences of a single moment of arrogance and self-superiority ("أنا خیر" - I am better)! "اهبِط, اُخرُج, انّك من الصاغرین..." All this humiliation and downfall is bestowed upon the arrogant one, because instead of apologizing, he decided to mislead the human race. Madh'ūm (مَذئُوم), from the root dhi'm (ذئم), means severe defect or fault. Madḥūr (مَدحور), from daḥr (دحر), means expulsion accompanied by disgrace.

Abu Hanifa held three beliefs which he propagated: first, that all actions of the servants are the actions of Allah and that they are compelled in their deeds; second, that Allah is visible; and third, that Satan cannot be burned by fire, because fire has no effect on fire. Bahlūl, a student of Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in condemnation and refutation of his beliefs, picked up a clod of earth and struck him on the head. When the complaint was taken to the Caliph and the reason was sought, Bahlūl said: By his own admission, Allah performed this action, and I am not at fault. Furthermore, he is lying that his head hurts, because if there is pain, he must show it. And finally, man is made of earth, and earth has no effect on earth (a clod of earth on man)?! (36)

36) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- Although Iblis lies in wait on the straight path and constantly whispers temptations, the sinner himself follows Satan and becomes destined for Hell. "لمن تبعك"

2- The deviants are so numerous that they will fill Hell, "لاملئنّ," but the sound, pure, and grateful individuals are few, as we read in Ayah 10: "قلیلاً ما تشكرون"

3- Following Satan leads to companionship with him in Hell. "لمن تبعك منهم لاملانّ جهنّم منكم اجمعین"

Ayah

وَيَـٰٓـَٔادَمُ ٱسْكُنْ أَنتَ وَزَوْجُكَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ فَكُلَا مِنْ حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَـٰذِهِ ٱلشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۹)
[Then He said to Adam,] ‘O Adam, dwell with your mate in paradise, and eat thereof whence you wish; but do not approach this tree, lest you should be among the wrongdoers.’ (19)

Key Points

A similar verse to this one appeared in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayah 35. Regarding whether the tree that was forbidden was wheat, grapes, or something else, Imam Reza عليه السلام says: Since the trees of Paradise yield diverse fruits, it encompasses all the mentioned opinions. [37] Allah had willed that man should live on earth as His vicegerent, «انّى جاعل فى الارض خلیفة» [38] and to demonstrate this worthiness to others, He taught man certain realities. Then He commanded the prostration to him, placed him in Paradise, and subsequently commanded him the first obligation (taklif). The subject of commitment, responsibility, and free will began, followed by the punishment for violating the obligation, the descent to Earth, and the striving for perfection. All these stages occurred based on Divine knowledge, just as Imam Ali عليه السلام, after describing these stages and the settlement of man on Earth, says: The knowledge of Allah concerning him came to pass. [39]

Regarding what is meant by «جنّة» (Jannah) and the Paradise where Adam resided, various opinions have been expressed. Some consider it to be the promised Paradise, believing that eternal residence (khulūd) therein belongs only to those who enter it through deeds and recompense, and this is not the case for others, just as the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله entered it during the night of Mi'raj and returned. However, Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Adam's Paradise was one of the gardens of this world. [40] This is because in the promised Paradise, there is no command, prohibition, or obligation (taklif).

37) Uyūn al-Akhbār, Vol. 1, p. 306 38) Baqarah, 30 39) Nahj al-Balāghah, Sermon 91 40) Tafsir Ithnā 'Asharī

Messages

1- First, open up the permissible options and correct paths, then warn against the forbidden matters. «فكُلا... لاتقربا»

2- Approaching the forbidden (harām) and the prohibited zones of Divine laws leads to contamination by sin. «لاتقربا... فتكونا...»

3- When a permissible path «فَكُلا» (so eat) exists, choosing the forbidden path is oppression (ẓulm). «فتكونا من الظالمین»

Ayah

فَوَسْوَسَ لَهُمَا ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ لِيُبْدِىَ لَهُمَا مَا وُۥرِىَ عَنْهُمَا مِن سَوْءَٰتِهِمَا وَقَالَ مَا نَهَىٰكُمَا رَبُّكُمَا عَنْ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلشَّجَرَةِ إِلَّآ أَن تَكُونَا مَلَكَيْنِ أَوْ تَكُونَا مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰلِدِينَ (۲٠)
Then Satan tempted them, to expose to them what was hidden from them of their nakedness, and he said, ‘Your Lord has only forbidden you from this tree lest you should become angels, or lest you become immortal.’ (20)

Key Points

Satan tempted Adam and Eve, suggesting that eating from this tree would turn them into angels or make them immortal, and that Allah commanded them not to eat from it because He did not want them to reach this rank.

Regarding why Prophet Adam ignored Allah's prohibition and ate from the forbidden tree, the answer is given under Ayah 21: the prohibition here was a matter of discouragement (karāhah), not absolute prohibition (taḥrīm). It was like a doctor forbidding a certain food to a patient; if the patient eats that food, he will suffer difficulty and experience its specific effects and consequences, but it is not forbidden (ḥarām).

Question: Were the angels superior to Adam, such that Prophet Adam was influenced by Satan's temptation because he desired to become an angel?

Answer: Adam's superiority over the angels is clear. However, Adam's desire and inclination toward becoming an angel was due to their perfections, meaning Prophet Adam عليه السلام wanted to possess those perfections in addition to what he already had, and this is not proof of their superiority. (41)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked whether angels or the children of Adam were superior. He narrated a tradition from Imam Ali عليه السلام who said: Allah created the angels from intellect without lust (shahwah), and animals with lust without intellect, but He created man with a combination of intellect and lust. If a man's intellect overcomes his lust, he is superior to the angels, and if his lust overcomes his intellect, he is lower than the animals. «فمن غلب عقله شهوتَه فهو خیر من الملائكة و من غلب شهوته عقله فهو شرّ من البهائم». (42)

The offspring of Adam is a strange compound, Molded from angel and animal. If he inclines toward this, he becomes better than this, If he inclines toward that, he becomes lower than that.

Yes, the goodness and ranks of human beings are different and possess various levels, just as is the case among the angels. Therefore, one cannot say that every human is superior to all angels, or vice versa. (43)

Allah did not create Satan with an inherently wicked nature; rather, He created him as a being capable of development and guided him through the path of existence. It was Satan who made himself deviant and wicked. Otherwise, if his nature had been wicked, his engagement in glorification and sanctification (tasbīḥ wa taqdīs), which he was occupied with for years, would have been meaningless. Just as Allah did not create Pharaoh and Ibn Muljam in that way; rather, they too possessed the potential for human growth and elevation, but they deviated by their own choice. (44)

Question: Why did Allah give Satan dominance over man?

Answer: The relationship between Satan and man, and his potential influence on man, is no greater than the influence of animalistic instincts on man. In opposition to those animalistic instincts, Allah granted thought, reason, and conscience, and provided man with the best means to moderate and regulate his instincts. Moreover, Satan has no dominion (sulṭah) over man and does not take away man's will and choice, as he himself says: «ما كان لى علیكم من سلطان الاّ أن دعوتكم فاستجبتم لى فلاتلومونى ولوموا انفسكم ما انا بمصرخكم و ما انتم بمصرخى» (Ibrahim, 22) His job is to assist the animalistic instincts, and if he wishes to exert influence, he must enter through one of the inner forces—namely, instincts, delusions (tawahhumāt), and temptations (wasāwis)—in opposition to which the two forces of intellect and conscience constantly invite man toward goodness and virtues. (46)

41) Tafsir Manhaj al-Sadiqin 42) Wasā’il, Vol. 15, p. 209 43) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan, Vol. 1, p. 512 44) Sharh Mathnavi, Allamah Ja'fari, Vol. 5, p. 207 45) Ibrahim, 22 46) Sharh Mathnavi, Vol. 5, p. 207

Messages

1- Satan does not leave even the righteous alone. «فوسوس لهما» But he has no authority over the sincere ones. «الاّ عبادك منهم المخلَصین» (47)

2- Satan's ultimate action is temptation (waswasah), not compulsion; showing the path of deviation, not insistence. «فوسوس لهما»

3- The result of committing sin and transgression is disgrace. «لیُبدى... سوءاتهما»

4- Disgrace, the lifting of the veil, and nakedness are among Satan's goals. «لیُبدى... ما وُرى»

5- Satan deceives man through desires. «ما نهاكما ربّكما... الاّ اَن تكونا ملَكین او تكونا من الخالدین» (Man's desire is eternal life in a peaceful, angelic environment. Therefore, Satan said: If you eat from that tree, you will reach your desire and have a permanent life free from annoyance and anxiety. He tempted them by utilizing man's instincts and inclination toward comfort and eternity.)

47) Hijr, 40

Ayah

وَقَاسَمَهُمَآ إِنِّى لَكُمَا لَمِنَ ٱلنَّـٰصِحِينَ (۲۱)
And he swore to them, ‘I am indeed your well-wisher.’ (21)

Key Points

Adam and his wife initially did not trust Iblis (Satan). To gain their trust, Satan swore an oath and emphasized it. [48] Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Adam and Eve had not heard a false oath sworn by God until that time, and for this reason, they trusted him and ate from that tree. Of course, this act occurred before the prophethood of Adam, and it was not a prohibitive prohibition (نَهی تحریمی) or a major sin that would warrant the Fire. [49] Therefore, the first false oath was sworn by Satan. The Quran states: The hypocrites are also people who swear false oaths. [50] And whoever swears oaths repeatedly is not fit for leadership in society. «لاتطع كلّ حلاّف مهين» [51]

48) «قاسمهما» (He swore to them) being in the form of mufā'alah (reciprocal action) might be a sign of emphasis 49) Uyun al-Akhbar, Vol. 1, p. 196 50) (Surah) At-Tawbah, 56, 62, 74, and 107 51) (Surah) Al-Qalam, 10

Messages

1- Swearing a false oath is a satanic act. «قاسمهما»

2- Not every oath should be trusted. «قاسمهما»

3- The enemy exploits our beliefs and convictions for his own benefit. «قاسمهما»

4- Sometimes the enemy infiltrates us and strikes a blow with a compassionate appearance and a claim of goodwill. «إنّي لكما لمن الناصحين» Joseph’s brothers also told him, in order to eliminate Joseph and separate him from their father: «إنّا له لناصحون» [52]

52) (Surah) Yusuf, 11

Ayah

فَدَلَّىٰهُمَا بِغُرُورٍ ۚ فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا ٱلشَّجَرَةَ بَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْءَٰتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخْصِفَانِ عَلَيْهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ وَنَادَىٰهُمَا رَبُّهُمَآ أَلَمْ أَنْهَكُمَا عَن تِلْكُمَا ٱلشَّجَرَةِ وَأَقُل لَّكُمَآ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ لَكُمَا عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ (۲۲)
Thus he brought about their fall by deception. So when they tasted of the tree, their nakedness became exposed to them, and they began to stitch over themselves with the leaves of paradise. Their Lord called out to them, ‘Did I not forbid you from that tree, and tell you, ‘‘Satan is indeed your manifest enemy?’’ ’ (22)

Key Points

«Dallā» (دَلّى) is derived from «Tadliyah» (تدلیه), meaning to bring close, and refers to the bucket and rope used to reach or attain a goal. It is as if Iblis (Satan) led them into the well of deception using the rope of pride. «Yakhṣifān» (یخصفان) is derived from «Khaṣf» (خصف), meaning to gather, sew, or patch. Allah's address came using the word «Nidā» (ندا), «Nādāhumā» (ناداهما). Nidā is used for addressing someone distant, suggesting that Adam and his wife, by eating from the forbidden tree, became distant from the proximity of Allah. A number of commentators, such as Sayyid Murtaḍā, Ṭabarsī, and Abū al-Futūḥ Rāzī, state that the expulsion of Prophet Adam from Paradise and his descent to Earth was based on expediency (Maṣlaḥah), not as a punishment or retribution. [53]

53) Tafsir Ithnā ‘Asharī

Messages

1- Both man and woman are within the range of Satan's temptation. «Fa dallāhumā»

2- Satan's weapon is deception and pride. «bi ghurūr»

3- Inclination toward Satan's temptations is like grasping a weak and short rope and falling into the well of sin. «Dallāhumā»

4- Disregarding Divine commands and guidance is serious, whether the violation is small or large. «Fa lammā dhāqā» In committing sin, the size (small or large) is irrelevant; what matters is the audacity to commit the sin. Therefore, sometimes even a small sin causes disgrace and the downfall of a person.

5- Nakedness is a form of Divine punishment (not a sign of perfection or civilization). «Fa lammā dhāqā ash-shajarata badat lahumā saw’ātuhumā...»

6- Sometimes the downfall is step-by-step: first, creating a deviant thought, «Fa dallāhumā bi ghurūr»; then, committing the forbidden acts and eating the prohibited things, «Fa lammā dhāqā ash-shajarata»; and finally, the downfall and nakedness. «Badat lahumā saw’ātuhumā»

7- Disregarding Divine guidance and eating the forbidden food is the prelude to nakedness. «Dhāqā... badat lahumā saw’ātuhumā»

8- The ugliness of nakedness and the dignity of covering are part of human nature (Fiṭrah). «Wa ṭafiqā yakhṣifān»

9- Covering is a value, even if achieved with the simplest means. «Waraq al-Jannah»

10- Allah’s rulings, commands, prohibitions, and warnings are always aimed at the growth and training of humanity. «Nādāhumā Rabbuhumā alam anhakumā»

11- After eating from that forbidden tree, Prophet Adam and Eve became distant from their high station. «Tilka ash-shajarah» (that tree) was used, and He did not say «Hādhihi ash-shajarah» (this tree).

12- Allah does not rebuke anyone without completing the argument (Ittmām al-Ḥujjah). «Alam anhakumā... aqul lakumā»

13- On the path of Allah, it is necessary to recognize the enemies. «‘Aduwwun mubīn»

14- Although Satan's enmity is manifest, man becomes heedless of his danger and hostility. «‘Aduwwun mubīn»

Ayah

قَالَا رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (۲۳)
They said, ‘Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves! If You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.’ (23)

Key Points

Both Satan and Adam disobeyed, but Satan, regarding his disobedience (refusing to prostrate), objected to Divine justice and wisdom, showed arrogance, asserted his racial superiority, and did not regret it. However, Adam and Eve confessed to their transgression and sought forgiveness from Allah. [54]

54) Tafsir Namuneh

Messages

1- In the face of the injustices we commit against ourselves, we must seek help from Allah and compensate for them. «قالا ربّنا ظلمنا...»

2- Adam and Eve were partners both in the transgression and in compensating for the past and apologizing. «ذاقا، قالا ربّنا»

3- Every transgression is an injustice against oneself, because opposing the command of Allah is opposing one's own true perfection and happiness. «ظلمنا أنفسنا»

4- Among the etiquettes of supplication (Du'a) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) is first confessing the sin. «قالا ربّنا ظلمنا أنفسنا»

5- The first request of mankind from Allah was the plea for pardon and mercy. «وان لم تغفرلنا»

6- The most important issue for a sinner is Divine forgiveness, followed by other requests. «ان لم تغفر لنا و ترحمنا»

7- The care, grace, and kindness of Allah prevent eternal loss. «ان لم تغفر لنا... لنكوننّ من الخاسرین»

Ayahs

قَالَ ٱهْبِطُوا۟ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ ۖ وَلَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمَتَـٰعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (۲٤)
He said, ‘Get down, being enemies of one another! On the earth shall be your abode and sustenance for a time.’ (24)
قَالَ فِيهَا تَحْيَوْنَ وَفِيهَا تَمُوتُونَ وَمِنْهَا تُخْرَجُونَ (۲٥)
He said, ‘In it you will live, and in it you will die; and from it you will be raised [from the dead].’ (25)

Key Points

The addressees of the phrase "اهبطوا" (descend/go down) are either Adam, Eve, and Iblis, or Adam, Eve, and their progeny. Of course, in one place Iblis is addressed alone, "فاهبط منها" [55], and in another place Adam and Eve are addressed, "قال اهبطا منها جمیعاً" [56]. If the place of their descent (ḥubūṭ) is the same, then "اهبطوا" might be the plural encompassing them all. The meaning of "هبوط" (descent) is coming down to Earth, because immediately following it, He says: "ولكم فى الارض مستقرّ" (and for you there is a dwelling place on the earth). Although Allah accepted the repentance of Adam and Eve, "فتاب علیه" [57], the consequential effect (athar wad'ī) of the sin remains. The consequential effect of that tark al-awlā (leaving the preferable) and disobedience by Adam and Eve was their expulsion from that heavenly abode and their descent to Earth. "اهبطوا"

55) Al-A'raf, 13 56) Taha, 123 57) Al-Baqarah, 37

Messages

1- One cannot escape the consequential effects of transgression and sin. "قال اهبطوا"

2- Sometimes the actions of parents affect the descent and fall of their progeny as well. "قال اهبطوا..."

3- The Paradise of Adam and Eve was a place other than Earth, and higher and superior to it. "اهبطوا"

4- The world is a place of conflict, friction, and contradiction, and humans clash with one another due to the friction of interests and instincts. "بعضكم لبعض عدوّ" (some of you are enemies to others).

5- Worldly life and the enjoyment derived from it are not eternal. "الى حین" (until a time).

6- Man will be brought back to life after death. "منها تخرجون" (from it you will be brought forth). Adam (عليه السلام) was distressed and saddened because he thought he would no longer reach Paradise and eternal life. Allah said: After worldly life, he can reach eternal Paradise.

7- The duration and end of worldly life are unknown to mankind. (The word "حین" [time] is indefinite/nākirah).

Ayah

يَـٰبَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ قَدْ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ لِبَاسًا يُوَٰرِى سَوْءَٰتِكُمْ وَرِيشًا ۖ وَلِبَاسُ ٱلتَّقْوَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ (۲٦)
‘O Children of Adam! We have certainly sent down to you garments to cover your nakedness, and for adornment. Yet the garment of Godwariness—that is the best.’ That is [one] of Allah’s signs, so that they may take admonition. (26)

Key Points

Following the narration of the story of Prophet Adam (عليه السلام), Allah addresses the children of Adam and the human race four times in this Surah (in this Ayah and Ayahs 27, 31, and 35), commanding them regarding matters such as: preserving the garment of piety (Taqwa), not being deceived by Satan, avoiding extravagance in food, drink, and luxuries, and accepting the invitation of the prophets. "Rīsh" (ریش) means the feathers of birds, which serve as both covering and a means of beauty for them. It is also applied to clothing that is considered a form of adornment for humans. All blessings are in the hand of Allah and flow from the Divine treasury of the unseen, as Allah says: «و اِنْ من شى ء الاّ عندنا خزائنه و ما ننزّله الاّ بقدر معلوم» (58) There is nothing except that its treasuries are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure. And He says: «وانزلنا الحدید» (59), and «وانزل لكم من الانعام...» (60). Therefore, the meaning of the descent (نزول) of clothing from Allah is its creation and placement at the disposal of man. Regarding «أنزلنا علیكم لباساً» (We have sent down upon you clothing), the intent of "Libās al-Taqwā" (لباس التّقوى - the garment of piety) is clothing based on piety, humility, purity, and chastity. It has also been interpreted as referring to awe (Khashyah), obedience, modesty ('Iffah), shyness (Hayā'), and righteous deeds. (61)

58) Hijr, 21 59) Hadid, 25 60) Zumar, 6 61) Tafsir Manhaj al-Sadiqin

Messages

1- Attention to Divine blessings is a factor leading to love for Allah and distance from heedlessness. «قد انزلنا علیكم... لعلّهم یذّكّرون»

2- Clothing is a Divine blessing when it covers the body. «یوارى سواتكم»

3- Although humans strive in the preparation of clothing, in addition to natural factors, all of this is in the hand of Allah. «انزلنا علیكم لباساً»

4- Covering and concealment is the work of Allah, «انزلنا علیكم لباساً یوارى», but nakedness and stripping is the work of Satan. «فوسوس... لیبدى لهما ما ورى عنهما من سواتهما»

5- Clothing is a Divine blessing, «لباساً یوارى», while nakedness and being stripped of clothing is the punishment for sin. «فلمّا ذاقا الشجرة بدت لهما سوءاتهما»

6- Covering and adornment with beautiful garments and clothing is desired and loved by Allah. «قد انزلنا علیكم لباساً... و ریشاً» Adorning oneself, beautifying, and benefiting from beautiful clothing is permissible, provided it does not lead to extravagance.

7- Spirituality alongside materiality, and piety alongside adornment, are necessary. «ریشاً و لباس التّقوى» Otherwise, clothing can become a means for extravagance, arrogance, corruption, showing off, fashion obsession, lust, boasting, and the like.

8- Just as material clothing involves covering defects, protection from cold and heat, and beauty, piety (Taqwa) is also a factor in covering defects, protecting against sin, and providing spiritual beauty for man. «لباس التّقوى»

9- Cotton grows from the soil, wool is produced from grass (which is animal feed), and silk comes from the saliva of a worm. These are all among the signs of Allah that can be a source of remembrance and awakening for man. «ذلك من آیات اللّه لعلّهم یذّكّرون»

Ayah

يَـٰبَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ لَا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ كَمَآ أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ يَنزِعُ عَنْهُمَا لِبَاسَهُمَا لِيُرِيَهُمَا سَوْءَٰتِهِمَآ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ يَرَىٰكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُۥ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا تَرَوْنَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۲۷)
‘O Children of Adam! Do not let Satan tempt you, like he expelled your parents from paradise, stripping them of their garments to expose to them their nakedness. Indeed he sees you—he and his hosts—whence you do not see them. We have indeed made the devils friends of those who have no faith.’ (27)

Key Points

The previous Ayah mentioned clothing as a blessing. Here, [Allah] warns that you must be careful lest Satan strip you of this clothing and blessing. Although Satan tempts the people of faith and causes them to stumble, he cannot gain complete guardianship (wilayah) or dominance over them, «وماكان لى علیكم من سلطان» (62) because the believer is saved through repentance and seeking refuge in Allah. Indeed, Satan only has dominance over the disbelievers. Just because we do not see Satan, we should not assume that he also does not see us. Rather, he is present wherever the ground for sin is prepared, seeking mischief and deception.

62) Ibrahim, 22

Messages

1- We should learn lessons from the history and destiny of others. «كما اخرج ابویكم»

2- We should not consider ourselves immune or safe from deviation. Adam (عليه السلام), who was prostrated to by the angels, was removed from his station through Satan's deception. «أخرج أبویكم ...»

3- Nudity and the removal of hijab (unveiling) are factors leading to departure from the station of Divine proximity. «أخرج أبویكم... ینزع عنهما لباسهما»

4- Any program or propaganda that leads to nudity is satanic. «لایفتننّكم الشیطان... ینزع عنهما لباسهما»

5- Satan's goal is the nudity of men and women before one another. «لیریهما سواتهما»

6- Satan is not alone; rather, he has a group and agents. «انّه یراكم هو وقبیله» Just as we read elsewhere: «و جنود ابلیس» (63)

7- Since we do not see Satan, we must not be heedless of him, and we must always be prepared. «لایفتننكم... لاترونهم» Because Satan's ability to deceive is due to his invisibility.

8- Nudity is a sign of lack of faith and the dominance of Satan. «ینزع عنهما لباسهما... اولیاء للذّین لایؤمنون»

9- The dominance of Satan is due to the actions of man himself. «أولیاء للذین لایؤمنون»

10- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah that Satan should rule over the disbelievers and deniers. «انّا جعلنا الشیاطین اولیاء للّذین لایؤمنون»

11- Faith in Allah prevents the guardianship (wilayah) and sovereignty of Satan over man. «اولیاء للّذین لایؤمنون»

63) Shu'ara, 95

Ayah

وَإِذَا فَعَلُوا۟ فَـٰحِشَةً قَالُوا۟ وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهَآ ءَابَآءَنَا وَٱللَّهُ أَمَرَنَا بِهَا ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَأْمُرُ بِٱلْفَحْشَآءِ ۖ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۲۸)
When they commit an indecency, they say, ‘We found our fathers practising it, and Allah has enjoined it upon us.’ Say, ‘Indeed Allah does not enjoin indecencies. Do you attribute to Allah what you do not know?’ (28)

Key Points

We read in the commentaries that this Ayah relates to the nakedness of the polytheists while performing the Tawaf (circumambulation), which was customary during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). They performed this act in imitation of their ancestors, but they claimed that Allah had commanded them to perform the Tawaf in this manner! Those who commit indecencies imitate their ancestors and attribute their polytheism to God, saying: «لو شاء اللّه ما أشركنا» (64), «لو شاء اللّه ما عبدنا من دونه من شى ء» (65). (They claimed) that it was the will of Allah that they became polytheists, and they imagined that because God had granted them respite, He was either pleased with their indecencies and evils or had commanded them to commit them. In this Ayah, they first mentioned their ancestors, «وجدنا علیها آبائنا» (We found our fathers doing it), and then the command of God, «واللّه امَرَنا بها» (And Allah commanded us to do it), perhaps because the actions of their ancestors were more important to them than the command of God.

64) An‘ām, 148 65) Naḥl, 35

Messages

1- Justifying sin is greater than the sin itself and is a sign of the dominance and influence of Satan. «اولیاء للّذین... قالوا وجدنا علیها آبائنا» (Whether it is a social justification (ancestors) or a religious justification (God commanding indecencies)).

2- Adherence to the ugly and wrong ways of ancestors and innovation (bid'ah) in religion are signs of the guardianship (wilayah) of Satan and lack of faith. «انّا جعلنا الشیاطین اولیاء للّذین... اذا فعلوا فاحشة قالوا وجدنا علیها آبائنا...»

3- The corruption and ugliness of some actions are clear and innate (fitrī). «فاحشة» (The term fāḥishah refers to a sin whose repulsiveness and ugliness are evident).

4- Sometimes human deviations are passed on to subsequent generations, and the deviants bear the burden of the sins of future generations as well. «وَجَدنا علیها آبائنا»

5- The ways of the predecessors are not always valuable, and inappropriate imitation (taqlīd) of the ancients is forbidden. «قالوا وجدنا علیها آبائنا»

6- Allah never commands indecency. «واللّه اَمرَنا... انّ اللّه لا یأمر» (He responds to the past tense verb [أمرنا] with a present tense verb [لا یأمر] to indicate continuity/permanence).

Ayah

قُلْ أَمَرَ رَبِّى بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ عِندَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ وَٱدْعُوهُ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ ۚ كَمَا بَدَأَكُمْ تَعُودُونَ (۲۹)
Say, ‘My Lord has enjoined justice,’ and [He has enjoined,] ‘Set your heart [on Him] at every occasion of prayer, and invoke Him, putting your exclusive faith in Him. Even as He brought you forth in the beginning, so will you return.’ (29)

Key Points

Qist (Justice/Equity) is the opposite of discrimination (or bias); meaning, the right of every person should be given to him, not to another. In this Ayah, several concepts are presented: nurturing/training (Rabbī), justice (al-Qist), worship (Aqīmū), congregation (wujūhakum), unity (masjid), supplication (wa-d‘ūh), intention and sincerity (mukhliṣīna lah), and resurrection and the Day of Judgment (ta‘ūdūn). The late Tabarsi considers the meaning of "وادعوه مخلصین له الدّین" (and call upon Him, making the religion sincerely His) to be an instruction for sincere supplication and humility after the prayer (Salat). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Tasbih (glorification) of Hadrat Zahra عليها السلام after every prayer is better than a thousand units (rak'ats) of prayer. [66] The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. Just as particles of dust have gathered within you through food, your decayed bones will also be gathered on the Day of Judgment. "كما بدأكم تعودون" (As He originated you, you will return). The same Power that created you from a single cell and a sperm can also revive your remains on the Day of Judgment. We also read in a narration that the meaning of "كما بدأكم تعودون" is that you will be resurrected upon the same belief on which you died: the believer upon his faith and the disbeliever upon his disbelief. "المؤمن على ایمانه و الكافر على كفره" [67] We read in a Hadith: On the Day of Judgment, people will be resurrected naked and barefoot, [68] just like the day they were born from their mother.

66) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 67) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan 68) Tafsir Furqan

Messages

1- Man is commanded to uphold equity and justice. "أمر ربّى بالقسط" (My Lord has commanded justice). And vile deeds are outside the scope of equity. "لا یأمر بالفحشاء... امر ربّى بالقسط" (He does not command indecency... My Lord has commanded justice).

2- Sincere adherence to religion is the foundation for the expansion of equity and justice. "امر ربّى بالقسط و اقیموا وجوهكم" (My Lord has commanded justice, and set your faces [towards Him]).

3- Preventing discrimination is mentioned alongside worship, and worship and prayer have greater value when accompanied by equity. "بالقسط و أقیموا" (with justice, and establish [prayer]).

4- Proper nurturing occurs within a just system, and equity (Qist) is among the affairs of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). "امر ربّى بالقسط" (My Lord has commanded justice).

5- Religion encompasses the social, devotional, and doctrinal dimensions of man. "امر ربّى بالقسط و اقیموا... وادعوه مخلصین له الدّین" (My Lord has commanded justice, and establish [prayer]... and call upon Him, making the religion sincerely His).

6- The mosque (masjid) is the base of sincerity, not hypocrisy (riya') or polytheism (shirk). "اقیموا وجوهكم عند كلّ مسجد... مخلصین" (Set your faces [towards Him] at every place of prostration... sincerely).

7- One of the conditions for supplication (Du'a) is sincerity of intention. "وادعوه مخلصین له الدّین" (and call upon Him, making the religion sincerely His).

8- Attention to and belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is a factor in man's establishment of justice and sincerity. "كما بدأكم تعودون" (As He originated you, you will return).

9- The initial creation is proof of the possibility of the Resurrection (Ma'ad). "كما بدأكم تعودون" (As He originated you, you will return).

Ayah

فَرِيقًا هَدَىٰ وَفَرِيقًا حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةُ ۗ إِنَّهُمُ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ (۳٠)
A part [of mankind] He has guided and a part has deserved [to be consigned to] error, for they took devils for guardians instead of Allah, and supposed they were guided. (30)

Messages

1- Guidance is the work of Allah, «فریقاً هَدى», but misguidance is due to our own poor choice. «اتّخذوا الشیاطین اولیاء»

2- As long as we have not cut ourselves off from Allah, all of Satan's temptation is compensable through His remembrance and repentance, «مَسّهم طائف من الشّیطان تذكّروا» (69). But cutting oneself off from Allah causes falling into the trap of Satan and his domination, which is not compensable. «حقّ علیهم الضّلالة انّهم اتّخذوا الشیطان اولیاء»

3- Man is free and can choose either the Divine guardianship or the guardianship of Satan. «انّهم اتّخذوا الشیاطین»

4- The viewpoint of the deviated is not realistic; rather, it is fanciful. «یحسبون انّهم مهتدون»

5- Intellectual deviation and compound ignorance (being misguided yet seeing oneself on the path of truth) is among the worst deviations. «یَحسبون انّهم مهتدون» As we read elsewhere: «قل هل ننبّئكم بالاخسرین أعمالاً. الّذین ضَلّ سعیهم فى الحیاة الدّنیا و هم یحسبون انّهم یحسنون صُنعاً» (70)

69) اعراف، ۲۰۱

70) كهف، ۱۰۳ تا ۱۰۴

Ayah

۞ يَـٰبَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ خُذُوا۟ زِينَتَكُمْ عِندَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ وَكُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ وَلَا تُسْرِفُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ (۳۱)
O Children of Adam! Put on your adornment on every occasion of prayer, and eat and drink, but do not waste; indeed, He does not like the wasteful. (31)

Key Points

Perhaps it can be said that whatever is expressed in the Quran with the address "O Children of Adam" relates to all human beings, all religions, and their commonalities. The Quran has called wealth and children "adornment," "المال و البنون زینة الحیاة الدّنیا" (Wealth and children are the adornment of the life of this world) [71]. Therefore, the verse might indicate that you should take your wealth and children with you when going to the mosque, so that with your wealth, you can address the economic problems of the Muslims, and with the presence of your children in mosques and congregations, you can solve the educational problems of the future generation. In narrations, the just Imam of the congregation, neatness of appearance, applying perfume and wearing beautiful clothes during prayer, raising hands during Ruku' (bowing) and Sujud (prostration) in prayer, and participation in Eid and Friday prayers have been counted among the instances of adornment (Zinah). [72] Imam Mujtaba عليه السلام used to wear his best clothes when praying and attending the mosque, and He would say: "انّ اللّه جمیل یحبّ الجمال فاتجمّل لربّى" (Verily, Allah is beautiful and loves beauty, so I adorn myself for my Lord), and then He would recite this verse. [73] Allah loves beauty and adornment; otherwise, He would not have commanded it, "خذوا زینتكم" (Take your adornment). Just as Islam is a natural religion, man also naturally enjoys adornment. Going to the mosque with adornment is a sign of respect for the servants of Allah, for the worship of Allah, and it also creates attraction and practically encourages others to come there. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Wealth is a divine trust (Wadi'ah) and deposit in the hands of man, and consumption must be far from extravagance (Israf). [74] Although the use of adornment and food is a natural and innate matter, in specific circumstances, such as the presence of the needy and the deprived, one must sympathize with them. Thus, we read in history that the type of clothing worn by Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, when people were living in relative prosperity, differed from the clothing of Imam Ali عليه السلام, when people were poor and destitute, because the social conditions of each were different. [75] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever possesses sustenance for one day but is not content and begs from people is among the extravagant (Musrifin). [76] Extravagance in food and overeating is the source of many physical and spiritual illnesses, leads to hard-heartedness, and deprives one of tasting the sweetness of worship. As the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "المعدة بیت كلّ داء" (The stomach is the home of every disease). A Christian physician, after hearing this verse and Hadith, said: The entire science of medicine is contained in this verse and this saying of your Prophet. [77] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Extravagance is only that which destroys wealth and harms the body. "انّما الاسراف فیما أتلف المال و اَضرّ بالبدن" [78] And in another narration, we read: Whatever is spent in the way of Allah, even if it is very much, is not extravagance, and whatever is used in the way of disobedience to Allah, even if it is little, is extravagance. [79]

71) كهف، 46 72) تفاسیر نورالثقلین، منهج الصادقین و اثنى عشرى 73) تفسیر مجمع البیان 74) تفسیر صافى 75) تفسیر اثنى عشرى 76) تفسیر فرقان 77) تفسیر مجمع البیان 78) كافى، ج 2، ص 499 79) تفسیر منهج الصادقین

Messages

1- The mosque, which is the base of the Muslims, must be neat, beautiful, and attractive. "خذوا زینتكم عند كلّ مسجد"

2- The best and most beautiful clothing is for the best place. "خذوا زینتكم عندكلّ مسجد"

3- Islam pays attention both to the inner aspect of prayer, "فى صلاتهم خاشعون" [80], and to its outer aspect. "زینتكم عند كلّ مسجد" Yes, in Islam, the outer and the inner, this world and the Hereafter, are intertwined.

4- Although adornment is valuable even in individual prayer, it holds special significance in congregation and the mosque. "عند كلّ مسجد"

5- First prayer, then food. "عند كلّ مسجد و كلوا و اشربوا" First attention to the soul and spirituality, then attention to the body.

6- Thriftiness is beloved by Allah, and the utilization of adornment and food must be far from extravagance. "ولا تسرفوا انّه لا یحبّ المسرفین"

80) مؤمنون، 2

Ayah

قُلْ مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَخْرَجَ لِعِبَادِهِۦ وَٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ مِنَ ٱلرِّزْقِ ۚ قُلْ هِىَ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا خَالِصَةً يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (۳۲)
Say, ‘Who has forbidden the adornment of Allah which He has brought forth for His servants, and the good things of [His] provision?’ Say, ‘These are for the faithful in the life of this world, and exclusively for them on the Day of Resurrection.’ Thus do We elaborate the signs for a people who have knowledge. (32)

Key Points

This Ayah considers adornment (zīnāt) lawful for humans. The Quran considers one of Allah's blessings to be the adornment of the heavens with stars for observers, «زیّنّاها للنّاظرین» (81). However, the desire for adornment must not lead a person to destruction, and its utilization must be controlled. Therefore, the Quran deems the display of women's adornment unlawful except to their husbands and their maḥram relatives. «لایبدین زینتهنّ الاّ لبعولتهنّ» (82).

Uthman ibn Maz'un came to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and informed him of his decision to castrate himself, cut off his lineage, and isolate himself, distancing himself from his wife, family, and society. The Prophet strictly forbade him from this action and said: To control desire, fast. Instead of rejected asceticism (riyāḍat) and monasticism (rahbāniyyat), go to the mosque and await the prayer. Instead of wandering the deserts, participate in battlefronts, Hajj, and Umrah. Use your wife and perfumes, give from your wealth to the deprived, and avoid and migrate from unlawful things. This is My path and method, and whoever turns away from this method and does not repent, the angels will prevent his entry to the Pool of Kawthar. (83)

Asim ibn Ziyad also decided to change his lifestyle and abstain from lawful things and pleasures. Imam Ali عليه السلام condemned that way of thinking. When he asked the Imam: Then why do you Yourself live so simply? The Imam replied: I am the leader of our community. Allah has made it incumbent upon leaders to keep their standard of living at the level of the weak members of society and to share in the grief of others. (84)

In dealing with the rigid Kharijites, when Imam Ali عليه السلام dispatched his representative, Ibn Abbas, to them, he instructed him to wear the best clothes, perfume himself, and ride a beautiful horse when approaching them. (85)

Some have interpreted the Ayah in this way: the provision and adornment of this world are mixed with various kinds of bitterness, but the provision and adornment of the Hereafter are pure and free from sorrow.

(81) Hijr, 16 (82) Nur, 31 (83) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi (84) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 410 (85) Tafsir Manhaj al-Sadiqin

Messages

1- The Prophet is responsible for combating innovations (bid‘ah). «قل مَن حرّم»

2- Islam opposes inappropriate asceticism (zuhd), unlawful self-mortification (riyāḍat), and monasticism (rahbāniyyat). «قل مَن حرّم»

3- The fundamental principle regarding the utilization of adornments and good things (ṭayyibāt) is permissibility, unless there is specific evidence for their prohibition. «مَن حرّم»

4- Appropriate use of adornment and encouraging people toward it is valuable. «زینة اللّه» because adornment is attributed to Allah.

5- The path to reaching Allah is not the abandonment of lawful and good things (ṭayyib), but rather their appropriate use and the observance of equity and justice. «قل مَن حرّم زینة اللّه...»

6- Islam is in harmony with human nature (fiṭrah) and is the religion of moderation. It responds positively to natural needs, considers beneficial things lawful, and prohibits harmful things. «مَن حرّم... أخرج لعباده»

7- Adornment, like food, is a human necessity. (Adornment is mentioned alongside edibles.) «زینة اللّه... و الطیّبات من الرّزق»

8- The primary purpose for creating blessings is the utilization and enjoyment of the believers, although the disbelievers also benefit. «قل هى للّذین آمنوا فى الحیاة الدنیا» Of course, elsewhere He says: «انّا جعلنا ما على الارض زینة لها لنبلوهم أیّهم احسن عملاً» (86) We have made whatever is on the earth an adornment for it, so that We may test them as to which of them is best in deed.

9- In utilizing worldly blessings, the believer and the disbeliever are equal, but the enjoyment of the Day of Resurrection is exclusive to the believers. « للّذین آمنوا خالصة یوم القیامة»

10- Knowledge and the knowledgeable hold a special status with Allah. «كذلك نفصّل الایات لقوم یعلمون»

(86) Kahf, 7

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ رَبِّىَ ٱلْفَوَٰحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ وَٱلْإِثْمَ وَٱلْبَغْىَ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَأَن تُشْرِكُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِۦ سُلْطَـٰنًا وَأَن تَقُولُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۳۳)
Say, ‘My Lord has only forbidden indecencies, the outward among them and the inward ones, and sin and undue aggression, and that you should ascribe to Allah partners for which He has not sent down any authority, and that you should attribute to Allah what you do not know.’ (33)

Key Points

«فواحش» (Fawāhish) is the plural of «فاحشة» (Fāhishah), meaning a sin whose ugliness is manifest to all, such as adultery (zinā). Since in the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) they considered its hidden performance permissible, this Ayah emphasizes its prohibition as well. «اِثم» (Ithm) is a sin that leads to the downfall of man, and «بَغى» (Baghy) is transgression against the rights of others. This Ayah encompasses various types of sins, including those related to belief (creed) and speech. In narrations (Riwāyāt), the meaning of the hidden sin is understood to be the acceptance of the guardianship (wilāyah) of oppressive leaders. [87] The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever issues a ruling without knowledge and awareness, the angels of the heavens and the earth curse him. [88] Zurarah asked Imam Sadiq علیه السلام: What is Allah's proof (Hujjah) upon His servants? The Imam replied: «أن یقولوا ما یعلمون و یقفوا عند ما لایعلمون» [89] (meaning) They should only say what they know, and if they do not know, they should remain silent and desist.

87) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn 88) Tafsir Namūneh 89) Tafsir Furqān

Messages

1- Divine prohibitions are for the purpose of man's training, growth, and perfection. «حرّم ربّى» (My Lord has forbidden)

2- Permissible things (Halāl) are numerous, but forbidden things (Harām) are few and limited, the instances of which are stated in the Quran and narrations. «انّما حرّم... ان یقولوا على اللّه ما لاتعلمون»

3- Sin is sin, and it possesses inherent and rational ugliness, even if people do not realize it. «وما بطن» (and what is hidden)

4- Polytheists (Mushrikūn) have no argument (proof) for their belief. «لم ینزّل به سلطاناً» (He has not sent down any authority for it)

5- In propagation (Tablīgh) and forbidding the evil (Nahy ‘anil-Munkar), first the permissible and recognized ways should be presented, and then the prohibitions and declaration of forbidden acts should follow. (The previous Ayah stated the permissible things, this Ayah states the forbidden things). «قل من حرّم زینة اللّه... انّما حرّم ربّى»

6- Proof (Burhān) and argumentation (Istidlāl) are a type of divine grace and favor that is cast upon the intellect and soul. «ما لم ینزّل به سلطاناً»

7- Attributing anything to Allah must be based on knowledge and proof. «ما لم ینزّل به سلطاناً» Yes, any claim, especially concerning existence (the universe), must be supported by proof and logic.

Ayah

وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (۳٤)
There is a [preordained] time for every nation: when their time comes, they shall not defer it by a single hour nor shall they advance it. (34)

Key Points

Based on this Ayah of the Quran, not only individuals, but also governments, nations, communities (Ummahs), and civilizations have a fixed term (ajal). When the term (ajal) arrives, it will not be delayed or advanced even for a single moment.

Messages

1- We should not be deceived by the world and its titles, and we should abstain from forbidden things (haram). «انّما حرّم ربّى... لكلّ اُمّة أجل»

2- In existence, no transformation is accidental or outside of the precise, calculated, and divine planning and order. Laws also govern communities. «لكلّ اُمّة أجل»

3- The opportunities and resources that are in our hands are temporary (will pass away), so we must make the most correct use of them to the best of our ability. «لكلّ اُمّة أجل»

4- Oppressors should not consider the divine respite as a sign of His favor upon them. «لكلّ اُمّة أجل» Because their time will also come to an end, just as we read elsewhere: «جعلنا لمهلكهم مَوعداً» (90)

5- Those who strive in the way of Allah (Mujahideen) should not fall into despair because of the dominance of the tyrants (Taghutian). They should strive, because the tyrants are also temporary. «لكلّ اُمّة أجل»

6- Groups continuously come to power, are tested, and are wiped out. «فاذا جاء أجلهم لایستأخرون ساعة و لایستقدمون»

90) Kahf, 59

Ayahs

يَـٰبَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ إِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّنكُمْ يَقُصُّونَ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِى ۙ فَمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰ وَأَصْلَحَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۳٥)
O Children of Adam! If there come to you apostles from among yourselves, recounting to you My signs, then those who are Godwary and righteous will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (35)
وَٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ عَنْهَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۳٦)
But those who deny Our signs and are disdainful of them, they shall be the inmates of the Fire and they shall remain in it [forever]. (36)

Key Points

| Khawf (fear) relates to future matters and is the opposite of security and comfort, while Huzn (grief) and sorrow relate to the past and what has been lost, and are the opposite of joy and happiness. [91]

91) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Qur'an

Messages

1- Sending messengers to convey Divine revelation is among the Divine traditions (Sunan). «یأتینّكم... یقصّون علیكم آیاتى»

2- One of the ways for speech to penetrate and be effective is the affinity (kinship) of the messenger with the people themselves. «منكم»

3- Man is free and possesses choice, and his destiny depends on his own inclination and action. «فمن اتّقى و أصلح»

4- The pious person (muttaqi) is a reformer and engages in positive activity, not isolation. «اتّقى وأصلح»

5- Piety (taqwa) and self-purification must precede the reformation of others, and reformers must themselves be pious. «اتّقى وأصلح»

6- The true believers and followers of the prophets are those who possess piety and engage in reformation. «یاتینّكم رسل... فمن اتّقى و اصلح» (And He did not say: «فمن آمن بهم»)

7- True tranquility is achieved under the shade of piety and reformation. «اتّقى و اصلح فلا خوف علیهم»

8- The consequence of denial, arrogance, and disobedience to the prophets is eternal punishment and Hellfire. «استكبروا... اصحاب النّار... فیها خالدون»

Ayah

فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِهِۦٓ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَنَالُهُمْ نَصِيبُهُم مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا يَتَوَفَّوْنَهُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالُوا۟ ضَلُّوا۟ عَنَّا وَشَهِدُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ كَـٰفِرِينَ (۳۷)
So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or denies His signs? Their share, as decreed in the Book, shall reach them. When Our messengers come to take them away, they will say, ‘Where is that which you used to invoke besides Allah?’ They will say, ‘They have forsaken us,’ and they will testify against themselves that they were faithless. (37)

Key Points

The intent behind slandering Allah in the verses of the Quran is usually associating partners with Him.
