[92] Of course, declaring something lawful or unlawful without proof is also innovation and slander against Allah, just as denying the Prophets is a type of attributing falsehood to Allah. In the Quran, individuals are introduced 15 times as the most unjust people, meaning those who have committed injustice both against themselves and their character and against society, and the root of all of this has been polytheism, disbelief, and obstinacy. [93] The phrase «نصیبهم من الكتاب» (their share of the Book) includes sustenance, life and death, happiness and wretchedness in this world, and the recompense for deeds and punishments on the Day of Resurrection, all of which are recorded in the Divine Preserved Tablet. [94]

92) Tafsir al-Mizan 93) Tafsir-e Nemooneh, Vol. 5, p. 184 94) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- Denying and slandering Allah and introducing innovation are among the greatest injustices. «فمن أظلم»

2- Every human being has a recorded and certain share in this world. «ینالهم نصیبهم من الكتاب»

3- Angels are appointed to seize the souls. «رسلنا یتوفّونهم»

4- The soul is the entire reality of man. «یتوفّونهم» (They take them [in death]) is used, and He did not say: «یتوفّون انفسهم» (They take their souls).

5- Upon seeing the signs of death, man awakens from the sleep of heedlessness and converses with the agents of soul-seizure, but to what avail? «یتوفّونهم قالوا... قالوا»

6- The first stage of man's interrogation begins from the moment of death. «أین ما كنتم»

7- Among the first questions on the Day of Resurrection is the question about the object of worship. «أین ما كنتم تدعون»

8- Anything other than Allah is nothing more than a mirage, and polytheists admit the futility of all objects of worship at the time of death. «قالوا ضلّوا عنّا»

9- The first judge on the Day of Resurrection is man's conscience. «شهدوا على انفسهم انّهم كانوا كافرین»

Ayah

قَالَ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ فِىٓ أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ فِى ٱلنَّارِ ۖ كُلَّمَا دَخَلَتْ أُمَّةٌ لَّعَنَتْ أُخْتَهَا ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا ٱدَّارَكُوا۟ فِيهَا جَمِيعًا قَالَتْ أُخْرَىٰهُمْ لِأُولَىٰهُمْ رَبَّنَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ أَضَلُّونَا فَـَٔاتِهِمْ عَذَابًا ضِعْفًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ قَالَ لِكُلٍّ ضِعْفٌ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تَعْلَمُونَ (۳۸)
He will say, ‘Enter the Fire, along with the nations of jinn and humans who passed before you!’ Every time that a nation enters [hell], it will curse its sister [nation]. When they all rejoin in it, the last of them will say about the first of them, ‘Our Lord, it was they who led us astray; so give them a double punishment of the Fire.’ He will say, ‘It is double for each [of you], but you do not know.’ (38)

Key Points

Among the shocking scenes of the Day of Judgment is the dialogue of the inhabitants of Hell with one another, especially with the leaders and elders who caused them to enter Hell. For this reason, even friends lacking piety become enemies there. «الاخلاّء یومئذ بعضهم لبعض عدوٌّ الاّ المتّقین» [95] Sometimes they request to see those who led them astray and to trample them underfoot. «أرِنَا الّذین اضلاّنا من الجنّ و الانس نجعلهما تحت أقدامنا» [96] And sometimes they consider obedience to their elders as the cause of their downfall. «أطعنا سادتنا و كُبرائنا و اضلّونا السبیلا» [97] However, man possesses free will and freedom of action and cannot place the blame for his deviation and error solely on others. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The leaders referred to in «هؤلاء اضلّونا» (These led us astray) are the corrupt leaders and the Imams of oppression and tyranny. [98] On the Day of Judgment, the leaders of tyranny and their followers will each be punished twofold, because the Quran, in response to the request of the misguided for double punishment for their leaders, states: Both the leaders and their followers will have double punishment, «لكلٍ ّ ضِعف» (for each is double). The leaders, because they both deviated themselves and led others astray; and their followers, both for their own deviation and for giving prominence to the false leaders through their obedience and support.

95) Zukhruf, 67 96) Fussilat, 29 97) Ahzab, 67 98) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- In Paradise, there is no malice or enmity; it is entirely peace and serenity. But in Hell, individuals curse and damn one another. «لَعَنت أختها» (one cursed her sister), just as we read in Ayah 25 of Surah Al-Ankabut: «یكفر بعضُكم ببعض و یَلعَنُ بعضُكم بعضاً»

2- Jinn, like humans, have obligations (Taklif), and the sinners of both have a similar fate and will be placed in the same Hell. «الجنّ والانس فى النار... لعنت اختها»

3- The disbelievers do not enter Hell all at once, but rather in sequence and turn. «كلما دخلت»

4- Non-Divine loyalties (Wilayat), affections, and supports will turn into malice, cursing, and enmity on the Day of Judgment. «لعنت اُختها»

5- On the Day of Judgment, everyone tries to shift the blame for their sin onto others or find an accomplice in crime. «هؤلاء اضلّونا»

6- Sometimes man experiences punishments of which he is unaware. «ولكن لا تعلمون»

Ayahs

وَقَالَتْ أُولَىٰهُمْ لِأُخْرَىٰهُمْ فَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا مِن فَضْلٍ فَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ (۳۹)
And the first of them will say to the last of them, ‘You have no merit over us! So taste the punishment because of what you used to earn.’ (39)
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ عَنْهَا لَا تُفَتَّحُ لَهُمْ أَبْوَٰبُ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَلَا يَدْخُلُونَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ حَتَّىٰ يَلِجَ ٱلْجَمَلُ فِى سَمِّ ٱلْخِيَاطِ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (٤٠)
Indeed, those who deny Our signs and are disdainful of them—the gates of the heaven will not be opened for them, nor shall they enter paradise until the camel passes through the needle’s eye, and thus do We requite the guilty. (40)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The deeds and souls of the believers are taken towards the heavens, and the gates of the heavens are opened for them. However, when the souls and deeds of the disbelievers are raised to the heavens, a sudden call is made: «اهبطوا الى السّجّین» (Take it down to Sijjin/the lowest hell). [99] «جَمَل» (Jamal) either means a camel, or a thick rope used to moor a ship on the shore. However, since the purpose here is to express the impossibility of disbelievers entering Paradise, the meaning of a camel and the camel passing through the eye of a needle is more appropriate. We read in Tafsir Namuneh: The word «جَمَل» is mostly used in the meaning of a camel, and considering the narrations, this meaning has greater relevance. It is also mentioned in the Gospel of Luke: It is easier for a camel to enter the eye of a needle than for the wealthy and statesmen to enter the highest Kingdom (of Heaven). [100] Perhaps it can be inferred from the apparent meaning of Ayah 40 that Paradise is in the heavens, and entry into Paradise requires passing through the heavenly gates. [101] Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The gates of the heavens are open at five times: during the battle of the Mujahidin (strugglers) of Islam, at the time of rainfall, during the recitation of the Quran, at the time of Fajr (dawn), and at the time of the call to Adhan (prayer). [102] Contrary to the fact that the gates of mercy of the heaven and the earth are opened for the pious believers, «ولو انّ اهل القُرى آمنوا واتّقوا لَفتَحنا علیهم بركات من السماء والارض» [103] (And if the people of the towns had believed and feared Allah, We would have opened upon them blessings from the heaven and the earth), the heavenly gates are closed upon the stubborn and denying disbelievers and will not be opened. «لاتُفتّح لهم ابواب السماء...»

99) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan 100) Gospel of Luke, Chapter 18, Verse 24 101) Tafasir Al-Mizan and Kabir 102) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 103) Al-A'raf, 96

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the misguided leaders will consider their followers partners in their punishment. «قالت اولاهم لاخراهم...»

2- The followers of polytheism have no advantage over their leaders to escape double punishment. «فما كان لكم علینا من فضل»

3- The destiny of man, even in the belief of the inhabitants of Hell, depends on his own actions. «فذوقوا العذاب بما كنتم تكسبون»

4- Human deeds and behavior are effective in the descent or non-descent of Divine mercy. «كذّبوا بآیاتنا و استكبروا عنها لا تفتّح لهم ابواب...»

5- Denying the Divine signs (Ayat) and rejecting religious knowledge causes all the gates of mercy and entry into Paradise to be blocked. «لا تفتّح لهم ابواب... ولا یدخلون الجنّة»

6- The Divine tradition is to deprive the criminals of His mercy on the Day of Judgment. «لا تفتّح لهم ابواب... و كذلك نجزى المجرمین»

Ayah

لَهُم مِّن جَهَنَّمَ مِهَادٌ وَمِن فَوْقِهِمْ غَوَاشٍ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤۱)
They shall have hell for their resting place, and over them shall be sheets [of fire], and thus do We requite the wrongdoers. (41)

Key Points

Mihaad (مِهاد) is derived from Mahd (مَهد), meaning a bed or resting place. The use of the term Mihaad for the inhabitants of Hell is a form of mockery and derision towards them, meaning their resting place is Hell. [104] Ghawāsh (غَواش) is the plural of Ghāshiyah (غاشیة), meaning a covering, and it is also used to refer to a tent. Allah has referred to the obstinate and turning-away group in Ayah 37 of this Surah as kāfir (disbeliever), in Ayah 40 as mujrim (criminal), and here as ẓālim (wrongdoer), because whoever denies the divine verses deserves all these titles. Just as we read in Ayah 254 of Surah Al-Baqarah: «والكافرون هم الظّالمون» The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, after reciting this Ayah, said: Hell has layers from above and below, and it is not known whether the upper layers are more numerous or the lower layers, and the disbelievers will be situated between the layers and will constantly be under pressure. Furthermore, the حضرت said: The bedding and covering of the disbelievers in the grave will be the fire of Hell. [105]

104) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an 105) Tafsir Namūneh

Messages

1- Hell will encompass the entire being of the disbelievers from all sides. «لهم من جهنّم مهاد و... غواش» Just as we read in other verses: «و انّ جهنّم لمحیطة بالكافرین» [106], «لهم من فوقهم ظلل من النار و من تحتهم ظلل» [107], and «یوم یغشاهم العذاب من فوقهم و من تحت أرجلهم» [108].

106) Ankabūt, 54 107) Zumar, 16 108) Ankabūt, 55

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ لَا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (٤۲)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds—We task no soul except according to its capacity—they shall be the inhabitants of paradise, and they shall remain in it [forever]. (42)

Messages

1- Eternal Paradise is the reward for the link between faith and righteous deeds. «والّذین آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات... اولئك اصحاب الجنّة»

2- Although the desired outcome is the performance of all worthy deeds, «و عملوا الصالحات», in practice, everyone is responsible only according to their capacity. «لا نكلّف نفساً الاّ وسعها»

3- In Islam, obligation (Taklif) is not overwhelming. «وسعها» As we read elsewhere: «وما جعل علیكم فى الدّین من حرج» [109] and «یرید اللّه بكم الیُسر و لا یرید بكم العُسر» [110]

109) حج، 77

110) بقره، 185

Ayah

وَنَزَعْنَا مَا فِى صُدُورِهِم مِّنْ غِلٍّ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهِمُ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ وَقَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى هَدَىٰنَا لِهَـٰذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لِنَهْتَدِىَ لَوْلَآ أَنْ هَدَىٰنَا ٱللَّهُ ۖ لَقَدْ جَآءَتْ رُسُلُ رَبِّنَا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ وَنُودُوٓا۟ أَن تِلْكُمُ ٱلْجَنَّةُ أُورِثْتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (٤۳)
We will remove whatever rancour there is in their breasts, and streams will run for them. They will say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, who guided us to this. We would have never been guided had not Allah guided us. Our Lord’s apostles had certainly brought the truth.’ And the call would be made to them: ‘This is paradise, which you have been given to inherit because of what you used to do!’ (43)

Key Points

Ghille (غِلّ) means secret penetration. The malice and envy that mysteriously penetrate the human soul are also called ghill. In the previous verses, we read that the inhabitants of Hell curse and damn one another. Here, [Allah] states that the inhabitants of Paradise hold no malice toward one another in their hearts; peace, purity, and love exist among all of them. Although there are differences in ranks within Paradise, none harbors malice, resentment, or envy toward another. The rivers of Paradise are full of water, because Allah said that the rivers are flowing instead of saying the water is flowing. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The rivers of Paradise flow from beneath the dwellings of the inhabitants of Paradise. [111] According to narrations (Ahadith), every believer and disbeliever has a place in Paradise and Hell, but the believer inherits the place of the disbeliever in Paradise, and the disbeliever inherits the place of the believer in Hell. [112] It is inferred from this narration that the doors of felicity and wretchedness are open to everyone, and no one is created for Paradise or Hell from the beginning; rather, his choice and action determine his path and destiny. [113] The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said to the people at Ghadir Khumm: "معاشر النّاس سلّموا على علىّ بامرة المؤمنین" (O people! Greet Ali as the Commander of the Faithful) and say: "الحمدللّه الّذى هدنا لهذا...". [114] Imam Hadi عليه السلام said: On the Day of Resurrection, the Prophet, Hadrat Ali, and all the Imams علیهم السلام will stand before the people, and at that moment, the Shi'a will say: "الحمدللّه الّذى هدانا لهذا...". [115]

111) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 160 112) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 113) Tafsir Namuneh 114) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 115) Tafsir Ithna Ashari; Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 418

Messages

1- Removing malice and creating intimacy is not easy or effortless. (Naza'na - «نزع», means to uproot with difficulty and effort.)

2- A life free of malice is a life of Paradise. («من غلّ»)

3- In Paradise, inner and outer purity are combined. (نزعنا ما فى صدورهم من غلّ تجرى من تحتهم الانهار)

4- The inhabitants of Paradise are engaged in the remembrance and praise of Allah. (قالوا الحمدللّه)

5- The inhabitants of Paradise are grateful for Allah's favor and are not arrogant about their guidance. (الحمدللّه الّذى هدانا)

6- The inhabitants of Paradise do not rely on their own deeds and actions. (هدانا اللّه)

7- Intellect, knowledge, and effort alone are not sufficient; Divine grace and assistance are also necessary for guidance. (لولا ان هدانا اللّه)

8- The Prophets are the means of guidance, and their guidance is accompanied by truth. They themselves, their words, their actions, their methods, and their promises are all truth. (لقد جاءت رسل ربّنا بالحقّ)

9- Paradise is in exchange for action, not hope or wishful thinking. (اورثتموها بما كنتم تعملون) Hadrat Ali عليه السلام says: Do not be among those who desire the Hereafter without performing deeds. (لاتكن ممن یرجو الاخرة بغیر العمل) [116]

116) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 135

Ayah

وَنَادَىٰٓ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلنَّارِ أَن قَدْ وَجَدْنَا مَا وَعَدَنَا رَبُّنَا حَقًّا فَهَلْ وَجَدتُّم مَّا وَعَدَ رَبُّكُمْ حَقًّا ۖ قَالُوا۟ نَعَمْ ۚ فَأَذَّنَ مُؤَذِّنٌۢ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن لَّعْنَةُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤٤)
The inhabitants of paradise will call out to the inmates of the Fire, ‘We found what our Lord promised us to be true; did you find what your Lord promised you to be true?’ ‘Yes,’ they will say. Then a caller will announce in their midst, ‘May Allah’s curse be on the wrongdoers!’ (44)

Key Points

Regarding who the caller (mu'adhdhin) of the Day of Judgment will be, various opinions have been stated, including: Allah, Israfil, Jibril, the guardians of Hell, and the guardians of Paradise. However, in Shi'a narrations [117] and some Sunni hadiths (such as the narrations of Hakim Haskani), we read that the caller is Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. Just as he recited Surah Bara'ah (which included the declaration of dissociation from the polytheists in this world) in Mecca, the recitation of the decrees of dissociation and divine curse upon the polytheists in the Hereafter will also be from the tongue of Ali عليه السلام. [118] Similarly, that noble figure will also greet the inhabitants of Paradise. [119] After the Battle of Badr, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله addressed the slain polytheists, saying: «انّا وجدنا ما وعد ربّنا حقّاً فهل وجدتم ما وعد ربّكم حقّاً» (Indeed, we have found what our Lord promised us to be true. Have you also found what your Lord promised you to be true?). The companions asked: How do you speak to bodies that are dead? The Prophet replied: By Allah! All of them heard my words better than you did. Similarly, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام also addressed the slain Kharijites using this same verse. [120]

117) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 118) Tafsir Namuneh 119) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 120) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the inhabitants of Paradise and the inhabitants of Hell will converse with each other. «نادى...» Paradise and Hell are such that the inhabitants of Paradise, after settling in, can inquire about the inhabitants of Hell.

2- On the Day of Judgment, believers and disbelievers alike will find Allah's promises to be true and realized. «قد وجدنا ما وعدَنا ربُّنا حقّاً»

3- Allah extracts confession from the inhabitants of Hell through the people of Paradise, in order to inflict greater shame and pressure upon them. [121] «قالوا نعم»

4- The court of the Day of Judgment concludes with the declaration, "The curse of Allah be upon the wrongdoers." «لعنة اللّه على الظالمین»

121) Tafsir Al-Mizan

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا وَهُم بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ كَـٰفِرُونَ (٤٥)
—Those who bar [others] from the way of Allah, and seek to make it crooked, and disbelieve in the Hereafter. (45)

Key Points

The path of Allah is the path of Monotheism (Tawhid), submission (Taslim), faith (Iman), migration (Hijrah), and struggle (Jihad). Oppressors, by creating doubts and temptations, through propaganda and undermining, by establishing innovations and superstitions, and also by confronting the leaders of truth, creating despair, and other acts of sabotage, seek to close the path of Allah or create change and deviation within it.

Messages

1- Any prevention of the path of Allah and the creation of deviation in it is oppression (zulm). "لعنة اللّه على الظالمین الّذین یصدّون..." (The curse of Allah is upon the oppressors who hinder...) And cultural oppression is among the greatest forms of oppression. "و من اظلم ممن افترى على اللّه" (And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah?)

2- If the enemy is able, he fights openly and completely closes the path of truth, "یصدّون" (they hinder), and through various schemes, he makes the path of truth appear crooked and creates deviation in its course. "یبغونها عوجاً" (they desire it to be crooked).

3- The oppressors, the hinderers of the path of truth, the destroyers of religion, and the deniers of the Day of Judgment will all be inhabitants of Hell. (This Ayah and the previous Ayah)

Ayah

وَبَيْنَهُمَا حِجَابٌ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱلْأَعْرَافِ رِجَالٌ يَعْرِفُونَ كُلًّۢا بِسِيمَىٰهُمْ ۚ وَنَادَوْا۟ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ أَن سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ لَمْ يَدْخُلُوهَا وَهُمْ يَطْمَعُونَ (٤٦)
There will be a veil between them. And on the Elevations will be certain men who recognize each of them by their mark. They will call out to the inhabitants of paradise, ‘Peace be to you!’ (They will not have entered it, though they would be eager to do so. (46)

Key Points

The meaning of al-hijab (the barrier) in this Ayah is perhaps the same wall that we read about in Ayah 13 of Surah Al-Hadid, which is erected between the people of Paradise and the people of Hellfire, the outer side of which is torment and the inner part of which is mercy. «فضرب بینهم بسور له باب باطنه فیه الرّحمة و ظاهره من قبله العذاب» (122)

A'raf is the plural of 'urf, meaning a high place. This Surah is also named Al-A'raf because of this very Ayah, and in the Quran, the A'raf and its inhabitants are mentioned only in this instance. In narrations and commentaries, various opinions are seen regarding the People of the A'raf (Ahl al-A'raf). Some consider them to be the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah) who are stationed on the heights between Paradise and Hell, recognizing everyone by their countenances. They greet and congratulate the people of Paradise and are concerned about the fate of the people of Hellfire. However, some introduce them as weak individuals who have both sins and acts of worship and are awaiting Allah's grace (as inferred from Ayah 106 of Surah At-Tawbah). Perhaps it can be said that the main occupants and core of the A'raf are the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah), while the weak individuals are on the periphery. The righteous go to Paradise, and the criminals go to Hell. The average and weak individuals remain detained on the A'raf, awaiting their fate, but the friends of Allah who are stationed on the A'raf rise to their aid and intercede for them. This explanation is derived from combining the views of the commentators and the narrations. In narrations, we read that the Companions of the A'raf (Ashab al-A'raf) are martyrs who have deficiencies in their records (123), or they are individuals whose good deeds and bad deeds are equal. If Allah sends them to Hell, it is because of their sins, and if He sends them to Paradise, it is out of His mercy. (124)

(122) Tafsir Al-Mizan (123) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur (124) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 381

Messages

1- Worldly behavior and characteristics shape a person's countenance in such a way that others recognize him by sight on the Day of Judgment. «یعرفون كلاًّ بسیماهم» Of course, recognition in this manner is also possible in this world, just as Allah says to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله regarding the hypocrites: «و لو نشاء لاریناكهم فلعرفتهم بسیماهم و لتعرفنّهم فى لحن القول» (125)

2- Divine grace and favor are necessary for entering Paradise and becoming inhabitants of Paradise. «اصحاب الجنّة... هم یطمعون»

(125) Muhammad, 30

Ayah

۞ وَإِذَا صُرِفَتْ أَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ تِلْقَآءَ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلنَّارِ قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا مَعَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤۷)
And when their look is turned toward the inmates of the Fire, they will say, ‘Our Lord, do not put us among the wrongdoing lot!’) (47)

Key Points

The dwellers of the Heights look at the people of Paradise, recognize them, and greet them with peace, but they do not look at the people of the Fire; rather, their eyes fall upon them unintentionally. «صُرِفَت ابصارهم»

The companions of A'raf do not say in their supplication, "Do not place us among the people of the Fire," but rather they say, "Do not make us companions of the wrongdoers." It seems that companionship with the oppressor is worse than the fire of Hell. [126]

126) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani

Ayah

وَنَادَىٰٓ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْأَعْرَافِ رِجَالًا يَعْرِفُونَهُم بِسِيمَىٰهُمْ قَالُوا۟ مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنكُمْ جَمْعُكُمْ وَمَا كُنتُمْ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ (٤۸)
The occupants of the Elevations will call out to certain men whom they recognize by their marks, ‘Your rallying did not avail you, nor what you used to disdain. (48)

Key Points

Salman al-Farsi says: I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله say more than ten times: «یا علىّ انّك و الاوصیاء من بعدك اعراف بین الجنّة و النار», [۱۲۷] O Ali! You and the Imams after you are the Companions of the A'raf (the Heights) between Paradise and Hell, and whoever has knowledge (recognition) of you will enter Paradise, and whoever denies your Wilayah (guardianship/authority) will enter Hell. [۱۲۸]

127) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 337 128) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- In the Hereafter, besides the divine punishment, the inhabitants of Hell will also receive reproaches from people. «قالوا ما اغنى عنكم...»

2- Wealth, power, friends, and supporters are not factors for salvation. «ما أغنى عنكم جمعكم» Man himself will confess to this truth in the Hereafter. «ما أغنى عنّى مالیه» [۱۲۹]

129) Al-Haqqah, 28

Ayah

أَهَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ٱلَّذِينَ أَقْسَمْتُمْ لَا يَنَالُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ ۚ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْجَنَّةَ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَآ أَنتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ (٤۹)
Are these the ones concerning whom you swore that Allah will not extend them any mercy?’ ‘Enter paradise! You shall have no fear, nor shall you grieve.’ (49)

Key Points

In this world, the arrogant disbelievers belittle the believers and say that the mercy of Allah does not include them. They are heedless that faith and righteous deeds make them worthy of Divine mercy, and they will receive the address, "اُدخلوا الجنّة" (Enter Paradise). Receiving Divine mercy requires faith and righteous deeds, not wealth or status. Furthermore, the mercy of Allah is not in the hands of the arrogant oppressors that they may distribute it among whomever they wish. "أهم یقسمون رحمة ربّك" (Is it they who distribute the Mercy of your Lord?) [130]

130) Az-Zukhruf, 32

Messages

1- The poverty and obscurity of the believers in this world are not signs of their deprivation in the Hereafter. "أهؤلاء الّذین..."

2- Let us not judge prematurely regarding who is worthy or unworthy of receiving Divine mercy. "أقسمتم لا ینالهم اللّه برحمة"

3- In Paradise, sorrow and grief have no place. "و لا أنتم تحزنون"

Ayah

وَنَادَىٰٓ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ أَنْ أَفِيضُوا۟ عَلَيْنَا مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ أَوْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ حَرَّمَهُمَا عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٥٠)
The inmates of the Fire will call out to the inhabitants of paradise, ‘Pour on us some water, or something of what Allah has provided you.’ They will say, ‘Allah has indeed forbidden these two to the faithless!’ (50)

Key Points

One of the names of the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah) is "Yawm al-Tanād" [131]. This means the day when calls are raised, and seeking help and shouting are prevalent, and the people of Paradise and the people of Hell call out to one another.

131) Ghāfir, 32

Messages

1- The people of Paradise and the people of Hell converse with each other and hear each other's voices. "و نادى ..."

2- The first request of the people of Hell is water. "من الماء..."

3- Those who did not prepare provisions in this world for the Hereafter will have their hands of begging and need stretched out before everyone on the Day of Resurrection. "افیضوا علینا من الماء..."

4- The lamentation of the criminals on the Day of Resurrection is useless. "انّ اللّه حرّمهما"

5- The blessings of the Hereafter are exclusive to the believers. "حرّمهما على الكافرین"

6- Allah and the people of Paradise are not stingy, but the punishment for the disbelievers who tormented the poor believers in this world is the prohibition of blessings on the Day of Resurrection. "حرّمهما على الكافرین"

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ دِينَهُمْ لَهْوًا وَلَعِبًا وَغَرَّتْهُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ فَٱلْيَوْمَ نَنسَىٰهُمْ كَمَا نَسُوا۟ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِهِمْ هَـٰذَا وَمَا كَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ (٥۱)
Those who took their religion for diversion and play and whom the life of the world had deceived. So today, We will forget them as they forgot the encounter of this day of theirs, and as they used to impugn Our signs. (51)

Key Points

"Lahw" (لَهو) means man's negligence regarding essential tasks, and "la'ib" (لَعِب) is when a person has an imaginary and unreal goal. [132] Imam Reza عليه السلام said regarding "ننساهم" (We will forget them): It means We will abandon them, because just as they had abandoned the Day of Judgment and preparation for that day, We too will abandon them to their own state on that day. [133]

132) Tafsir al-Mizan 133) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- Mocking religion, being deluded by the world, forgetting the Hereafter, and denying divine signs are all characteristics of the disbelievers. «الكافرین الّذین اتّخذوا»

2- The disbelievers treat the laws of religion, which are serious matters, as a game. «دینهم لهواً» Conversely, they took the world, which is nothing more than a plaything, seriously.

3- Belittling religion leads to humiliation in the Hereafter. Those who treat religion as a game will plead desperately on the Day of Judgment. «أفیضوا علینا - دینهم لهواً»

4- Worldly life is deceptive. «غرّتهم الحیاة الدنیا»

5- Being deceived by the world is the basis for treating religion as a game. «لهواً و لعباً و غرّتهم...»

6- Divine punishment is proportionate to our deeds. «ننساهم كما نسوا»

7- Continuous denial of Allah's signs leads to deprivation from Paradise and its blessings. «ما كانوا بآیاتنایجحدون»

8- Forgetting Allah in this world causes man to be forgotten on the Day of Judgment. «ننساهم كما نسوا» Just as in another place, He considers the remembrance of Him to be the cause of His remembrance of man. «فاذكرونى أذكركم» [134]

9- Continuous denial without repentance is the key to human misery and destruction. «كانوا... یجحدون»

134) Baqarah, 152

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ جِئْنَـٰهُم بِكِتَـٰبٍ فَصَّلْنَـٰهُ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ هُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥۲)
Certainly We have brought them a Book, which We have elaborated with knowledge, as a guidance and mercy for a people who have faith. (52)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «انّ اللّه سبحانه لم یعظ احداً بمثل هذا القرآن...» Allah has not admonished anyone like He has admonished with this Quran, because the Quran is the firm and secure rope of Allah, the spring of hearts and the fountainhead of knowledge, and there is no polish for the human heart and soul except the Quran, despite the fact that those who remembered have gone, and a handful of forgetful ones remain. (135)

135) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 176

Messages

1- Allah completes the argument (establishes proof) through the Book and saves man from being deceived by the world, forgetting the Hereafter, and denying the verses (mentioned in the previous Ayah). «لقدجئناهم بكتاب»

He has hundreds of lights, yet he goes astray. Let him fall and see his own recompense.

2- Bringing the Book of guidance and mercy is the work of Allah, Who revealed it section by section and gradually. «لقد جئناهم بكتاب فصّلناه»

3- The revelation of Allah is based on Divine knowledge of the needs of man and the universe. «بكتاب فصّلناه على علم»

4- The Divine Book and knowledge are extremely magnificent. «كتابٍ... علمٍ» Both are indefinite (Nakhirah).

5- Guidance through the Quran is among the greatest mercies and favors of Allah upon the believers. «هدىً و رحمةً لقوم یؤمنون»

6- The Quran guides those who possess faith, not stubborn and fault-finding people. «لقوم یؤمنون»

Ayah

هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا تَأْوِيلَهُۥ ۚ يَوْمَ يَأْتِى تَأْوِيلُهُۥ يَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ نَسُوهُ مِن قَبْلُ قَدْ جَآءَتْ رُسُلُ رَبِّنَا بِٱلْحَقِّ فَهَل لَّنَا مِن شُفَعَآءَ فَيَشْفَعُوا۟ لَنَآ أَوْ نُرَدُّ فَنَعْمَلَ غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ ۚ قَدْ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (٥۳)
Do they not consider [the consequences of] its fulfillment? The day when its fulfillment comes, those who had forgotten it before will say, ‘Our Lord’s apostles had certainly brought the truth. If only we had some intercessors to intercede for us, or we would be returned, so that we may do differently from what we did!’ They have certainly ruined their souls, and what they used to fabricate has forsaken them. (53)

Key Points

The meaning of ta'wil (interpretation/outcome) in this Ayah, based on the context of the subsequent sentences, is the realization of Allah's threats and promises regarding punishment and reward on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). In Ayah 43 of this Surah, the people of Paradise were saying: «لقد جاءت رسل ربّنا بالحقّ» (Indeed, the messengers of our Lord had come with the truth). Here, the inhabitants of the Fire (Dozakh) make the same confession. Since the previous Ayah relates to the revelation of the "Book" (Kitab), some commentators have said that the meaning of «نسوه» (they forgot Him/it) is the forgetting of the Divine Book.

Messages

1- Allah, in the Quran, reproaches the deniers who will not believe until they witness the realization of His threats. «هل ینظرون...»

2- Cries, confessions, and wishes on the Day of Judgment will be futile. Therefore, we must take the warnings of the Quran seriously in this world. «یوم یأتى تأویله یقول...»

3- The great loss is forgetting the Quran and the Day of Judgment. «نسوه»

4- Qiyamah is the Day of awareness and awakening. «قد جاءت رسل ربّنا بالحقّ»

5- The mission of the prophets is aimed at the education of humanity and is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «قد جاءت رسل ربّنا بالحقّ»

6- Disbelievers do not take religion and the Hereafter seriously. When they realize their mistake on the Day of Judgment, they wish to return, but this wish is futile. «او نردّ فنعمل» (Or could we be sent back so we could act [righteously]). In another place, He says: Even if they were returned, they would continue the same deeds. «ولو ردّوا لعادوا لما نُهوا عنه» (136)

7- Intercession (Shafa'ah) is not for everyone. «فهل لنا من شفعاء»

8- On the Day of Judgment, there will be no sign or effect of tyrants (Taghut), idols, wealth, or power. «ضلّ عنهم...»

(136) Al-An'am, 28

Ayah

إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ يُغْشِى ٱلَّيْلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ يَطْلُبُهُۥ حَثِيثًا وَٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ وَٱلنُّجُومَ مُسَخَّرَٰتٍۭ بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ ۗ أَلَا لَهُ ٱلْخَلْقُ وَٱلْأَمْرُ ۗ تَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٥٤)
Indeed your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, and then settled on the Throne. He draws the night’s cover over the day, which pursues it swiftly, and [He created] the sun, the moon, and the stars, [all of them] disposed by His command. Look! All creation and command belong to Him. Blessed is Allah, the Lord of all the worlds. (54)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the vanishing of everything and everyone on the Day of Judgment, except Allah, "ضلّ عنهم..." (they vanished from them...). Here, the true Lord is being introduced.

Yawm (یوم) is different from Nahār (نهار). Nahār refers to the day in contrast to the night, but Yawm sometimes means day, sometimes means a full day and night (24 hours), and sometimes refers to an era or a period of time (dawr). In this Ayah, the latter meaning is intended.

Tabārak (تبارك) comes from Barakah (بركت), meaning permanence and endurance. Barak (بَرَك) is the chest of the camel; when the camel lays its chest on the ground, it signifies its stability and remaining in place. Consequently, Barakah is applied to any enduring and lasting quality, and to any being that has a long life or continuous effects. A Birkah (بِركه) is also a place where water remains and is stored for a long period.

Considering the context clues present in this Ayah and other verses of the Quran, the intent of khalq (خلق - creation) is the initial creation, and the intent of amr (امر - command/affair) is the establishment and setting of the laws governing existence, which guide the created beings along their path. (137)

Istawa ‘ala al-‘Arsh (استوى على العرش) is a metaphor for complete power, dominion, planning, guidance, and administration of the universe. In the Persian language, too, seating someone on the throne or sitting on the throne means gaining mastery over affairs and taking control of power and governance. Of course, it is possible that the ‘Arsh (Throne) is a metaphor for the world beyond matter, and the Kursī (Footstool) is a metaphor for the material world, just as we read in Ayah al-Kursī: "وسع كرسیّه السموات و الارض" (His Kursī encompasses the heavens and the earth). (138)

Just as Allah made the sun, moon, and stars subservient to His creatures for their guidance and benefit, He also placed the Prophethood (Risālah), the Imamate (Imāmah), and the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام in the path of their benefit and guidance, as the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said, according to both Shi'a and Sunni narrations: "النّجوم أمان لاهل الارض و اهل بیتى أمان لامّتى" (The stars are a security for the people of the earth, and my Ahl al-Bayt are a security for my nation). (139)

Although Allah has the power to create the entire universe in an instant, deliberation (ta’annī) and gradualness are His tradition (sunnah). Therefore, He created existence in six periods (or days). Similarly, baseless haste and rush are a satanic matter and a path for Satan's influence and dominance. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "التّأنّى من الرّحمن و العجلة من الشیطان" (Deliberation is from the All-Merciful, and haste is from Satan). Imam Ali علیه السلام also said: If God had willed, He would have created the heavens and the earth in less than the blink of an eye, but He made order and passage of time an example for His trustees and a proof against His creation. (140)

Mawlawi says: Haste and rush are the deceit of Satan, Patience and reckoning are the nature of the All-Merciful. With deliberation, existence came from God, Until in six days, this earth and the spheres were made. Otherwise, He was capable, by Kun Fayakūn (Be, and it is), To bring forth a hundred earths and spheres. That Glorious One (Allah) brings man forth little by little, Until He makes him a complete man at forty years. This deliberation is for your teaching, Be patient in your work, come late but correctly.

(137) Tafsir Namūneh (138) Tafsir Namūneh (139) Tafsir Furqān (140) Tafsir Ithnā ‘Asharī

Messages

1- The Creator of existence is worthy of planning and managing it. "ربّكم اللّه الّذى خلق..." (Your Lord is Allah, Who created...)

2- The Throne (‘Arsh) is the center of Allah's management and decision-making. "استوى على العرش" (He established Himself upon the Throne)

3- The continuous pursuit of night and day is only possible if the Earth is spherical and moving. "یطلبه حثیثاً" (it pursues it rapidly)

4- Existence has both order and submission to Allah's will. "مسخرات بأمره" (subservient by His command)

5- Both creation (khalq) and the administration (amr) of the created beings belong to Allah. "له الخلق والامر" (To Him belongs the creation and the command)

6- Allah is the source of blessings (barakāt). "تبارك اللّه" (Blessed is Allah)

7- The descent of blessings stems from the Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah. "تبارك اللّه ربّ العالمین" (Blessed is Allah, Lord of the worlds)

8- The entire universe is in a state of perfection and under Divine nurturing. "ربّ العالمین" (Lord of the worlds)

Ayah

ٱدْعُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ تَضَرُّعًا وَخُفْيَةً ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ (٥٥)
Supplicate your Lord, beseechingly and secretly. Indeed, He does not like the transgressors. (55)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, during a journey, saw some companions praying loudly. The Prophet told them: Pray calmly. [141] Prophet Zachariah also called upon Allah secretly and with sincerity. «نادى ربّه نداءً خفیّاً» [142] Because it is a sign of etiquette, just as Allah tells the believers: Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, like the loud speech of some of you to others. «لاترفعوا اصواتكم فوق صوت النبىّ كجهر بعضكم لبعض» [143] And it is possible that a loud voice in worship and supplication may pave the way for ostentation and hypocrisy (Riya). Supplication (Tadarru') and lamentation in prayer are signs of a person's attention to his own shortcomings and needs, and a factor in breaking pride and selfishness. Because whoever considers himself strong and wealthy will not lament or make requests before anyone. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Supplication (Tadarru') in prayer is raising the hands towards the sky and praying. [144] The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله also prayed in this manner, and Salman al-Farsi narrated from Him that He said: Allah is too shy to turn back empty a hand that is raised in prayer. [145]

141) Tafsirs Majma‘ al-Bayan and Kabir Fakhr Razi 142) Maryam, 3 143) Hujurat, 2 144) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 481 145) Tafsir Qurtubi

Messages

1- After knowing Allah (Theology), worshipping Allah is necessary. «اُدعوا ربّكم» In the previous Ayah, knowing Allah was discussed; in this Ayah, worshipping Allah (is discussed).

2- It is better for supplication to be done by seeking help through the word Rabb (Lord), accompanied by humility (Tadarru'), lamentation, and secrecy. «اُدعوا ربّكم تضرّعاً و خفیة» Secret supplication is far from Riya (hypocrisy/show-off) and closer to sincerity.

3- Supplication is effective when it is sincere and accompanied by humility. «تضرّعاً و خفیة»

4- We should call upon Allah with our whole being and with complete submission, and our tongue should represent our entire existence. «اُدعوا... تضرّعاً»

5- The arrogant and the transgressors are deprived of Divine grace and affection. «انّه لا یحبّ المعتدین»

6- Not supplicating, supplicating without humility, and hypocritical supplication (Riya'i Du'a) are examples of rebellion and transgression. «المعتدین»

Ayah

وَلَا تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلَـٰحِهَا وَٱدْعُوهُ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا ۚ إِنَّ رَحْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ قَرِيبٌ مِّنَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (٥٦)
And do not cause corruption on the earth after its restoration, and supplicate Him with fear and hope: indeed Allah’s mercy is close to the virtuous. (56)

Key Points

The previous Ayah discussed the relationship between man and Allah, while this Ayah discusses the relationship between man and people. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Allah reformed the earth through the Prophet and the Quran, and He said: Do not cause corruption after its reformation. (146) Indeed, the advent of every prophet brings about a great social reformation, and deviation from their religion and commands is a factor leading to corruption and ruin in society. Between the two commands of "اُدعوا" (call upon Him) in the previous Ayah and this Ayah, the phrase "لا تفسدوا فى الارض" (do not cause corruption on the earth) is placed to indicate that verbal supplication must be accompanied by efforts toward social reform, not merely praying with the tongue while causing corruption in action. This Ayah and the previous Ayah have outlined the conditions for the perfection of supplication, its etiquette, and the grounds for its acceptance, which are:

A: Supplication accompanied by humility/earnestness. "تضرّعا" B: Supplication secretly and far from ostentation (Riya). "تضرّعا و خفیة" C: Supplication without transgressing the bounds of truth. "انّه لا یحبّ المعتدین" (Indeed, He does not like the transgressors) D: Supplication accompanied by fear and hope. "خوفاً و طمعا" E: Supplication without causing ruin/corruption. "لا تفسدوا" F: Supplication accompanied by goodness/virtue. "المحسنین"

Luqman the Wise said to his son: My son! If they were to split open the heart of a believer, they would find two lights in it: one, the light of divine fear, and the other, the light of hope and mercy, both of which are equal. (147)

146) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 58 147) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- Even a reformed society is exposed to danger. "ولاتفسدوا... بعد اصلاحها"

2- Reformist revolutionaries are not independent of supplication and intimate prayer (Munajat). "وادعوه خوفاً..."

3- Do not destroy the flourishing parts of the earth. "ولاتفسدوا فى الارض بعد اصلاحها"

4- Man must be between fear and hope (Khawf wa Raja). Allah has called this balance "Ihsan" (goodness). "قریب من المحسنین"

5- The conduct and method of the virtuous are to avoid corruption. "لاتفسدوا... قریب من المحسنین"

6- Ihsan (goodness) is the ground for receiving divine mercy, and without it, the expectation of mercy is misplaced. "قریب من المحسنین" Indeed, answered supplication is accompanied by virtuous action.

7- Extending the hand of need toward the Truth (Allah) and avoiding corruption is Ihsan. "و لاتفسدوا... و ادعوه... قریب من المحسنین"

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُرْسِلُ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ بُشْرًۢا بَيْنَ يَدَىْ رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَقَلَّتْ سَحَابًا ثِقَالًا سُقْنَـٰهُ لِبَلَدٍ مَّيِّتٍ فَأَنزَلْنَا بِهِ ٱلْمَآءَ فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦ مِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (٥۷)
It is He who sends forth the winds as harbingers of His mercy. When they bear [rain-] laden clouds, We drive them toward a dead land and send down water on it, and with it We bring forth all kinds of crops. Thus shall We raise the dead; maybe you will take admonition. (57)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the knowledge of Allah (Theology) and the worship of Allah were discussed; here, the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is discussed. For both the Origin (Mabda') and the Resurrection (Ma'ad), arguments are presented using natural phenomena and the system of creation. The word "أَقَلَّتْ" (aqallat) means to carry easily, and this indicates that the winds are so strong that they effortlessly move heavy, water-laden clouds.

Messages

1- Natural laws, despite all their complexity and order, are under the will of the Lord and should not distract man from the Origin of existence. «هو الّذى یرسل الریاح» (Do not become engrossed in scientific formulas and material laws; the guidance and movement of the winds, the descent of rain, and the growth of plants are all by the planning of Allah.)

2- Deniers of the Resurrection have no proof; they merely find it improbable (istib'ad). Presenting examples of life in nature—such as the gathering of the constituent materials of pears, apples, and pomegranates that are scattered in the soil—is similar to the gathering of the particles of the dead. These examples are sufficient to remove that sense of improbability. «كذلك نخرج الموتى»

3- Death does not mean annihilation and non-existence, but rather a change of state, just as dead earth does not mean non-existent or destroyed earth. «سقناه لبلد میّت... فاخرجنا... كذلك نخرج الموتى»

Ayah

وَٱلْبَلَدُ ٱلطَّيِّبُ يَخْرُجُ نَبَاتُهُۥ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِۦ ۖ وَٱلَّذِى خَبُثَ لَا يَخْرُجُ إِلَّا نَكِدًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُصَرِّفُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَشْكُرُونَ (٥۸)
The good land—its vegetation comes out by the permission of its Lord, and as for that which is bad, it does not come out except sparsely. Thus do We paraphrase the signs variously for a people who give thanks. (58)

Key Points

The word nakd (نَكد) means something in which there is no good. Nabat nakd (نبات نَكد) means a scarce and useless plant. Salty ground does not yield hyacinths; Do not waste seed and effort in it. If we look at this Ayah with a broad perspective, perhaps one of the manifestations of the pure land that yields pure produce is the pure family. A pure essence is required to be receptive to grace, Otherwise, not every stone and clay becomes a pearl or coral. The verses of the Quran are like the rain of mercy. When recited to receptive individuals, they bring knowledge, love, faith, and effort. But for the unworthy, they only increase obstinacy and enmity: «و ننزّل من القرآن ما هو شفاء و رحمة للمؤمنین و لایزید الظالمین الاّ خساراً» [148] The rain, whose nature's gentleness is undeniable, Grows tulips in the garden and weeds in the salty ground. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «مثل ما بعثنى اللّه من الهدى و العلم كمثل الغیث الكثیر اصاب ارضاً...» My mission to the people with knowledge and guidance is like the falling of abundant rain, which some lands absorb and some do not. Just as some accept My instructions and become scholars ('alim) and jurists (faqih), others do not raise their heads and refuse to accept the guidance. [149] Thus, the pure heart (qalb tayyib) is like the pure land (tayyib), and the wicked heart (qalb khabith) is like the wicked and barren land.

148) Isra, 82 149) Tafsir Manhaj al-Sadiqin

Messages

  1. The mere descent of mercy is not enough for felicity; the receptivity and capacity of the location are also necessary. «والبلد الطیّب»
  2. Although the natural order is based on its specific laws, everything takes place under the supervision, will, and permission of the Lord. «باذن ربّه»
  3. The fundamental principle is purity and being pure; impurity is an accidental matter. «البلد الطیّب... و الّذى خَبُثَ»
  4. Neither a single reminder is sufficient, nor are monotonous reminders effective. Rather, guidance and propagation must be numerous and varied so that we may approach the desired result. «نصرّف الآیات»
  5. Although the Quran is a source of guidance for everyone, only the grateful benefit from it. «نصرّف الآیات لقوم یشكرون»

Ayahs

لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ فَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (٥۹)
Certainly We sent Noah to his people. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.’ (59)
قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (٦٠)
The elite of his people said, ‘Indeed we see you in manifest error.’ (60)

Key Points

The example of the "wicked land" (بلد خبیث) mentioned in the previous Ayah was the people of Noah, whom Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) called out to, but his efforts were fruitless. The story of Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) is narrated in Surahs Noah, As-Saffat, Al-Isra, Al-Ahzab, Yunus, Al-Mu'minun, and Al-Qamar.

Khawf (fear) also has a positive aspect, but Jubn (cowardice) is only negative and undesirable fear. Jubn stems from low self-esteem, while Khawf is sometimes born of realism and awareness of dangers.

The Noble Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: Noah (عليه السلام) is the first Prophet of firm resolve (Ulul-'Azm) who was sent by Allah with a universal mission. [150] The call of Prophet Noah was universal because his people constituted all the inhabitants of the earth at that time, and the Prophet cursed all the disbelievers on earth in his supplication: «و قال نوح ربّ لا تذر على الارض من الكافرین دیّاراً» [151], and all the inhabitants of the world were drowned.

Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) invited people to Monotheism for 950 years, «مائة الف الاّ خمسین عاما» [152]. Not only did they not believe, but they also distanced themselves from the Prophet, «فلم یزدهم دعائى الاّ فرارا» [153], and subjected him to harassment and torture. They would beat him so severely that he would fall unconscious to the ground, but when he regained consciousness, he would say: «الّلهم اِهدِ قومى فانّهم لایعلمون» (O Allah! Guide my people, for they are ignorant).

150) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur 151) Noah, 26 152) Al-'Ankabut, 14 153) Noah, 6

Messages

1- Although the mission of Noah (عليه السلام) was universal, he began with his own people. «یا قوم»

2- The call to Monotheism (Tawhid) and the worship of Allah is the starting point of the Prophets' missions and is common to all Divine religions. «اُعبدوا اللّه»

3- The reason for Monotheism is the Oneness and lack of partners of Allah. «اعبدوا اللّه... ما لكم من اله غیره»

4- Knowledge of Allah and faith in Him have existed from the very beginning and throughout history. (Prophet Noah, who preceded Prophets Abraham, Moses, and Jesus (علیهم السلام), told the people to worship Allah, which indicates their recognition of Him.) «یا قوم اعبدوا اللّه»

5- The Prophets are the true well-wishers of humanity. «یا قوم... انّى أخاف علیكم»

6- The opponents of the Prophets have always been a group of elites and leaders whose glitter of wealth and power captivated the eyes of the people. «الملأ من قومه»

7- Worse than not believing is considering the Prophets misguided. «انّا لنراك فى ضلال مبین» Indeed, when the innate nature (Fitrah) is distorted, one considers the true guide to be misguided. Today, too, a group who consider themselves intellectuals regard the followers of the Prophet as reactionary and naive.

8- Whoever seeks to establish a Monotheistic system and overthrow the system of polytheism must expect all kinds of slander and insult. «لنراك فى ضلال مبین»

9- Opponents are harsh and serious in leveling accusations. «انّا لنراك فى ضلال مبین»

Ayahs

قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِى ضَلَـٰلَةٌ وَلَـٰكِنِّى رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٦۱)
He said, ‘O my people, I am not in error. Rather, I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds. (61)
أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّى وَأَنصَحُ لَكُمْ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٦۲)
I communicate to you the messages of my Lord, and I am your well-wisher, and I know from Allah what you do not know. (62)

Key Points

The word nush (نُصح) means being pure, unadulterated, and free from deceit. Statements made out of sincere intention and goodwill are called nush, and pure honey is also called nāsih al-asal (ناصح العسل).

Messages

1- We must be patient in the face of insults from the ignorant. «لیس بى ضلالة» (I am not in error). The first Prophet of firm resolve (Ulul-'Azm) spoke using the gentlest method when faced with the accusation of being misguided.

2- The mission of the Prophets is aligned with the nurturing (tarbiyah) of the worlds and is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «لكنّى رسول من ربّ العالمین» (But I am a Messenger from the Lord of the worlds).

3- Contrary to the deviant beliefs that imagined a separate lord (Rabb) for everything, the Quran states that all existence has one Lord and Sustainer. «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the worlds).

4- The Divine Prophets are infallible (ma'sūm). «لیس بى ضلالة و لكنّى رسول» (I am not in error, but I am a Messenger).

5- The educator and preacher must be both a well-wisher and compassionate, and possess sufficient knowledge and awareness. «انصح، أعلم» (I advise, I know).

6- The Prophet's compassion and goodwill were directed toward the benefit of the people, not personal gain. «انصح لكم» (I advise you).

7- The Prophets consider Allah to be the source of all their knowledge and awareness. «و اعلم من اللّه» (And I know from Allah).

8- The Prophets possess knowledge and awareness from Allah that is beyond the reach of mankind. «اعلم من اللّه ما لا تعلمون» (I know from Allah what you do not know).

Ayah

أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ أَن جَآءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنكُمْ لِيُنذِرَكُمْ وَلِتَتَّقُوا۟ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (٦۳)
Do you consider it odd that a reminder from your Lord should come to you through a man from among yourselves, to warn you so that you may be Godwary and so that you may receive His mercy?’ (63)

Messages

1- Sometimes the veil of contemporaneity and being of the same era prevents the acceptance of the truth and His sound logic. «أو عجبتم» The people of Noah used to say: Why should he be a prophet and receive revelation, and we do not? He is just like us and has no difference from us!

2- The goal of revelation is the education of humanity. «جائكم ذكر من ربّكم»

3- Divine knowledge must always be kept in mind. «ذكر من ربّكم»

4- Prophets arise from the people and are among the people. «رجل منكم»

5- The philosophy of the mission (Bi'that) and prophethood, and its stages, can be expressed as follows:

A: Warning and drawing attention. «لینذركم»

B: Creating immunity and piety (Taqwa). «لتتّقوا»

C: Receiving divine favors and mercy. «لعلّكم ترحمون»

6- Piety (Taqwa) is the groundwork for the descent of divine mercy. «لتتّقوا و لعلّكم ترحمون»

Ayah

فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا عَمِينَ (٦٤)
But they denied him. So We delivered him and those who were with him in the ark, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a blind lot. (64)

Key Points

«عَمین» (Ameen) is the plural of «عَمى» (Amā), and it refers to someone whose eye of insight (basīrat) and inner vision has ceased to function. However, «أعمى» (A‘mā) refers both to someone whose inner self is blind and someone whose physical eye is sightless. [154]

Imam Reza عليه السلام, in response to the question of why Allah drowned all the people of the world during the time of Prophet Noah, even though there were children and innocent individuals among them, said: "There were no children among them, because they had become sterile and barren for forty years. As for the innocent people, they were satisfied with the actions of others, and satisfaction with the actions of others is like performing the action oneself." [155]

154) Tafsirs Al-Mizan and Namūneh 155) Tafsir Ithnā ‘Asharī

Messages

1- Faith is the cause of salvation, and denial (takdhīb) is the means and basis for destruction. «أنجینا... أغرقنا» (We saved... We drowned).

2- Natural events, natural factors, and historical transformations are in the hand of Allah and occur based on the thoughts, deeds, and morals of the people. «أنجینا... أغرقنا الّذین كذّبوا بآیاتنا» (We saved... We drowned those who denied Our signs).

3- Blindness of the heart (kūrdilī) is the main factor in denying the call of the prophets. «عمین» (blind). Therefore, faith and following the prophets are signs of insight (basīrat).

Ayah

۞ وَإِلَىٰ عَادٍ أَخَاهُمْ هُودًا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (٦٥)
And to [the people of] ‘Ad [We sent] Hud, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Will you not then be wary [of Him]?’ (65)

Key Points

The story of Prophet Hud عليه السلام and the people of 'Ad, which is mentioned in this Surah, is detailed in the two Surahs of Ash-Shu'ara' and Hud. The people of 'Ad lived in the land of Yemen, in the Al-Ahqaf region in Southern Arabia. They were very powerful in terms of physical strength and resources for livestock and agriculture, but they became afflicted with idol worship and moral corruption. Prophet Hud was one of their relatives, who was commissioned with prophethood, and like Prophet Nuh (Noah), he invited them to Monotheism (Tawhid).

Messages

1- The forefront of the prophets' program is the invitation to Monotheism (Tawhid) and avoidance of all types of polytheism (Shirk) and idol worship. «اُعبدوا اللّه ما لكم من اله غیره»

2- The prophets have been the most popular and compassionate leaders of humanity. «أخاهم» (Their brother). Yes, the educator and preacher must be like a brother to the people and love them dearly.

3- Belief in the One God must be a factor leading to awareness, obedience, piety (Taqwa), and avoidance of denying the religion. «أفلا تتّقون»

Ayahs

قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِى سَفَاهَةٍ وَإِنَّا لَنَظُنُّكَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (٦٦)
The elite of his people who were faithless said, ‘Indeed we see you to be in folly, and indeed we consider you to be a liar.’ (66)
قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِى سَفَاهَةٌ وَلَـٰكِنِّى رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٦۷)
He said, ‘O my people, I am not in folly. Rather, I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds. (67)

Key Points

The audacity of the opponents of Hūd عليه السلام was greater than that of the people of Nūḥ عليه السلام. The opponents of Prophet Nūḥ called him misguided, "فى ضلال مبین" (in manifest error), but the opponents of Hūd considered him foolish and a liar. "فى سفاهة... من الكاذبین" (in foolishness... among the liars).

Messages

1- The Prophets faced the harshest and most explicit propaganda, atmosphere creation (negative campaigning), opposition, and accusations. "ضلال مبین - سفاهة... كاذبین" In the path of guiding people, we must tolerate hearing the ugliest words.

2- The forbearance and magnanimity of the Prophets were such that even after all that audacity, they still kindly said: O my people. "یا قوم"

3- We should deny accusations against ourselves, but we do not have the right to direct the accusation toward others. "لیس بى سفاهة"

4- The Prophets do not commit even an iota or a moment of action that is unreasonable. "لیس بى سفاهة" (In Arabic literature, whenever an indefinite noun [nakirah] is placed next to a negation, it signifies a general negation, meaning: I have never committed a foolish act at any time.)

5- He who believes in his goal and enjoys Divine support reacts calmly to the bad behavior of others. "لیس بى سفاهة"

6- The educator and preacher must not forget their primary duty. "لكنّى رسول"

7- The Divine commands brought by the Prophet for humanity are in the path of human development and are among the aspects of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). "رسول من ربّ العالمین"

Ayah

أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّى وَأَنَا۠ لَكُمْ نَاصِحٌ أَمِينٌ (٦۸)
I communicate to you the messages of my Lord and I am a trustworthy well-wisher for you. (68)

Messages

1- The speech and commands of the prophets are divine messages; they do not speak anything from themselves. «اُبلّغكم رسالات ربّى»

2- The prophets were under special divine training, and their commands are aimed at the growth and development of humanity. «رسالات ربّى» They exert their utmost effort in conveying the message and spare nothing. «اُبلّغكم...» And they have no fear or dread of anyone other than Him. «الّذین یبلّغون رسالات اللّه و یخشونه و لایخشون احداً الاّ اللّه» [156]

3- Sincerity and trustworthiness are two main conditions for education and propagation. «ناصحٌ أمین» Yes, if sincerity is not accompanied by trustworthiness, it is dangerous, because due to sincerity, rights might be ignored or laws might be violated.

4- Stating one's own positive characteristics is permissible in necessary situations and when it is constructive for others. [157] «أنا لكم ناصح أمین»

156) Ahzab, 39

157) Based on a narration from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام. Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Ayah

أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ أَن جَآءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنكُمْ لِيُنذِرَكُمْ ۚ وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَزَادَكُمْ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ بَصْۜطَةً ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ ءَالَآءَ ٱللَّهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (٦۹)
Do you consider it odd that there should come to you a reminder from your Lord through a man from among yourselves, so that he may warn you? Remember when He made you successors after the people of Noah, and increased you vastly in creation. So remember Allah’s bounties so that you may be felicitous.’ (69)

Key Points

The physical strength of the people of 'Ad was so great that they used to say: «مَن أشدّ منّا قوّة» (158) Who is mightier than us in strength? The Quran states: They were so massive that after their destruction, it was as if the trunks of date palms had fallen to the ground. «كأنّهم أعجاز نخل خاویة» (159) The word «آلاء» (Alā') is more comprehensive than ni'mah (blessing) and includes every apparent and hidden, material or spiritual blessing, as it is frequently mentioned in Surah Ar-Rahman. It even includes punishment and Hellfire, which are necessities of Divine justice. (160) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, regarding «فاذكروا آلاء اللّه» (So remember the bounties of Allah), said: The Alā' (bounties) of Allah are the greatest blessing of Allah upon His creation, which is the Wilayah (guardianship/authority) of us, the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. (161)

158) Fussilat, 15 159) Al-Haqqah, 7 160) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran 161) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- The Prophet's commands are the commands of God and reminders of human nature (fitrah). They pertain to the growth and nurturing of humanity and are among the affairs of God's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «جائكم ذكر من ربّكم»

2- Prophets arise from the people and live among the people. «رجل منكم»

3- Historical and social transformations are under the will and decree of Allah and based on His tradition (Sunnah). «جعلكم خلفاء...»

4- Physical ability is among the Divine blessings that must be expended in the correct path. «زادكم فى الخلق بصطة فاذكروا»

5- Remembering Divine blessings is the key to salvation/felicity (falah). «فاذكروا... لعلّكم تفلحون» Because remembering blessings brings love and affection; affection leads to obedience; and obedience, in turn, leads to salvation.

Ayah

قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا لِنَعْبُدَ ٱللَّهَ وَحْدَهُۥ وَنَذَرَ مَا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَا ۖ فَأْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۷٠)
They said, ‘Have you come to [tell] us that we should worship Allah alone and abandon what our fathers have been worshiping? Then bring us what you threaten us with, should you be truthful.’ (70)

Messages

1- Bigotry instead of logic is condemned, and the imitation of the ignorant by the ignorant, and following due to kinship ties, are rejected. «یعبد آباؤنا»

2- Preserving the customs and traditions of ancestors is not valuable everywhere. «یعبد آباؤنا»

3- Undue bigotry and imitation are obstacles to recognizing the truth and lead man to obstinacy. «فأتنا بما تعدنا»

4- Sometimes habit overcomes innate nature (Fitrah), such as the dominance of the habit of ancestor idolatry over the truth-seeking innate nature of the new generation. «نَذَر ما كان یعبد آباؤنا»

5- The Prophets have been tradition-breakers in confronting superstitions and deviations. «و نَذَر ما كان یعبد آباؤنا»

Ayah

قَالَ قَدْ وَقَعَ عَلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ رِجْسٌ وَغَضَبٌ ۖ أَتُجَـٰدِلُونَنِى فِىٓ أَسْمَآءٍ سَمَّيْتُمُوهَآ أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُم مَّا نَزَّلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَـٰنٍ ۚ فَٱنتَظِرُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُنتَظِرِينَ (۷۱)
He said, ‘Punishment and wrath from your Lord has become due against you. Do you dispute with me regarding names, which you have named—you and your fathers—for which Allah has not sent down any authority? So wait! I too am waiting along with you.’ (71)

Key Points

This Ayah indicates that any divine position, title, or designation that is without the permission of Allah, especially concerning general guardianship (Wilayah al-Ammah), is falsehood and slander. Just as the title Amir al-Mu'minin (Commander of the Faithful) belongs solely to Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, and to no one else. Therefore, when Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was addressed by the title Amir al-Mu'minin, the Imam forbade this action and said: Whoever describes himself with this title other than Ali عليه السلام, his abode is the Fire. [162]

162) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- The result of obstinacy and opposing the truth is divine punishment, disgrace, and destruction. «قد وقع علیكم» (Setting aside the fact that obstinacy itself is a type of spiritual impurity.)

2- Man is inherently a monotheist and seeks Monotheism (Tawhid), but sometimes fanaticism overtakes and governs him. «وقع علیكم»

3- Divine punishments are part of the process of human training and are among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «من ربّكم»

4- Stone and wood do not become "God" merely by being named so. (Grandiose and meaningless names are also one of the afflictions of our century.) «أسماء سمّیتموها»

5- The deities of the polytheists are names without substance (Asma bi Musamma) that lack reality and legitimacy, and Allah has not commanded them in any religion. «أسماء... مانَزّل اللّه بها من سلطان»

6- Beliefs must be based on proof and evidence; demand evidence even from idolaters. «ما نزّل اللّه بها من سلطان» (Ask those who say the idol is a means of proximity to Allah: Do you have proof that Allah has made the worship of the idol a means of drawing near to Him?)

7- Reasoning and evidence are a type of divine grace (Faydh Ilahi) that reaches the heart and mind of man. «ما نزّل اللّه بها من سلطان»

8- The Prophets were certain of the correctness of their goal, their victory, and the downfall of their opponents. «انتظروا انّى معكم من المنتظرین»

Ayah

فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَقَطَعْنَا دَابِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ۖ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ (۷۲)
Then We delivered him and those who were with him by a mercy from Us, and We rooted out those who denied Our signs and were not faithful. (72)

Key Points

Dābir (دابر) means the end or conclusion of something, and qaṭ‘ dābir (قطع دابر) means uprooting and destroying everyone down to the last person. The destruction of the people of 'Ād occurred through a barren, shattering, noisy, cold, and poisonous storm that Allah unleashed upon them for seven nights and eight consecutive days. The storm annihilated them so completely that they all fell to the ground like the trunks of hollowed-out palm trees. «فتَرَى القوم فیها صَرعى كانّهم اعجاز نخل خاویة» (163)

163) Al-Hāqqah, 7

Messages

1- Only the Prophets and their followers are included in Allah's special, saving mercy. «فانجیناه و الّذین معه برحمة منّا»

2- We must learn lessons from history. «قطعنا دابر الّذین كذّبوا»

3- Where there is no hope whatsoever for people to believe, destruction is fundamental and root-based. «و قَطَعنا دابر... ما كانوا مؤمنین»

Ayah

وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ ءَايَةً ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۷۳)
And to [the people of] Thamud [We sent] Salih, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. There has certainly come to you a manifest proof from your Lord. This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. Let her alone to graze [freely] in Allah’s land, and do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by a painful punishment. (73)

Key Points

In the Quran, the she-camel (nāqah) is mentioned seven times, and the people of Thamūd are mentioned twenty-six times. The story of the people of Thamūd appears in Sūrahs Ash-Shu‘arā, Al-Qamar, Ash-Shams, and Hūd.

A miracle (mu‘jizah) differs significantly from extraordinary and strange human acts, including: A: A miracle does not require practice, study, or experience, whereas other extraordinary acts require practice, suggestion (or indoctrination), and study. B: A miracle is issued by individuals of good standing and infallibility (ma‘sūm), whereas other rare and extraordinary acts may also be issued by unqualified individuals. C: The purpose of a miracle is to complete the proof (itmām al-ḥujjah), and to guide and instruct people, whereas the goal of others is self-display, fame, acquiring wealth, and creating entertainment. D: The act of the prophets in a miracle is necessarily accompanied by a challenge (taḥaddī), a call to contest, and the assertion of others' inability, but these signs and indications are not necessary in the work of geniuses, inventors, and ascetics (or mystics).

We read in a Ḥadīth: The camel requested by the people of Thamūd was a large, pregnant, heavily furred red camel. [164] Of course, the type of request people made of the prophets was sometimes related to the intellectual conditions and the social and economic context of the people at that time. Perhaps if a prophet were to appear today, they would ask Him to bring an airplane out from the heart of a mountain. In any case, this camel possessed unique characteristics that led it to be named Nāqatullāh (the She-Camel of Allah). It emerged pregnant from the heart of the mountain without contact with any other camel, and after emerging, it gave birth. It drank all the water of the town every other day, and in return, it provided milk to all of them on that day. This was a Divine miracle that they could not replicate, and it was a completion of the proof (itmām al-ḥujjah) such that any malicious intent toward it would bring the punishment of Allah. [165]

164) Tafsīr Nūr ath-Thaqalayn 165) Tafsīr Manhaj aṣ-Ṣādiqīn

Messages

1- The mission of Prophet Ṣāliḥ was limited to the people of Thamūd. «الى ثمود اخاهم صالحاً»

2- The prophets are like brothers to the people. «أخاهم»

3- In propagation and education, we should also utilize emotions. «یا قوم»

4- Belief in Allah has a history as long as time itself. «اعبدوا اللّه» The command to worship Allah indicates the people's faith and recognition.

5- Monotheism is the central theme of the prophets' invitation. «اعبدوا اللّه»

6- The orientation of action is based on belief and thought, «اعبدوا اللّه» because «مالكم من اله غیره»

7- The goals of all Divine prophets are uniform. The phrase «اعبدوا اللّه مالكم من اله غیره» has been stated by all the prophets.

8- The presentation of proof and miracles by the prophets is in the path of educating people and is among the affairs of Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «بیّنة من ربّكم» Of course, miracles were sometimes in accordance with the people's request, such as the splitting of the moon (shaqq al-qamar) and the she-camel of Ṣāliḥ.

9- Whatever receives special Divine care and favor gains sanctity. «ناقة اللّه»

10- The punishment for disrespecting and destroying sacred things is Divine torment. «لاتمسّوها بسوء فیأخذكم عذاب الیم»

Ayah

وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ عَادٍ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِن سُهُولِهَا قُصُورًا وَتَنْحِتُونَ ٱلْجِبَالَ بُيُوتًا ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ ءَالَآءَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ (۷٤)
Remember when He made you successors after [the people of] ‘Ad, and settled you in the land: you build palaces in its plains, and hew houses out of the mountains. So remember Allah’s bounties, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’ (74)

Key Points

'Athaw' (عَثو) means exceeding the limit of moderation, and 'la ta'thaw' (لاتَعثوا) means 'la tufsidu' (لاتفسدوا) [do not cause corruption]. The word 'mufsidīn' (مفسدین) [corrupters] is either for emphasis, or it means that corruption should not become your way of life. The intent of 'fī al-arḍ' (فى الارض) [in the land] is the territory between Hijaz and Sham (Syria). The geographical region of the people of Thamūd consisted of two parts: plains and mountains, 'suhūlihā... al-jibāl' (سهولها... الجبال). They were wealthy and civilized people, knowledgeable in the arts of house-building and palace-building. 'quṣūran... buyūtan' (قصوراً... بیوتاً).

Messages

1- Historical and social transformations are based on divine laws and traditions (Sunan). 'idh ja'alakum khulafā' (اذ جعلكم خلفاء).

2- All our life's resources are from Allah. 'bawwa'akum fī al-arḍ' (بوّأكم فى الارض).

3- We must learn lessons from the history of those who passed away. 'wa-dhkurū... ba'da 'Ād' (واذكروا... بعد عاد).

4- Man can benefit from all natural conditions. 'suhūlihā... al-jibāl' (سهولها... الجبال).

5- Dwelling (housing) is among the special divine blessings. 'quṣūran... buyūtan... ālā' Allāh' (قصوراً... بیوتاً... آلاء اللّه).

6- Prosperity, housing, and palaces, if separated from the remembrance of Allah, become grounds for corruption. 'quṣūran... buyūtan... mufsidīn' (قصوراً... بیوتاً... مفسدین).

7- If we consider blessings as a divine trust and see ourselves exposed to destruction, we will avoid transgression. 'wa-dhkurū... lā ta'thaw' (واذكروا... لاتعثوا).

8- Palace dwellers and those who possess greater resources are more in need of remembering Allah so that they are not drawn into corruption. (The word 'qaṣr' [palace] appears between the two phrases 'idh-kurū' [remember]) [166].

166) Of course, this is provided that 'qaṣr' in the Ayah carries the well-known meaning of our time, and not merely the meaning of an ordinary house.

Ayahs

قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِمَنْ ءَامَنَ مِنْهُمْ أَتَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ صَـٰلِحًا مُّرْسَلٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلَ بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنُونَ (۷٥)
The elite of his people who were arrogant said to those who were oppressed—to those among them who had faith—‘Do you know that Salih has been sent by his Lord?’ They said, ‘We indeed believe in what he has been sent with.’ (75)
قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِٱلَّذِىٓ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ (۷٦)
Those who were arrogant said, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you have believed.’ (76)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Usually, the elite and influential individuals oppose the path of the prophets and resort to creating doubts among the believers. «قال الملأ الّذین استكبروا»

2- Neither poverty and oppression (being mustad'af) is a virtue, nor is affluence and dwelling in palaces; what is important is choosing the path of Allah through awareness, knowledge, faith, piety, Jihad, and Hijra. (In the previous Ayah, He counted building palaces as a divine blessing, provided it does not lead to corruption. Here too, the believing oppressed are praised, not all of them.) «آمن منهم»

3- The majority of the followers of the prophets were from among the oppressed. «للّذین استضعفوا لِمَن آمن منهم»

4- The factor for the faith of the oppressed in the prophets is not merely material poverty, because some of the oppressed did not believe either. «آمن منهم»

5- Creating doubt and skepticism is one of the methods used to weaken faith. «أتعلمون...؟»

6- Weakening the leader is one of the main methods and goals of the enemy. «أتعلمون انّ صالحاً مرسل»

7- Although the environment and society affect the individual, they do not necessitate compulsion. (The deprived oppressed stood explicitly against the arrogant and said: We believe in Salih). «انّا بما ارسل به مؤمنون»

8- The doubts raised by opponents must be answered decisively. «انّا بما ارسل به مؤمنون»

9- One must have faith in all the commands of the Prophet. «بما ارسل به مؤمنون»

10- The arrogant, besides their own disbelief and obstinacy, are sensitive toward the faith of the oppressed. «انّا بالّذى آمنتم به كافرون» and they did not say: «انّا به كافرون»

Ayah

فَعَقَرُوا۟ ٱلنَّاقَةَ وَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ وَقَالُوا۟ يَـٰصَـٰلِحُ ٱئْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۷۷)
So they hamstrung the She-camel and defied the command of their Lord, and they said, ‘O Salih, bring us what you threaten us with, if you are one of the apostles.’ (77)

Key Points

| 'Aqr' (عقر) means hamstringing (or hocking), which is cutting the strong, specific nerve and vein at the back of the leg of a horse or camel, which is the main factor in their movement. By cutting it, the animal falls to the ground and is prevented from walking. In Surah Al-Qamar, the word 'aqar' (عَقَر) is used in the singular form, indicating that the killer of the she-camel was one person. However, here (and in Surahs Ash-Shams, Ash-Shu'ara, and Hud), it is used in the plural form, 'aqarū' (عقروا), attributing the hamstringing to the entire people. This is because of their silence and consent, which made them partners in the crime. Just as Imam Ali عليه السلام also states: The destiny of people depends on their consent and their enmity (towards an act), just as one person hamstrung the she-camel of Salih, but because the rest of the people were also pleased with that act, they became partners in his sin and were all punished. [167]

167) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 201

Messages

1- Disbelief (Kufr) is the basis for transgression. «كافرون فعقروا...»

2- Silence and consent regarding a sin are considered participation in that sin. «عقروا», even though the killer was one person.

3- Disobedience and transgression against the command are more significant than killing the camel. «و عَتَوا عن أمر ربّهم»

4- Divine commands arise from His Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «امر ربّهم»

5- Arrogance paves the way for audacity and insolence. «ائتنا بما تعدنا»

Ayah

فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ (۷۸)
Thereupon the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes. (78)

Key Points

This Ayah describes the destruction of the people of Thamūd by rajfah (tremor/earthquake), but Sūrah Fussilat, Ayah 14, and Sūrah Adh-Dhāriyāt, Ayah 44, attribute it to the thunderbolt (ṣā'iqah). «فأخذتهم الصاعقة و هم ینظرون» Therefore, perhaps the two punishments, the thunderbolt and the earthquake, occurred simultaneously at the same moment. And perhaps the intent of rajfah is the trembling of the limbs arising from fear and terror, not an earthquake. Jāthim (جاثم) is derived from jatham (جَثم), which rhymes with khashm (خَشم), meaning sitting on the knees or falling upon the chest.
