It seems that when the punishment came, they woke up during the night while they were resting, but they found no opportunity or power to flee, and they were destroyed in that half-rising state. [168]

168) Tafsir Namūneh

Messages

1- Allah's wrath is sudden; let us be vigilant. «فأخذتهم الرّجفة»

2- Many earthquakes and natural disasters are divine punishments. «فأخذتهم الرجفة»

3- The promises of the Prophets are realized, so we must take their warnings seriously. (In the preceding Ayahs, there was a threat: «لا تمسّوها بسوء فیأخذكم عذاب الیم», and this rajfah is the fulfillment of that threat and the painful punishment). «فأخذتهم الرّجفة»

Ayah

فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَالَةَ رَبِّى وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تُحِبُّونَ ٱلنَّـٰصِحِينَ (۷۹)
So he abandoned them [to their fate], and said, ‘O my people! Certainly I communicated to you the message of my Lord, and I was your well-wisher, but you did not like well-wishers.’ (79)

Key Points

It is probable that the speech of Prophet Salih was before the descent of the punishment, serving as the completion of the proof (Hujjah), and it is possible that it was after their destruction. Just as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله also spoke to the bodies of the disbelievers at the edge of the well of Badr. They asked: Do they hear? He replied: Yes, you are not more capable of hearing than they are. [169]

169) Bihar, Vol. 6, pp. 207 and 254

Messages

1- Sometimes anger (or displeasure) and severing ties are necessary in education (Tarbiya) and propagation (Tabligh). «فتولّى عنهم»

2- Divine punishments occur after the completion of the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah). «لقد أبلغتُكم»

3- The mission of the prophets is the conveyance of the message accompanied by compassion and goodwill, not the dry and spiritless delivery of administrative regulations and circulars. «لقد أبلغتُكم...و نصحتُ لكم»

4- We should love those who advise sincerely, because disregard for the words of the compassionate members of society prepares the ground for Allah's wrath. «ولكن لا تحبّون الناصحین»

5- Loving is the key to following. «ولكن لا تحبّون الناصحین»

Ayahs

وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ أَتَأْتُونَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةَ مَا سَبَقَكُم بِهَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنَ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۸٠)
And Lot, when he said to his people, ‘What! Do you commit an outrage none in the world ever committed before you?! (80)
إِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ ٱلرِّجَالَ شَهْوَةً مِّن دُونِ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ مُّسْرِفُونَ (۸۱)
Indeed you come to men with desire instead of women! Indeed, you are a profligate lot.’ (81)

Key Points

Prophet Lot (Lut) was the relative of Prophet Abraham (Ibrahim) and the only man who believed in him and migrated with him. «فآمن له لوط» (170) Prophet Abraham sent him to a region where sin and immorality (fahsha) were prevalent. Therefore, a significant part of Prophet Lot’s عليه السلام mission was fighting against immorality and sexual vices, which were the greatest problem in that society. The people of Prophet Lot committed the vile act of sodomy (liwāṭ) with their guests so that people would be afraid of hosting visitors. According to the Qur'an, Prophet Lot عليه السلام offered them marriage with his daughters, but they refused. Marriage possesses characteristics and effects that are absent in sodomy (liwāṭ) and fornication (zinā), including: 1. Affection, mercy, and intimacy. 2. The formation of the family structure. 3. Children and the continuation of the lineage. 4. Consistency with innate nature (fiṭrah) and humanity. Unfortunately, today the Western world has also formalized the vile act of homosexuality and given it legal status in some countries. In addition to sodomy, the people of Lot were involved in gambling, obscenity, insult, harassment of passersby, and public exposure of private parts. (171) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام stated regarding the philosophy of prohibiting sodomy: Sodomy reduces the familial bond between husband and wife, cuts off lineage and offspring, destroys natural sexual intercourse, and leads to numerous great corruptions. (172) In Islam, the punishment for homosexuality and sodomy (whether the active or passive partner) is execution. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The curse of Allah be upon men who make themselves like women and expose themselves to lustful men. (173) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام also said: When homosexuality occurs, the Throne of Allah trembles, and the perpetrator of this act will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment in an impure form, will incur the wrath of Allah, and his abode will be Hellfire. (174)

170) Ankabut, 26 171) Safinat al-Bihar 172) Wasā’il, Vol. 14, p. 252 173) Tafsir Namuneh; Wasā’il, Vol. 14, p. 255 174) Tafsir Namuneh; Wasā’il, Vol. 14, p. 249

Messages

  1. Homosexuality and sodomy are among the major sins (Kabā’ir) and are extremely vile acts. «الفاحشة»

  2. If idolaters had the excuse of following the idolatry of their ancestors, the followers of immorality did not even have this excuse; the innovation of the crime and sin was their own. «ما سبقكم بها»

  3. The innovators of vile acts commit a greater sin and deserve more blame. «ما سبقكم بها من احد من العالمین»

  4. Whoever abandons the natural and innate path is an extravagant transgressor (musrif). «مسرفون» Satisfying lust through unnatural means is extravagance (isrâf).

  5. Sodomy is a violation of the rights of women in society. «دون النساء بل انتم قوم مسرفون»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوٓا۟ أَخْرِجُوهُم مِّن قَرْيَتِكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ أُنَاسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ (۸۲)
But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Expel them from your town! They are indeed a puritanical lot.’ (82)

Messages

1- Uglier than disobedience and sin is the baseless and illogical confrontation with those who advise (the sincere advisors). «ما كان جواب قومه الاّ ان قالوا اخرجوهم...»

2- In forbidding evil (Nahy al-Munkar), one must be prepared for exile and the acceptance of difficulties. «اخرجوهم»

3- Criminals do not recognize any right for the pure within society. «اخرجوهم من قریتكم»

4- Whenever corruption becomes widespread in a society, they isolate the pure, and forbidding evil and maintaining purity are considered crimes. «أخرجوهم... انّهم اُناس یتطهّرون»

5- The people of Lot, despite having become accustomed to sin, still recognized Prophet Lot and his companions as pure. «انّهم اُناس یتطهّرون»

6- More important than being pure is remaining pure and causing the unworthy to despair of exerting influence. «یتطهّرون» (The present tense verb indicates continuity/persistence.)

Ayah

فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَهُۥ كَانَتْ مِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (۸۳)
Thereupon We delivered him and his family, except his wife; she was one of those who remained behind. (83)

Key Points

Ghabir (غابِر) means 'the one who remains behind.' It refers to someone whose companions leave while he stays. Therefore, Lot (Lut) and his followers exited the city, and the stage for the punishment was set. Ahl (اهل) is used both for close relatives and for true followers. However, considering Ayah 36 of Surah Adh-Dhariyat, no one except his close relatives believed in the Prophet (Lot).

Messages

1- Man is free and chooses his own path. (The wife of a prophet, even though she is sustained in the house of revelation, might choose the path of disbelief, just as the wife of Pharaoh chose the path of Monotheism.) «الاّ امرأته» (Except his wife). Therefore, thought, morality, and decision-making are not always dependent on economics or being under the patronage of others.

2- In Islam, the factor for human salvation is ideological connection, not kinship. «الاّ امرأته» (Except his wife). Thus, the destiny of every person is tied to his or her own deeds.

3- We must separate the account of the relatives of the Divine leaders from their own account. «الاّ امرأته» (Except his wife).

4- In Islam, woman, like man, is an independent being. «الاّ امرأته» (Except his wife). Furthermore, the position and sanctity of a person should not be used to justify the wrongdoings of his relatives.

Ayah

وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًا ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۸٤)
Then We poured down upon them a rain [of stones]. So observe how was the fate of the guilty! (84)

Key Points

The destruction of the people of Lot was by the raining of stones upon their heads, stones made of clay, like clods, as He says in Surah Hud: «وامطرنا علیهم حجارة من سجّیل منضود مسوّمة عند ربّك» (175)

175) Hud, 82 to 83

Messages

1- Deviating from the path of nature (sexual gratification through men and homosexuality) leads to the change of blessings. Instead of rain, stones fall. «وامطرنا علیهم مطراً» In Du'a Kumayl, we read: «اللّهم اغفر لى الذّنوب الّتى تغیّر النعم» (O Allah, forgive me the sins which change the blessings).

2- Divine wrath is not exclusive to the Hereafter; sometimes it occurs in this world as well. «امطرنا»

3- We must learn lessons from the history and destiny of others. «فانظر... عاقبة المجرمین»

4- The wrath of Allah is not specific to one group of criminals; all criminals should be aware that the Divine tradition makes no exceptions. «عاقبة المجرمین»

Ayah

وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَأَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلَـٰحِهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۸٥)
And to [the people of] Midian [We sent] Shu‘ayb, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. There has certainly come to you a manifest proof from your Lord. Observe fully the measure and the balance, and do not cheat the people of their goods, and do not cause corruption on the earth after its restoration. That is better for you, if you are faithful. (85)

Key Points

This is the fifth story of the prophets in this Surah. The story of Shu'ayb عليه السلام has also been narrated in several Surahs, including Surah Hud and Surah Ash-Shu'ara. Madyan was one of the cities of Sham (Greater Syria), which in today's geography is near Jordan and is named Ma'an. It was a lush region inhabited by idol-worshipping and affluent people who practiced short-changing (under-measuring/under-weighing) in their transactions and trade. Prophet Shu'ayb عليه السلام was sent to them, but they were destroyed due to their obstinacy and disobedience to Him. Then, the Prophet was sent to the people of "Al-Aykah," and He guided them, but they also did not accept the divine commands and were ultimately destroyed. «كذّب اصحاب الایكة المرسلین اذ قال لهم شعیب...» (176) Of course, it is probable that "Madyan" is the same as "Al-Aykah," because Al-Aykah refers to a region full of trees, thickets, and palm groves. This means the city of Shu'ayb had two names: Madyan and Al-Aykah. The faithless human is always afflicted by deviation and corruption, but in a different manner in every era. For example, during the time of Prophet Lut (Lot), sodomy and moral corruption were prevalent, and during the time of Prophet Shu'ayb, short-changing and economic corruption were prevalent.

176) Ash-Shu'ara, 176 to 189

Messages

1- The conduct of Prophet Shu'ayb towards the people was compassionate and brotherly, and perhaps He had a kinship with the people of Madyan. «اخاهم شعیباً»

2- Propagation and education must be accompanied by emotion and affection. «قال یا قومِ»

3- Monotheism and the worship of the One God are the axis of the prophets' invitation. «اعبدوا اللّه...»

4- The miracles and proofs of the prophets are in the path of the people's growth and education, and are dependent on the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah. «بیّنة من ربّكم»

5- After polytheism and ideological deviation, economic deviations were the most important issue for the people of Prophet Shu'ayb. «اعبدوا اللّه... أوفوا الكیل»

6- Short-changing, fraud in transactions, devaluing people's goods, and causing disruption in the economy are forbidden (haram) and are instances of corruption. «لا تبخسوا النّاس اشیائهم و لاتفسدوا»

7- The prophets also supervised the economic issues of society and sought the well-being and improvement of people's lives. «لاتبخسوا النّاس اشیائهم» Religion and its programs are not separate from worldly life.

8- Under-performance (in work) is like short-changing (in trade). Instead of «أموالهم» (their wealth), He said: «أشیائهم» (their things/goods) so that it includes everything.

9- Complete faith and a sound economy ensure happiness in this world and the Hereafter. «ذلكم خیر»

10- The warnings and commands of the prophets are entirely good (khayr) and lead to human happiness. «ذلكم خیر»

11- Faith guarantees proper business practices and social justice. «لا تبخسوا النّاس... ان كنتم مؤمنین»

Ayah

وَلَا تَقْعُدُوا۟ بِكُلِّ صِرَٰطٍ تُوعِدُونَ وَتَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ مَنْ ءَامَنَ بِهِۦ وَتَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا ۚ وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ كُنتُمْ قَلِيلًا فَكَثَّرَكُمْ ۖ وَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۸٦)
And do not lie in wait on every road to threaten and bar those who have faith in Him from the way of Allah, seeking to make it crooked. Remember when you were few, and He multiplied you, and observe how was the fate of the agents of corruption. (86)

Key Points

The meaning of sitting on every path might be sitting beside streets and alleys for instigating mischief, or perhaps the meaning is striving toward corruption by every means and method, just as Satan swore that he is lying in wait on the path of truth to mislead man, where the intention is not physical sitting. «لاقعدنّ لهم صراطك المستقیم» (177)

(177) Al-A'raf, 16

Messages

1- The enemies of truth lie in wait everywhere to obstruct the path of Allah, utilizing every means and method. «و لاتقعدوا بكلّ صراط»

2- Intimidating people and creating harassment for them is forbidden. «توعدون»

3- If the enemies cannot make the believers irreligious, they strive to distort the image of religion and make it appear ugly. «تبغونها عِوَجاً»

4- Sometimes, an increase in population (with organization and planning) can be a value. «فكثّركم»

5- One of the factors for the education and guidance of people is paying attention to blessings and recalling the history of predecessors. «واذكروا... و انظروا»

6- Studying history and taking lessons from it is a Divine recommendation. «وانظروا كیف ...»

7- When studying the lives of people, look at the end and outcome of their deeds, not their temporary successes. «عاقبة»

8- Blocking or diverting the path of Allah is a type of corruption. «تصدّون... تبغونها عوجا... المفسدین»

Ayah

وَإِن كَانَ طَآئِفَةٌ مِّنكُمْ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱلَّذِىٓ أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِۦ وَطَآئِفَةٌ لَّمْ يُؤْمِنُوا۟ فَٱصْبِرُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَحْكُمَ ٱللَّهُ بَيْنَنَا ۚ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلْحَـٰكِمِينَ (۸۷)
If a group of you have believed in what I have been sent with, and a group have not believed, be patient until Allah judges between us, and He is the best of judges.’ (87)

Key Points

The opponents of Prophet Shu'ayb (عليه السلام) audaciously asked: "So, where is the wrath of Allah?" The believers and followers of the Prophet were also awaiting divine aid. This Ayah is a response to both groups, ensuring that the disbelievers do not become arrogant and the believers do not become disheartened or despairing.

Messages

1- The duty of the Prophet is to convey the religion and divine commands, "اُرسلتُ" (I have been sent). People are also free and possess the choice to accept or reject it. "طائفة منكم آمنوا... و طائفة لم یؤمنوا" (A group among you believed... and a group did not believe).

2- In the school of the Prophets, faith in their goal and path is necessary. "اُرسلتُ" (I have been sent) means faith in that for which I have come.

3- The history of the followers of truth and falsehood must be studied with attention to their ultimate end and outcome. "فاصبروا" (So be patient).

4- The inclination of a group of people towards disbelief should not destabilize us, for Allah is the Judge. "فاصبروا حتّى یحكم اللّه بیننا" (So be patient until Allah judges between us).

5- Whoever knows that Allah is the best and supreme Judge is patient and does not rush punishment or reward. "فاصبروا... و هو خیر الحاكمین" (So be patient... and He is the best of judges). Likewise, he has no fear of the judgment of others.

Ayah

۞ قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لَنُخْرِجَنَّكَ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَكَ مِن قَرْيَتِنَآ أَوْ لَتَعُودُنَّ فِى مِلَّتِنَا ۚ قَالَ أَوَلَوْ كُنَّا كَـٰرِهِينَ (۸۸)
The elite of his people who were arrogant said, ‘O Shu'ayb, we will surely expel you and the faithful who are with you from our town, or else you shall revert to our creed.’ He said, ‘What! Even if we should be unwilling?! (88)

Key Points

Bullying and the threat of expulsion and exile have, in some form, been directed at all the Prophets, as we read elsewhere: the disbelievers made such threats against their Prophets. «قال الّذین كفروا لرسلهم لنخرجنّكم من أرضنا أو لتعودنّ فى ملّتنا» (178) None of the Prophets were polytheists or idolaters before their mission, but their interaction with the polytheists was calm. However, after the mission, they guided the people with stronger resolve and determination and confronted the polytheists. Therefore, the address "لتعودنّ" (that you return) is directed at the followers of the Prophets, not the Prophets themselves. (179)

178) Ibrahim, 13 179) Tafsir Namuneh

Messages

1- The arrogant elite were enemies of the Prophets. «قال الملأ الّذین استكبروا... لنخرجنّك»

2- Argumentation and logic are the method of the Prophets, «جاءتكم بیّنة» (180) but the method of the disbelievers is threat and bullying. «لنخرجنّك»

3- Preachers must not fear the threats of opponents, because in this path, the threat of murder and exile has always existed. «لنخرجنّك»

4- Imposing beliefs is the method of the disbelievers, and an imposed religion is of no benefit. «لتعودنّ فى ملّتنا... كارهین»

5- When dealing with the ignorance of the disbelievers, we should use courtesy, soft speech, and wisdom. «أوَ لو كنّا كارهین»

180) Al-A'raf, 85

Ayah

قَدِ ٱفْتَرَيْنَا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا إِنْ عُدْنَا فِى مِلَّتِكُم بَعْدَ إِذْ نَجَّىٰنَا ٱللَّهُ مِنْهَا ۚ وَمَا يَكُونُ لَنَآ أَن نَّعُودَ فِيهَآ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّنَا ۚ وَسِعَ رَبُّنَا كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عِلْمًا ۚ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا ٱفْتَحْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ قَوْمِنَا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْفَـٰتِحِينَ (۸۹)
We would be fabricating a lie against Allah should we revert to your creed after Allah had delivered us from it. It does not behoove us to return to it, unless Allah, our Lord, should wish so. Our Lord embraces all things in [His] knowledge. In Allah we have put our trust.’ ‘Our Lord! Judge justly between us and our people, and You are the best of judges!’ (89)

Key Points

Millah (ملّت) means religion and creed, just as we read elsewhere: «ملّة ابیكم ابراهیم» (the creed of your father Abraham). [181] Fath (فتح) in such contexts means judgment and arbitration for opening the way (resolution), meaning Allah speaks the final word so that the deadlock is broken and the path is opened. Since the companions of Shu'ayb عليه السلام accepted the religion based on clear evidence (bayyinah) and reasoning, not whims and desires, they will not abandon it. Unless Allah commands it, and Allah never commands a return to polytheism (shirk) and disbelief (kufr), because usually, someone who goes back on His command is one who regrets His action, and Allah is never like that.

181) Hajj, 78

Messages

1- Apostasy and turning back from true ideals and beliefs, and accepting a partner for Allah, is a form of fabrication against God and a breach of covenant. «قد افترینا... ما یكون لنا أن نعود»

2- Polytheism (shirk) is captivity, and monotheism is salvation. «بعد اذ نجّانا اللّه»

3- A believer never deviates from the truth and is not willing to compromise on his beliefs. «ما یكون لنا...» (Retreat and regression are forbidden, and leniency and tolerance are not always virtues.)

4- Guidance and misguidance are within the sphere of Allah’s Will. «الاّ أن یشاء اللّه»

5- One must submit to Allah and obey His command in all circumstances. «الاّ أن یشاء اللّه»

6- The reason for submitting and accepting Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) is His boundless knowledge. «الاّ ان یشاء اللّه... وسع ربّنا كلّ شى ء علماً»

7- The reason for our reliance (tawakkul) upon Allah is also His absolute knowledge of everything. «وسع ربّنا كلّ شى ء علماً على اللّه توكّلنا»

8- For steadfastness in faith, we must seek help from Allah and rely upon Him. «مایكون لنا ان نعود... على اللّه توكّلنا»

9- In the face of the ominous proposals of enemies, one must stand firm and decisively, relying upon Allah. «ما یكون لنا... توكّلنا»

10- Entrusting judgment to Allah is an example and manifestation of reliance (tawakkul) and submission. «على اللّه توكّنا ربّنا افتح...»

11- In supplication (du'a), the appropriateness between the request and the Divine Names must be considered. Where we need fath (resolution/victory), we call upon God as Khayr al-Fātihīn (the Best of Deciders). «افتح... خیر الفاتحین»

12- The prerequisite for correct judgment is comprehensive knowledge and awareness. Judgment is the right of the One Who knows everything. «وسع ربّنا كلّ شى ء علما... ربّنا افتح...»

13- Judgment is among the affairs of Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّنا افتح»

14- The goal of the prophets is to establish and uphold the word of truth, not their own words. «افتح بیننا و بین قومنا بالحقّ»

Ayahs

وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لَئِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتُمْ شُعَيْبًا إِنَّكُمْ إِذًا لَّخَـٰسِرُونَ (۹٠)
The elite of his people who were faithless said, ‘If you follow Shu'ayb, you will indeed be losers.’ (90)
فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ (۹۱)
So the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes. (91)

Key Points

Perhaps it can be inferred from the use of the word rajfah («رَجفة») in this verse, the word sayhah («صیحة») in verse 94 of Surah Hud, and the phrase ‘adhābu yawm al-ẓullah («عذاب یوم الظُلّة») (the punishment of the Day of the Shade) in verse 189 of Surah Shu‘arā’, that the earthquake was accompanied by a cry and a dark cloud. [182] It is possible that the meaning of rajfah («رجفه») is the anxiety and trembling of the limbs resulting from fear, rather than an earthquake. As mentioned in verse 78, jāthim («جاثم») refers to someone who has fallen face down on the ground or is kneeling and unable to rise.

182) Tafsir Namūneh

Messages

1- The aristocratic class has generally been opposed to the Prophets. «و قال الملأ»

2- One of the methods used by the disbelievers to prevent people from following the Prophets (when they see that the believers will not abandon their faith) is material and economic threat. «انّكم اذاً لخاسرون» Indeed, deprivation of the income gained through short-changing (in measure or weight) is considered a loss from the perspective of the disbelievers.

3- Divine punishments are often sent down at night. [183] «فأصبحوا» The night holds secrets and mysteries; Divine favors and revelation have also been sent down at night.

183) Tafsir al-Mīzān

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ شُعَيْبًا كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَا ۚ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ شُعَيْبًا كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (۹۲)
Those who impugned Shu'ayb became as if they had never lived there. Those who impugned Shu‘ayb were themselves the losers. (92)

Key Points

The word "یَغنَوا" (yaghnaw) is derived from "غنى" (ghina), meaning residence in a place. Considering the original meaning of ghina (self-sufficiency/independence), it is as if the one who has a ready place of residence and home is self-sufficient. The great loss belongs to the polytheists (mushrikeen) who commit polytheism (shirk) instead of believing in the One God, and who turn to others instead of following an infallible leader. Therefore, instead of a secure life, their homes are ruined; instead of Paradise, they go to Hellfire; and instead of Divine pleasure (Ridwan), they incur Divine wrath.

Messages

1- Paying attention to the dreadful fate of the disbelievers is a lesson to be learned. «الّذین كذّبوا شعیباً»

2- The schemes of the people of falsehood are foiled. «كأن لم یغنوا فیها» (As if they had never dwelt therein). (The disbelievers sought to expel Prophet Shu'ayb, but they themselves perished in their own homes).

3- When Allah's wrath arrives, the true losers become apparent. «هم الخاسرین» (They are the losers). (In the previous Ayah, the disbelievers considered supporting Prophet Shu'ayb a loss; here, Allah states that they themselves are the losers.)

Ayah

فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّى وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ ءَاسَىٰ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ كَـٰفِرِينَ (۹۳)
So he abandoned them [to their fate] and said, ‘O my people! Certainly, I communicated to you the messages of my Lord, and I was your well-wisher. So how should I grieve for a faithless lot?’ (93)

Messages

1- Divine wrath occurs after the completion of the proof (Hujjah). «فاخذتهم الرجفة... اَبلَغتُكم رسالات ربّى»

2- Admonition, forbearance, and attention toward people have limits; sometimes one must turn away from them. «فتولّى عنهم»

3- Propagation (Tabligh) must be accompanied by kindness and sincere concern. «اَبلَغتُكم... نَصحتُ»

4- We should not mix advice and goodwill with personal interests. «نصَحتُ لكم»

5- When we have fulfilled our duty, we should not worry about the outcome. «اَبلَغتُكم...كیف آسى »

6- Undue regret and misplaced emotion are forbidden. «فكیف آسى »

(In other instances, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was addressed: «لاتحزن علیهم» (184), meaning, do not grieve for them.)

7- Where the spirit of disbelief, obstinacy, and enmity prevails, the admonition of the prophets will not be effective. «فكیف آسى على قوم كافرین»

(184) Al-Hijr, 88; An-Nahl, 127 and An-Naml, 70

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا فِى قَرْيَةٍ مِّن نَّبِىٍّ إِلَّآ أَخَذْنَآ أَهْلَهَا بِٱلْبَأْسَآءِ وَٱلضَّرَّآءِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَضَّرَّعُونَ (۹٤)
We did not send a prophet to any town without visiting its people with stress and distress so that they might entreat [for Allah’s forgiveness]. (94)

Key Points

The term «بأساء» refers to physical calamities and afflictions, such as illness and death, while «ضرّاء» refers to financial afflictions and losses.

Messages

1- Bitterness and unpleasantness exist as a divine tradition (sunnah) for all nations. «ما ارسلنا فى قریة من نبىّ الاّ اخذنا...»

2- Hardships and difficulties are factors for self-building, removing heedlessness, and turning attention toward Allah. «لعلّهم یضّرّعون»

3- Calamities are not always divine wrath; sometimes they are a grace that manifests in the form of affliction. «لعلّهم یضّرّعون» (Just as melting iron in a fiery furnace causes it to soften and become moldable, so too do events and hardships soften people and compel them toward humility and seeking their needs.)

Ayah

ثُمَّ بَدَّلْنَا مَكَانَ ٱلسَّيِّئَةِ ٱلْحَسَنَةَ حَتَّىٰ عَفَوا۟ وَّقَالُوا۟ قَدْ مَسَّ ءَابَآءَنَا ٱلضَّرَّآءُ وَٱلسَّرَّآءُ فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۹٥)
Then We changed the ill [conditions] to good until they multiplied [in numbers] and said, ‘Adversity and ease befell our fathers [too].’ Then We seized them suddenly while they were unaware. (95)

Key Points

  • 'Afawoo' (عَفَوا), means abundance of wealth and children, or it means abandoning and turning away. Allah's favor reached them in abundance, but they did not come to their senses and did not take heed. A similar verse appears in Surah Al-An'am, Ayah 44: «فلمّا نسوا ما ذكّروا به فتحنا علیهم أبواب كلّ شى ء حتّى اذا فرحوا بما أوتوا أخذناهم بغتة» (When they forgot what they had been reminded of, We opened upon them the doors of all things until, when they rejoiced in what they had been given, We seized them suddenly.) Because they forgot Our reminders, We opened the doors of blessings upon them, and after their enjoyments, We suddenly afflicted them with Our wrath. It is like a patient whose doctor has despaired of his recovery and says: Give him whatever he desires; his time is up. It is possible that the verse has another meaning, including: After the removal of the affliction, the next generation would say: The bitter events were specific to our fathers, and we are safe. (185) They were heedless that Allah's tradition is to afflict all generations, and such heedlessness is the ground for Divine wrath.

185) Translation by Elahi Qomsha'i

Messages

1- Sometimes prosperity, along with incorrect perceptions and analyses, becomes the cause of forgetfulness and transgression. «قد مسّ آباءنا الضرّاء»

2- Not every kind of happiness and prosperity is a sign of favor; sometimes it paves the way for Divine wrath. «حتّى عفواْ... فاخذناهم بغتة»

3- Most of those who fail the Divine tests are the prosperous, not the deprived. «حتّى عفوا وقالوا...»

4- Divine wrath gives no warning and arrives suddenly. «بغتة»

5- Passing indifferently by bitter and sweet events and failing to take heed is a sign of lack of understanding. «لا یشعرون»

6- Becoming immersed in the world and heedless of the remembrance of Allah subjects man to a sudden and surprising punishment. «فاخذناهم بغتة و هم لایشعرون»

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱتَّقَوْا۟ لَفَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِم بَرَكَـٰتٍ مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَـٰكِن كَذَّبُوا۟ فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۹٦)
If the people of the towns had been faithful and Godwary, We would have opened to them blessings from the heaven and the earth. But they denied; so We seized them because of what they used to earn. (96)

Key Points

Barakāt (بركات), the plural of Barakah (بركت), refers to stable and enduring gifts, in contrast to fleeting things. In the meaning of Barakah, there is abundance, goodness, and increase. Blessings (Barakāt) include material and spiritual blessings, such as blessing in lifespan, wealth, knowledge, books, and the like.

Question: If faith (Iman) and piety (Taqwa) are the cause of the descent of blessings, then why do non-believing countries have a better situation, while Islamic countries face many problems?

Answer: Firstly, those countries are advanced in terms of science and industry, but they lack spiritual and psychological peace. In this regard, they also face many problems. Secondly: Most Islamic countries only carry the name of Islam, and the laws and guidance of the religion of Islam are not dominant there. Furthermore, sometimes material prosperity is a form of divine wrath. As the Quran states: «فلمّا نسوا ما ذكّروا به فتحنا علیهم أبواب كلّ شى ء» (186) Because they forgot the divine reminders, We opened the doors of everything upon them so that they would become intoxicated (with pleasure).

In the Quran, two types of opening (prosperity/relief) are presented for two groups: A: Prosperity and opening for the righteous, which is accompanied by blessings (Barakāt), «فَتَحنا علیهم بركات». B: Prosperity and opening for the disbelievers (Kuffār) and the unworthy, which is no longer accompanied by blessing, «فَتَحنا علیهم ابواب كلّ شى ء», because sometimes blessings are unstable and lead to heedlessness, arrogance, and transgression. Allah has made this prosperity and opening for the disbelievers a means of respite and the filling of their measure (of sin). Therefore, one should not be pleased with every apparent blessing, because if these blessings are for the believers (Mu'minin), they are a source of blessing (Barakah), and if they are for the disbelievers, they are unstable and a means of divine wrath.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Sometimes a servant has a need and a request from Allah, and Allah answers his prayer and decrees that it will be fulfilled after some time. But after that, the servant commits a sin which causes his need not to be fulfilled. (187)

Among the clear manifestations of this Ayah is the era of the appearance of Hadrat Mahdi (عجّل اللّه تعالى فرجه), when, according to narrations, blessings will pour forth from the heaven and the earth. (188)

186) An'am, 44 187) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 188) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

  1. For society to believe and become pious is a very difficult task. «لو... آمنوا واتّقوا» (Law (if) is a sign of a very difficult and nearly impossible task).
  2. Individual faith and piety are not enough to receive Allah's social favors and blessings; the majority of society must be people of faith and piety. (189) «أهل القرى آمنوا واتّقوا»
  3. Faith alone is not enough; rather, piety is necessary. «آمنوا واتّقوا»
  4. Investing in the culture and spirituality of society also yields economic returns. «آمنوا و اتّقوا لفتحنا علیهم بركات»
  5. We must take divine promises seriously. With faith and piety, the descent of blessings is certain. (The letter Lām in «لفتحنا»)
  6. Closing and opening (restricting and granting) are in the hands of Allah, «لفتحنا», but they are a reflection of our performance.
  7. Heavenly religions desire the improvement of people's economic situation. «لفتحنا علیهم»
  8. Man is instinctively interested in receiving blessings and goodness, and the Prophets used these natural inclinations for their goals. «لفتحنا علیهم بركات»
  9. What we receive of blessings is only a part of the divine blessings. «بركات» (The word Barakāt is without alif and lām [the definite article 'al'], thus it does not include all blessings).
  10. The earth and the heaven are the sources of blessings. «بركات من السماء و الارض»
  11. The role of the heaven in delivering blessings precedes the role of the earth. «بركات من السماء و الارض» (The precedence of heaven over earth).
  12. Faith and piety cause the descent of blessings, but not every blessing and prosperity is a result of faith and piety. «آمنوا واتّقوا... بركات»
  13. The cause of deprivations and problems is our own performance/actions. «بما كانوا یكسبون»
  14. Obstinacy and persistence in sin lead to divine wrath and punishment. «فاخذناهم بما كانوا یكسبون»

189) Tafsir Furqan

Ayahs

أَفَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ أَن يَأْتِيَهُم بَأْسُنَا بَيَـٰتًا وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ (۹۷)
Do the people of the towns feel secure from Our punishment overtaking them at night while they are asleep? (97)
أَوَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ أَن يَأْتِيَهُم بَأْسُنَا ضُحًى وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ (۹۸)
Do the people of the towns feel secure from Our punishment overtaking them at midday while they are playing around? (98)

Key Points

|

Ayah

أَفَأَمِنُوا۟ مَكْرَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَلَا يَأْمَنُ مَكْرَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلْقَوْمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۹۹)
Do they feel secure from Allah’s devising? No one feels secure from Allah’s devising except the people who are losers. (99)

Key Points

Although makr (مكر) in Persian means trickery and deceit, in the Arabic language it means planning and devising a solution to divert someone from a goal, whether that goal is righteous or false. The makr of Allah is the Divine plan that frustrates the schemes of the disbelievers. Even the Prophet and the infallible Imams علیهم السلام did not consider themselves safe and were always watchful of their deeds lest a slip should occur from them. Thus, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «انّى اخاف اِن عصیتُ ربّى عذاب یوم عظیم» (190) The narrator says that he was praying behind Imam Sadiq علیه السلام when the Imam said during the prayer: «الّلهم لاتؤمنّى مَكرك» (O Allah! Do not make me heedless or safe from Your makr). Then the Imam said: «فلایأمن مكر اللّه الاّ القوم الخاسرون» (191) Imam Ali علیه السلام says: Do not feel safe from the Divine punishment, even for the righteous ones of this nation, because Allah says: «فلایأمن مكر اللّه الاّ القوم الخاسرون». (192) In reality, not feeling safe from the Divine makr means paying attention to responsibilities and duties and fearing negligence in performing obligations.

190) An'am, 15 191) Tafsir Namuneh 192) Nahj al-Balaghah, Hikmah 377

Messages

1- The probability of danger is sufficient for feeling responsibility. «أفَأمِن...»

2- We should not be deceived by the existing peaceful order and should never consider ourselves immune. «أفَأمِن اهل القُرى...» Yes, punishments are not exclusive to previous nations; the Divine law applies to all nations at all times.

3- If Allah wills, He can send down His wrath at a time when there is no recourse, destroying everyone overnight. «بیاتاً و هم نائمون»

4- Man, despite all His planning, innovation, and progress, should not feel secure from Divine punishment. «أفأمِن... او أمن...»

5- One of the means of attention and removing heedlessness is raising the possibility of danger. «أفأمن، أو أمِن»

6- The sleep of heedlessness and inappropriate amusements are grounds for Divine wrath. «نائمون... یلعبون»

7- Life without faith and piety is nothing more than a game. «و هم یلعبون»

8- Never feel safe from Divine wrath. «أفأمن... او أمن... أفأمنوا مكر اللّه» Yes, living accompanied by fear and hope is desirable.

9- Those who are careless regarding the makr and wrath of Allah are losers. «فلایأمن... الخاسرون»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ لِلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ ٱلْأَرْضَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَهْلِهَآ أَن لَّوْ نَشَآءُ أَصَبْنَـٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ۚ وَنَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ (۱٠٠)
Does it not dawn upon those who inherited the earth after its [former] inhabitants that if We wish We will punish them for their sins, and set a seal on their hearts so they would not hear? (100)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The heart of the believer is like a white page, and with every sin, a black spot appears on it. If he repents, that blackness is cleansed, but if he persists in his sin, that spot increases until it covers his entire heart, preventing him from reaching goodness, and he no longer has a path to felicity, just as Allah says: «بل ران على قلوبهم» [193] Sin and evil deeds become their disposition, habit, and nature. [194] Man sometimes steps so firmly on the wrong path, insisting upon and repeating deviant actions, that the transgression is imprinted upon his soul and heart, becoming his characteristic and disposition, and he no longer accepts any guidance. Of course, Allah, Who is the Creator of causes and the Destroyer of causes (the Ultimate Power), has ordained this effect and influence; therefore, man has brought himself to this stage through his own will. [195]

193) Mutaffifin, 14 194) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 195) Tafsir Namuneh

Messages

1- Negligent individuals require warning and deserve reproach. «أو لم یَهدِ للّذین یرثون...» Groups who attain power must be aware of the records of previous governments and utilize their bitter and sweet experiences.

2- Divine punishments are due to human sins. «بذنوبهم» Sin is the prelude to the sealing of the heart, an obstacle to correct understanding, and a cause for the withdrawal of Divine success (Tawfiq) from man.

3- Failure to comprehend religious truths is among the Divine punishments. «نطبع على قلوبهم فهم لایسمعون»

4- The laws governing history are constant. Therefore, the history of those who passed away can be a guide for the actions of future generations. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

تِلْكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآئِهَا ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِمَا كَذَّبُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱٠۱)
These are the towns some of whose accounts We recount to you. Their apostles certainly brought them manifest proofs, but they were not the ones to believe in what they had denied earlier. Thus does Allah put a seal on the hearts of the faithless. (101)

Key Points

The meaning of «قُرى» (towns/cities) here refers to the settlements to which Prophets such as Salih, Shu'ayb, Lut, and Hud علیهم السلام, were sent to guide their people.

Messages

1- The part of history that serves as a lesson should be narrated. «نقصّ علیك من أنبائها»

2- All the Prophets had miracles and spared no clear evidence for the guidance of the people. «رسلهم بالبیّنات»

3- The denial of the people should not cause weakness among religious preachers, as this has been the case throughout history. «لقد جائتهم رسلهم... فما كانوا لیؤمنوا»

4- Fanaticism, obstinacy, and stubbornness are the roots of disbelief and infidelity. «فما كانوا لیؤمنوا بما كذّبوا... الكافرین»

5- Rejecting the invitation and miracles of the Prophets is a sign of spiritual blindness and the sealing of the heart. «فما كانوا لیؤمنوا... كذلك یطبع اللّه على قلوب»

6- Disbelief and obstinacy on the part of the people cause the heart to be sealed by Allah. «یطبع اللّه على قلوب الكافرین»

Ayah

وَمَا وَجَدْنَا لِأَكْثَرِهِم مِّنْ عَهْدٍ ۖ وَإِن وَجَدْنَآ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَفَـٰسِقِينَ (۱٠۲)
We did not find in most of them any [loyalty to] covenants. Indeed, We found most of them to be transgressors. (102)

Key Points

The meaning of the "covenant" ('ahd) is either the primordial covenant (Peyman-e Fitrat), "ألم أعهد الیكم یا بنى آدم..." (196), or the invitation of the prophets, or specific covenants that people sometimes made with the prophets, for example, saying, "If you perform such-and-such a miracle, or solve such-and-such a problem for us, we will believe." They said to Prophet Moses: "لئن كشفت عنّا الرّجز لنؤمننّ لك و لنرسلنّ معك بنى اسرائیل. فلمّا كشفنا عنهم الرّجز الى أجل هم بالغوه اذا هم ینكثون" (197) If you remove this affliction from us, we will certainly believe in you and send the Children of Israel with you. But as soon as We removed the affliction from them, they broke their promise and covenant. Allah has clarified truths for humanity through the primordial nature (Fitrah) and the prophets so that they may submit and commit to them, but a group of people has always forgotten the call of the Fitrah and the prophets, deviated from the path of truth, and become wicked (fāsiq). Imam Kazim عليه السلام said: "اذا جاء الیقین لم یجز الشك" When certainty regarding a matter is achieved, doubt and skepticism are no longer justified. He then recited this Ayah. (198) Perhaps the intent of the Hadith is that if someone recognizes the truth through proof but casts doubt upon it due to personal desires (hawā al-nafs), he becomes an instance of the above Ayah and is a covenant-breaker. Allah has praised the faithful and condemned the covenant-breakers in the Quran. He has praised the faithful with titles such as: "مؤمنون" (199), "متّقین" (200), "اولوا الالباب" (201), and "ابرار" (202). However, in condemning the covenant-breakers, He has used descriptions such as: "فاسقین" (203), "كافرین" (204), "مشركین" (205), "خاسرون" (206), "شرّ الدّوابّ" (207), "قلوبهم قاسیة" (208), and "لهم اللّعنة و لهم سوء الدّار" (209), and in one instance, He deems fighting them necessary: "الا تقاتلون قوماً نكثوا ایمانهم" (210).

196) Yā Sīn, 60 197) Al-A‘rāf, 134 to 135 198) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 399 199) Al-Mu’minūn, 1 and 8 200) Āl ‘Imrān, 76 201) Ar-Ra‘d, 20 202) Al-Insān, 5 and 7 203) Al-A‘rāf, 102 204) Al-Anfāl, 55 to 56 205) At-Tawbah, 7 206) Al-Baqarah, 27 207) Al-Anfāl, 56 208) Al-Mā’idah, 13 209) Ar-Ra‘d, 25 210) At-Tawbah, 13

Messages

1- The destruction of previous nations (mentioned in the preceding verses) was due to their covenant-breaking and rebellion. "ما وجدنا لاكثرهم من عهد"

2- Fairness must be observed in judgment. "لاكثرهم" (for most of them), not all of them.

3- The historical record of past generations, despite the presence of prophets and clear proofs, is dark and bleak; therefore, we should not be arrogant about our own religion and faith. "ما وجدنا لاكثرهم من عهد..."

4- Covenant-breaking is a sign of wickedness (fisq). "ما وجدنا... من عهد... لفاسقین"

Ayah

ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ فَظَلَمُوا۟ بِهَا ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۱٠۳)
Then after them We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his elite, but they wronged them. So observe how was the fate of the agents of corruption! (103)

Key Points

This Surah is the first Makkan Surah that presents the story of Moses. The name of Prophet Moses (Musa) is mentioned 136 times in the Quran, and the name of no other person has been mentioned this many times. According to Tafsir al-Mizan, the Quran has not narrated as many miracles for any other prophet as it has for Prophet Moses. The story of Prophet Moses عليه السلام in the Quran can be divided into five stages: 1- The period of birth and childhood. 2- Fleeing from Egypt to Madyan and staying with Prophet Shu'ayb عليه السلام. 3- The prophethood (Bi'that) and his encounters with Pharaoh. 4- His and his people's salvation from Pharaoh's domination and the return to the Sinai desert. 5- His encounters with the people of Bani Israel.

Messages

1- Prophet Moses was commissioned (as a prophet) after Prophets Noah, Hud, Salih, Lot, and Shu'ayb علیهم السلام, and that too after a long interval and period of time. «ثمّ بعثنا من بعدهم»

2- One of the philosophies behind the commissioning of the prophets is the struggle against the tyrant (Taghut). «الى فرعون وملأه»

3- To reform society, one must address the main figures and purify the water at its source. «الى فرعون و ملائه»

4- Denying the prophets and the divine signs is an instance of oppression (zulm). «فظلموا بها»

5- We must analyze history and take lessons from it. «فانظر»

6- What is important is the outcome and ultimate end of the matter, not the fleeting appearances. «عاقبة المفسدین»

7- Faith reforms society, while disbelief (kufr) and atheism lead it to corruption. «المفسدین»

8- When a tyrant (Taghut) rules society instead of the religion of Allah, he resorts to every corruption to preserve and strengthen himself. «المفسدین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ يَـٰفِرْعَوْنُ إِنِّى رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٠٤)
And Moses said, ‘O Pharaoh, I am indeed an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds. (104)

Messages

1- Initially, words were exchanged between Moses (Musa) and Pharaoh, and only after that did Moses announce his mission. «و قال موسى» (The letter waw indicates that some words were spoken, and then the mission was announced.)

2- In the path of their call, the prophets addressed the tyrants (Taghuts) without any fear or weakness. «و قال موسى یا فرعون»

3- To reform a society that has an organized structure, one must first approach the leaders. «یا فرعون»

4- The prophets claimed nothing except the call to truth. «قال انّى رسول»

5- The mission of the prophets is linked to the governance of the entire universe. «رسول من ربّ العالمین»

6- Pharaoh considered himself the supreme Lord of the people, «أنا ربّكم الاعلى» (211). Therefore, Musa courageously and explicitly introduced himself as the messenger of the «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the Worlds), to state that: You, too, are under the nurturing and dominion of the Lord of the Worlds. «انّى رسول من ربّ العالمین»

211) An-Nazi'at, 24

Ayah

حَقِيقٌ عَلَىٰٓ أَن لَّآ أَقُولَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلْحَقَّ ۚ قَدْ جِئْتُكُم بِبَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعِىَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (۱٠٥)
It behooves me to say nothing about Allah except the truth. I certainly bring you a manifest proof from your Lord. So let the Children of Israel go with me.’ (105)

Messages

1- The Prophets say nothing but the truth, thus they are infallible in conveying the message. «لا أقول على اللّه الاّ الحقّ»

2- Prophets must possess miracles, «قد جئتكم ببیّنة», and in their mission, they have no dictatorship or imposition.

3- All people have one Lord, thus the claim of Lordship is baseless. «ربّكم»

4- Until people achieve freedom from the domination of tyrants, a complete cultural and guidance program cannot be planned for them. «فأرسل معى بنى اسرائیل»

5- The liberation of the Children of Israel is a Divine command and part of Moses' mission, not a personal decision. «انّى رسول من ربّ العالمین... فارسل...»

6- The freedom of human beings is among the primary goals of the Prophets. «فأرسل معى بنى اسرائیل» And whoever attains power besides them enslaves the people. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «مَن نال استطال» (212)

(212) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 216

Ayahs

قَالَ إِن كُنتَ جِئْتَ بِـَٔايَةٍ فَأْتِ بِهَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۱٠٦)
He said, ‘If you have brought a sign, produce it, should you be truthful.’ (106)
فَأَلْقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ ثُعْبَانٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱٠۷)
Thereat he threw down his staff, and behold, it became a manifest python. (107)

Key Points

Other miracles were also manifested from the staff of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), including that after it turned into a serpent, it swallowed all the fabrications of the sorcerers, «تلقف ما یأفكون» [213]. It struck the water of the sea and split it, «أنِ اضرب بعصاك البحر فانفلق» [214]. It struck the stone and springs of water gushed forth from it. «اِضرب بعصاك الحجر فانفجرت منه اثنتا عشرة عَینا» [215]. In the presence of Moses (عليه السلام) himself, the staff became a small, highly active snake, and Moses fled, «جانّ ولّى مدبرا» [216]. In the presence of the people, it moved like an ordinary snake, «حیّة تسعى » [217]. And in the presence of Pharaoh, it became a large snake and a serpent (dragon). «فاذا هى ثعبان». Therefore, in propagation (Da'wah) and education, the type and inclination of the audience must be considered, and each one must be convinced according to their own situation. Imam Reza (عليه السلام) said: During the time of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), sorcery and magic were very prevalent, and that Prophet, by bringing a miracle that they were incapable of performing, nullified their magic and completed the argument (Hujjah) against them. [218] There was no delay between the throwing down of the staff and its transformation into a serpent. «فاذا هى ثعبان».

213) Shu'ara, 45 214) Shu'ara, 63 215) Baqarah, 60 216) Naml, 10 217) Taha, 20 218) Uyūn Akhbār al-Ridā, Vol. 2, p. 80

Messages

1- The miracle is proof of the truthfulness of prophethood. «فأت بها ان كنت من الصادقین» (Bring it forth if you are among the truthful). Pharaoh, by uttering this truthful statement, had a false intention and did not acknowledge anyone superior to himself.

2- The miracle must be clear to everyone and leave no room for doubt. «مبین» (Manifest/Clear).

Ayahs

وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُۥ فَإِذَا هِىَ بَيْضَآءُ لِلنَّـٰظِرِينَ (۱٠۸)
Then he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white to the onlookers. (108)
قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسَـٰحِرٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱٠۹)
The elite of Pharaoh’s people said, ‘This is indeed an expert magician; (109)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Prophets, alongside warning (the staff turning into a serpent, which involved a type of fear and terror), also show glad tidings and the luminous hand. «بیضاء» (When facing the arrogant, one must first shatter his pride, and then also show the hand of kindness.)

2- Moses' hand turning white was another miracle of that Prophet, an unexpected event visible to everyone. «فاذا هى بیضاء للناظرین»

3- The entourage of the tyrants shares in their crimes. «قال الملأ من قوم فرعون»

4- While acknowledging the greatness of the work of Prophet Moses, the entourage still tried to preserve the Pharaonic regime. «انّ هذا لساحر مبین»

5- Arrogant and stubborn individuals, when faced with proof, logic, and the possibility of defeat, resort to justification and become more obstinate. «انّ هذا لساحر علیم» (The particle «انّ», the letter «لام», and the nominal sentence are signs of emphasis.)

6- Opponents try to ruin the reputation of the Prophets through slander. «لساحر علیم»

Ayah

يُرِيدُ أَن يُخْرِجَكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِكُمْ ۖ فَمَاذَا تَأْمُرُونَ (۱۱٠)
he seeks to expel you from your land.’ ‘So what do you advise?’ (110)

Key Points

The Pharaohites, in order to divert public opinion, leveled accusations against Prophet Moses (عليه السلام). They considered him a sorcerer from a doctrinal perspective and portrayed him as a seditionist from a social and political perspective.

Messages

1- One of the weapons used by opponents is accusing men of truth of seeking power. «یرید أن یخرجكم»

2- Sometimes enemies exploit the feelings and emotions of the people, such as patriotism, against the men of God. «یخرجكم من ارضكم»

3- The disbeliever judges everyone by his own standard. «یخرجكم من ارضكم» Since Pharaoh was attached to materialism and the land, he thought Moses (عليه السلام) was also attached to their wealth and territory.

4- Tyrants (Taghuts), despite their despotism, are sometimes forced to consult with their entourage during political crises. «فماذا تأمرون»

Ayahs

قَالُوٓا۟ أَرْجِهْ وَأَخَاهُ وَأَرْسِلْ فِى ٱلْمَدَآئِنِ حَـٰشِرِينَ (۱۱۱)
They said, ‘Put him and his brother off for a while, and send heralds to the cities, (111)
يَأْتُوكَ بِكُلِّ سَـٰحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ (۱۱۲)
to bring you every expert magician.’ (112)

Key Points

The word "أرجِ" (Arji'), an imperative verb derived from the root "rajā'," means both "to imprison" and "to postpone" (or "to delay"). However, considering the widespread wave of Moses' invitation and his miracles, imprisoning him was not suitable for Pharaoh; therefore, the meaning of "to postpone" is more appropriate. Here, the phrase "ساحر علیم" (a knowledgeable sorcerer) is used, but in Ayah 37 of Surah Ash-Shu'ara, the expression "سحّار علیم" (a highly skilled sorcerer) is used, which indicates the complete expertise and proficiency of the magicians.

Messages

1- In tyrannical Pharaonic governments, enlightening the people and every voice of truth leads to punishment. "قالوا أرجِه"

2- Character assassination is more important than murder and punishment. "أرجِه" (Based on the interpretation that "أرجِه" refers to the suggestion to delay Moses' punishment in order to disgrace him publicly.)

3- Tyrants organize nationwide and global gatherings of specialists to defeat the truth. "یأتوك بكلّ ساحر علیم"

4- Even the disbelievers know that to counter cultural work, stronger cultural work must be performed. "بكلّ ساحر علیم"

5- Sometimes knowledge and expertise fall into the hands of misguided individuals and are used in the path of fighting the truth. "یأتوك بكلّ ساحر علیم"

Ayahs

وَجَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ فِرْعَوْنَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبِينَ (۱۱۳)
And the magicians came to Pharaoh. They said, ‘We shall indeed have a reward if we were to be the victors?’ (113)
قَالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبِينَ (۱۱٤)
He said, ‘Of course! And indeed you shall be among those near [to me].’ (114)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Whoever is not dependent upon Allah will be attracted by every call. «یأتوك... جاء السحرة فرعون»

2- The various assurances and the demand for a commitment from Pharaoh regarding the payment of the reward indicate Pharaoh's lack of commitment and stinginess. «انّ لنا لاجرا»

3- One of the differences between the prophets and the sorcerers is the difference in goals and motivations. The prophets worked to guide the people and sought no reward, «ما اسئلكم علیه من أجر» (219), but the sorcerers work only for the world and material gains. «انّ لنا لاجراً»

4- Those who turn to the court of the tyrant (Taghut) have worldly goals. «انّ لنا لاجراً»

5- Tyrants (Taghuts) and oppressors support scholars and specialists who oppose the truth, and they invest in suppressing the truth and the men of truth. «قال نعم» (Have we sought out religious scholars or supported them for the destruction of falsehood!)

6- Even tyrants (Taghuts) sometimes become so helpless that they surrender to every request and yield to every expectation and proposal. «قال نعم»

7- Political rewards, status, and social position are more valuable than wealth to the worldly-minded. «لمن المقرّبین»

(219) Shu'ara, 109, 127, 145, 164 and 180

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِمَّآ أَن تُلْقِىَ وَإِمَّآ أَن نَّكُونَ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُلْقِينَ (۱۱٥)
They said, ‘O Moses, will you throw [first], or shall we throw?’ (115)
قَالَ أَلْقُوا۟ ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَلْقَوْا۟ سَحَرُوٓا۟ أَعْيُنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَٱسْتَرْهَبُوهُمْ وَجَآءُو بِسِحْرٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱۱٦)
He said, ‘Throw [yours].’ So when they threw, they bewitched the people’s eyes and overawed them, producing a tremendous magic. (116)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- The sorcerers produced magic that outwardly resembled the miracle of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), becoming manifest when cast upon the ground. «تلقى... ملقین»

2- In the pursuit of knowledge, one should not rely solely on the apparent senses, as sometimes the eye itself is enchanted and influenced. «سحروا أعین النّاس»

3- The effect of the sorcerers' magic is upon the eyes of the people, not reality, similar to a mirage that appears to be water. «سحروا أعین النّاس» Indeed, the Prophets deal with the people's insight (basirat), while the sorcerers deal with the people's sight (basar).

4- Tyrants (Taghuts) and claimants of falsehood always strive to frighten people by creating dread and terror. «استرهبوهم»

5- We must not view the enemy and his work as small, simple, or superficial. «جاءو بسحر عظیم»

6- To undermine any truth, similar means are employed: religion against religion, and the pseudo-cleric against the true cleric. Here, too, magic was used against the miracle. «بسحر عظیم»

Ayah

۞ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَلْقِ عَصَاكَ ۖ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ (۱۱۷)
And We signalled to Moses: ‘Throw down your staff.’ And behold, it was swallowing what they had faked. (117)

Key Points

The word "تَلقَف" (talqaf), derived from "لَقف" (laqf), means to seize something with power and speed, and here it means to swallow. The word "یأفكون" (ya'fakūn), derived from "اِفك" (ifk), refers to a lie presented in the guise of truth and honesty, or falsehood that has the appearance of truth. If the miracle and staff of Prophet Moses neutralized and destroyed the magical fabrications of the sorcerers, then seeking aid from the eternal miracle of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, namely the Quran and its recitation, will certainly foil the fabrications and conspiracies of the followers of falsehood.

Messages

1- Divine Prophets are directly aided by the unseen (Ghayb) during critical situations. "أوحینا الى موسى أن القِ عصاك" Yes, Allah aids His sincere servants in international gatherings and in the presence of the enemies of truth, and makes them victorious over them.

2- Falsehoods that affect the eyes and hearts of the people and cause them to tremble—"سحروا أعین الناس و استرهبوهم"—are exposed and shattered by the Prophet through Divine power. "تلقف ما یأفكون"

Ayahs

فَوَقَعَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَبَطَلَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۱۸)
So the truth came out, and what they had wrought was reduced to naught. (118)
فَغُلِبُوا۟ هُنَالِكَ وَٱنقَلَبُوا۟ صَـٰغِرِينَ (۱۱۹)
Thereat they were vanquished, and they retreated, humiliated. (119)

Key Points

|

Ayah

وَأُلْقِىَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سَـٰجِدِينَ (۱۲٠)
And the magicians fell down in prostration. (120)

Key Points

With the victory of the miracle of Prophet Moses, a severe blow was dealt to the Pharaonic regime, but more importantly, the sorcerers believed in Moses all at once, and Pharaoh's prestige collapsed from within. The referent of the pronoun in the phrase «ما كانوا یعملون» (what they were doing) is either the sorcerers, or the Pharaoh's people, or the combination of both groups. Therefore, the Ayah can be interpreted in three ways.

Messages

1- The Truth is victorious, and falsehood is annihilated. «فوقع الحقّ» (So the Truth was established). Falsehoods made by human hands cannot withstand the Truth.

2- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), by stating the Truth and making reality manifest, falsehood can be driven out of the arena. «فوقع الحقّ و بطل ما كانوا یعملون»

3- One manifestation from the side of the Truth obliterates thousands of deceptive manifestations of falsehood. «فوقع الحقّ و بطل ما كانوا یعملون» They labored for a long time and employed all types of magic to combat the miracle of Moses, but all of it was useless.

4- After their defeat, the sorcerers, along with Pharaoh's people, shamefully left the arena of contest. «انقلبوا صاغرین»

5- Those who claimed victory, «نحن الغالبین» [220], became the defeated ones of history. «فغلبوا... صاغرین»

6- The greatness of the Divine miracle was such that the sorcerers seemingly fell into prostration involuntarily and unconsciously. «اُلقى» (were cast down) is used in the passive voice.

7- Recognizing the Truth is the basis for humility and submission. «اُلقى السحرة ساجدین» (The sorcerers were cast down, prostrating). Through correct recognition, one can change belief and attain happiness in a single moment.

8- Prostration (Sajdah) is the most prominent manifestation of submission and has a very long history. «ساجدین»

220) A'raf, 113

Ayahs

قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۲۱)
They said, ‘We have believed in the Lord of all the worlds, (121)
رَبِّ مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ (۱۲۲)
the Lord of Moses and Aaron.’ (122)

Key Points

The sorcerers who had come to the scene before Pharaoh to disgrace Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) and gain wealth and status, upon seeing the miracle of Moses (عليه السلام), believed, declared their faith, and affirmed the principles of Tawhid (monotheism), Prophethood, and the Resurrection (Ma'ad). They declared Tawhid and Prophethood with the phrases, «آمنّا بربّ العالمین، ربّ موسى وهارون» (We believe in the Lord of the worlds, the Lord of Moses and Aaron), and they declared faith in the Resurrection in Ayah 125 with the phrase, «الى ربّنا منقلبون» (To our Lord we shall return). In fact, by gathering the sorcerers, Pharaoh caused them to believe in Moses all at once. Indeed, an enemy may become a source of good, if Allah wills. The sorcerers mentioned Moses and Aaron (عليهما السلام) together, saying: «ربّ موسى و هارون» (the Lord of Moses and Aaron), because Aaron also played a role alongside Moses in guiding them. Alternatively, if they had only said: «ربّ موسى» (the Lord of Moses), there was a possibility of misuse by the Pharaohites, who might claim that the Lord of Moses was Pharaoh, since Moses had grown up for years in Pharaoh's house and under his tutelage. Just as Pharaoh himself said to Moses (عليه السلام): Did We not raise you among us as a child? «ألم نربّك فینا ولیداً» [221] Therefore, the sorcerers foiled this plot and said: «ربّ موسى و هارون» (the Lord of Moses and Aaron). Imam Ali (عليه السلام) said: «كُن لما لا ترجو أرجى منك لما تَرجو» (Be more hopeful for that which you do not hope for, than for that which you do hope for). Just as Moses (عليه السلام) set out to bring fire for his family, but returned with the status of Prophethood; the Queen of Sheba left her city to see Prophet Solomon (عليه السلام), but returned as a believer; and the sorcerers came to Pharaoh's court to attain status and position, but returned with faith and as believers. [222]

221) Shu'ara, 18 222) Tafsir Ithna Ashari, Tuhaf al-'Uqul, p. 208

Messages

1- The sorcerers had heard the content of Moses' invitation before the contest, and thus, after seeing his miracle, they affirmed those very things they had heard. «قالوا آمنّا» (They said: We believe).

2- Man is flexible and free, and after understanding the truth, he can change his belief and thought. «قالوا آمنّا»

3- The repentance for intellectual deviation and polytheism (Shirk) is faith. «آمنّا»

4- The sorcerers understood the difference between a miracle and magic better than others, and thus they believed very quickly. «آمنّا»

5- Sometimes people's lack of faith is due to the truth being unknown; as soon as the truth becomes clear, a group believes. «آمنّا»

6- Misuse must be prevented. So that Pharaoh would not claim: I am the very «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the worlds), the sorcerers said: «ربّ موسى و هارون» (the Lord of Moses and Aaron).

7- There is no dispute regarding Allah's Creatorship (Khaliqiyyah); the conflict is over Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah) and the administration of the world by His hand. «ربّ العالمین ربّ موسى و هارون»

Ayah

قَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَمَكْرٌ مَّكَرْتُمُوهُ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ لِتُخْرِجُوا۟ مِنْهَآ أَهْلَهَا ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (۱۲۳)
Pharaoh said, ‘Do you profess faith in Him before I may permit you? It is indeed a plot you have devised in the city to expel its people from it. Soon you will know [the consequences]! (123)

Messages

1- In the cultural colonization of governments, freedom of thought and the right to choose belief are denied to the people. «قال فرعون آمنتم به قبل أن آذن لكم»

2- One of the methods of the tyrants (Taghuts) is slandering the men of truth. «انّ هذا لمكر مكرتموه» They do not even trust their own agents and view every spiritual movement as political, just as elsewhere, Pharaoh considered the sorcerers to be students of Moses. «انّه لكبیركم الّذى علّمكم السّحر» (223)

3- Tyrants exploit the people's sense of patriotism and emotions in the pursuit of their own objectives. «لتخرجوا منها أهلها»

4- Threats of murder and torture are among the tools of the tyrants' dominance. «فسوف تعلمون»

223) Taha, 71

Ayahs

لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلَـٰفٍ ثُمَّ لَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱۲٤)
Surely I will cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and then I will surely crucify all of you.’ (124)
قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ (۱۲٥)
They said, ‘Indeed we shall return to our Lord. (125)

Key Points

We read similar verses in Surah Taha as well. Although the Qur'an does not explicitly mention the execution of these threats, according to narrations and history, Pharaoh did cut the believers in Musa (عليه السلام) into pieces and hung them on the branches of date palms. As Tabari says: «كانوا أوّل النّهار كفّاراً سحرة و آخر النّهار شهداء بَررَة» (They were disbelieving sorcerers at the beginning of the day, and righteous martyrs at the end of the day.) Pharaoh and the Pharaohites, despite all their preparations and commotion, were ultimately humiliated, «انقلبوا صاغرین» (they turned back humbled). But the sorcerers, through faith, achieved growth and felicity, preferring death in the path of the true belief over life under Pharaoh's dominion. «الى ربّنا منقلبون» (to our Lord we are returning).

Messages

1- Violence and threats are among the weapons of the oppressors and the powerful. «لاُقطّعنّ»

2- Tyrants (Taghuts), in order to prevent the spread of reformist and revolutionary ideas, threaten, torture, and kill the pioneers of the revolution. «لاصلّبنّكم»

3- Man is not condemned by systems and environments; through faith and will, he can resist all of them. «لاُقطّعنّ - قالوا»

4- He who believes with insight does not abandon his faith amidst various waves (of opposition) and is not afraid of threats. «قالوا انّا الى ربّنا» Yes, faith transforms a person and elevates his capacity. The sorcerers who yesterday were awaiting Pharaoh's reward, today, because of faith, those things hold no value for them.

5- Death in the path of truth is better than life in the path of falsehood. «الى ربّنا منقلبون»

6- One should not be arrogant about a history of faith and worship; sometimes a few sorcerers and disbelievers, through a transformation, surpass everyone else. «الى ربّنا منقلبون»

7- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), we should recall the chivalry and courage of those who came before. «قالوا...»

8- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and remembering it in the face of dangers and threats insures a person. «الى ربّنا منقلبون» Just as they told Pharaoh: Whatever you decide to do, your dominion is only limited to this world. «فاقض ما أنت قاض انّما تقضى هذه الحیاة الدّنیا» (So decree whatever you are going to decree. You can only decree [for] this worldly life.) [224]

9- We should not despair of the guidance of misguided individuals, even their leaders, for perhaps they will repent and change suddenly. «قالوا انّا الى ربّنا منقلبون»

224) Taha, 72

Ayah

وَمَا تَنقِمُ مِنَّآ إِلَّآ أَنْ ءَامَنَّا بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّنَا لَمَّا جَآءَتْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَآ أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَتَوَفَّنَا مُسْلِمِينَ (۱۲٦)
You are vindictive toward us only because we have believed in the signs of our Lord, when they came to us.’ ‘Our Lord! Pour patience upon us, and grant us to die as Muslims.’ (126)

Key Points

This verse is a response to the propaganda and accusations leveled by Pharaoh against the believers and the companions of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), which were alluded to in previous verses, such as [the claim] that you are conspirators and intend to seize the people's homeland and dwellings and expel them. They said: You also know that our crime is only our faith, not what you are saying. Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: Whoever considers himself superior to others is arrogant (mustakbir). They said to the Imam: Sometimes a person considers himself superior because others are engaged in sin and he is not! The Imam said: Perhaps he has repented and been forgiven, but you are unaware and still see him that way. Have you not heard the story of the sorcerers who came with the motive of defeating Moses, but repented, were guided, and had a good end? (225)

225) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- Faith in Allah and refusing to submit to the tyrant (Taghut) is not without trouble. «و ما تنقم»

2- Faith is valuable when it is based on proof and comes after seeing the signs of truth. «آمنّا بآیات ربّنا لمّا جائتنا»

3- Tyrants (Taghuts) oppose the monotheistic idea and belief, not the people themselves. «الاّ أن آمنّا»

4- The signs of true believers are tranquility, resistance, clarity, courage, supplication, and prayer, especially during hardships. «آمنّا... ربّنا افرغ علینا صبراً»

5- In prayer, we should appeal to the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah. «ربّنا»

6- The best method against the threats of tyrants (Taghuts) is prayer, reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul), and maintaining faith and steadfastness. «ربّنا أفرغ علینا صبراً و توفّنا مسلمین»

7- When threats are severe, they require greater patience and resistance. The sorcerers said: «أفرغ علینا صبراً» (meaning: fill our hearts completely with patience) and they did not say: «أنزل علینا» (send down upon us).

8- A good end (Husn al-'Aqibah) requires prayer, seeking aid, and Divine success (Tawfiq). «ربّنا... توفّنا مسلمین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِ فِرْعَوْنَ أَتَذَرُ مُوسَىٰ وَقَوْمَهُۥ لِيُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَيَذَرَكَ وَءَالِهَتَكَ ۚ قَالَ سَنُقَتِّلُ أَبْنَآءَهُمْ وَنَسْتَحْىِۦ نِسَآءَهُمْ وَإِنَّا فَوْقَهُمْ قَـٰهِرُونَ (۱۲۷)
The elite of Pharaoh’s people said, ‘Will you leave Moses and his people to cause corruption in the land, and to abandon you and your gods?’ He said, ‘We will kill their sons and spare their women, and indeed we are dominant over them.’ (127)

Key Points

After his defeat against Moses, Pharaoh ceased his persecution of him (عليه السلام) and the Children of Israel. During this period, the propagation efforts of Moses' followers increased greatly, to the extent that the elite (al-Mala') of Pharaoh's people felt threatened by his silence and demanded that he prevent and confront them.

Pharaoh did not consider himself the Creator (Khāliq); rather, he considered himself the Lord (Rabb): «انا ربّكم الاعلى» (I am your Lord, the Most High) [226]. And he would say: "I do not know of any god for you other than myself," «ما عَلمتُ لكم من اله غیرى» [227]. However, he and his people did have deities (ma'būdāt), and they worshipped them as manifestations of the Creator. «آلهتك» (Your gods).

226) An-Nāzi‘āt, 24 227) Al-Qaṣaṣ, 38

Messages

1- A portion of the corruption of the tyrants (ṭāghūts) is due to the corruption of their entourage. «وقال الملأ» (And the chiefs said).

2- False tyrannical regimes become subject to contradictory and confused decisions when confronting the truth and the men of truth. Thus, Pharaoh sometimes decides to kill Moses, «ذرونى اَقتُل موسى» (Leave me to kill Moses) [228], and sometimes leaves him free, to the extent that he is criticized by his entourage. «أتذر موسى و قومه» (Will you leave Moses and his people?).

3- The reformative movement of the prophets is viewed by the tyrants as corruption, sedition (fitnah), chaos, and contrary to public interest. «لیفسدوا فى الارض» (That they may cause corruption in the land).

4- Eliminating the sons and preserving the women is a Pharaonic policy intended to strip men of chivalry and zeal (ghayrah), and to turn women into tools for their programs and policies. This is similar to today's colonial policies. «سنقتّل ابناءهم و نستحیى نساءهم» (We will kill their sons and let their women live).

5- The opponents of the prophets have plans for both young groups: boys and girls. «سنقتّل ابناءهم و نستحیى نساءهم» (We will kill their sons and let their women live).

6- Uttering hollow slogans to preserve oneself is a Pharaonic policy. «انّا فوقهم قاهرون» (Indeed, we are dominant over them). (This phrase is a metaphor for complete dominance).

228) Ghāfir, 26

Ayah

قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ ٱسْتَعِينُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱصْبِرُوٓا۟ ۖ إِنَّ ٱلْأَرْضَ لِلَّهِ يُورِثُهَا مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ ۖ وَٱلْعَـٰقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۲۸)
Moses said to his people, ‘Turn to Allah for help and be patient. The earth indeed belongs to Allah, and He gives its inheritance to whomever He wishes of His servants, and the outcome will be in favour of the Godwary.’ (128)

Key Points

In this Ayah, two commands are presented alongside two glad tidings: the command for seeking help (استعانت) and patience (صبر), and the glad tidings of inheriting the earth and a good end for the righteous (متّقین). Imam Baqir عليه السلام narrates from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام that He said: My Household (Ahl al-Bayt) and I are the manifestation of this Ayah. And Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said in the interpretation of this Ayah: «فما كان للّه فهو لرسوله و ما كان لرسول اللّه فهو للامام بعد رسول اللّه» (229) Therefore, if the Ayah states: the earth belongs to Allah, it means that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and the Imams علیهم السلام are the inheritors of the earth from Allah.

229) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- The leader comforts the community during sensitive times. «قال موسى لقومه...» Prophet Moses gave his companions the glad tidings of the destruction of the Pharaohites and the dominance of the believers over them.

2- Among the factors for victory and protection against threats are seeking help, reliance, resistance, and piety. «استعینوا باللّه واصبروا» (We must both seek help from Him and be patient ourselves.)

3- The destruction of oppressive governments and the succession of patient believers is certain by the Will and Decree of Allah. «یورثها من یشاء»

4- The condition for achieving sovereignty is patience, resistance, and seeking help from Allah. «استعینوا باللّه و اصبروا انّ الارض للّه یورثها من یشاء»

5- Seeking help from Allah and being patient are manifestations of piety and God-fearingness. «استعینوا باللّه و اصبروا... و العاقبة للمتّقین»

6- The people of piety have a good end both in this world, «یورثها من یشاء», and they are the true winners in the Hereafter. «والعاقبة للمتّقین»

7- Hope for a bright future is a promise of the divine religions, including Islam. «و العاقبة للمتّقین»

Ayah

قَالُوٓا۟ أُوذِينَا مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَأْتِيَنَا وَمِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جِئْتَنَا ۚ قَالَ عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَن يُهْلِكَ عَدُوَّكُمْ وَيَسْتَخْلِفَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرَ كَيْفَ تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۲۹)
They said, ‘We were tormented before you came to us and [also] after you came to us.’ He said, ‘Maybe your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors in the land, and then He will see how you act.’ (129)

Key Points

The Children of Israel expected that after the uprising of Moses (عليه السلام), things would be set right overnight, and the country of Egypt, with all its resources, would be placed at their disposal, and the Pharaohites would be destroyed. Therefore, they claimed that the uprising of Prophet Moses had not brought them prosperity. But in response to them, He says: Victory requires conditions such as patience, effort, and reliance (on God), and when these are provided, Divine help arrives.

Messages

1- Prosperity and security are the first demands people make of governments. «قالوا اُوذینا...»

2- People weak in faith expect prosperity from the Prophets under all circumstances, and if not, they criticize them as well. «قالوا اُوذینا من قبل...»

3- Sometimes, heavenly leaders were also subjected to criticism by their followers who lacked capacity and patience. «قالوا اُوذینا من قبل أن تأتینا و من بعد ما جئتنا»

4- Most people perceive happiness and joy in comfort and ease, and consider the absence of these as failure, unaware that heavenly religions have come to regulate and give proper direction to life, not to eliminate all worldly problems for people. «من قبل أن تأتینا و من بعد ما...»

5- Natural and social problems must be solved through natural means and prudence, not through miracles. (Considering the previous Ayah, «اصبروا», and the phrase: «بعد ما جئتنا»)

6- A leader must listen to criticism and deliver a message of hope. «عسى ربّكم»

7- Belief in Allah's supervision leads to precision and piety. «یستخلفكم فى الارض فینظر»

8- Governing people is a means of trial (test), not a pursuit of pleasure. «فینظر كیف»

9- People are responsible regarding governance and are tested through it. «كیف تعملون» and He did not say: «كیف أعمل»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَخَذْنَآ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ بِٱلسِّنِينَ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ (۱۳٠)
Certainly We afflicted Pharaoh’s clan with droughts and loss of produce, so that they may take admonition. (130)

Key Points

| "Sinīn" (سنین) is the plural of "sanah" (سنة), meaning year. However, when used with the word "akhadh" (أخذ - We seized/took), it often means famine and drought. Therefore, the mention of "naqṣ min al-thamarāt" (نقص من الثمرات - decrease in fruits/crops) is perhaps to illustrate the clear manifestation of famine. Sometimes calamities and hardships are meant for man's awakening and trial, or they are the prelude to his destruction. "و لنبلونّكم بشى ء من الخوف و الجوع و نقص من الاموال و الانفس و الثمرات" (230) Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Allah tests the sinners by decreasing crops, withholding blessings, and closing the doors of good treasures, so that they may become aware, remember, repent, and abandon their sins. (231)

230) Baqarah, 155 231) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 143

Messages

1- In education and training, the leverage of hardship and constraint must sometimes be used. "و لقد أخذنا"

2- The Will of Allah governs natural factors. "أخذنا... بالسنین"

3- Famine and drought are either divine punishment or a warning and an alarm clock for awakening. "بالسنین و نقص من الثمرات لعلّهم یذّكّرون" Yes, the transformations of existence are purposeful.

4- The potential for guidance and felicity exists even within the family of the most arrogant individuals. "آل فرعون... لعلّهم یذّكّرون"

5- Despite efforts to guide people, no means or plan may be effective for some, because man is free in acceptance, not compelled. "أخذنا... لعلّهم"

Ayah

فَإِذَا جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلْحَسَنَةُ قَالُوا۟ لَنَا هَـٰذِهِۦ ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ يَطَّيَّرُوا۟ بِمُوسَىٰ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥٓ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّمَا طَـٰٓئِرُهُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۳۱)
But whenever any good came to them, they would say, ‘This is our due.’ And if any ill visited them, they took it for ill omens attending Moses and those who were with him. (Look! Indeed the cause of their ill omens is from Allah, but most of them do not know.) (131)

Key Points

Yataṭayyarū (یَطیّروا) is derived from taṭayyur (تَطَیُّر), meaning to take a bad omen (or ill-omen). Since Arabs often associated bad omens with the flight or sound of a bird, any kind of bad omen is called ṭiyarah (طَیَره).
