(232) In other verses of the Quran, taking a bad omen regarding the Prophets, even the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, has been mentioned. (233) Taking a bad omen has no natural or logical effect on the occurrence of events and incidents, but it has many psychological effects. We read in narrations: Taking a bad omen is a type of polytheism (shirk) towards Allah, and whenever you take a bad omen, continue with your work and pay no attention to it. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The effect of a bad omen is only as great as the extent to which you accept it; if you take it lightly, its effect will be minimal, and if you take it seriously, it will be highly effective, and if you pay no attention to it, it will have no effect whatsoever. (234) Taking a bad omen has existed and continues to exist among all past and present nations and peoples, and it causes pessimism and stagnation in affairs, hence it has been forbidden. However, taking a good omen (fāl-e nīk) is permissible because it leads to movement, love, and hope. (235)

232) Tafsir Nemooneh 233) Nisa, 78, Nahl, 47 and Ya Sin, 18 234) Tafsirs Nemooneh and Al-Mizan 235) Tafsir Nemooneh

Messages

1- The Pharaohites considered only themselves worthy and believed the right to prosperity belonged exclusively to them. «قالوا لنا هذه»

2- Prosperity and well-being were known and ordinary matters for the Pharaohites, but hardships were unknown and unbelievable to them. (The word al-ḥasanah (الحسنة) accompanied by alif and lām [the definite article] and the particle idhā (اذا) indicates familiarity, while the word sayyi’ah (سیّئة) without alif and lām next to in (اِن) indicates unfamiliarity and disbelief.)

3- Instead of being awakened by the famine and divine warnings, most of the Pharaohites resorted to false analysis and attributed it to Moses. «یطّیّروا بموسى»

4- Attributing good things to oneself and evil and misfortune to the Prophets and religion is a sign of self-conceit and ignorance. «فاذا جائتهم الحسنة قالوا لنا هذه و ان تصبهم سیئة یطیّروا بموسى... اكثرهم لایعلمون»

5- The root of superstitions and bad omens is ignorance. «یطیّروا... لكن اكثرهم لا یعلمون»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ مَهْمَا تَأْتِنَا بِهِۦ مِنْ ءَايَةٍ لِّتَسْحَرَنَا بِهَا فَمَا نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۳۲)
And they said, ‘Whatever sign you may bring us to bewitch us, we are not going to believe you.’ (132)

Key Points

From the fact that they said to Moses: "Whatever sign you bring," it becomes clear that the enemy also knew that Moses' work was not magic, but rather a divine miracle. However, due to obstinacy and arrogance, they would not believe. Only the sorcerers, when they realized that Moses' work was not sorcery, believed without obstinacy. Of course, perhaps the Pharaohites' naming of Moses' work as a "sign" was done out of mockery and ridicule.

Messages

1- Sometimes a person falls so low that he confronts the prophets and divine guides and sees the clear signs of guidance as inverted, dark, and obscure. «و قالوا مهما تأتنا به من آیة لتسحرنا»

2- The accusation of sorcery against the miracles of the prophets has been one of the most common accusations. «لتسحرنا» Elsewhere we read: «كذلك ما أتى الّذین من قبلهم من رسول الاّ قالوا ساحر او مجنون» (236)

3- Man is free in choosing his belief, and the prophets did not force people to believe. Therefore, some people showed obstinacy in the face of their miracles and did not believe. «فما نحن لك بمؤمنین»

236) Adh-Dhariyat, 52

Ayah

فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلطُّوفَانَ وَٱلْجَرَادَ وَٱلْقُمَّلَ وَٱلضَّفَادِعَ وَٱلدَّمَ ءَايَـٰتٍ مُّفَصَّلَـٰتٍ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا مُّجْرِمِينَ (۱۳۳)
So We sent against them a flood and locusts, lice, frogs and blood, as distinct signs. But they acted arrogantly, and they were a guilty lot. (133)

Key Points

The word ṭūfān (طوفان) in Persian means a strong wind (gale), but the Arabs use it to mean a crushing flood. However, some have applied ṭūfān to any widespread and terrifying incident. Qummal (قُمَّل) refers to small creatures such as lice, ants, pests that affect grains, and a type of insect. (237) Blood was one of Allah's signs against the people (of Pharaoh). The intent is the turning of the waters into blood or the widespread nosebleeds among the general populace, which was a form of divine punishment. The punishments of the flood, blood, and the locust invasion were directed only at the Pharaohites, and the Children of Israel (Bani Israel) were safe from them. The story of these punishments is also mentioned in the Torah. (238) Every time a calamity struck them, they would appeal to Moses (عليه السلام) and promise to believe if God removed the affliction. That Prophet would accept, request (relief) from God, and the calamity would be lifted, but they would again abandon their faithlessness and obstinacy, as will also be stated in the subsequent verses.

237) Mufradāt al-Rāghib 238) In the Torah, the turning of waters into blood is mentioned in the Book of Exodus, Chapter 7, Verse 20; the invasion of gnats/lice, in Chapter 8, Verse 7; the hail storm, in Chapter 9, Verse 25; and the invasion of locusts, in Chapter 10, Verse 14.

Messages

1- After Allah's warning, the completion of the argument (itmām al-ḥujjah), and the people's obstinacy and heedlessness, the time for severe punishments arrives. Following the previous verses, He said: «فأرسلنا علیهم...» (So We sent upon them...)

2- That which is a source of mercy is by Divine Will. If He wills, water becomes a means of mercy, and if He wills, it will be a means of punishment. «فارسلنا علیهم الطوفان» (So We sent upon them the flood.)

3- Animals are Divine agents. Sometimes they are agents of mercy, such as the spider's web at the mouth of the cave to protect the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and sometimes they are agents of punishment, such as the Abābīl birds, frogs, locusts, etc. «فارسلنا علیهم... الجراد و القمّل و الضفادع» (So We sent upon them... the locusts, the lice, and the frogs). In reality, existence is obedient to Allah. «و للّه جنود السموات و الارض» (And to Allah belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth.) (239)

4- Calamities often have an educational aspect, and sometimes after every punishment, there is a respite for reflection, repentance, and return. «مفصّلات» (distinct signs/detailed).

5- Sin paves the way for arrogance, and arrogance paves the way for denial. «فاستكبروا و كانوا قوماً مجرمین» (But they were arrogant and were a criminal people.)

239) Al-Fath, 4 and 7

Ayahs

وَلَمَّا وَقَعَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلرِّجْزُ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَى ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ بِمَا عَهِدَ عِندَكَ ۖ لَئِن كَشَفْتَ عَنَّا ٱلرِّجْزَ لَنُؤْمِنَنَّ لَكَ وَلَنُرْسِلَنَّ مَعَكَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (۱۳٤)
Whenever a plague fell upon them, they would say, ‘O Moses, invoke your Lord for us by the covenant He has made with you. If you remove the plague from us, we will certainly believe in you and let the Children of Israel go along with you.’ (134)
فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمُ ٱلرِّجْزَ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ هُم بَـٰلِغُوهُ إِذَا هُمْ يَنكُثُونَ (۱۳٥)
But when We had removed the plague from them until a term that they should have completed, behold, they broke their promise. (135)

Key Points

The word nakth (نَكث) originally means to untwist a spun rope; it was subsequently used to mean breaking a covenant. The term ajal (أجَل) in this Ayah might refer to the period that Prophet Moses عليه السلام specified for the removal of the affliction—for example, that it would be lifted on a certain day or hour—so that they would understand that this was a Divine punishment, not a coincidence. Or, it might mean that that stubborn nation would eventually be afflicted by inevitable wrath, but until the arrival of that fixed term (ajal) and their drowning in the sea, the punishment would be temporarily lifted. The covenant (‘ahd) refers either to the acceptance of Moses’ prayer or to his status of prophethood and messengership. [240]

240) Tafsir Namuneh

Messages

1- Need and affliction break human arrogance. «لمّا وقع علیهم الرجز قالوا...» (When the plague fell upon them, they said...)

What turns lions into foxes is need, it is need, it is need.

2- Even the disbelievers achieved results through seeking intercession (tawassul) with the friends of Allah. «یا موسى ادع لنا» (O Moses, pray to Him for us.)

3- The Pharaohites also believed in the existence of an effective God belonging to Prophet Moses. «یا موسى ادع لنا ربّك» (O Moses, pray to your Lord for us.)

4- Prayer is effective in attracting Allah’s mercy and repelling hardships and afflictions. «اُدع لنا ربّك» (Pray to your Lord for us.)

5- The liberation of human beings is among the missions of the prophets. «لنرسلنّ معك بنى اسرائیل» (We will certainly send forth the Children of Israel with you.)

6- The removal of Divine punishment and wrath is solely in His hands. «فلمّا كشفنا» (But when We removed...)

7- We should not rely heavily on the promises of others during times of distress and necessity. «فلمّا كشفنا... ینكثون» (But when We removed... they broke [their promise].)

8- Human transgression often occurs during times of comfort and feelings of self-sufficiency. «كشفنا... ینكثون» As we read elsewhere: «انّ الانسان لیطغى ان رآه استغنى» [241] (Indeed, man transgresses when he sees himself self-sufficient.)

9- Bitter and sweet events in the cosmic order are time-bound. «الى اجل» (until a term/period.)

241) Alaq, 6-7

Ayah

فَٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ فَأَغْرَقْنَـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَكَانُوا۟ عَنْهَا غَـٰفِلِينَ (۱۳٦)
So We took vengeance on them and drowned them in the sea, for they denied Our signs and were oblivious to them. (136)

Key Points

The word "انتقام" (Intiqam/retribution) here means punishment and chastisement, not vindictiveness. "یَمّ" (Yamm) in ancient Egypt referred to the sea and the river. Since the story of Moses and the drowning of Pharaoh and the Pharaohites relates to Egypt, the Quran uses that ancient word. [242]

That one (Moses) crossed the Nile, with companions like lightning, While this one (Pharaoh) was drowned in humiliation in the river. Observers see with the eye of witnessing (Shahūd) Where that one went, and where this one remained due to denial (Juhūd).

242) Tafsir Namūneh

Messages

1- Paying attention to Allah's retribution (Intiqam) is the basis for remembrance (Tadhakkur) and abandoning sin. «فانتقمنا» (Then We took retribution). Allah is both the Most Merciful of the merciful (Arham ar-Rāhimīn) and the Taker of retribution.

2- Our destiny and the root of misfortunes and calamities are in our own hands. «فاغرقناهم... بانّهم كذّبوا» (So We drowned them... because they denied).

3- Negligence regarding divine signs (Ayāt) carries a heavy price. «فاغرقناهم... كانوا عنها غافلین» (So We drowned them... they were heedless of them).

Ayah

وَأَوْرَثْنَا ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَانُوا۟ يُسْتَضْعَفُونَ مَشَـٰرِقَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَغَـٰرِبَهَا ٱلَّتِى بَـٰرَكْنَا فِيهَا ۖ وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ عَلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ بِمَا صَبَرُوا۟ ۖ وَدَمَّرْنَا مَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ فِرْعَوْنُ وَقَوْمُهُۥ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْرِشُونَ (۱۳۷)
We made the people who were abased the heirs to the east and west of the land which We had blessed, and your Lord’s best word [of promise] was fulfilled for the Children of Israel because of their patience, and We destroyed what Pharaoh and his people had built and what they used to erect. (137)

Key Points

"Inheritance" (Irth) refers to the wealth and property that a person acquires without earning it through work or trade, whether it comes from the deceased or the living. [243] The story of the crossing of the sea by Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) and his followers, and the drowning and punishment of the Pharaohites therein, is a depiction of the Day of Judgment, where everyone enters Hell, and then the righteous (the Muttaqin) are saved. «و ان منكم الاّ واردها... ثمّ ننجّى الّذین اتّقوا و نَذر الظالمین فیها جثیّاً» [244] The lands that were under the control of the Pharaohites were so vast that they had multiple horizons and different sunrises and sunsets. «مشارق الارض و مغاربها» Furthermore, they possessed many material and spiritual products and blessings, as it was the place where divine prophets were commissioned and the burial site of many of them. Before human discoveries, the Quran introduced the Earth as spherical. This is because the necessity of having multiple Easts (Mashariq) and multiple Wests (Magharib) implies that the Earth is spherical and in motion. In this verse, Allah also gives the Prophet of Islam the good news of victory. The use of the word Rabbuka (Your Lord) in the phrase «و تمّت كلمة ربّك» means that you, too, are included in Our support.

243) Tafsir Namuneh 244) Maryam, 71-72

Messages

1- Allah's support for the oppressed (Mustad'afin) is not exclusive to the Children of Israel, but is a permanent tradition (Sunnah). «اورثنا القوم الّذین كانوا یستضعفون» (We made inherit the people who were oppressed) and He did not say: «اورثنا بنى اسرائیل» (We made the Children of Israel inherit).

2- The Children of Israel had been weakened by the Pharaohites over a long period. «و كانوا یستضعفون»

3- The governance of the prophets is the governance of the oppressed. «یستضعفون»

4- The condition for victory is patience and resistance. The oppressed and any nation that is patient and resistant will inherit the earth. «أورثنا... بما صبروا»

5- Allah fulfills His promises completely and without fail. «وتمّت كلمة ربّك»

6- The destruction of the Pharaohites and the establishment of the Children of Israel in power are among the beautiful words and promises of the Lord. «كلمة ربّك الحسنى»

7- Social and historical transformations are in the hands of Allah. «اورثنا... دمّرنا»

8- All manifestations of the power of the tyrant (Taghut) must be eliminated from the divine society. «دمّرنا»

9- The people of Pharaoh were advanced in terms of industry and construction. «یصنع... یعرشون» But growth and development separated from religion and faith do not have a good outcome.

Ayahs

وَجَـٰوَزْنَا بِبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱلْبَحْرَ فَأَتَوْا۟ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ يَعْكُفُونَ عَلَىٰٓ أَصْنَامٍ لَّهُمْ ۚ قَالُوا۟ يَـٰمُوسَى ٱجْعَل لَّنَآ إِلَـٰهًا كَمَا لَهُمْ ءَالِهَةٌ ۚ قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ (۱۳۸)
We carried the Children of Israel across the sea, whereat they came upon a people cleaving to certain idols that they had. They said, ‘O Moses, make for us a god like the gods that they have.’ He said, ‘You are indeed an ignorant lot. (138)
إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ مُتَبَّرٌ مَّا هُمْ فِيهِ وَبَـٰطِلٌ مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۳۹)
What they are engaged in is indeed bound to perish, and what they have been doing shall come to naught.’ (139)

Key Points

|

Ayah

قَالَ أَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ أَبْغِيكُمْ إِلَـٰهًا وَهُوَ فَضَّلَكُمْ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٤٠)
He said, ‘Shall I find you a god other than Allah, while He has graced you over all the nations?’ (140)

Key Points

The word mutabbar (مُتبّر) is derived from tabār (تبار), meaning destruction (or perdition). Those who spent a lifetime in sorcery and magic became so faithful upon seeing one miracle that Pharaoh's threats did not shake them. However, the followers of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), despite seeing all those miracles and achieving victory over Pharaoh, were led astray by the deviation and idol worship of a certain people, and they even requested an idol from Moses (عليه السلام). One day, a Jew, protesting to the Muslims, said: "You had not yet buried the body of your Prophet when you disagreed among yourselves!" Imam Ali (عليه السلام) replied to him: "Our disagreement was concerning the Prophet's words, not concerning the Prophet Himself or concerning Allah. But as for you, your feet had not yet dried from the sea water when you requested an idol from Moses." [245]

245) Nahj al-Balaghah, Saying 317

Messages

  1. Worship (whether true or false) has existed throughout human history. «یعكفون على اصنام لهم» (Even during the time of Moses (عليه السلام), people were worshipping idols.)

  2. Sometimes man neglects the most important blessings and shows ingratitude towards them. «جاوزنا... اجعل لنا»

  3. Sometimes man becomes so misguided in thought that he makes an improper request even from divine leaders. «یا موسى اجعل لنا آلهة»

  4. Individuals and nations are constantly exposed to the danger of deviation. «قالوا یا موسى...»

  5. Superficial faith is fleeting. «وجاوزنا... قالوا... اجعل لنا آلهة»

  6. Worse than knowing external and foreign enemies are ignorant internal friends. «جاوزنا... قالوا پیا موسى اجعل...»

  7. The demands of the people are not always correct, and their majority status or desire is not always a value. «قالوا... اجعل لنا آلهة»

  8. Accepting role models, imitation, and modeling are human characteristics. «اجعل لنا آلهاً كما لهم آلهة»

  9. Ignorance (jahl) is the root of idol worship, and idol worship is an act devoid of reason. «تجهلون» In the culture of the Quran, jahl is placed opposite reason ('aql), not knowledge ('ilm); meaning, an irrational act is called jahl.

  10. Requesting a tangible god (like an idol) from a noble Prophet like Moses is a sign of deep and rooted ignorance. «تجهلون»

  11. Both intellectual deviations and practical deviations are destined to perish. «متّبر ما هم فیه و باطل ما كانوا یعملون» Yes, the end of falsehood is annihilation, so its appearance should not deceive us.

  12. Worship must be based on reason and gratitude. «أغیراللّه ابغیكم الهاً و هو فضّلكم» Allah is to be sought, not fabricated, «ابغیكم» and the worship of anyone other than Allah is incompatible with reason and a spirit of gratitude.

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَنجَيْنَـٰكُم مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ۖ يُقَتِّلُونَ أَبْنَآءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَآءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِى ذَٰلِكُم بَلَآءٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ (۱٤۱)
And when We delivered you from Pharaoh’s clan who inflicted on you a terrible torment, slaughtering your sons and sparing your women, and there was a great test in that from your Lord. (141)

Key Points

«یَسومونكم» (Yasūmūnakum) is derived from sūm, meaning to pursue something or to impose/inflict. In the previous Ayah, which spoke of the superiority and virtue of the Children of Israel, it attributed this solely to Allah, saying: «هو فضّلكم على العالمین» (He preferred you over the worlds). But this Ayah says: We saved them, «انجینا» (Anjaynā). This difference between the singular (He) and the plural (We) is perhaps because Allah’s salvation was accomplished through the patience and resistance of the people themselves and the leadership of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام). Usually, in cases where divine favors are granted through intermediaries, the plural pronoun is used. For example, regarding rain, He says: «أنزلنا» (We sent down) (246), because the sea, heat, clouds, wind, and other factors worked together to bring about the rain.

246) Al-Mu’minūn, 18

Messages

1- The true Savior is Allah, even if the salvation was accomplished through the leadership of Moses (عليه السلام). «انجیناكم»

2- Remembering divine blessings leads to steadfastness and avoidance of polytheism (Shirk). «أغیراللّه ابغیكم... و اذ انجیناكم»

3- Leaders must constantly remind people of divine blessings so that they do not fall into heedlessness. «واذ أنجیناكم»

4- Tyrants (Taghuts) have no power without their helpers and entourage. «آل فرعون» (Pharaoh's people/clan)

5- Tyrants kill the innocent to preserve their rule. «یقتّلون أبناءكم»

6- Women and young men are the targets of the tyrant's schemes. «یقتّلون... یستحیون...»

7- Keeping alive mothers whose children have been killed before their eyes is the worst torment. «سوء العذاب یقتّلون ابنائكم و یستحیون نسائكم»

8- Sometimes bitter and unpleasant events are a means of divine trial. «بلاءُ من ربّكم»

9- We should consider hardships and afflictions, no matter how great, as part of human development and a divine test. «و فى ذلكم بلاء من ربّكم عظیم»

Ayah

۞ وَوَٰعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰ ثَلَـٰثِينَ لَيْلَةً وَأَتْمَمْنَـٰهَا بِعَشْرٍ فَتَمَّ مِيقَـٰتُ رَبِّهِۦٓ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً ۚ وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِأَخِيهِ هَـٰرُونَ ٱخْلُفْنِى فِى قَوْمِى وَأَصْلِحْ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۱٤۲)
We made an appointment with Moses for thirty nights, and completed them with ten [more]; thus the tryst of his Lord was completed in forty nights. And Moses said to Aaron, his brother, ‘Be my successor among my people, and set things right and do not follow the way of the agents of corruption.’ (142)

Key Points

In Ayah 51 of Surah Al-Baqarah, the discussion is about the promise of forty nights with Moses, "واذ واعدنا موسى أربعین لیلة" (And [remember] when We appointed for Moses forty nights), but here [in this Surah], it is thirty nights plus ten nights, the philosophy of which, according to Imam Baqir عليه السلام, was the testing of the Children of Israel. We read in narrations: thirty of those nights were in the month of Dhul-Qa'dah and ten nights were at the beginning of the month of Dhul-Hijjah. [247] Although Prophet Moses spent forty full days and nights on Mount Tur (as also mentioned in the Book of Exodus in the Torah), the expression "forty nights" might be because supplications (munajat) often take place at night, or because in ancient times the calendar was based on the moon, and the moon is visible at night, thus night was used to count the days.

Changing a plan based on wisdom, like a doctor changing a prescription under specific conditions for a patient, is permissible. Thus, thirty nights changed to forty nights with the addition of ten nights. Similarly, Bada' (divine alteration of destiny) concerning Allah means changing a plan based on changing conditions. Of course, Allah knows all these changes beforehand, just as a doctor sometimes knows in advance that after two days of following this prescription, the patient's condition will change and they will need a different medicine and prescription. Regarding Allah, Bada' never means regret, changing the goal, discovering a new point, or changing position, because these meanings stem from ignorance and limitation, which pertain to humans. However, Allah implements these changes in the course of testing and educating humanity.

Secrets are hidden in the Arba'een (forty) and the number forty. This number holds a special place in the culture of religions and in Islamic narrations, as we read: During the time of Prophet Noah عليه السلام, rain fell for forty days to punish the disbelievers. The people of Prophet Moses wandered in the desert for forty years. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله separated from Khadijah for forty days and performed I'tikaf (seclusion for worship) until heavenly food descended, preparing the ground for the birth of Lady Fatimah عليها السلام. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was commissioned (as a Prophet) at the age of forty. A person has the potential for spiritual and moral perfection until the age of forty, but after that, it becomes difficult. Forty days of sincerity in action is the cause for wisdom to flow from the heart to the tongue. Reading certain Surahs and supplications forty times is recommended for relief (faraj) and the removal of difficulties. Forty days of non-acceptance of deeds, including prayer, is the consequence of certain sins. If forty believers testify to the goodness of a deceased person, Allah forgives him... [248] Some books have also been written focusing on the number forty, such as collections of forty Hadiths. [249]

How can it be accepted that Prophet Moses appointed a successor for his temporary absence, but the Seal of the Prophets صلى الله عليه وآله did not appoint a successor for His eternal absence?! Especially since the Hadith of Position, where the Prophet addressed Ali عليه السلام saying: "أنت منّى بمنزلة هارون من موسى" (You are to me in the position of Aaron to Moses), has been narrated frequently (mutawatir) in Sunni sources. [250] And have people been abandoned during the occultation (Ghaybah) of Imam Al-Mahdi عليه السلام?!

Two tests occurred for the people of Moses: one was Moses staying ten days longer at the appointed time, and the other was the calf of the Samiri. As Allah says: "فانّا قد فتّنا قومك من بعدك و اضلّهم السامرىّ" [251] (Indeed, We have tested your people in your absence, and the Samiri has led them astray). When Moses عليه السلام said: I am going to the appointed place for thirty nights to receive divine commands, when thirty days and nights passed and Moses did not return, the people said: Moses has broken his promise. Therefore, they abandoned him and pursued deviant programs. Yet, when Moses went to Mount Tur, his God had not vanished, prompting the people to turn to idol worship.

The intent of reform (islah) in the Ayah is reform in social and communal matters, not reform in individual and personal behavior. (The word "اَصلِح" [reform] comes after "اُخلُفنى" [succeed me]).

During the time of Prophet Moses, there were mischievous plotters whom Moses was concerned about, and he advised Aaron: Do not let them take control of affairs, and do not follow them, whether willingly or unwillingly. "ولا تَتّبع سبیل المفسدین" (And do not follow the way of the mischief-makers).

247) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 248) Safinat al-Bihar, Vol. 1, pp. 504 to 505 249) Tafsir Furqan and Arba'een in Islamic Culture, Sayyid Reza Taqavi 250) Sahih Bukhari, Vol. 6, p. 3; Sahih Muslim, Vol. 4, p. 187; Sunan Ibn Majah, Vol. 1, p. 42; and Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Vol. 1 pp. 173, 175, 177, 179 and 182 251) Taha, 85

Messages

  1. The Heavenly Book is not revealed to Prophets without preparation and self-purification; rather, worthiness along with worship and nocturnal, mystical supplications are necessary. "و واعدنا موسى ثلاثین لیلة..."

  2. Responsibilities, even at the level of leadership, should not prevent worship, I'tikaf (seclusion), and private communion with Allah. "و واعدنا موسى..."

  3. Divine law is so sacred that its reception requires complete spiritual readiness. "و واعدنا..."

  4. Allah's appointed meeting place with the Prophets is a specific location like Mount Tur or the Cave of Hira, and the Divine feast for His righteous servants is prayer and supplication. "واعدنا..."

  5. Night is the best opportunity for supplication. "لیلة"

  6. Worship performed over forty full nights has specific effects. "اتممناها... فتمّ"

  7. A duration and term must be specified for tasks. "أربعین"

  8. The new leadership must have gained experience alongside the old leadership during the ups and downs of the movement. "قال موسى لاخیه هارون اخلفنى فى قومى..."

  9. Society should never be without a leader. "اُخلُفنى"

  10. Prophets have the right, by Divine permission, to appoint a successor. "اُخلُفنى"

  11. The Imamate and leadership of the people must be based on the Prophet's recommendation, "اُخلُفنى فى قومى" (Succeed me among my people), be in the path of reforming society, "اصلح" (reform), and be far from deviation "ولاتتّبع سبیل المفسدین" (and do not follow the way of the mischief-makers).

  12. There cannot be two leaders at one time. Aaron عليه السلام was a Prophet like Moses, but the leadership belonged to Moses عليه السلام. "اُخلُفنى" (Yes, Imamate and leadership are distinct from prophethood; thus, Moses appointed Aaron as his successor and the leader of the people during his absence.)

  13. Prophets are so compassionate toward their nation and consider themselves so responsible that they do not leave the people unattended even for a few days. "اُخلُفنى"

  14. The general duty of the leader is the reform of the nation. "اَصلِح"

  15. If the people are drawn toward corruption or demand corruption, the leadership must not abandon the position of reform. "و لا تتّبع سبیل المفسدین"

  16. Admonition and reminder are not signs of inability or weakness. Although Aaron عليه السلام was a Prophet and infallible (ma'sum), Prophet Moses still drew his attention to the two main duties: reform and avoidance of corruption. "اَصلِح و لا تَتّبع..."

  17. Reforming society is not possible alongside corrupt elements or by following mischief-makers. "اَصلِح و لاتَتّبع سبیل المفسدین"

  18. Society needs a reforming leader, "اَصلِح," a leader who never submits to the views and plots of the mischief-makers. "ولا تَتَّبع سبیل المفسدین"

Ayah

وَلَمَّا جَآءَ مُوسَىٰ لِمِيقَـٰتِنَا وَكَلَّمَهُۥ رَبُّهُۥ قَالَ رَبِّ أَرِنِىٓ أَنظُرْ إِلَيْكَ ۚ قَالَ لَن تَرَىٰنِى وَلَـٰكِنِ ٱنظُرْ إِلَى ٱلْجَبَلِ فَإِنِ ٱسْتَقَرَّ مَكَانَهُۥ فَسَوْفَ تَرَىٰنِى ۚ فَلَمَّا تَجَلَّىٰ رَبُّهُۥ لِلْجَبَلِ جَعَلَهُۥ دَكًّا وَخَرَّ مُوسَىٰ صَعِقًا ۚ فَلَمَّآ أَفَاقَ قَالَ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَأَنَا۠ أَوَّلُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٤۳)
When Moses arrived at Our tryst and his Lord spoke to him, he said, ‘My Lord, show [Yourself] to me, that I may look at You!’ He said, ‘You shall not see Me. But look at the mountain: if it abides in its place, then you will see Me.’ So when his Lord disclosed Himself to the mountain, He levelled it, and Moses fell down swooning. When he recovered, he said, ‘Immaculate are You! I turn to You in penitence, and I am the first of the faithful.’ (143)

Key Points

«دَكّ» means a flat land, and «جعله دكّا» means the mountain was pulverized and turned into dust until it became a level ground. «خَرَّ» means to fall down or collapse, and «صَعِقَ» means fainting due to a terrifying sound. The Divine force that struck the mountain—was it immense atomic energy, the power of sound waves, or some other mysterious force? Whatever it was, it pulverized the mountain, and Moses fell unconscious due to the roaring sound of the mountain's destruction (or due to this inner revelation and spiritual ecstasy). (252)

The request to see Allah was the ignorant demand of the Children of Israel, who asked Moses to show them Allah with their physical eyes (which we read about in Ayah 155). Those ignorant disbelievers were unaware that the eye can never see Allah, «لاتدركه الابصار» (253), but rather, Allah must be seen with the eye of the heart and believed in. As Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «رأته القلوب بحقایق الایمان» (Hearts see Him through the realities of faith). (254)

Open the eye of the heart so you may see the soul; you will see what is unseen.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, regarding «انا اوّل المؤمنین» (I am the first of the believers), said: «انا اوّل مَن آمن و صَدّق بانّك لاتُرى» (I am the first of those who believe and affirm that You cannot be seen). (255)

In a session, Ma'mun said to Imam Reza عليه السلام: Do you not say that the prophets are infallible? Then why did Moses request the vision of Allah? «أرنى أنظر الیك» (Show me, that I may look upon You). Did Moses not know that Allah is invisible?

The Imam عليه السلام replied to him: Prophet Moses عليه السلام knew that Allah cannot be seen with the eye. However, when God spoke to Moses and the Prophet announced it to the people, they said: We will not believe you unless we hear the Divine speech. Seventy men from the Children of Israel were chosen and came to the appointed place on Mount Tur. Prophet Moses عليه السلام requested their demand from God. At this moment, they heard the Divine speech from all directions, but they said they would not believe unless they saw God speaking. A thunderbolt came from the sky and killed them all. Prophet Moses said: If I return in this state, the people will say you are not truthful in your claim, as you killed others. By Divine permission, they were all resurrected. This time they said: We will believe you only if you yourself see God. Moses said: «انّ اللّه لایُرى بالابصار و لا كیفیّة له و انّما یعرف بآیاته و یكلّم باعلامه» (Allah is not seen by the eyes, and He has no modality; He is only known by His signs and speaks through His declarations). But they persisted. A voice came: Moses, ask what they ask, and I will not hold you accountable for their ignorance. Prophet Moses عليه السلام said: «ربّ ارنى انظر الیك». The reply came: «لن ترانى» (You shall never see Me). But look at the mountain; if it remains firm, you might see Me. By Divine command, the mountain was pulverized and turned into flat ground. Moses, upon regaining consciousness, said: «سبحانك تبتُ الیك» (Glory be to You! I turn to You in repentance). O God! Due to the people's ignorance and heedlessness, I return to the knowledge and recognition I already possessed, and I am the first to confess that God cannot be seen with the physical eye. Ma'mun was ashamed by this answer. (256)

In reality, by stating the phrase «ارنى» and receiving the answer «لن ترانى», Prophet Moses عليه السلام wanted to make the people understand that God is not visible to him, let alone to them.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked: If God is invisible, what do you say about the narrations that claim the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله saw God, or that believers will see God in Paradise on the Day of Judgment? The حضرت smiled and said: It is very ugly for a person to live 70-80 years on God's earth and use His sustenance, yet not know Him properly. The Prophet did not see God with his eye, and anyone who claims this is a liar and a disbeliever, just as the Prophet said: «مَن شبّه اللّه بخلقه فقد كفر» (Whoever likens Allah to His creation has disbelieved).

Imam Ali عليه السلام, in response to the question, "O brother of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله! Have you seen God?" replied: «لم أعبد ربّاً لم أره و لم تره العیون بمشاهدة الاعیان ولكن تراه القلوب بحقایق الایمان» (I do not worship a Lord Whom I have not seen, but the eyes do not see Him through physical observation; rather, hearts see Him through the realities of faith). (257) In another place, He said: «ما رأیتُ شیئاً الاّ و قد رأیتُ اللّه قبله و بعده و معه و فیه» (I did not see anything except that I saw Allah before it, after it, with it, and in it). (258)

Yes, the eye cannot perceive Him, «لاتدركه الابصار و هو یدرك الابصار» (259), but with the eye of the heart, God can be seen, just as the Quran says: «لقد رآه نَزلَة اُخرى عند سِدرة المنتهى» (260) (The Prophet, during the Mi'raj (ascension), saw the signs of Divine greatness). Of course, what is mentioned in the Torah regarding the story of Prophet Moses on Mount Tur is among the distortions of the Torah. (261)

252) Tafsir-e Nemooneh 253) Al-An'am, 103 254) Tafsir Safi 255) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 256) Tafasir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Furqan 257) Tafsir Furqan, Ma'ani al-Akhbar 258) Tafsir Furqan 259) Al-An'am, 103 260) An-Najm, 13 to 14 261) Torah, Book of Exodus, Chapter 23, Verse 9

Messages

  1. God speaking to Moses was for his growth and education. «كلّمه ربّه»
  2. Coming to the appointed place (Miqat) and hearing God's speech also awakened the desire for vision in Moses. «لمّا جاء... قال ارنى انظر الیك»
  3. Allah is not visible with the physical eye, neither in this world nor in the Hereafter. «لن ترانى»
  4. The manifestation (Tajāllī) of Allah upon natural objects is possible. «تجلّى ربّه للجبل»
  5. Allah must be known through His effects and His manifestations must be observed. (The reality of the manifestation itself is not clear, but its effects are visible). «تجلّى... جعله دكّاً»
  6. God is not visible; otherwise, Moses would have the worthiness and capability. «سبحانك»
  7. Seeing Allah with the eye implies His corporeality, which Allah must be declared free from. «سبحانك»
  8. Wrong thoughts and requests also require repentance. Whenever a false notion is conceived about Allah, He must be immediately declared pure (Tanzīh). «سبحانك»
  9. After the truth becomes clear, one must accept it and confess to it. «قال... انا اوّل المؤمنین»
  10. Prophets are the pioneers of the school of thought (the faith). «أوّل المؤمنین»

Ayah

قَالَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنِّى ٱصْطَفَيْتُكَ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ بِرِسَـٰلَـٰتِى وَبِكَلَـٰمِى فَخُذْ مَآ ءَاتَيْتُكَ وَكُن مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (۱٤٤)
He said, ‘O Moses, I have chosen you over the people with My messages and My speech. So take what I give you, and be among the grateful.’ (144)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Allah said to Moses عليه السلام: Do you know why I chose you? Because your humility and reverence before Me were unparalleled; whenever you stood for prayer, you would place your cheeks upon the earth. [262] In another tradition, it is stated that Allah said: Because among My servants, you considered yourself more humble before Me. [263]

262) Tafsir Safi 263) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- Prophet Moses was a sincere servant of Allah. «اصطفیتك» (Iṣṭifā’ means the selection of the pure essence.)

2- Prophethood and Messengership are positions of appointment, not election. «انّى اصطفیتك»

3- The mention of the people's inappropriate request does not prevent Moses' selection and prophethood. «اصطفیتك»

4- Forty nights of worship at the appointed place became a prelude to receiving the Torah and implementing it. «جاء موسى لمیقاتنا - فخذ ما آتیتك»

5- After the overthrow of the tyrant (Taghut) and the establishment of a religious government, divine law and ordinances must be implemented. «فخذ ما آتیتك»

6- Gratitude (Shukr) is an emphatic command from Allah and an obligatory matter (wajib), not merely recommended (mustahabb) or a moral suggestion. Therefore, thanking Allah for His blessings is necessary. «و كن من الشاكرین»

7- Religious and guidance responsibilities are divine blessings for which gratitude must be shown. «و كن من الشاكرین» (Gratitude, besides being expressed verbally, must also be demonstrated by implementing divine commands.)

Ayah

وَكَتَبْنَا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْأَلْوَاحِ مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ مَّوْعِظَةً وَتَفْصِيلًا لِّكُلِّ شَىْءٍ فَخُذْهَا بِقُوَّةٍ وَأْمُرْ قَوْمَكَ يَأْخُذُوا۟ بِأَحْسَنِهَا ۚ سَأُو۟رِيكُمْ دَارَ ٱلْفَـٰسِقِينَ (۱٤٥)
We wrote for him in the tablets advice concerning all things and an elaboration of all things, [and We said], ‘Hold on to them with power, and bid your people to hold on to the best of [what is in] them. Soon I shall show you the abode of the transgressors. (145)

Key Points

The meaning of al-Alwāḥ (the Tablets) is the stone inscriptions of the Torah that were referred to in the previous Ayah. Regarding what is meant by aḥsan (the best) in «و امُر قومك یأخذوا باحسنها» (and command your people to take the best of it), various opinions have been stated, including: taking it with the best strength, or taking the best of it in terms of knowledge and action, or performing the best action when choosing between the more important and the important, or that the commands of the Torah are the best (aḥsan) of all laws and commands of that era. However, the absolute best (aḥsan) is the Quran, as Allah says: «و اتّبعوا احسن ما انزل الیكم من ربّكم من قبل أن یأتیكم العذاب بغتة و انتم لاتشعرون» (264) It is possible that the meaning of the Ayah is that if you act upon the Torah with strength, you will overcome your enemies and acquire their homes. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The phrase «من كلّ شى ء» (of everything) indicates that Allah did not explain all matters to Moses عليه السلام. (265) Although, in the section that was presented, all the needs of the people of that era were explained in detail. «تفصیلاً لكلّ شى ء» (in detail for everything). The meaning of Dār al-Fāsiqīn (the home of the transgressors) is either the palace and court of the Pharaohs, or the homes of the inhabitants of Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis) before the arrival of the people of Moses, and it may also refer to the station of those who violated the commands of Moses on the Day of Judgment. (266)

264) Zumar, 55 265) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 266) Tafsir Rāhnamā

Messages

1- The Torah was revealed in written form. «كتبنا» (We wrote).

2- Implementing the law and leading the nation requires strength, resolve, and seriousness. «فخذها بقوّة و امُر قومك» (So take it with strength and command your people).

3- Among several paths and programs, the best method must be chosen and executed with the best ability. «بأحسنها» (the best of it).

4- Opposition to divine law is transgression (fisq) and carries punishment. «ساوریكم دار الفاسقین» (I will show you the home of the transgressors).

5- The leader must be a pioneer in action. (Allah first commands Moses عليه السلام himself, then the people). «خذ... یأخذوا» (Take... they take).

Ayah

سَأَصْرِفُ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَكَبَّرُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ كُلَّ ءَايَةٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِهَا وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ سَبِيلَ ٱلرُّشْدِ لَا يَتَّخِذُوهُ سَبِيلًا وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ سَبِيلَ ٱلْغَىِّ يَتَّخِذُوهُ سَبِيلًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَكَانُوا۟ عَنْهَا غَـٰفِلِينَ (۱٤٦)
Soon I shall turn away from My signs those who are unduly arrogant in the earth: [even] though they should see every sign, they will not believe in it, and if they see the way of rectitude they will not take it as [their] way, and if they see the way of error they will take it as [their] way. That is because they deny Our signs and are oblivious to them.’ (146)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: When the world becomes appealing in the eyes of Muslims, the majesty of Islam departs from them, and when they abandon enjoining good and forbidding evil, they are deprived of the blessing of revelation. «و اذا تركوا الامر بالمعروف و النهى عن المنكر حرّمت بركة الوحى» [267]

267) Tafsir Safi

Messages

1- The arrogant should know that they will be deprived of guidance and faith in the divine signs. «سأصرف عن آیاتى الّذین یتكبّرون»

2- Allah does not withdraw His grace from anyone without reason; rather, this is the result of man's own actions. «سأصرف... یتكبّرون»

3- Man's arrogance stems from his illusory perceptions. «یتكبّرون... بغیر الحقّ»

4- Arrogance is never befitting for a weak and needy creature like man. «یتكبّرون... بغیر الحقّ»

5- With the continuation of arrogance, even numerous signs (Ayat) will not guide man. «یتكبّرون... كلّ آیة»

6- Man is free to choose one of the two paths: guidance or misguidance. «لا یتّخذوه سبیلاً... یتّخذوه سبیلاً»

7- The continuation of heedlessness and denial of divine signs leads to arrogance and deviation in perspective and choice. «ذلك بانّهم كذّبوا بآیاتنا و كانوا عنها غافلین»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَلِقَآءِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ ۚ هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤۷)
Those who deny Our signs and the encounter of the Hereafter, their works have failed. Shall they not be requited for what they used to do? (147)

Key Points

'Habt' (nullification) means the destruction of a deed. "Habitat an-Nāqah" means a camel that has eaten poison, its belly has swelled, and it has burst. Some human actions, like poison, destroy and ruin the performance of a person's entire life. (268) The nullification of deeds is not contrary to Divine Justice; rather, it is an inevitable and natural (cosmic) matter, and the result of the person's own actions. «هل یجزون الاّ ما كانوا یعملون»

(268) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Qur'an

Messages

1- Disbelief and denial cause the nullification of past deeds. «كذّبوا... حبطت»

2- The rewards and punishments of the Day of Judgment are the embodiment of our deeds. «هل یجزون الاّ ما كانوا یعملون»

3- What is worse than sin is persistence upon it. «كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

وَٱتَّخَذَ قَوْمُ مُوسَىٰ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ مِنْ حُلِيِّهِمْ عِجْلًا جَسَدًا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٌ ۚ أَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّهُۥ لَا يُكَلِّمُهُمْ وَلَا يَهْدِيهِمْ سَبِيلًا ۘ ٱتَّخَذُوهُ وَكَانُوا۟ ظَـٰلِمِينَ (۱٤۸)
The people of Moses took up in his absence a calf [cast] from their ornaments—a body that gave out a lowing sound. Did they not regard that it did not speak to them, nor did it guide them to any way? They took it up [for worship] and they were wrongdoers. (148)

Key Points

‘Ijl refers to a male calf, the word ‘ḥullī’ means jewelry, and ‘khuwār’ is the specific sound of a cow (mooing). Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Moses عليه السلام asked Allah, "The Samiri has made a golden calf for the people, but what is the source of its sound?" Revelation came: "This is a means of testing the people, «یاموسى انّ تلك فتنة فلاتفحص عنها» (O Moses, that is a trial, so do not investigate it further)." (269) The Quran also states: «اِن هى الاّ فتنتك تضلّ بها من تشاء و تهدى من تشاء» (It is nothing but Your trial by which You let go astray whom You will and guide whom You will). (270) The Quran refers to the issue of the Children of Israel worshipping the calf in four Surahs. Of course, the calf worship of the Children of Israel had cultural and social backgrounds, because: Firstly, they had seen idols in the shape of cows in Egypt for many years. Secondly, as we read in Ayah 138, after crossing the Nile River, they saw a polytheistic people and were influenced, and this remained deep within their souls. Thirdly, the extension of the period from thirty nights to forty nights created the rumor of Moses' death at the hands of opponents, preparing the ground for deviation. Finally, the ignorance of the people and the skill of the Samiri caused the people of Moses to turn from monotheism to calf worship. (271) Therefore, the factors of deviation are several: 1. Absence of leadership or its occultation (Ghaybah). 2. Ignorance and unawareness of the people. 3. Deviant scholars and artists and the misuse of knowledge and art. 4. Superficial beauty and glamour. 5. Noise and false propaganda. In Ayah 87 of Surah Taha, the making of the calf is attributed to the Samiri, but surprisingly, in the Torah, it is attributed to Aaron?! (272) Of course, in this Ayah, it is attributed to the people of Moses, because many of them were pleased with his action and assisted him. (273) Prophet Moses used tangible evidence to guide the people and free them from deviation and the trap of the Samiri. For example, to negate the divinity of the calf, He guided them by explaining that it was manufactured, not the Creator; it required a location, possessed weight, volume, and shape, and offered no guidance or direction to anyone. (274)

269) Tafsir Furqan 270) A'raf, 155 271) Tafsir Namuneh 272) Torah, Book of Exodus, Chapter 32 273) Tafsir Namuneh 274) Tafsir Al-Mizan

Messages

  1. Sometimes man gives value to something that has no reality and goes so far as to worship it. «و اتّخَذَ»
  2. The call to calf worship occurs after disobedience to the leader and in the absence of the rightful leadership. «من بعده» (When Prophet Moses had gone to the appointed meeting place).
  3. The enemy uses art and carnal desires to deviate people. (The golden statue) «حُلِیّهم عجلاً»
  4. The apostasy (Riddah) of nations has a long history. «واتّخذ... عجلاً...»
  5. The Samiri’s calf was nothing more than a mere statue. «جسداً»
  6. Glamour, glitter, and noise play a major role in human deviation. «حلیّهم... خوار» Therefore, one should not follow every sound, every glitter, or every beauty.
  7. Man’s object of worship must be his guide. «لا یهدیهم»
  8. Polytheism (Shirk) is oppression (Ẓulm), and whoever abandons the truth without reason and solely based on obstinacy, becoming captive to the creations of his own hands, is an oppressor. «كانوا ظالمین»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا سُقِطَ فِىٓ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَرَأَوْا۟ أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ ضَلُّوا۟ قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ يَرْحَمْنَا رَبُّنَا وَيَغْفِرْ لَنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (۱٤۹)
But when they became remorseful and realised they had gone astray, they said, ‘Should our Lord have no mercy on us, and forgive us, we will be surely among the losers.’ (149)

Key Points

Since a person usually places his chin on his hands and contemplates when feeling remorse and regret, as if he has fallen into his own hands (i.e., trapped by his actions), the Quran therefore states: «سُقط فى ایدیهم» (275). It is also possible that this is an allusion that the calf fell down and was destroyed right before them, just as we read: «و انظُر اِلهك الّذى ظَلتَ علیه عاكفاً لَنُحرِّقَنّه ثمّ لَننسِفَنّه فى الیَمّ نَسفاً» (276).

275) Tafsir Rahnama 276) Taha, 97

Messages

1- Some people do not realize their mistake until they see the result of their actions. «لمّا سُقِط...»

2- Even with a history of calf-worship, we should not despair of Allah's mercy. «لئن لم یرحمنا ربّنا»

3- Man is in loss without divine mercy and forgiveness. «الخاسرین»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا رَجَعَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ غَضْبَـٰنَ أَسِفًا قَالَ بِئْسَمَا خَلَفْتُمُونِى مِنۢ بَعْدِىٓ ۖ أَعَجِلْتُمْ أَمْرَ رَبِّكُمْ ۖ وَأَلْقَى ٱلْأَلْوَاحَ وَأَخَذَ بِرَأْسِ أَخِيهِ يَجُرُّهُۥٓ إِلَيْهِ ۚ قَالَ ٱبْنَ أُمَّ إِنَّ ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱسْتَضْعَفُونِى وَكَادُوا۟ يَقْتُلُونَنِى فَلَا تُشْمِتْ بِىَ ٱلْأَعْدَآءَ وَلَا تَجْعَلْنِى مَعَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٥٠)
When Moses returned to his people, angry and indignant, he said, ‘Evil has been your conduct in my absence! Would you hasten on the edict of your Lord?’ He threw down the tablets and seized his brother by the head, pulling him towards himself. He said, ‘Son of my mother, indeed this people thought me to be weak, and they were about to kill me. So do not let the enemies gloat over me, and do not take me with the wrongdoing lot.’ (150)

Key Points

Following the opposition faced by Imam Ali عليه السلام, one day he addressed the people, saying: My conduct and model are the Prophets... But my model regarding my brother Harun is that Harun said to his brother [Moses]: «یابن امّ انّ القوم استضعفونى و كادوا یقتلوننى» (O son of my mother, indeed the people oppressed me and were about to kill me). Now, if you say that they did not oppress him, nor humiliate him, nor take any action to kill him, then you have denied the Quran. And if the situation was indeed as described and you accept it, then Harun was excused, and his silence was necessary. [277] Just as the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله also told him: «یا على... فانّك منّى بمنزلة هارون من موسى و لك بهارون اسوة اذ استضعفه قومه و كادوا یقتلونه» (O Ali... indeed your status to me is like the status of Harun to Musa, and you have an example in Harun when his people oppressed him and were about to kill him). Therefore, you too must adopt patience in the face of the enmity of the Quraysh. [278] The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Prophet Musa (Moses) displayed that reproachful behavior toward Harun upon returning to the people only to show the extent of his distress, otherwise, Allah had already informed Musa on Mount Tur about His people's calf-worship. [279] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Harun remained among his people despite all the persecution and harm he suffered from them, because if he had left them and gone toward Musa, divine punishment would certainly have descended upon them. «اذا فارقهم ینزّل بهم العذاب» (If he had separated from them, the punishment would have descended upon them). [280]

277) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 278) Tafsir Furqan 279) Tafsir Furqan 280) Tafsirs Nur al-Thaqalayn, Furqan, and Ithna Ashari

Messages

1- In the face of ideological deviations, religious zeal (ghayrah) must be shown. «غضبان أسفاً» (furious, sorrowful) (Like the reaction of Imam Khomeini (r.a.) to the plot of the satanic verses by the apostate Salman Rushdie). 2- Even the anger of the friends of Allah stems from sorrow over the condition of the people. «غضبان أسفاً» 3- Among the afflictions of every revolution and reform movement are apostasy and regression. «بئسما خلفتمونى» (How evil is that by which you succeeded me!) (Therefore, the leader must anticipate and prevent it.) 4- In a corrupt and deviant society, sometimes even the efforts of the Prophets remain fruitless. «بئسما خلفتمونى» 5- One must not preempt the command and decree of Allah. «أعجلتم أمر ربّكم» (Did you hasten the command of your Lord?) 6- Musa’s delay and the extension of his stay on Mount Tur was a divine command, not Musa’s own desire, and it certainly had an educational aspect. «أمر ربّكم» (the command of your Lord) 7- Whenever fundamental principles are endangered, secondary matters (furu') must be set aside. «القى الالواح» (He threw down the tablets). When Musa saw that the people had become polytheists and abandoned the principle of monotheism, he set aside the Tablets of the Torah, which contained a series of divine instructions and laws, and pursued the more important principle. 8- To create a shock in a deviant society, a striking and memorable act of reproach must be performed. «القى الالواح و أخذ برأس أخیه یجرّه» (He threw down the tablets and seized his brother by the head, dragging him). Just as we read in Nahj al-Balaghah: Imam Ali عليه السلام, in order to motivate indifferent listeners, sometimes struck his own blessed face. «ثمّ ضرب على لحیته الكریمة» (Then he struck his noble beard). [281] 9- Long hair is not an impediment for a man. «اخذ برأس اخیه یجرّه» 10- We must not overlook the mistakes of others due to their status, position, or kinship. «اخذ برأس اخیه» (He seized his brother by the head). Sometimes, timely reproach of close associates in public is meant to demonstrate the importance of the matter, serve as a warning, and intimidate outsiders. «اخذ برأس اخیه» Just as Allah addressed His Prophet in this manner: «لئن اشركت لیحبطنّ عملك و لتكوننّ من الخاسرین» (If you should associate [anything] with Allah, your work would surely become worthless, and you would surely be among the losers) [282], while the actual goal is to warn others. 11- One should adopt an emotional approach when dealing with an angry person. «یاابن اُمّ» (O son of my mother). Although Musa and Harun علیهما السلام shared the same father and mother, Harun addressed Musa as the son of his mother. 12- The oppressed (mustad'afun) of yesterday, who were saved from Pharaoh by Musa, have today become the agents of oppression against their own leaders. «استضعفونى» (They oppressed me). 13- The moral decline of man reaches the point where, due to whims, desires, and calf-worship, he threatens to kill his own benefactor. «و كادوا یقتلوننى» (and were about to kill me). 14- Not all companions of the Prophets were just; sometimes some of them even became apostates. «و كادوا یقتلوننى» Just as some became calf-worshippers. 15- When reproaching friends, one must be careful that the enemy does not exploit the situation. «فلاتشمت بى الاعداء» (So do not make the enemies rejoice over me). 16- Silence and indifference regarding oppression and deviation place a person on par with the oppressor. «لاتجعلنى مع القوم الظالمین» (Do not place me among the wrongdoing people). Harun considered silence to be oppression, hence he said: I did not remain silent; rather, they boycotted me and threatened to kill me.

281) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 182 282) Az-Zumar, 61

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِأَخِى وَأَدْخِلْنَا فِى رَحْمَتِكَ ۖ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ (۱٥۱)
He said, ‘My Lord, forgive me and my brother, and admit us into Your mercy, for You are the most merciful of the merciful. (151)

Key Points

The request for forgiveness by Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) is either to teach others a lesson, or because he rebuked Aaron in front of the people. «ربّ اغفر لى و لاخى» Of course, by stating this sentence, Prophet Moses gave a better response to the emotional reaction of Aaron, who had said: «یابن امّ».

Messages

1- Let us also pray for our brothers and colleagues. «ربّ اغفر لى و لاخى»

2- In the storm of societal deviations, leaders are in greater need of prayer and Divine favor than others. «وأدخلنا فى رحمتك»

3- Forgiveness is the prelude to entering Divine mercy. «اغفر... أدخلنا»

4- In prayer, let us mention the favors and attributes of Allah. «أرحم الرّاحمین»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ ٱلْعِجْلَ سَيَنَالُهُمْ غَضَبٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَذِلَّةٌ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُفْتَرِينَ (۱٥۲)
Indeed those who took up the calf [for worship] shall be overtaken by their Lord’s wrath and abasement in the life of the world.’ Thus do We requite the fabricators [of lies]. (152)

Key Points

It was understood from Ayah 149 that the Children of Israel regretted their actions. This Ayah shows that their repentance was not accepted, except in the manner described in Ayah 54 of Surah Al-Baqarah, where, for repentance, they had to kill one another. «انّكم ظلمتم أنفسكم باتّخاذكم العجل فتوبوا الى بارئكم فاقتلوا أنفسكم» (Indeed, you wronged yourselves by taking the calf [for worship]. So turn in repentance to your Creator and kill [those guilty] among yourselves.) Yes, the repentance for apostasy and calf-worship after having worshipped the Truth is not merely saying «أستغفر اللّه» (I seek forgiveness from Allah), but rather they had to execute one another with their own hands so that this action would become a culture in the method of combating polytheism and remain throughout the generations. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Whoever introduces an innovation (bid'ah) into the Divine religion will be disgraced and humiliated in this very world. [283]

283) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- Man is free in choosing his religion, although sometimes he inclines towards something that causes his downfall. «انّ الّذین اتّخذوا العجل سینالهم غضب من ربّهم»

2- Apostates are subject to Allah's wrath. «انّ الّذین اتّخذوا العجل سینالهم غضب من ربّهم»

3- The wrath of Allah's chosen servants (Awliya') is the wrath of Allah. «غضبان أسفاً - غضب من ربّهم»

4- Those who abandon faith and pursue flashy and noisy idols will be humiliated even in this world. «ذلّة فى الحیاة الدنیا»

5- Divine wrath is not exclusive to the Children of Israel; rather, it is a Divine tradition (Sunnah) and has been and will be applied to other apostate nations as well. «كذلك نجزى المفترین»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا۟ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ ثُمَّ تَابُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِهَا وَءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهَا لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٥۳)
Yet [to] those who commit misdeeds but repent after that, and believe—indeed, after that, your Lord shall surely be all-forgiving, all-merciful. (153)

Key Points

Although repentance is not accepted unless there is faith, in this Ayah, repentance (Tawbah) comes first, followed by faith. Perhaps this is because sin destabilizes a person's faith and renders the light of faith ineffective, and through repentance, faith is renewed once again. [284]

284) Tafsir Namuneh

Messages

1- The path of repentance is always open, even if it is after a long time. "ثمّ تابوا" (The word "ثمّ" [thumma] indicates the passage of a long time.)

2- We should not despair of Allah's Mercy, even if we intended to kill the prophets. "كادوا یقتلوننى - ثمّ تابوا من بعدها"

3- The repentance for every transgression is the compensation for that very act; the repentance for calf-worship is returning to true faith in Allah. "آمنوا"

4- A suitable context must be provided when inviting people to repentance. "انّ ربّك من بعدها لغفور رحیم"

5- Allah, in addition to granting forgiveness to true repenters, also includes them in His Mercy. "لَغفور رحیم" Yes, the Names and Attributes of Allah have wise manifestations.

Ayah

وَلَمَّا سَكَتَ عَن مُّوسَى ٱلْغَضَبُ أَخَذَ ٱلْأَلْوَاحَ ۖ وَفِى نُسْخَتِهَا هُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِرَبِّهِمْ يَرْهَبُونَ (۱٥٤)
When Moses’ indignation abated, he picked up the tablets whose inscriptions contained guidance and mercy for those who are in awe of their Lord. (154)

Messages

1- The celestial law is Divine mercy, and guidance accompanied by mercy encompasses those who fear Allah. «هدىً و رحمة للّذین... یرهبون»

2- Not only is the Quran a source of guidance for the pious, «هدىً للمتّقین», but the unadulterated Torah is also a means of guidance for the God-fearing. «هدىً... یرهبون»

3- The fear of Allah opens the doors of mercy to humanity. «رحمة للّذین... یرهبون»

4- No authority other than Allah is worthy for man to be fearful and submissive before Him. «لربّهم یرهبون»

Ayah

وَٱخْتَارَ مُوسَىٰ قَوْمَهُۥ سَبْعِينَ رَجُلًا لِّمِيقَـٰتِنَا ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ قَالَ رَبِّ لَوْ شِئْتَ أَهْلَكْتَهُم مِّن قَبْلُ وَإِيَّـٰىَ ۖ أَتُهْلِكُنَا بِمَا فَعَلَ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ مِنَّآ ۖ إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّا فِتْنَتُكَ تُضِلُّ بِهَا مَن تَشَآءُ وَتَهْدِى مَن تَشَآءُ ۖ أَنتَ وَلِيُّنَا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا وَٱرْحَمْنَا ۖ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْغَـٰفِرِينَ (۱٥٥)
Moses chose seventy men from his people for Our tryst, and when the earthquake seized them, he said, ‘My Lord, had You wished, You would have destroyed them and me before. Will You destroy us because of what the fools amongst us have done? It is only Your test, by which You lead astray whomever You wish and guide whomever You wish. You are our master, so forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of those who forgive. (155)

Key Points

There are discussions regarding whether Prophet Moses عليه السلام had one appointed time (Miqat) or several, and whether the death and destruction at the Miqat were due to the request to see God or due to the people's calf-worship. These discussions do not affect the understanding of the Ayah, so we will overlook them. Despite witnessing all those miracles, the companions of Moses عليه السلام still requested to see God or hear His voice. Furthermore, those seventy individuals were chosen by Prophet Moses from among seven hundred people who went to Mount Tur and were afflicted by Divine wrath, because whoever witnesses a miracle should not make excuses. In the number "seventy," just like the number "forty," a secret and symbolism is hidden. When the chosen ones of a Prophet like Moses عليه السلام turn out this way, how can it be argued that ordinary people should choose the Imam?! Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, in response to someone who asked why people do not have the right to choose the Imam, said: Moses chose seventy people, and it became clear that they were unworthy. Therefore, if the chosen ones of a Prophet like Moses deviate, what will be the condition of those chosen by the people? [285]

285) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn

Messages

1- For presence in important centers and sensitive matters, selection must be carried out, and individuals must be chosen; not just anyone, at any level, should be present. «واختار موسى»

2- Dying is easier than enduring the pressure of people's words. «لو شئت أهلكتهم من قبل و ایّاى» (Meaning: "I wish You had taken our lives before the people came with me to Mount Tur, because enduring the accusation that you chose the best people, led them to the place of slaughter, and destroyed them, is harder.")

3- Prophets act based on outward appearances, not on the knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb). Therefore, sometimes even the chosen ones of the Prophets turn out to be unworthy. «بما فعل السفهاء منّا»

4- Sometimes the fire of Divine wrath is so widespread that it burns the dry and the wet together. «أتهلكنا بما فعل السفهاء» (The good people [are burned] due to their silence and indifference.)

5- Making excuses is a sign of foolishness (Safahah). «السفهاء»

6- Calamities and events are sometimes a Divine test, «اِن هِىَ الاّ فتنتك...» in which individuals become known and the ranks are separated. «تضلّ... تهدى»

7- Praising Allah before supplication is among the manners (Adab) of prayer. «أنت ولیّنا فاغفرلنا»

8- Unless the slate of the soul is cleansed of the contamination of sin, it will not attract Divine mercy. «اغفر لنا و ارحمنا»

9- Divine forgiveness and pardon (unlike the forgiveness of people, which is either delayed or accompanied by obligation and humiliation) is the best of pardons. «خیر الغافرین»

Ayah

۞ وَٱكْتُبْ لَنَا فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ إِنَّا هُدْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ۚ قَالَ عَذَابِىٓ أُصِيبُ بِهِۦ مَنْ أَشَآءُ ۖ وَرَحْمَتِى وَسِعَتْ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ ۚ فَسَأَكْتُبُهَا لِلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ وَيُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَٱلَّذِينَ هُم بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٥٦)
And appoint goodness for us in this world and the Hereafter, for indeed we have come back to You.’ Said He, ‘I visit My punishment on whomever I wish, but My mercy embraces all things. Soon I shall appoint it for those who are Godwary and give the zakat and those who believe in Our signs (156)

Key Points

| This Ayah expresses another supplication of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام), following his previous request. "هُدنا" (We have returned/repented) refers to their turning towards Allah and repenting from the path of the misguided and the path of those who made the inappropriate request to see Allah. According to Ibn Abbas, when the Ayah "و رحمتى وَسِعَت كلّ شى ء" (And My Mercy encompasses all things) was revealed, Satan also became greedy for divine mercy and said: The phrase "كلّ شى ء" (all things) includes me too. To dispel this misconception, Allah said: "سأكتبها للّذین یتّقون..." (I shall decree it for those who fear [Him]...). Yes, the table of divine mercy is spread wide and reaches everyone, but the inclusion of the non-believers in this mercy is due to divine grace (tafaddul), and through the blessing and favor of the believers. [286] It is narrated that a person was saying in his prayer: "اللهمّ ارحمنى و محمّداً و لاترحم معنا احداً" (O Allah, have mercy on me and Muhammad, and do not have mercy on anyone else with us). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, criticizing him after the prayer, said: You have restricted the endless divine mercy and the vast, unlimited scope [of Allah's favor]! [287]

286) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 287) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Namuneh

Messages

1- The best steps and deeds are the most enduring ones. "واكتب لنا" (And decree for us).

2- Prophets are concerned with securing the happiness of people in this world and the Hereafter. "واكتب لنا فى هذه الدنیا حسنة و فى الآخرة" (And decree for us in this world good, and in the Hereafter).

3- The best supplications are the most comprehensive ones. "فى هذه الدّنیا حسنة و فى الآخرة" (in this world good, and in the Hereafter).

4- Sincere return (repentance) to Allah is the way to receive divine favors. "هُدنا" (We have returned/repented).

5- Divine mercy is unlimited; if someone does not attain it, the fault lies with him. "رحمتى وسعت كلّ شى ء" (My Mercy encompasses all things). There is no deficiency in the effectiveness (fa'iliyyah) of divine mercy, but rather a deficiency in our receptivity (qabiliyyah) and welcome.

6- The Mercy of Allah is comprehensive, but His punishment is not. "رحمتى وسعت... عذابى اُصیب به مَن اشاء" (My Mercy encompasses... My punishment—I inflict it upon whom I will).

7- The fundamental principle is divine mercy, and His punishment is incidental. (For mercy, the past tense verb "وَسعت" [encompassed] is used, but for punishment, the present/future tense verb "اُصیب" [I inflict] is used).

8- Even the supplications of the Prophets are answered within the framework of divine wisdom and law. "اكتب لنا... سأكتبها للّذین..." (Decree for us... I shall decree it for those...).

9- Given piety (taqwa) and adherence to duties, the inclusion in divine mercy is certain, "سأكتبها للّذین..." (I shall decree it for those...). But given sin and disobedience, punishment is not certain, because through repentance and seeking forgiveness, the possibility of pardon exists. "عذابى اصیب به من اشاء" (My punishment—I inflict it upon whom I will).

10- Divine mercy is infinite, but the condition for benefiting from it is piety (taqwa), paying Zakat, and faith. "یتّقون... الزّكاة... یؤمنون" (who fear [Him]... the Zakat... believe). Therefore, whoever does not pay Zakat or lacks piety is deprived of divine mercy and exposed to divine punishment.

11- Zakat (alms) was also present in the Jewish Sharia (Law). "یؤتون الزكاة" (who give the Zakat).

12- What causes the reception of divine favors is faith in all the signs (Ayat), not just some of them. "بآیاتنا یؤمنون" (who believe in Our signs).

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلرَّسُولَ ٱلنَّبِىَّ ٱلْأُمِّىَّ ٱلَّذِى يَجِدُونَهُۥ مَكْتُوبًا عِندَهُمْ فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَٱلْإِنجِيلِ يَأْمُرُهُم بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَىٰهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ وَيُحِلُّ لَهُمُ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ وَيُحَرِّمُ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْخَبَـٰٓئِثَ وَيَضَعُ عَنْهُمْ إِصْرَهُمْ وَٱلْأَغْلَـٰلَ ٱلَّتِى كَانَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ فَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَعَزَّرُوهُ وَنَصَرُوهُ وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ ٱلنُّورَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ مَعَهُۥٓ ۙ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۱٥۷)
—those who follow the Apostle, the untaught prophet, whose mention they find written with them in the Torah and the Evangel, who bids them to do what is right and forbids them from what is wrong, makes lawful to them all the good things and forbids them from all vicious things, and relieves them of their burdens and the shackles that were upon them—those who believe in him, honour him, and help him and follow the light that has been sent down with him, they are the felicitous.’ (157)

Key Points

The word Ummi, related to Umm (mother), means someone who has not studied, like someone who has just been born from his mother. Some have said: Ummi means arising from the Ummah (community) and the masses, not the elite. Others have considered it related to Umm al-Qura, which is the name of Mecca, so Ummi means Meccan. The Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله was Ummi and unstudied, "the Messenger, the unlettered Prophet" (Al-Rasūl al-Nabī al-Ummī), just as we read in Ayah 48 of Surah Al-Ankabut: "And you did not recite before it any scripture, nor did you inscribe it with your right hand." Of course, that noble person had the ability to both write and read. (288) In all of Mecca during the era of the Prophetic mission, there were no more than 17 literate men and one literate woman, and if the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله had learned even a single word from anyone, He could not have called Himself Ummi among the people. (289)

The word Isr (اِصر), originally means holding and confining, and is applied to any difficult and arduous task or obligation that prevents a person from activity, just as it is also applied to covenants, promises, and punishment. Aghlāl (اَغلال), the plural of Ghull (غُلّ), meaning chain or fetter, includes false beliefs, superstitions, idol worship, innovation (bid'ah), and the restrictive traditions of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah).

Although for reasons we consider the Torah and the Gospel to be distorted, even in the existing Torah and Gospel, there are references and glad tidings regarding the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, which cause the People of the Book to recognize the Prophet of God, just as a father recognizes his own son. (290) If the name and description of the Prophet of Islam were not in the Torah and the Gospel, the opponents would have brought those two books and said, "We did not find it," and would have entered into hostility through this route, rather than through military campaigns, paying such heavy costs, and paying Jizyah (tribute).

In numerous narrations from both Shia and Sunni sources, we read that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Every Ayah in the Quran that concerns the believers and their good qualities, Ali is at its head and is their leader. "Mā anzala Allāhu āyatan fīhā 'Yā ayyuhā al-ladhīna āmanū' illā wa ‘Alīyun ra’suhā wa amīruhā." (291)

The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله told His companions: After you, a people will come who have not seen the Prophet, and they will believe only through a book and a written text, and then He recited this Ayah. (292)

288) Tafsir Namūneh 289) Tafsir Namūneh, quoting Futūh al-Buldān by Balādhuri, p. 459 290) Including: Torah, Genesis, Chapter 17, Verse 18; Genesis, Chapter 49, Verse 10; and Gospel of John, Chapter 14, Verse 15 and Chapter 15, Verse 26 291) Tafsir Furqān 292) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān

Messages

  1. Piety (Taqwa), Zakat, and faith are valuable, but what is important is following the Prophet. "for those who fear [Allah] and give Zakat and... believe, those who follow" (lil-ladhīna yattaqūna wa yu’tūna al-zakāta wa... yu’minūna al-ladhīna yattabi‘ūna)

  2. The followers of the Prophet of Islam benefit from Allah's special mercy. "I will prescribe it... those who follow the Messenger." (sa-aktubuhā... al-ladhīna yattabi‘ūna al-rasūl)

  3. The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is the channel for Allah's vast mercy. "And My Mercy encompasses all things, so I will prescribe it... those who follow the unlettered Messenger." (wa raḥmatī wasi‘at kulla shay’in fa-sa-aktubuhā... al-ladhīna yattabi‘ūna al-rasūl al-ummī)

  4. The Prophet of Islam held both the station of Prophethood (Nubuwwah) and the station of Messengership (Risālah). "the Messenger, the Prophet" (Al-Rasūl al-Nabī). A Nabī (Prophet) is someone who is aware of Divine revelation and gives news, but a Rasūl (Messenger) is also responsible for conveying, implementing, and supervising.

  5. The signs and descriptions of the Prophet mentioned in the Torah and the Gospel corresponded so closely to Him that it was as if they found Him Himself in those two books. "They find Him" (Yajidūnahu), and He did not say, "They find His signs" (Yajidūna ‘alā’imahu).

  6. For the influence of speech and the establishment of truth, official documentation and written evidence are necessary. "written" (maktūban).

  7. Previous Prophets gave glad tidings of the coming of the Prophet of Islam. "written with them in the Torah and the Gospel..."

  8. Enjoining good and forbidding evil are at the forefront of the missions of the Prophets. "He enjoins them with the good..."

  9. The subject of consumption and nutrition is given attention in Islam. "He makes lawful... He makes unlawful." (Before the revelation of the Quran, Jews and Christians were trapped in superstitions, arduous programs, the prohibition of lawful things, and innovations, all of which were removed with the advent of Islam.)

  10. To reform society, one must first provide lawful opportunities, and then create restrictions for unlawful matters. ("He makes lawful" comes before "He makes unlawful").

  11. Allah's lawful and unlawful matters are based on innate nature (fitrah). "He makes lawful for them the good things and makes unlawful for them the foul things." (Tayyib (good thing) is said of something that conforms to human nature.)

  12. Wrong customs and traditions are chains upon people's thoughts. Without the Prophets, humanity is dependent and captive. "He relieves them of their burden (isra) and the shackles (aghlāl)."

  13. Respect and honor must be accompanied by aid and cooperation. "They honor Him and they support Him." (‘Azzarūhu is from Ta‘zīr, meaning support with the sword, and refers to those who aided the Prophet of Islam on the battlefronts.)

  14. Mere faith in the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is not enough; support is also necessary. "They honor Him... they support Him."

  15. The Quran is a light that illuminates hearts and thoughts, and it was always associated with the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. "the Light which was sent down with Him." (The Prophethood of the Prophet was sent down by God along with the Quran.)

  16. The factor for salvation and success (falāh) is faith in the Prophet, supporting Him, and following the Quran. "So those who believe in Him and follow... those are the successful ones."

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنِّى رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ إِلَيْكُمْ جَمِيعًا ٱلَّذِى لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ ۖ فَـَٔامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ٱلنَّبِىِّ ٱلْأُمِّىِّ ٱلَّذِى يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ وَٱتَّبِعُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (۱٥۸)
Say, ‘O mankind! I am the Apostle of Allah to you all, [of Him] to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. There is no god except Him. He gives life and brings death.’ So have faith in Allah and His Apostle, the untaught prophet, who has faith in Allah and His words, and follow him so that you may be guided. (158)

Key Points

Contrary to the baseless claim of some who say: The Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله initially intended to guide the people of His own region, and only after succeeding did He think of guiding the world! The Qur'an, with phrases such as: «كافّة للناس» (293), «لانذركم به و مَن بَلغ» (294), «للعالمین نذیراً» (295), and the like, states the universality of the mission of the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله from the very beginning. (296) Of course, His universal mission, like any plan, progresses step by step: first, the invitation to His family and clan, then the people of Mecca, and then all people. Imam Mujtaba عليه السلام said: A group of Jews came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and asked: Do you claim to be a prophet like Moses? After a brief silence, the Prophet replied: Yes, I am the Seal of the Prophets, the leader of the pious, and the Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds. They asked: To whom have you been sent? Arabs, non-Arabs, or us? The above Ayah was revealed. (297) Meaning, I am a Messenger to all the worlds. The repetition of the word «ummi» (unlettered) in this Ayah and the previous Ayah is a sign of its importance in proving the truthfulness of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Qur'an. Considering the previous Ayah which stated that they find the name and description of the Prophet of Islam clearly in the Torah and the Gospel, the audience of this Ayah includes all people, even the Jews and Christians. This Ayah initially emphasizes three points: 1. Proving the Creatorship of Allah. «له ملك السموات و الارض» 2. Monotheism and the Oneness of Allah. «لا اله الاّ هو» 3. The Resurrection and the Gathering. «یحیى و یمیت» Finally, the Ayah states: Now that the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad) are in the hands of Allah, then believe in Him: (298) «فآمنوا باللّه و رسوله»

293) Saba, 28 294) An'am, 19 295) Furqan, 1 296) Tafsir Namuneh 297) Tafasir Safi and Namuneh 298) Tafsir Ithna Ashari

Messages

  1. The mission of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله is universal. «یا ایّها النّاس انّى رسول اللّه الیكم جمیعاً» A universal doctrine requires a universal leader.

  2. He who has complete sovereignty over all existence also has the power to give life and cause death. «له ملك السموات و الارض... هو یحیى و یمیت»

  3. Prophethood is a necessary consequence of Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad). Since the One God is the Owner of existence, «له ملك السموات والارض», and life and death are in His hand, «هو یحیى و یمیت», then the leadership and guidance of humanity must also come from Him. «فآمنوا باللّه و رسوله النبىّ الامّى»

  4. The leader must have complete faith in his path. «یؤمن باللّه و كلماته»

  5. Faith in Allah and the Messenger, coupled with obedience to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, is the key to guidance. «آمنوا باللّه و رسوله... واتّبعوه لعلّكم تهتدون»

  6. Following both the Qur'an and the Sunnah and conduct (Sirah) of the Prophet is necessary. «واتّبعوه» (In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about following the light and the Qur'an, and here it is about following the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله).

  7. Even the People of the Book must believe in the Prophet of Islam and follow Him. «یا ایّها النّاس... فآمنوا باللّه و رسوله... و اتّبعوه»

  8. Imitation and obedience that leads a person to guidance and perfection are valuable. «و اتّبعوه لعلّكم تهتدون»

Ayah

وَمِن قَوْمِ مُوسَىٰٓ أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَبِهِۦ يَعْدِلُونَ (۱٥۹)
Among the people of Moses is a nation who guide [the people] by the truth and do justice thereby. (159)

Key Points

Guidance towards the truth (al-Haqq) is a sign of being far removed from fanaticism, recognizing the truth, and following it. This guided group of Jews had nothing to do with the rest of the stubborn, fault-finding people. Perhaps the intent behind this group refers to the Jews who answered the call of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله. Of course, in another place, the reference is absolute and not exclusive to the Jewish people: «و ممّن خلقنا امّة یهدون بالحقّ و به یعدلون» (299)

In numerous narrations, it is stated that these are a group who acknowledged the prophethood of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and will appear alongside Hadhrat Hujjat (the Mahdi) علیه السلام. (300) The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: O people!
