The Straight Path whose following is obligatory is Me, and after Me, it is Aliّ علیه السلام, and then My children from his lineage, «ائمّة یهدون بالحقّ و به یعدلون» (Imams who guide people to the truth and dispense justice based on the truth). (301) Hadhrat Ali علیه السلام said: After Musa علیه السلام, the Children of Israel split into seventy-one sects, all of whom are in the Fire, except for one sect among them. He then recited this Ayah. (302)

299) Al-A'raf, 181 300) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 301) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 302) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Ayyashi

Messages

1- Even when dealing with opponents, one must be fair and not overlook their services and merits. «و من قوم موسى اُمّة یهدون بالحقّ»

2- Those who guide must themselves be committed to, believe in, and follow the truth. «یهدون بالحقّ و به یعدلون»

Ayah

وَقَطَّعْنَـٰهُمُ ٱثْنَتَىْ عَشْرَةَ أَسْبَاطًا أُمَمًا ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱسْتَسْقَىٰهُ قَوْمُهُۥٓ أَنِ ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْحَجَرَ ۖ فَٱنۢبَجَسَتْ مِنْهُ ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا ۖ قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ ۚ وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْغَمَـٰمَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ ۚ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (۱٦٠)
We split them up into twelve tribal communities, and We revealed to Moses, when his people asked him for water, [saying], ‘Strike the rock with your staff,’ whereat twelve fountains gushed forth from it. Every tribe came to know its drinking-place. And We shaded them with clouds, and We sent down to them manna and quails: ‘Eat of the good things We have provided you.’ And they did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong [only] themselves. (160)

Key Points

Asbāt (اَسباط) is the plural of sibt (سِبط), referring to the children, grandchildren, and branches of a family. Each tribe of the Children of Israel branched off from one of the sons of Jacob. Inbijās (انبجاس) means a slight and limited gushing or springing forth. Mann (مَنّ) is a food like honey or plant sap (similar to manna/gazangabin), and Salwā (سَلوى) is a bird whose meat is lawful (halal), resembling a pigeon (or quail). The number "twelve" is the number of months in the year, the number of the chieftains (nuqabā) of the Children of Israel, the number of water springs that appeared through the miracle of Prophet Moses, and the number of the infallible Imams علیهم السلام. Opponents of the Ahl al-Bayt have tried hard to apply this tradition of the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله (which both Shia and Sunni sources have narrated with dozens of chains of transmission) that the Caliphs after me are twelve, and all are from Quraysh, [303] to the rulers of Banu Umayyah and Banu Abbas, but they have failed. By striking the stone with the staff, two miracles occurred: the gushing forth of abundant water, and the appearance of springs equal to the number of the tribes. We read in narrations that Prophet Moses علیه السلام performed the miracle by seeking intercession through the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام. [304] Prophet Shu'ayb علیه السلام came to visit his son-in-law Moses علیه السلام and saw a long line of people waiting to meet Moses علیه السلام. He suggested to Moses that he divide the people into groups and appoint a representative for each group so that they could solve some of the problems, referring only the more important issues to Moses علیه السلام. [305] Some topics and events appear to be repeated in multiple verses of the Quran, including this story, which also appears in Surah Al-Baqarah, verse 57 onwards. This indicates that the repetition of a subject for educational purposes is permissible.

303) Al-Ghadir and Ithbat al-Hudat, Vol. 1, p. 433 304) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 305) Tafsir Kashif

Messages

  1. In managing affairs, organization, division of labor, and classification play an important role. «و قطّعناهم»
  2. The twelve groups of the Children of Israel all originated from one root. «قطّعناهم» (We divided them) instead of «جعلنا» (We made them).
  3. Being divided into groups, while maintaining unity of purpose, is not harmful. «قطّعناهم»
  4. Provision following a request has greater significance. «استسقاه... اضرب بعصاك»
  5. The means are not important; the Divine Will is important, which sometimes makes a wooden staff effective everywhere. «بعصاك» (The staff, in one place, becomes a snake and a dragon, and in another place, a means for creating water springs.)
  6. Seeking intercession through the Prophets to be freed from difficulties is permissible, and indeed, close to acceptance. People would share their economic and material needs with the Prophets and seek intercession through them. «استسقاه... فانبجست»
  7. If every group knows where they should refer, many social problems will be solved. «قد علم كلّ اُناس مشربهم»
  8. We should inform the people; they will act themselves. «قد علم كلّ اُناس مشربهم» (They knew their drinking place), and He did not say: «قد شرب» (They drank).
  9. Allah provided both the water needed by the Children of Israel and their food. «اثنتا عشرة عينا... المنّ و السلوى»
  10. The formation, movement, and function of the clouds are all under Divine management and Will. «وظلّلنا علیهم الغمام»
  11. The Mann and Salwā are among the pure foods. (The phrase «كلوا من الطیّبات» (Eat of the pure things) follows the mention of «المنّ و السلوى»).
  12. Allah is the Provider for the people, «رزقناكم», and desires the pure utilization of clean and pure blessings. «كلوا من طیّبات»
  13. With one strike of Moses' staff, springs gushed forth from the heart of the stone, yet despite all those miracles, the hearts of the unworthy were unmoved. «و ما ظلمونا و لكن كانوا انفسهم یظلمون»
  14. Ingratitude for blessings leads to deprivation from them, and its harm reaches the ungrateful. «ولكن كانوا انفسهم یظلمون»
  15. Those who do not utilize material blessings for their own growth and spirituality have wronged themselves. «كانوا أنفسهم یظلمون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱسْكُنُوا۟ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْقَرْيَةَ وَكُلُوا۟ مِنْهَا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمْ وَقُولُوا۟ حِطَّةٌ وَٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْبَابَ سُجَّدًا نَّغْفِرْ لَكُمْ خَطِيٓـَٔـٰتِكُمْ ۚ سَنَزِيدُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱٦۱)
And when they were told, ‘Settle in this town and eat thereof whence you wish; and say, ‘‘Relieve [us of the burden of our sins],’’ and enter prostrating at the gate, that We may forgive your iniquities, and soon We shall enhance the virtuous.’ (161)

Key Points

The word "حطّة" (hittah) means the descent of something from above (the word inhiṭāṭ [decline/fall] is also derived from this root), and it signifies the request for the descent of Divine mercy and forgiveness. The command was that when the Children of Israel entered the Holy Land, they should seek forgiveness from Allah using this word. However, out of mockery, they changed the word and said: "حنطة" (ḥinṭah - wheat). [306] A verse similar to this, with a slight difference, also appears in Surah Al-Baqarah (verses 58 and 59). In numerous narrations, the infallible Imams علیهم السلام stated: "نحن باب حطّتكم" (We are your gate of ḥiṭṭah). This means that if you enter the sphere of our governance and guardianship (Wilayah), you will receive Divine favor. [307] Many commentators (Mufassirūn) have identified the village (qaryah) as Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis). The natural sequence is that they would first enter the city with the recommended etiquette, then settle there, and then eat from its foods. However, in this verse, dwelling and food are mentioned first, followed by repentance, forgiveness, and entry into the city. Perhaps this is because the provision of dwelling and food creates the necessary context and environment for them to accept moral instructions.

306) Tafsir Namūneh 307) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq

Messages

1- The story of the Children of Israel entering Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis), accompanied by seeking forgiveness from Allah for their obstinacy and changing the commanded words, is an instructive and noteworthy story. "و اذ" (And [remember] when).

2- For refugees, dwelling is the most important issue. (The discussion of dwelling is mentioned first.) "اُسكنوا هذه القریة" (Dwell in this town).

3- Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis) is a land full of blessings. "كلوا منها حیث شئتم" (Eat therefrom wherever you wish).

4- Allah provides for all the material and spiritual, worldly and otherworldly needs of humanity. To benefit from blessings such as dwelling, food, forgiveness, and favor, He commands them to supplicate, seek forgiveness, and prostrate. "اسكنوا... كلوا... قولوا حطّة" (Dwell... eat... say ḥiṭṭah).

5- Allah forgives many sins with one sincere apology and repentance, "قولوا حطّة" (Say ḥiṭṭah). "نغفر لكم خطیئاتكم" (We will forgive you your sins). Just as we read in supplication: "یا مَن یقبل الیَسیر و یَعفو عن الكثیر" (O He who accepts the little and pardons the much).

6- To receive Divine favor and forgiveness, both seeking forgiveness (Istighfār) and supplication are necessary, as is action. "قولوا حطّة و ادخلوا الباب" (Say ḥiṭṭah and enter the gate).

7- Entering sacred places has specific etiquette and manners. "وادخلوا الباب سجّداً" (And enter the gate prostrating).

8- There must be a difference between the righteous and the sinner. When wrongdoers are forgiven, the righteous also benefit from a higher degree and greater favor. "سنزید المحسنین" (We will increase [reward] the doers of good).

9- In systems of management and encouraging others, the righteous must always be given distinction. "سنزید المحسنین" (We will increase [reward] the doers of good).

Ayah

فَبَدَّلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ قَوْلًا غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِجْزًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَظْلِمُونَ (۱٦۲)
But the wrongdoers changed the saying with other than what they had been told. So We sent against them a plague from the sky because of the wrongs they used to commit. (162)

Key Points

Distortion and alteration are sometimes obvious, such as changing the word 'Hittah' (حِطّة) to 'Hintah' (حِنطة). And sometimes, while preserving the linguistic form, they change the content and the spirit of a matter, similar to the trick of the Children of Israel for catching fish on the Sabbath, which is discussed in the next Ayah.

The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «لاتركبوا ما ارتكب الیهود فتستحلّوا محارم اللّه بادنى الحیل». Do not commit what the Jews committed, who made lawful the prohibitions of Allah through the smallest trick or legal loophole. [308]

The Quran discusses three types of distortion in the Divine Law:

1- Changing words, such as the Children of Israel who, instead of saying the word 'Hittah' (حطّة), meaning seeking forgiveness and pardon, said 'Hintah' (حِنطة), meaning wheat.

2- Changing time, such as when the Children of Israel built pools on the seashore. On Saturdays, when fishing was forbidden, fish would gather in them, and on Sunday, when fishing was permissible, they would catch them, saying: We did not fish on the Sabbath or the holiday. «و لقد علمتم الّذین اعتدوا منكم فى السَبت» [309]

3- Intellectual change and tampering with the natural calendar, such as delaying the sacred months during the Age of Ignorance to continue fighting. Because they did not want to stop the war due to the arrival of the four sacred months, they delayed the months, upon which the Ayah was revealed: «انّما النسى ء زیادة فى الكفر» [310]

308) Tafasir Furqan and Durr al-Manthur

309) Baqarah, 65

310) Tawbah, 37

Messages

1- The punishment for distorting and altering Allah's commands is wrath and torment. «فبدّل... فارسلنا»

2- Obstinate changes based on mockery are unforgivable. «رجزاً من السماء»

3- Not all punishments relate to the Day of Judgment; rather, sometimes punishment is given in this world as well. «رجزاً من السماء»

4- Man's destiny is in his own hands, and the torment is the result of his own oppression. «بما كانوا یظلمون»

Ayah

وَسْـَٔلْهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ٱلَّتِى كَانَتْ حَاضِرَةَ ٱلْبَحْرِ إِذْ يَعْدُونَ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ إِذْ تَأْتِيهِمْ حِيتَانُهُمْ يَوْمَ سَبْتِهِمْ شُرَّعًا وَيَوْمَ لَا يَسْبِتُونَ ۙ لَا تَأْتِيهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَبْلُوهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ (۱٦۳)
Ask them about the town that was situated on the seaside, when they violated the Sabbath, when their fish would come to them on the Sabbath day, visibly on the shore, but on days when they were not keeping Sabbath they would not come to them. Thus did We test them because of the transgressions they used to commit. (163)

Key Points

The word "Sabt" means cutting off and suspending work for rest and tranquility, which is interpreted as a holiday. Therefore, "Yawm al-Sabt" means the day of rest, which was Saturday for the Jews, and "Yawm lā yasbitūn" means the day they did not rest (or take a holiday). "Shurra'an" is the plural of "shāri'," meaning the shore or waterside. On Saturday, when fishing was prohibited, the fish would bring themselves from the deep water to the shore. Some have also said that the meaning of "shurra'an" refers to fish that surface and show themselves. [311]

A group of the Children of Israel (the people of Eilah) who lived on the seashore were forbidden by Allah's command from fishing on Saturdays. But on that very day, their desired fish, "hītānuhum," would display themselves more prominently, "shurra'an," and tempt the people more! This itself was a divine test. This community hypocritically broke the divine law. By building coastal pools and blocking the escape routes of the fish that entered them on Saturdays, they easily caught them on Sunday, claiming that they had not violated the divine command and had not fished on Saturday.

Although Allah created fish for the benefit of people, He prohibited fishing one day a week as a test. Therefore, not every prohibition necessarily has a health aspect; the fish on Saturday do not differ from other days in terms of protein content. Imam Ali عليه السلام strongly criticized the justification of sin and the making lawful of the forbidden by justifying wine as nabīdh (date drink), bribery as a gift, and usury as a transaction. [312]

Sometimes the divine test occurs in situations where human desires reach their peak, just as in this verse the prohibition and test of fishing occur when the fish show themselves more prominently, "ta'tīhim ḥītānuhum." Or, in Ayah 94 of Sūrat al-Mā'idah, which speaks of the prohibition of hunting while in the state of Ihrām (consecration), we read: "li-yabluwannakumu Allāhu bi-shay'in mina aṣ-ṣaydi tanāluhu aydīkum wa-rimāḥukum" (Allah will surely test you with something of the game that your hands and your spears can reach). Where the game is accessible and within range, the prohibition of hunting in that state is a divine test.

311) Tafsir Rahnama 312) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 156

Messages

  1. The lawbreaking of ancestors shames subsequent generations as well. "Wa s'alhum..."
  2. Relating the ugly traits of ancestors for which they were punished is permissible for the sake of others' admonition. "'ani al-qaryati allatī"
  3. Living on the coast and earning a livelihood through fishing has existed throughout history. "Kānat ḥāḍirata al-baḥr..."
  4. The true nature of sin is not changed by legal trickery or deception. (Building pools beside the sea for fishing the next day). "Ya'dūna fī as-sabt"
  5. By divine will, the fish of the sea gain the ability to distinguish the days of the week and the fishermen. "Ta'tīhim... lā ta'tīhim"
  6. Worldly and material displays are one of the divine tests. "Ka-dhālika nablūhum"

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَتْ أُمَّةٌ مِّنْهُمْ لِمَ تَعِظُونَ قَوْمًا ۙ ٱللَّهُ مُهْلِكُهُمْ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ قَالُوا۟ مَعْذِرَةً إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ (۱٦٤)
When a group of them said, ‘Why do you advise a people whom Allah will destroy, or punish with a severe punishment?’ They said, ‘As an excuse before your Lord, and [with the hope] that they may be Godwary.’ (164)

Key Points

Bani Israel were three groups: one group of lawbreakers, another group of compassionate advisors, and the third group was the indifferent group. The indifferent ones would tell the advisors: Do not tire yourselves, because your words have no effect on the wicked, and they are destined for Hell anyway. But those who forbade evil would say: Our work is not ineffective; at the very least, we are excused before Allah. And perhaps they might desist and adopt piety because of our words. In Ayah 5 and 6 of Surah Al-Mursalat, Allah swears by those who convey the remembrance of Allah to others, whether for the purpose of completing the argument (proof) or for the purpose of influence and warning. «فالملقیات ذكراً عُذراً أو نُذراً» In the book Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy 'anil Munkar (Enjoining Good and Forbidding Evil), I have mentioned more than ten benefits for enjoining and forbidding, even if it has no effect, the summary of which I present here [313], so that perhaps silence, fear, and indifference in the face of abandoning good and committing evil may be eliminated:

  1. Sometimes our enjoining and forbidding does not have an immediate effect, but it affects history, innate nature (fitrah), and the judgment of others. Just as Imam Husayn عليه السلام was martyred in the path of enjoining good and forbidding evil, so that the conscience of people throughout history might be awakened.
  2. Sometimes enjoining and forbidding preserves the atmosphere for others. Just as the call to prayer (Adhan) is recommended, even if there is no listener, stopping when the traffic light turns red is necessary, even if there is no car. Because preserving the law and the atmosphere of respect for the law is necessary.
  3. Sometimes our enjoining and forbidding does not stop sinners from sinning, but continuous speaking makes the pleasure of sin bitter in their mouth, and at least they do not sin with a clear conscience.
  4. To preserve freedom, one must enjoin and forbid, because silence turns society into an environment of suffocation, fear, and silence.
  5. Enjoining and forbidding creates status for the person himself, even if others do not listen. The Quran says: «و من احسن قولاً ممّن دعا الى اللّه» [314]
  6. Enjoining good and forbidding evil, if it does not affect others, is at least a form of proximity to Allah for us, an inculcation and practice of courage, and a manifestation of zeal and commitment.
  7. Sometimes our enjoining and forbidding does not take effect today, but the day the transgressor reaches a dead end and his conscience awakens, he will understand that you spoke the truth. Therefore, if it does not take effect today, it will take effect someday.
  8. Enjoining and forbidding calms the human conscience; the person tells himself that I have fulfilled my duty. This peace of conscience is valuable, even if others do not listen.
  9. Enjoining good and forbidding evil is the practice (Sīrah) of the Prophets, even if others do not listen. The Quran says: «و اذا ذكّروا لایذكرون» [315] or states repeatedly: people did not listen to the words and guidance of the Prophets and turned away from them. Therefore, we should not expect everyone to listen to our words.
  10. Enjoining good is a completion of the argument (Itmām al-Hujjah) for the transgressor so that he cannot say on the Day of Judgment that no one told him, besides being an excuse for the speaker so that he is not asked why he did not speak.
  11. During Divine wrath, those who enjoin good will be saved. (The next Ayah refers to this.)

In any case, the Prophets and the Successors (Awṣiyā') were martyred in the path of enjoining good and forbidding evil so that the truth would not be lost or vanish, just as the Quran states in several verses: «یقتلون الانبیاء» [316], «یقتلون النبیّین» [317] and «یقتلون الّذین یأمرون بالقسط» [318], from which it is understood that sometimes in enjoining good, one must proceed to the point of martyrdom.

Imam Ali عليه السلام rebuked his representative in a letter for attending the banquet of the aristocrats. [319] Meaning that the Imam sacrificed an individual for the sake of the school (of thought) so that the conduct of asceticism would not lead to aristocracy.

313) It is interesting that I am writing these lines during the Week of Enjoining Good and the Decade of Ashura, when Imam Husayn عليه السلام considered the philosophy of his uprising to be the failure of society to act upon the truth and the failure to forbid evil. 314) Fussilat, 33 315) Saffat, 13 316) Aal-i Imran, 112 317) Baqarah, 61 318) Aal-i Imran, 21 319) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 45

Messages

  1. Some people neither preach themselves nor tolerate others preaching. «و اذ قالت اُمّة منهم لِمَ تعظون...»
  2. One should not justify the sin of indifference with "Divine Will." «لِمَ تعظون قوماً اللّه مهلكهم»
  3. The indifferent ones protest against those who enjoin good instead of protesting against the people of sin. «لم تعظون»
  4. One must resist those who consider preaching and educational efforts ineffective. «لِمَ تَعِظون... قالوا معذرةً» Because failing to restrain people from sin carries responsibility, and we must be accountable.
  5. Hasty judgment is forbidden. One cannot say: Allah will punish or destroy this group; perhaps they will become pious. «مهلِكُهم أو معذّبهم... لعلّهم یتّقون»
  6. Forbidding evil is obligatory, either so that the wicked individuals become faithful, «لعلّهم یتّقون», or so that the person himself has an excuse before Allah. «معذرةً» (Thus, it is not necessary for it to definitely take effect for it to become obligatory upon us, because we are commanded to perform the duty, not guaranteed the result.)
  7. Men of Allah do not despair of reforming society and do not abandon enjoining good and forbidding evil. «و لعلّهم یتقون»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا نَسُوا۟ مَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَنجَيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلسُّوٓءِ وَأَخَذْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ بِعَذَابٍۭ بَـِٔيسٍۭ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ (۱٦٥)
So when they forgot what they had been reminded of, We delivered those who forbade evil [conduct] and seized the wrongdoers with a terrible punishment because of the transgressions they used to commit. (165)
فَلَمَّا عَتَوْا۟ عَن مَّا نُهُوا۟ عَنْهُ قُلْنَا لَهُمْ كُونُوا۟ قِرَدَةً خَـٰسِـِٔينَ (۱٦٦)
When they defied [the command pertaining to] what they were forbidden from, We said to them, ‘Be you spurned apes.’ (166)

Key Points

The word ba'īs (بئیس) is derived from ba's (بأس), meaning severe, and ‘ataw (عَتوا) means rebellion and insolence. According to narrations, when the admonishers among the Children of Israel saw that their advice was ineffective among their people, they separated themselves from the people. That very night, divine wrath and punishment descended and encompassed the sinners and the silent ones. [320] And this transformation and change is among the creative (takwīnī) dispositions of Allah. In Ayah 60 of Sūrat Al-Mā’idah, it is mentioned that a group was transformed into pigs and monkeys due to disobedience, while here only monkeys are mentioned. Ibn ‘Abbās says that both verses concern the same group. It is understood from the apparent meaning of the Ayah that not only their character but also their physical appearance was transformed into that of monkeys. Indeed, the God Who, by a single command, turned the fire into a garden for Prophet Ibrāhīm, can transform one creature into another, and a human being into a monkey. Of course, those who were transformed (masūkh) did not reproduce, and according to the statement of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, they did not live for more than a few days. [321]

320) Tafsīr-e Namūneh 321) Tafsīr Majma‘ al-Bayān

Messages

1- Enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (Nahy ‘anil Munkar), even if it does not lead to the guidance of others, leads to the salvation of the forbidder himself. «أنجینا الّذین ینهون»

2- Forgetfulness, if it results from carelessness and indifference, incurs punishment. «فلما نسوا ...»

3- Whoever closes the path of admonition upon himself has opened the path of Divine wrath upon himself. «نسوا - أخذنا»

4- Forbidding the evil is a continuous duty. «ینهون» (The imperfect verb indicates continuity).

5- The indifferent are oppressors (ẓālimūn). «الّذین ظلموا» (Those who committed wrong). (In the previous Ayah, the Children of Israel were divided into three groups: the transgressors, the admonishers, and the silent/indifferent ones. In this Ayah, the silent ones are also counted among the destroyed oppressors, because only those who forbade the evil were saved, «أنجینا الّذین ینهون». Therefore, silence is both oppression and a cause for destruction.)

6- Among the greatest and most severe punishments is the fall from the stage of humanity. «عذاب بئیس، كونوا قردةً خاسئین»

7- The transgressor deserves to lose his humanity and be transformed (masūkh), and the punishment for the arrogant is rejection and humiliation. «عتوا، قردةً خاسئین» Indeed, a nation that shows insolence against Divine law will imitate others like a monkey.

8- Those who distort and transform the command of Allah through trickery will have their faces transformed (masūkh). The one who plays games with religion turns into a playful animal (a monkey). «كونوا قردة»

Ayah

وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكَ لَيَبْعَثَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مَن يَسُومُهُمْ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَسَرِيعُ ٱلْعِقَابِ ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٦۷)
And when your Lord proclaimed that He would surely send against them, until the Day of Resurrection, those who would inflict on them a terrible punishment. Indeed your Lord is swift in retribution, and indeed, He is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (167)

Key Points

«تَأذَّن» means He swore an oath and announced. «یَسُوم» from «سَوم» (sawm), means to impose or inflict. Imam Ali عليه السلام repeatedly addressed the disloyal people of his era, saying: May Allah take me away from you and make others dominate you. [322]

322) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermons 25 and 70

Messages

1- The Divine punishment concerning the Jews and their destiny is noteworthy. «واذ...»

2- Although the Divine tradition is to grant respite to the deviants, this respite is not everywhere and forever. «لیبعثنّ علیهم»

3- The domination of corrupt people over the Children of Israel is a Divine punishment due to their own actions. «لیبعثنّ علیهم»

4- Nations have an interconnected history, and the sin of one generation continues in future generations and throughout history, even until the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah). «الى یوم القیامة»

5- Those who mock the Divine guardians (Awliya) will be humiliated. «یسومهم...»

6- The oppressive Jewish people will always be humiliated and disgraced. «الى یوم القیامة»

7- Fear of punishment, coupled with hope for Divine forgiveness and mercy, is the source of human training and growth. «انّ ربّك لسریع الحساب و انّه لغفور رحيم»

8- The path of repentance (Tawbah) and receiving Divine forgiveness is open to everyone. «انّه لغفور رحيم»

Ayah

وَقَطَّعْنَـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أُمَمًا ۖ مِّنْهُمُ ٱلصَّـٰلِحُونَ وَمِنْهُمْ دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ وَبَلَوْنَـٰهُم بِٱلْحَسَنَـٰتِ وَٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (۱٦۸)
We dispersed them into communities around the earth: some of them were righteous, and some of them otherwise, and We tested them with good and bad [times] so that they may come back. (168)

Key Points

The Children of Israel sometimes attained honor and power, so that perhaps they might be grateful, and sometimes they were afflicted with hardships, so that the feeling of repentance and turning back might be revived in them. Of course, in the midst of this, some were righteous and good-doers and believed in the truth of Islam, while others were pursuing worldliness and their own obstinacy.

Messages

1- Man is free in choosing his path and method. «منهم الصالحون و منهم دون ذلك»

2- When criticizing a group, the right of their good members must not be overlooked. «منهم الصالحون»

3- Bitter and sweet events are tools for divine testing. «بلوناهم بالحسنات و السیئات»

4- Encouragement and punishment, kindness and wrath, both are constructive and educational. «بالحسنات و السیئات لعلّهم یرجعون»

5- The spiritual transformation of human beings and their return to the truth is among the wisdoms of Allah's testing and trial. «لعلّهم یرجعون»

Ayah

فَخَلَفَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ خَلْفٌ وَرِثُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَأْخُذُونَ عَرَضَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْأَدْنَىٰ وَيَقُولُونَ سَيُغْفَرُ لَنَا وَإِن يَأْتِهِمْ عَرَضٌ مِّثْلُهُۥ يَأْخُذُوهُ ۚ أَلَمْ يُؤْخَذْ عَلَيْهِم مِّيثَـٰقُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ أَن لَّا يَقُولُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلْحَقَّ وَدَرَسُوا۟ مَا فِيهِ ۗ وَٱلدَّارُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۱٦۹)
Then they were succeeded by an evil posterity, which inherited the Book: they grab the transitory gains of this lower world, and say, ‘It will be forgiven us.’ And if similar transitory gains were to come their way, they would grab them too. Was not the covenant of the Book taken with them that they shall not attribute anything to Allah except the truth? They have studied what is in it, and [know that] the abode of the Hereafter is better for those who are Godwary. Do you not exercise your reason? (169)

Key Points

Khulf (خَلْف), is often used for an unworthy offspring, while Khalaf (خَلَف) is used for a righteous offspring. [323] ‘Araz (عَرَض) means any kind of capital, but ‘Ardh (عَرْض) refers only to cash. Likewise, a temporary, short-lived, and unstable entity is called ‘Araz. Therefore, because the world is unstable, it is referred to as ‘Araz. Faydh Kashani says: The meaning of ‘Araz (عَرَض) in the Ayah is bribery. Dars (درس) means repetition. Since the material is repeated when studying and learning something from a teacher, it is called dars. Things that have been used repeatedly and become worn out are also called mundaris (worn out). [324]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah has commanded people not to say what they do not know. Then the Imam recited this Ayah: «ألم یؤخذ علیهم میثاق الكتاب ان لایقولوا على اللّه الا الحقّ» [325] Imam Hassan عليه السلام said: «عجب لمن یتفكّر فى مأكوله كیف لا یتفكّر فى معقوله فیجنب بطنه ما یوذیه و یودع صدره ما یرویه» (Wondrous is he who reflects upon what he eats, yet does not reflect upon what he comprehends, avoiding what harms his stomach while depositing into his heart what satisfies it). It is astonishing that someone reflects and contemplates his physical food and sustenance, but gives no thought to choosing his intellectual food, allowing anything to settle in his heart and belief. [326]

323) Tafsir Namuneh 324) Tafsir Namuneh 325) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 43 326) Tafsir Furqan; Bihar, Vol. 1, p. 218

Messages

1- History bears witness to generations of worldly and sinful Jews. «فخلف من بعدهم خلف...»

2- Worldliness is one of the afflictions of religion. «من بعدهم خلف... یأخذوا عرَض هذا الاَدنى»

3- The Jews had both access to the Torah and full knowledge of it. «ورثوا الكتاب»

4- It is not fitting for one who has become aware of the teachings of the Divine Book to adopt worldliness and consuming unlawful earnings as their profession. «ورثوا الكتاب یأخذون...»

5- Bribery and consuming unlawful earnings were also subject to criticism and reproach in other religions. «یأخذون عرض هذا الادنى»

6- The Jews confessed to their sin, but claimed they would be forgiven. «سیغفر لنا»

7- The Jews were so self-conceited that they imagined they would be forgiven without repentance. «سیغفر لنا» Indeed, one who considers forgiveness guaranteed for himself is worthy of reproach.

8- Undue hope, like undue fear, is destructive. «سیغفرلنا» Hoping for Divine mercy despite persistence in sin is undue.

9- The Jewish consumers of unlawful earnings persisted in their sin. «و ان یأتهم عرض مثله یأخذوه»

10- The sign of true repentance is that if the opportunity for transgression arises again, one does not commit it. «اِن یأتهم عرض مثله یأخذوه»

11- Anyone who considers Divine forgiveness certain for himself despite committing sin has attributed falsehood to Allah. «أن لا یقولوا على اللّه الاّ الحقّ»

12- Knowing the teachings of the Divine Book and studying it repeatedly is not enough; action is necessary. «ورثو الكتاب... درسوا»

13- The Hereafter is a superior world possessing eternal and lasting blessings. «و الدّار الآخرة خیر»

14- Abstaining from consuming unlawful earnings is a sign of piety (Taqwa). (The phrase «یتّقون» is placed opposite the phrase «یأخذون عرض».)

15- Benefiting from the entirely good life of the Hereafter depends on piety and God-fearingness (Taqwa). «و الدّار الآخرة خیر للّذین یتّقون»

16- Anyone who pursues the world and abandons the Hereafter is not wise. «أفلا تعقلون»

17- One of the methods of propagation and education is questioning the audience, which awakens consciences. «أفلا تعقلون»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُمَسِّكُونَ بِٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ إِنَّا لَا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُصْلِحِينَ (۱۷٠)
As for those who hold fast to the Book and maintain the prayer—indeed, We do not waste the reward of those who bring about reform. (170)

Key Points

Narrations state that this Ayah describes the characteristics of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام and their followers (Shi'a). [327]

The Quran always mentions the righteous minority alongside the corrupt majority. (The previous Ayah was a critique, and this Ayah is praise.)

"الكتاب" (The Book) includes all heavenly scriptures, and the meaning of the Ayah is not restricted to the followers of a specific religion or a specific book. However, considering the preceding verses, the Torah might be intended.

In this Ayah, the group that adheres firmly to the heavenly scripture is praised, but elsewhere, those who possess the Book yet do not adhere to it are likened to a donkey carrying a load of books: «مَثَل الّذین حمّلوا التورات ثمّ لم یحملوها كمثل الحمار یحمل اسفاراً» [328]

Muṣliḥ (reformer/one who sets things right) refers both to the person who seeks to reform himself and the person who strives to reform society.

Although prayer (Ṣalāt) is part of the content of the heavenly scripture, it is mentioned separately due to its importance, because prayer is the pillar of religion, and the establishment of religion depends on it.

As the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «الصلاة عمود الدّین» [329] And elsewhere He said: Everything has a face and an appearance, and the countenance of your religion is prayer, so none of you should spoil the countenance of his religion. «و وجه دینكم الصلاة فلایشیننّ احدكم وجه دینه» [330]

327) Tafsir Ithnā ‘Asharī 328) Al-Jumu‘ah, 5 329) Tafsir Ithnā ‘Asharī; Al-Tahdhīb, Vol. 2, p. 237 330) Tafsir Furqān; Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 270

Messages

  1. Recitation, memorization, printing, and publishing the heavenly scripture are not enough; one must act upon it for it to be salvific. «یمسّكون» (Just as following a doctor's prescription is the only way to cure.)

  2. Prayer (Ṣalāt) existed in past religions and heavenly scriptures as well. «اقاموا الصلاة»

  3. Individuals who neglect prayer and are ignorant of God’s Book cannot be complete reformers (muṣliḥīn). «یمسّكون بالكتاب... اقاموا الصلاة... المصلحین»

  4. Religion, besides its effects in the Hereafter, also plays a role in reforming worldly life. «المصلحین»

  5. Establishing prayer is a means of reforming society. «اقاموا الصلاة، المصلحین» (Establishment [Iqāmah] means complete commitment to upholding prayer in all aspects of life, not merely performing the prayer.)

  6. True reform has two conditions and axes: adherence to Divine law and a firm relationship with God through the establishment of prayer. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

۞ وَإِذْ نَتَقْنَا ٱلْجَبَلَ فَوْقَهُمْ كَأَنَّهُۥ ظُلَّةٌ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَنَّهُۥ وَاقِعٌۢ بِهِمْ خُذُوا۟ مَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰكُم بِقُوَّةٍ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ مَا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (۱۷۱)
When We plucked the mountain [and held it] above them as if it were a canopy (and they thought it was about to fall on them): ‘Hold on with power to what We have given you and remember that which is in it, so that you may be Godwary.’ (171)

Key Points

This Ayah is similar to Ayah 63 of Surah Al-Baqarah and is the last Ayah in this Surah that speaks about the Children of Israel (Bani Israel). From Ayah 103 up to this point, it has continuously presented issues concerning them. "Nataq" (نَتق) means to uproot and throw, and "Dhullah" (ظُلَّة) means a piece of cloud, a roof, or a canopy. When Moses (عليه السلام) went to Mount Tur for the Divine appointment (Miqat), he brought the Torah back with him to guide the Children of Israel. However, they opposed it. Allah uprooted the mountain and placed it above their heads. They became terrified, fell into prostration, and promised obedience, but it was not long before they reverted from the religion again.

Question: Does taking a covenant and demanding obedience under duress have any value?

Answer: Firstly, not all compulsion is bad. Sometimes an addict is forced to quit their addiction, which is itself a value. Of course, heartfelt belief cannot be imposed by force, but correct action can be promoted in society through compulsion. Secondly, sometimes an action is initially imposed by force, but gradually it is performed out of awareness and free choice.

Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) was asked: Does the strength (quwwah) mentioned in this Ayah refer to physical strength or spiritual strength? He replied: Both. [331]

331) Tafsir Ayyashi and Mahasin Barqi, Vol. 1, p. 261

Messages

1- The placement of the mountain above the heads of the Children of Israel and the threat against them is an event that must not be forgotten. «و اذ نتقنا الجبل...»

2- Learning is not enough; it must be remembered (recalled). «خذوا... اذكروا»

3- For the general education of society and to prevent deviation, sometimes the leverage of pressure must be used. «اذ نتقنا ... خذوا ما آتیناكم ...»

4- Divine laws must be received and acted upon with seriousness and strength. «بقوّة»

5- To achieve the perfection of piety (taqwa), decisiveness in religion and serious resolve are necessary. «خذوا... بقوّة... لعلّكم تتّقون»

6- The goal of the heavenly books and religious commands is the fear of God and the emergence of piety (taqwa) among the people. «لعلّكم تتّقون»

7- By explaining the philosophy and effects of laws, commands, and ordinances, we should encourage others to pay attention to them and act upon them. «خذوا... لعلّكم تتّقون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِنۢ بَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ مِن ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ ۛ شَهِدْنَآ ۛ أَن تَقُولُوا۟ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ إِنَّا كُنَّا عَنْ هَـٰذَا غَـٰفِلِينَ (۱۷۲)
When your Lord took from the Children of Adam, from their loins, their descendants and made them bear witness over themselves, [He said to them,] ‘Am I not your Lord?’ They said, ‘Yes indeed! We bear witness.’ [This,] lest you should say on the Day of Resurrection, ‘Indeed we were unaware of this,’ (172)

Key Points

  • Dhurriyyah (ذریّة) is either derived from dharr (ذَرّ), meaning very small particles of dust (or tiny ants), which here refers to the components of human semen (nutfah) or young, small children; or it comes from the root dharw (ذَرو), meaning to scatter, or from dhara'a (ذَرأ), meaning to create. [332] However, it is most often used to refer to children/offspring. The manner in which Allah took the covenant from the Children of Adam is not specified in this Ayah, but exegetes (mufassirun) have presented several views, the two most famous of which are:
    • A: According to narrations, after the creation of Adam (عليه السلام), all his descendants until the end of the world came forth from his back in the form of tiny, scattered particles, like ants. They were addressed and questioned by Allah and, by Divine power, they confessed to the Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah. Then they all returned to Adam's loins and clay, so that they might gradually and naturally come into this world. This realm is called the "World of Particles" (‘Alam al-Dharr), and that covenant is called the "Covenant of Alast" (Am I not?). [333] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Some of Adam's progeny confessed verbally in the World of Particles, but they did not possess heartfelt faith. [334] It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله that this confession took place on the Day of 'Arafah. [335]
    • B: The intent of the World of Particles is the covenant of innate nature (fitrah) and creation (takwīn). That is, when the children of Adam emerge from the loins of their fathers into the wombs of their mothers, being nothing more than particles, Allah places the monotheistic and truth-seeking innate nature (fitrah) within their disposition, and this Divine secret is deposited in the core and fitrah of everyone as an inner sense. Likewise, belief in God is imprinted upon their intellect and wisdom as a self-aware truth. Therefore, human fitrah and intellect bear witness to the Lordship of Allah. In some narrations where Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about fitrah, the Imam considered fitrah to be the same as the World of Particles. [336]
    • In some narrations, fitrah is the effect of the World of Particles, not the World itself: «ثبت المعرفة فى قلوبهم و نسوا الموقف» (Knowledge was established in their hearts, but they forgot the standing/position). Therefore, humans confessed at a certain time and position, but they have forgotten it, and the effect of that confession is the fitrah—the inclination of the heart towards Him. In any case, the Ayah is a subject of discussion among theologians (mutakallimīn), traditionists (muhaddithīn), and exegetes (mufassirīn). Therefore, we entrust the knowledge of it to its people, who are those firmly rooted in knowledge (rāsikhūn fī al-'ilm). [337]

332) Tafsir Namūneh 333) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 13 334) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn 335) Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr 336) Tafsir Burhān and Nūr al-Thaqalayn, Payām-e Qur’ān, Vol. 3, p. 117 337) For information on various views and opinions, refer to the books "Payām-e Qur’ān" by Ayatollah Makarem Shirazi, "Manshūr-e Jāvid" by Ayatollah Subhani, and Tafsir Atyab al-Bayān.

Messages

1- All human beings are the descendants of Adam عليه السلام, and Allah took a confession from them regarding His Lordship. «و اذ اخذ ربّك من بنى آدم... ألست بربّكم»

2- Allah has placed Monotheism (Tawhīd) in the innate nature (fitrah) and disposition of human beings. «أشهدهم على أنفسهم ألست بربّكم» Every human being has somehow perceived the Lordship and Oneness of Allah and bears witness to it.

3- After creating man, Allah revealed His Lordship to him so that he might bear witness when necessary. «ألستُ بربّكم»

4- The covenant of fitrah (innate nature) and the search for God is for the completion of the proof (Itmām al-Hujjah). «أن تقولوا یوم القیامة»

5- On the Day of Judgment, the claim of being heedless of Allah's Lordship will not be accepted. «انّا كنّا عن هذا غافلین» Ignorance and heedlessness are not acceptable excuses before Allah.

Ayahs

أَوْ تَقُولُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَشْرَكَ ءَابَآؤُنَا مِن قَبْلُ وَكُنَّا ذُرِّيَّةً مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ ۖ أَفَتُهْلِكُنَا بِمَا فَعَلَ ٱلْمُبْطِلُونَ (۱۷۳)
or lest you should say, ‘Our fathers ascribed partners [to Allah] before [us] and we were descendants after them. Will You then destroy us because of what the falsifiers have done?’ (173)
وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (۱۷٤)
Thus do We elaborate the signs, so that they may come back. (174)

Key Points

If there were no testimony to Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) in the world of the pre-existence (ʿālam al-dharr) or in the innate nature (fiṭrah), man would not easily become a worshipper of God in this world, and he would constantly imitate his ancestors. «او تقولوا انّما اشرك آبائُنا...»

Messages

1- The innate nature (fiṭrah) of knowing Allah within man is for the completion of the proof (itmām al-ḥujjah) by Allah, and the lamp of fiṭrah has the power to illuminate the darkness of the environment. «او تقولوا انّما اشرك...»

2- Society and the environment do not compel man. «أو تقولوا انّما أشرك آبائُنا...»

3- Imitation (taqlīd) in the fundamentals of religion (Uṣūl ad-Dīn) is not permissible. «أشرك آبائُنا»

4- The belief, action, and polytheism (shirk) of ancestors become the foundation for the belief, action, and polytheism of their descendants. «أشرك آبائُنا»

5- Shifting one's own sin and deviation onto others is not justifiable. «تقولوا انّما أشرك آبائُنا»

6- Obedience and respect for ancestors are limited to the extent that they do not lead man to polytheism (shirk). «ذرّیة من بعدهم»

7- Polytheists are followers of falsehood (bāṭil). «أشرك آبائُنا فعل المبطلون»

8- In Allah's system of retribution, no one is held accountable for the sin of another. «أفتهلكنا بما فعل المبطلون»

9- Divine verses are meant to draw people's attention to the covenant of the innate nature (mīthāq al-fiṭrah) and the monotheistic disposition (tawḥīdī). «نفصّل الآیات ولعلّهم یرجعون»

10- The origin (aṣl) is Monotheism (Tawḥīd), and polytheism (shirk) is incidental; therefore, the Quran calls for the polytheists to return to the original nature of man. «لعلّهم یرجعون»

Ayah

وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ ٱلَّذِىٓ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ءَايَـٰتِنَا فَٱنسَلَخَ مِنْهَا فَأَتْبَعَهُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ فَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلْغَاوِينَ (۱۷٥)
Relate to them an account of him to whom We gave Our signs, but he cast them off. Thereupon Satan pursued him, and he became one of the perverse. (175)

Key Points

“Insilākh” (اِنسلاخ) means the stripping off of skin, and it is used where something is firmly attached to another. Here, it is an allusion to his complete awareness and mastery of divine knowledge. The phrase “fa-atba’ahu” (فاتبعه) indicates that this person had reached such a high station on the path of truth that Satan had given up hope concerning him, but with the emergence of signs of deviation, Satan quickly pursued him, and he ultimately ended up among the misguided and the wretched. [338] This Ayah refers to the story of a scholar from the Children of Israel named Bal’am Ba’ura, who was initially among the believers and the bearer of divine signs and knowledge, but was led astray by the whispers of Satan and the tyrant. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Bal’am son of Ba’ur knew the Greatest Name of Allah (Ism al-A’zam) and his prayers were answered, but he was drawn to Pharaoh’s court, and although he was initially a preacher for Prophet Moses عليه السلام, he eventually acted against Moses and his companions and met a terrible end. [339] Indeed, the glitter of the world and the royal court are factors in the downfall of scholars and scientists. The Quran does not mention this person’s name, but recounts his deed, so that, according to Imam Baqir عليه السلام, it applies to anyone who prioritizes carnal desires over the truth [340], and such people are found in every era and are not limited to a specific time or person like Bal’am Ba’ura. The story of Bal’am Ba’ura is also mentioned in the current Torah. [341]

338) Tafsir Namuneh 339) Tafsirs Nur al-Thaqalayn and Kanz al-Daqa’iq 340) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn 341) Torah, Book of Numbers, Chapter 22

Messages

1- The leader must inform the people about foreseeable dangers and warn them. “Wa-utlu ‘alayhim...”

2- Sometimes tyrants deceive even scholars. The fate of a scholar like Bal’am Ba’ura must be a lesson for history. “Wa-utlu ‘alayhim naba’a...” because it is an important and beneficial story. (“Naba’” refers to important and beneficial news)

3- Man is free and can change direction. “Ātaynāhu āyātinā fa-insalakha”

4- No matter how high a person rises, he should not become arrogant, because there is a possibility of downfall. The outcome of the deed is important. The higher one's position, the greater the danger. “Fa-insalakha minhā”

5- Whoever separates himself from Allah becomes prey for Satan. “Fa-insalakha minhā fa-atba’ahu ash-Shaytān”

6- Satan whispers and lies in wait, pursuing a person whenever he sees an opening. “Fa-insalakha fa-atba’ahu” (The word “fa-atba’ahu” next to the word “fa-insalakha” shows that Satan is lying in wait and pursues the person immediately upon his separation.)

7- Satan cannot overcome a divine scholar. (Until Bal’am abandoned the signs, Satan had no dominance over him.) “Fa-insalakha minhā fa-atba’ahu ash-Shaytān”

8- Knowledge alone is not salvific, because a worldly scholar becomes a captive of Satan. “Fa-atba’ahu ash-Shaytān”

9- One must contemplate a bad outcome and fear an evil end, lest blessings turn into misfortune. “Ātaynāhu āyātinā... fa-kāna min al-ghāwīn”

10- Abandoning the path of Allah is far from reason and wisdom. “Fa-kāna min al-ghāwīn”

11- The downfall of man occurs in several stages: separation from divine signs, following Satan, and joining the company of the misguided. “Fa-insalakha minhā fa-atba’ahu ash-Shaytān fa-kāna min al-ghāwīn”

Ayah

وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَرَفَعْنَـٰهُ بِهَا وَلَـٰكِنَّهُۥٓ أَخْلَدَ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ ۚ فَمَثَلُهُۥ كَمَثَلِ ٱلْكَلْبِ إِن تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْهِ يَلْهَثْ أَوْ تَتْرُكْهُ يَلْهَث ۚ ذَّٰلِكَ مَثَلُ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ۚ فَٱقْصُصِ ٱلْقَصَصَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۱۷٦)
Had We wished, We would have surely raised him by their means, but he clung to the earth and followed his [base] desires. So his parable is that of a dog: if you make for it, it lolls out its tongue, and if you let it alone, it lolls out its tongue. Such is the parable of the people who deny Our signs. So recount these narratives, so that they may reflect. (176)

Key Points

In this Ayah, since the word "أرض" (earth) is placed in contrast to spiritual elevation, what is meant by it are trivial, material, and worldly matters. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: "If a person's knowledge and information increase, but his guidance does not increase, that knowledge has caused him to distance himself further from Allah." [342] The stories of the Quran are founded upon truth and reality, and they serve to console and strengthen the resolve of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and the people of faith, to bring despair to the enemies, and to be a means of admonition and reminder for the believers [343], and a source of lesson for the wise. [344]

342) بحار، ج 2، ص 37 343) هود، 120 344) یوسف، 111

Messages

1- Adherence to the divine verses (Ayat) leads to proximity to Allah and high stations. «لرفعناه بها»

2- Despite the sovereignty of Allah's will and desire over the universe, man is free and possesses choice. «و لو شئنا لرفعناه بها ولكنّه اخلد الى الارض...»

3- Allah's desire is based on our own actions. «و لو شئنا لرفعناه بها ولكنّه اخلد الى الارض و اتّبع الهوى» Yes, reaching the station of proximity (to Allah) is conditional upon avoiding worldliness and lust.

4- Negligent people resemble livestock, but the worldly scholar is like a greedy dog. «كمثل الكلب»

5- A scholar without action is detested. «كمثل الكلب ان تحمل علیه یلهث...»

6- One enslaved by the world never finds peace. «ان تحمل علیه یلهث أو تتركه یلهث» No matter how much a greedy person possesses, the tongue of his covetousness is still hanging out, because worldliness and greed have no end.

7- Attachment to the world and following desires makes a person indifferent to other matters. «ان تحمل ... أو تتركه...»

8- If religious scholars become worldly, they expose themselves to denying the divine verses and inclining toward disbelief (Kufr). «اخلد الى الارض... كذّبوا بآیاتنا»

9- The fate of deceived scholars and learned individuals must be a source of lesson and contemplation for the people. (In the previous Ayah, it came: «وَاتلُ» [Recite], here we read: «فاقصص» [Relate the story]).

10- Story writing and storytelling must be aimed at the intellectual growth of man, not sedation and mere entertainment. Purposeful storytelling is the work of the Prophets. «فاقصص القصص لعلّهم یتفكّرون»

Ayahs

سَآءَ مَثَلًا ٱلْقَوْمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَأَنفُسَهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَظْلِمُونَ (۱۷۷)
Evil is the parable of the people who deny Our signs and wrong themselves. (177)
مَن يَهْدِ ٱللَّهُ فَهُوَ ٱلْمُهْتَدِى ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلْ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (۱۷۸)
Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray—it is they who are the losers. (178)

Key Points

Perhaps the reason that the singular word muhtad («مهتد») is used for the guided ones, and the plural khāsiroon («خاسرون») is used for the misguided ones, is that the path of the guided is one, and they are united, but the deviants are scattered and their paths are multiple. The souls of wolves and dogs are separate; The souls of the Lions of God are united. Although guidance and misguidance are in the hand of Allah, they are not compulsory and are not without reason or calculation. Allah is Wise and Merciful, and unless a person prepares the ground by his own hand, he will not be covered by Divine grace or wrath.

Messages

1- An evil consequence awaits the deniers. «ساء مثلاً»

2- Denying the Divine signs is oppression against oneself, not against Allah. «أنفسهم كانوا یظلمون» The precedence of anfusahum (أنفسهم) over yaẓlimūn (یظلمون) indicates exclusivity (or restriction).

3- Guidance is in the hand of Allah, and if His grace is absent, knowledge alone does not lead to salvation and guidance. «من یهد اللّه»

4- The guided ones are far removed from any kind of loss or detriment, because misguidance is the source of loss. «مَن یضلل اللّه فاولئك هم الخاسرون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ذَرَأْنَا لِجَهَنَّمَ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ ۖ لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ بِهَا وَلَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ لَّا يُبْصِرُونَ بِهَا وَلَهُمْ ءَاذَانٌ لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ بِهَآ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ كَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْغَـٰفِلُونَ (۱۷۹)
Certainly We have winnowed out for hell many of the jinn and humans: they have hearts with which they do not understand, they have eyes with which they do not see, they have ears with which they do not hear. They are like cattle; indeed, they are more astray. It is they who are the heedless. (179)

Key Points

Dhara'a (ذَرَأ) from Dhar' (ذَرْء) originally means to scatter and spread, but here it means creation, origination, and manifestation. Question: In this Ayah, the Qur'an states that the creation of many Jinn and humans is for Hell, while in another Ayah, it counts the creation of Jinn and humans as being for worship: «و ما خلقت الجنّ و الانس الاّ لیعبدون» (345). Which one is correct? Answer: The primary goal of creation is monotheism and servitude (to Allah), but the result of the actions of many humans, due to disobedience, rebellion, and persistence in disbelief and obstinacy, is Hell. It is as if they were originally created for Hell. The letter lām (ل) in li-jahannam (لجهنّم) is for expressing the consequence, not the goal. This is like a carpenter whose primary goal in procuring wood is to build beautiful doors and windows from those pieces of wood, but the work ends up with the burning of useless wood in the stove, which is a secondary goal. This point resembles the saying of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, who states: Allah has an angel who proclaims loudly every day: «لِدوا للموت و اجمعوا للفناء و ابنوا للخراب» (346) (Give birth for death, accumulate for annihilation, and build for ruin). That is, the end of birth is death, the end of accumulating wealth is annihilation, and the end of building is ruin. Indeed, for humans who have lost their human identity, there is no place for them except the Fire: «ان تحسب انّ اكثرهم یسمعون او یعقلون ان هم الاّ كالانعام بل اضلّ سبیلاً» (347). Just as they themselves admit that if they had listened to the divine commands and used their intellect, they would no longer be in Hell: «قالوا لو كنّا نسمع او نعقل ما كنّا فى اصحاب السعیر» (348). Man resembles an animal in possessing eyes, ears, and a tongue, but the quality and goal of man's utilization of these blessings must be better and greater; otherwise, he is like an animal, or even lower than it. Man must see the spiritual realm (malakūt) in addition to the physical appearance, and hear the inner, spiritual whispers in addition to the external noises.

The child of Adam is a wondrous mixture, Kneaded from the angel and the animal. If he tends toward the former, he becomes better than the angel; If he tends toward the latter, he becomes lower than the animal.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked: Why did Allah not create all His servants obedient and monotheistic? He replied: If that were the case, reward and punishment would have no meaning, because they would have been compelled and would have had no free will. However, Allah created man with free will, and in addition to intellect and innate nature (fitra), He illuminated the path of his guidance through the teachings of the Prophets and the heavenly Books. He commanded him to obey and forbade him from disobedience, so that the obedient might be distinguished from the disobedient. Although Allah created all the means for obedience and disobedience, He has not commanded or forbidden anything unless man is also able to do the opposite and is not compelled (349). Indeed, the capacity to accept divine responsibility (taklīf) is the sole value and distinguishing feature of man compared to other creatures.

345) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 56 346) Nahj al-Balāghah, Short Saying 132 347) Al-Furqān, 44 348) Al-Mulk, 10 349) Tafsīr Ithnā ‘Asharī; Bihār, Vol. 3, p. 6

Messages

  1. The ultimate fate of many humans and Jinn is Hell. «ذرأنا...»
  2. The Jinn, like humans, have divine responsibility (taklīf), free will, punishment, and reward. «من الجنّ و الانس»
  3. The criterion for humanity is the understanding and acceptance of religious knowledge and duties; otherwise, man is like animals. «اولئك كالانعام»
  4. One who, despite having the ability, does not make proper use of divine blessings is worse than a creature that fundamentally lacks them. «بل هم اضلّ»
  5. Negligent and sightless humans (in indifference, gluttony, lust, being exploited, and deprivation from the pleasure of knowledge) are like cattle. «اولئك كالانعام»
  6. The negligent are worse than animals. «بل هم أضلّ اولئك هم الغافلون»
  7. Humans lacking insight are heedless of the goal, Allah, themselves, their capabilities, the Hereafter, their progeny, the divine signs, the law of Allah, past favors, and their own sins. «اولئك هم الغافلون»
  8. The consignment of many humans to Hell is due to their failure to utilize divine blessings on the path of guidance and perfection, for despite having eyes, ears, and hearts, they have chosen to be negligent. «اولئك هم الغافلون»

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ ٱلْأَسْمَآءُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ فَٱدْعُوهُ بِهَا ۖ وَذَرُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِىٓ أَسْمَـٰٓئِهِۦ ۚ سَيُجْزَوْنَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۸٠)
To Allah belong the Best Names, so supplicate Him by them, and abandon those who commit sacrilege in His names. Soon they shall be requited for what they used to do. (180)

Key Points

Although all the Names and Attributes of Allah are beautiful, and God possesses all perfections which are beyond enumeration, the narrations emphasize 99 Names. These are also mentioned in the books of the Ahl al-Sunnah, such as Sahih Muslim, Bukhari, and Tirmidhi. And whoever calls upon Allah using them, his prayer will be answered (350). And whoever enumerates them will be among the people of Paradise. Of course, the intention is not merely verbal counting or moving the lips, but rather paying attention to, being inspired by, and connecting with these Attributes. These 99 Names are: «اللّه, اله, الواحد, الاحد, الصّمد, الاوّل, الآخر, السّمیع, البصیر, القدیر, القاهر, العلىّ, الاعلى, الباقى, البدیع, البارّ, الاكرم, الظاهر, الباطن, الحىّ, الحكیم, العلیم, الحلیم, الحفیظ, الحقّ, الحسیب, الحمید, الحفىّ, الربّ, الرّحمن, الرّحیم, الذّارء, الرّازق, الرّقیب, الرؤوف, الرّائى, السّلام, المؤمن, المهیمن, العزیز, الجبّار, المتكبّر, السیّد, السُبّوح, الشهید, الصادق, الصانع, الطاهر, العدل, العفو, الغفور, الغنىّ, الغیاث, الفاطر, الفرد, الفتّاح, الفالق, القدیم, الملك, القدّوس, القوىّ, القریب, القیّوم, القابض, الباسط, قاضى الحاجات, المجید, المولى, المنّان, المحیط, المبین, المقیت, المصوّر, الكریم, الكبیر, الكافى, كاشف الضرّ, الوتر, النور, الوهّاب, الناصر, الواسع, الودود, الهادى, الوفىّ, الوكیل, الوارث, البرّ, الباعث, التوّاب, الجلیل, الجواد, الخبیر, الخالق, خیر النّاصرین, الدیّان, الشكور, العظیم, اللطیف, الشافى» (351).

In the Quran, the Names of God reach up to 145. The number 99 mentioned in the narrations is either because some names can be merged and applied to others, or the intention is that these specific names are also found in the Quran, not that the total number is limited to this count. In some verses, the meaning of these names exists. For example, Sadiq (The Truthful) is not explicitly a Name of God in the Quran, but the verse «و من أصدق من اللّه قیلاً» (352)—meaning, "Who is more truthful than Allah in statement?"—has been revealed.

In some narrations and supplications, such as the Du'a Jawshan Kabir, other Names for God have been mentioned. Of course, some of the Beautiful Names of Allah have specific effects, blessings, and distinctions.

Fakhr Razi says: All the Attributes of Allah return to two things: His independence and the need of others for Him (353).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: By Allah, we are the Asma al-Husna (Beautiful Names) (354). Meaning, the Divine Attributes are reflected in us, and we are the path to the true knowledge of God. According to these traditions, the phrase «ذروا الّذین یلحدون» (Leave those who deviate) tells us not to rely on or pay attention to those who deny the virtues of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام.

In another narration, Imam Reza عليه السلام said: We, the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, are the Beautiful Names of God, such that Allah does not accept the deed of anyone unless it is accompanied by our recognition. «نحن واللّه الاسماء الحسنى الّذى لایقبل اللّه من احد عملاً الاّ بمعرفتنا» (355).

The phrase «الاسماء الحسنى» appears four times in the Quran (356). The Asma al-Husna have three manifestations: Divine Attributes, Divine Names, and Divine Saints (357).

Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Whenever difficulties and hardships befall you, seek help from Allah through us. He then recited: «وللّه الاسماء الحسنى فادعوه بها» (358).

According to narrations, whoever knows the Greatest Name (Ism al-A'zam) of Allah, his prayer is answered, and he can influence nature. Bal'am Ba'ura (who was mentioned in verse 175) knew the Divine Greatest Name. But what is the Greatest Name? Some have said it is one of the Divine Names that is hidden from us. Others say the Greatest Name is not truly a word or a name, but rather a perfection and Attribute of Allah, such that whoever can manifest a reflection of it within his own being, his spiritual power reaches a level where he can influence nature. Otherwise, it is not the case that an individual, merely by learning a word and uttering it, can have his prayers answered and affect the world (359).

Imam Reza عليه السلام said: «انّ الخالق لا یوصف الاّ بما وصف به نفسه» (The Creator is not described except by what He has described Himself with). That is, one cannot invent names for God on his own, such as calling Him Afif (Chaste) or Shuja' (Brave), and the like (360).

A name represents the named. The Essence of Allah is sacred, so His Name must also be sacred. Therefore, both the Essence of God must be held transcendent, «سبحانه عمّا یشركون» (361), and His Name must be purified, «سبّح اسم ربّك الاعلى» (362). Thus, placing the names of others alongside the Name of God is not permissible, and one cannot say: "In the Name of God and the creation."

Martyr Mutahhari says: The Names of Allah do not function merely as symbols; rather, they represent an attribute and a reality among the realities of His Sacred Essence (363).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, regarding this verse, said: Do not name others with the Divine Names. Then, concerning the phrase «یلحدون فى اسمائه» (they deviate concerning His Names), He said: «یضعونها فى غیر مواضعها» (They place them in inappropriate contexts). By doing this, they become polytheists, and for this reason, Allah said: «و ما یؤمن اكثرهم باللّه و هم مشركون» (364) (The faith of most people is accompanied by polytheism) (365).

350) Tafasir Al-Mizan and Namuneh 351) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Nur al-Thaqalayn; Tawhid al-Saduq 352) An-Nisa, 122 353) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi 354) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 143 355) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 356) Al-Isra, 110; Ta Ha, 8; Al-Hashr, 24, and this verse 357) Tafsir Furqan 358) Bihar, Vol. 91, p. 5 359) Tafsir Namuneh 360) Tafsir Furqan 361) At-Tawbah, 31 362) Al-A'la, 1 363) Ashna'i ba Quran (Familiarity with the Quran), p. 14 364) Yusuf, 106 365) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 324

Messages

  1. The word «اللّه» is the axis of all Divine Names. «وللّه الاسماء الحسنى» (The word «اللّه» encompasses all Divine Attributes).

  2. All good qualities belong to God; others must turn to Him to attain al-Husna (the beautiful). «للّه الاسماء الحسنى»

  3. Invitation and supplication must be directed toward beauties and goodness. «الحسنى فادعوه بها»

  4. The Divine Names are His signs; one can reach Him through the signs. «للّه الاسماء الحسنى فادعوه بها»

  5. Belief that Allah possesses all perfections and is far removed from all defects compels man toward supplication and praise. «فادعوه بها»

  6. The cure for heedlessness is the remembrance of God. In the previous verse, we read: «هم الغافلون» (They are the heedless ones); in this verse, we read: «فادعوه بها» (So call upon Him by them).

  7. Islam pays attention to beautiful naming and avoiding deviation in nomenclature. «الاسماء الحسنى... یلحدون فى أسمائه»

  8. We must show reaction toward the deviants and those who commit ilhad (deviation). «ذروا الّذین یلحدون»

  9. The path of truth is innate, and deviation from it is contrary to the path of nature (fitrah). (Remembering Allah with a description that is not worthy of Him is deviation and departure from truth and moderation.) «الّذین یُلحِدون» (Ilhad means deviation).

  10. Attributing the Names and Attributes of God to others is ilhad, and attributing the names of others to God is also ilhad. «یُلحدون فى أسمائه»

Ayah

وَمِمَّنْ خَلَقْنَآ أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَبِهِۦ يَعْدِلُونَ (۱۸۱)
Among those We have created are a nation who guide by the truth and do justice thereby. (181)

Key Points

It is mentioned in narrations that the intent of this Ayah is the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophet علیهم السلام and their followers (Shi'a). [366] In another narration, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The intent is a group from My Ummah whose entire rulings, commands, grants, and transactions are based upon truth (Haqq). [367] Imam Ali علیه السلام, while referring to the various groups that will emerge in the Islamic Ummah, said: The only group that is worthy of salvation are the followers (Shi'a) of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. [368]

366) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan 367) Tafasir Furqan and Durr al-Manthur 368) Tafsir Namuneh; Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 44

Messages

1- Not all people have cultivated within themselves the capacity to accept guidance and justice; rather, only a group possesses this quality. «و ممّن خلقنا امّة»

2- Guiding others through false methods and means, or guiding towards falsehood (Batil), is forbidden. «یهدون بالحقّ»

3- Guidance, governance, and judgment must be in the path of the "Truth" (Haqq). «یهدون بالحقّ و به یعدلون»

4- Those who possess greater worth are those who, besides accepting guidance, are also concerned with establishing a system of truth (Haqq). Indeed, mere personal knowledge and action are not enough; spreading the truth is also important. («به یعدلون», meaning «به یحكمون» [by it they judge/rule]).

5- Society is in need of a group that guides and judges according to the truth. (The entire Ayah)

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۸۲)
As for those who deny Our signs, We will draw them imperceptibly [into ruin], whence they do not know. (182)
وَأُمْلِى لَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ كَيْدِى مَتِينٌ (۱۸۳)
And I will grant them respite, for My devising is indeed sure. (183)

Key Points

Istidrāj (gradual entrapment), which is one of the divine traditions concerning the deniers and the affluent, derives from darajah (degree/step) meaning gradual enfolding. [369] This tradition is also mentioned in Ayah 44 of Sūrat Al-Qalam. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Those who live in comfort and ease should be mindful of the danger of Istidrāj, lest blessings become a means for their slumber and negligence. [370] He also said: Whoever lives amidst abundant gifts and resources and does not consider it a punishment of Istidrāj has remained oblivious to the signs of danger. [371] Yes, Allah grants respite, but He does not neglect (or abandon). As the elders have said: «انّ اللّه یمهل و لایهمل» (Indeed, Allah grants respite but does not neglect). Imam Ali عليه السلام said: When Allah intends good for a servant, He chastises him when he commits a sin so that he may repent. But when evil and harm are decreed for him due to his deeds, He bestows a blessing upon him when he sins, so that he forgets repentance and seeking forgiveness, and continues in sin, which is in reality a type of hidden and gradual punishment. As Allah says: «سنستدرجهم من حیث لایعلمون» (We will gradually lead them [to ruin] from where they do not perceive). [372] The divine scheme (kayd), granting respite, and long life as a form of Istidrāj have been mentioned repeatedly in the verses of the Quran, such as these verses: «لا تحسبنّ الّذین كفروا انّما نُملى لهم خیرٌ لانفسهم انّما نُملى لهم لیزدادوا اثماً» [373] The disbelievers should not think that the respite We grant them is good for them; rather, We grant them respite so that they may increase their sins. «فذرهم فى غمرتهم حتّى حین» [374] (So leave them in their overwhelming ignorance for a time). And also the verses: 196 of Sūrat Āl ‘Imrān, 44 of Sūrat Al-An‘ām, 55 of Sūrat At-Tawbah, and 55 and 56 of Sūrat Al-Mu’minūn. Sometimes divine blessings, Allah’s concealments (of faults), and people’s praises are all means of pride and distraction, and in a way, Istidrāj. [375] Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Those who distort (the truth) by justifying their actions, and who obey a tyrant every day, are subject to the law of Istidrāj. [376] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah warns His friends through hardships, and leaves the unworthy in comfort. [377]

369) Mufradāt Rāghib 370) Tafsīr Nūr Ath-Thaqalayn 371) Tafsīr Namūneh 372) Tafāsīr Namūneh and Burhān 373) Āl ‘Imrān, 178 374) Al-Mu’minūn, 54 375) Tafsīr Namūneh 376) Tafsīr Nūr Ath-Thaqalayn 377) Tafsīr Namūneh

Messages

1- Denying the clear divine signs leads to gradual downfall and hidden destruction. «و الّذین كذّبوا بآیاتنا سنسستدرجهم من حیث لا یعلمون» (God’s rod makes no sound).

2- Granting respite to people is one of Allah’s traditions, so that everyone may reach the result of the path they have chosen, and the doors remain open to all, giving them both the opportunity for transgression and the chance for repentance and rectification. «سنستدرجهم»

3- The downfall of man is often step-by-step and gradual. «سنستدرجهم من حیث لا یعلمون»

4- Life and lifespan are in the hand of Allah, and the sinner has no escape from His power. «و اُملى لهم»

5- Allah gives the disbelievers the opportunity for repentance and rectification, but they are unworthy. «و اُملى لهم»

6- Blessings are not always a sign of Allah’s favor; sometimes they are a prelude to His sudden wrath. «اُملى - كیدى»

7- The negligent affluent are dealing with Allah’s plan. «اُملى لهم انّ كیدى» (Just as kayd (scheme) is a hidden action, Istidrāj is also a hidden punishment).

8- The danger of pride and negligence is so great that Allah has addressed it using three successive expressions: «سنستدرجهم - اُملى لهم - كیدى»

9- The divine plan and scheme are invincible. «انّ كیدى متین»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ ۗ مَا بِصَاحِبِهِم مِّن جِنَّةٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱۸٤)
Have they not reflected that there is no madness in their companion, [and that] he is just a manifest warner? (184)

Key Points

The word jinnah (جِنّة) means madness (junūn) and originally means covering or concealment. It is as if, during madness, a covering is placed over the intellect. The Prophet had not yet migrated to Medina when, one night on Mount Safa until late into the night, He invited people to Allah and warned and advised them about divine punishments. The polytheists said: "Our companion has gone mad, that such words flow from his tongue." The above verses were revealed. They were accusing someone of madness whom they had named Muhammad al-Amin (the Trustworthy) before his prophethood.

Messages

1- Slander and insolence are not the methods or actions of people of thought and intellect. «أولم یتفكّروا»

2- The Prophet is the companion and interlocutor of the people. If He were mad, why did they not say so before, and why did they converse with Him for years? «صاحبهم»

3- In a corrupt system, those who speak the truth are accused of madness. «من جِنّة»

4- Prophets have been subjected to accusations of sorcery or madness by their opponents. «من جِنّة» Just as we read in another verse: «كذلك ما أتى الّذین من قبلهم من رسول الاّ قالوا ساحر أو مجنون» (378)

5- In the method of propagation, education, and audience analysis, one must be more of a warner (nadhīr) than a bearer of good news (bashīr) when educating the heedless. «ان هو الاّ نذیر»

6- For arrogant and sleepy people, we must speak clearly and openly. «مبین»

(378) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 52

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَنظُرُوا۟ فِى مَلَكُوتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ مِن شَىْءٍ وَأَنْ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ قَدِ ٱقْتَرَبَ أَجَلُهُمْ ۖ فَبِأَىِّ حَدِيثٍۭ بَعْدَهُۥ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۸٥)
Have they not contemplated the dominions of the heavens and the earth, and whatever things Allah has created, and that maybe their time has already drawn near? So what discourse will they believe after this?! (185)

Key Points

The term Malakūt (Dominion) is derived from Mulk (Sovereignty), meaning governance and ownership. Here, it refers to the absolute rule of Allah over existence.

Messages

1- A thoughtful, deep, and reflective perspective is fruitful and effective. (Attention and reflection upon the inner reality of existence and its connection to the Creator links man to Allah, just as Monotheism (Tawhid) and Prophethood must be understood through intellect and thought, not through blind imitation.) «أولم یتفكّروا، أولم ینظروا»

2- Monotheism (Tawhid) is the source and foundation of Prophethood, and careful attention to the Dominion (Malakūt) of existence clarifies that this system is not abandoned. «أولم ینظروا ... فباىّ حدیث»

3- Most misfortunes are due to heedlessness regarding the remembrance of death. «عسى ان یكون قداقترب أجلهم» (The remembrance of death is effective in reducing obstinacy and calls people to utilize the opportunity and believe before death arrives.)

4- The creation of no particle is without purpose. «من شى ء»

5- The Quran and the Divine verses are the best book and speech, and there is no excuse for not accepting them. «فبأىّ حدیث بعده»

6- Whoever does not believe in the Quran and its teachings will not believe in any other guiding speech. «فبأىّ حدیث بعده یؤمنون»

7- Careful attention to the system of creation (Takwīn) leads to belief in the system of legislation (Tashrī‘). «أولم ینظروا... بعده یؤمنون»

Ayah

مَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَلَا هَادِىَ لَهُۥ ۚ وَيَذَرُهُمْ فِى طُغْيَـٰنِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (۱۸٦)
Whomever Allah leads astray has no guide, and He leaves them bewildered in their rebellion. (186)

Messages

1- The recompense for those who do not listen to the warnings of the prophets and do not reflect upon their sayings is that they become afflicted by Divine wrath and are left to their own devices. «فبأىّ حدیث بعده یؤمنون... و یذرهم»

2- Misguidance (Idhlal) is the work of Allah, but the groundwork for it is the intention and action of man himself, which causes his heart to rust and reject Divine guidance. «یضلل اللّه» Other verses also state this reality, including: «وما یُضلّ به الاّ الفاسقین» [379] and «بل ران على قلوبهم ماكانوا یكسبون» [380]

3- The transgression and rebellion of people is not compulsory, and the misguidance of humans is the result of their own choice. «طغیانهم» (Man was not created evil from the beginning.)

4- The tyrants (Taghuts) are bewildered. «فى طغیانهم یعمهون»

5- If Divine guidance is absent, the misguidance and bewilderment of man is continuous and ongoing. «یعمهون»

Ayah

يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلسَّاعَةِ أَيَّانَ مُرْسَىٰهَا ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّى ۖ لَا يُجَلِّيهَا لِوَقْتِهَآ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ ثَقُلَتْ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ لَا تَأْتِيكُمْ إِلَّا بَغْتَةً ۗ يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ كَأَنَّكَ حَفِىٌّ عَنْهَا ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۸۷)
They question you concerning the Hour, when will it set in? Say, ‘Its knowledge is only with my Lord: none except Him shall manifest it at its time. It will weigh heavy on the heavens and the earth. It will not overtake you but suddenly.’ They ask you as if you were in the know of it. Say, ‘Its knowledge is only with Allah, but most people do not know.’ (187)

Key Points

The disbelievers of Quraysh sent a group to the Jewish scholars to learn difficult topics and questions to ask the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, hoping that He would be unable to answer and thus be defeated. One of those questions was determining the time of the occurrence of the Resurrection (Qiyāmah). [381] As-Sā'ah (The Hour) is the time the Resurrection begins, and Al-Qiyāmah (The Resurrection) is the time of reckoning, reward, and recompense. Mursā [382] means establishment and occurrence. Jibāl Rāsiyāt means firm and stable mountains. Hafiyy means pursuit and investigation. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله being hafiyy means that it is as if the Prophet has inquired about and investigated the coming of the Day of Resurrection from Allah and knows it. The heaviness (gravity/burden) of the Resurrection upon the heavens and the earth is perhaps due to the disruption of the celestial bodies, the darkening of the sun, and the overturning of the earth. And perhaps the intent behind the heaviness of the Resurrection is the severity of the punishments on that Day. In Du'a Kumayl, we read: «وهذا ما لاتقوم لها السموات و الارض» (And this is what the heavens and the earth cannot bear), meaning the heavens and the earth do not have the strength to endure the torment of Hellfire. They asked the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله about the appearance and advent of Hadrat Qā'im عليه السلام. The Prophet said: «مثلهُ مثل الساعة» (His likeness is like The Hour); His rising is sudden, like the establishment of the Resurrection. Then He recited this Ayah: «لایجلّیها لوقتها الاّ هو ثقلت فى السموات والارض لاتأتیكم الاّ بغتةً». This tradition has also been narrated from Imam Reza عليه السلام. [383]

381) Tafsir Burhan 382) Tafsir Maraghi 383) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn

Messages

  1. People repeatedly questioned the Prophet, sometimes asking about the time of the Resurrection's occurrence. «یسئلونك» (The present tense verb indicates continuity and persistence).

  2. No one knows the time of the establishment of the Resurrection except Allah. «انّما علمها عند ربّى» Yes, not knowing the time of the Resurrection's establishment is better for human preparedness.

  3. The Resurrection is unpredictable and will occur suddenly when it is not expected. «بغتةً» (Baghatah is used in a case where a person does not even have the possibility or guess of it in their mind).

  4. The Resurrection is extremely burdensome and heavy, even upon the heavens and the earth, let alone upon human beings. «ثقلت فى السموات و الارض»
