  5. Regarding what we do not know, we should not fear saying, "I do not know." In this Ayah, the Prophet is commanded twice to say: «قل انّما علمها عند ربّى... قل انّما علمها عند اللّه» And in another place, He says: I do not know my destiny or yours. «قل ما كنت بدعاً من الرسل و ما ادرى ما یفعل بى و لا بكم ان اتّبع الاّ ما یوحى الىّ» [384]

  6. Not knowing the specifics and details of the Resurrection does not harm the principle of prophethood or the Hereafter. No one is aware of the time and place of their own death, but this does not justify denying the principle of death itself. «انّما علمها عند ربى»

384) Al-Ahqaf, 9

Ayah

قُل لَّآ أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِى نَفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَلَوْ كُنتُ أَعْلَمُ ٱلْغَيْبَ لَٱسْتَكْثَرْتُ مِنَ ٱلْخَيْرِ وَمَا مَسَّنِىَ ٱلسُّوٓءُ ۚ إِنْ أَنَا۠ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۸۸)
Say, ‘I have no control over any benefit for myself, nor [over] any harm except what Allah may wish. Had I known the Unseen, I would have acquired much good, and no ill would have befallen me. I am only a warner and bearer of good news to a people who have faith.’ (188)

Key Points

In the Quran and narrations, matters have been stated which indicate that the Prophets and Divine Awliya (saints) possessed knowledge of the unseen (Ilm al-Ghayb). Similarly, verses and narrations, like this very verse, are found which indicate that they do not possess knowledge of the unseen! Reconciling these two categories of verses and narrations is possible in several ways:

A: Where it states that they do not know the knowledge of the unseen, the intention is that those great figures do not know the unseen independently (from themselves). And where it says they do know the unseen, it means they know it through the will, inspiration, and revelation of Allah. This is similar to when we say: "That city does not have oil," or "It does have oil." Our intention in saying it does not have oil is that its land is not oil-rich, meaning it does not inherently possess oil. And the intention in saying it does have oil is that oil reaches it via pipes, cars, ships, and trains.

B: The knowledge of the unseen (Ilm al-Ghayb) is of two types. The Prophets know the greater part of it, such as the news that the Quran revealed to the Prophet of Islam as the unseen: «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك» [385]. And a portion of the knowledge of the unseen is exclusive to Allah, and no one has knowledge of it, such as the knowledge of the time the Resurrection will be established. Therefore, where they do not know, it means they do not know the portion exclusive to Allah; and where they do know, it means they know the other portion.

C: The audiences of those great figures were different. Some were prone to exaggeration (ghuluww) and hyperbole, and the Prophets and the Pure Imams علیهم السلام would tell them: "We do not know the unseen," so that they would not exaggerate concerning them. And some were deficient in their recognition (ma'rifah) of those great figures, so the Prophet and the Imam would present a corner of their knowledge of the unseen for their growth.

D: The intention behind not having knowledge of the unseen is the lack of immediate mental presence (of that knowledge). However, according to the narrations, there is a pillar of light for the Infallible Imams (Ma'sumeen) which, by referring to it, they gain awareness of everything. This is similar to a person who says, "I do not know so-and-so's phone number," but has a directory that allows him to know all the numbers by referring to it.

E: Awareness of the unseen is not always a sign of perfection; rather, sometimes it is a deficiency. For example, on the night when Hadrat Ali علیه السلام slept in the place of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, if He had known that He would not be in danger, it would not have been considered a perfection for Him, because in that case, everyone would have been willing to sleep in the Prophet's place. Here, perfection lies in not knowing.

F: Allah does not bestow upon them the knowledge of the unseen when the expectation involves personal profit or loss, similar to the current verse under discussion. However, in cases where the purpose of the knowledge of the unseen is the guidance and instruction of the people, Allah informs them, just as Jesus علیه السلام told his companions: "I can tell you what you people have stored in your houses." «و ماتدّخرون فى بیوتكم» [386] Some commentators have said that the people of Mecca told the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله: "If you have a connection with God, why are you not informed about the future rise and fall of prices so that you can gain profit and benefit, and avoid what is harmful to you?" It was then that the above verses were revealed. [387]

385) Hud, 49 386) Al Imran, 49 387) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Namuneh

Messages

1- Whoever has greater faith is more submissive to the Divine duty and will, and feels his own helplessness more acutely. «لا أملك لنفسى ...»

2- We must attribute all profits and losses to the will and desire of Allah. «الاّ ما شاء اللّه»

3- The Prophet does not know the unseen independently or for his personal life. News of the unseen is given to Him in His capacity as a Prophet, and that too, only from Allah. «لو كنت ...»

4- The time of the establishment of the Resurrection is among the matters of the unseen. «یسئلونك عن الساعة... و لو كنت اعلم الغیب»

5- Knowing the future is the basis for welfare and comfort, and ignorance of it is the basis for loss and difficulties. «لو كنت اعلم الغیب لاستكثرت...»

6- The life of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, like that of other people, was mixed with hardship and difficulty. «و ما مسّنى السوء»

7- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is a bearer of glad tidings and a warner for all humanity, but only the believers are affected (by it). «نذیر و بشیر لقوم یؤمنون»

Ayah

۞ هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ وَجَعَلَ مِنْهَا زَوْجَهَا لِيَسْكُنَ إِلَيْهَا ۖ فَلَمَّا تَغَشَّىٰهَا حَمَلَتْ حَمْلًا خَفِيفًا فَمَرَّتْ بِهِۦ ۖ فَلَمَّآ أَثْقَلَت دَّعَوَا ٱللَّهَ رَبَّهُمَا لَئِنْ ءَاتَيْتَنَا صَـٰلِحًا لَّنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (۱۸۹)
It is He who created you from a single soul, and made from it its mate, that he might find comfort with her. So when he had covered her, she bore a light burden and passed [some time] with it. When she had grown heavy, they both invoked Allah, their Lord: ‘If You give us a healthy [child], we will be surely grateful.’ (189)

Messages

1- The essential nature of man and woman is the same. «وجعل منها زوجها»

2- Marriage and a spouse are sources of spiritual and life tranquility, removing psychological unrest. «لیسكن الیها»

3- The foundation of life is based on intimacy and affection, not discord and schism. «لیسكن الیها»

4- Sexual matters should be expressed through allusion. «تغشّاها»

5- Intimacy between husband and wife must be in private. «تغشّاها»

6- Fetal growth is gradual, allowing the woman time to prepare. «خفیفاً، أثقلت»

7- Until a person's burden becomes heavy, he does not realize his responsibility. «فلما أثقلت دعوا»

Difficulties are a means of turning attention to Allah, fostering spiritual and moral states, and preparing the human heart and conscience. This is similar to pregnant women who, being unaware of the Divine decree, live in constant anxiety and are receptive to all forms of advice and guidance.

8- Parents feel responsibility for the destiny of their child. «دَعَوا»

9- Man naturally desires the continuation of his lineage and offspring. «آتیتنا»

10- We must consider the child as a gift from Allah, not merely the result of other means or ourselves. «آتیتنا»

11- Sexual intimacy is not solely for pleasure and desire, but for the survival and continuation of righteous progeny. «صالحاً»

12- Man naturally seeks righteousness and reform, not indifference and corruption. «آتینا صالحاً» (Give us a righteous one) and He did not say: «آتیتنا ولداً» (Give us a child).

13- For the righteousness and proper upbringing of the child, one must take action and seek assistance from Allah before the child's birth. «فلمّا اثقلت دعوا اللّه ربّهما لئن آتیتنا صالحا»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّآ ءَاتَىٰهُمَا صَـٰلِحًا جَعَلَا لَهُۥ شُرَكَآءَ فِيمَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمَا ۚ فَتَعَـٰلَى ٱللَّهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (۱۹٠)
Then when He gave them a healthy [child], they ascribed partners to Him in what He had given them. Exalted is Allah above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]! (190)
أَيُشْرِكُونَ مَا لَا يَخْلُقُ شَيْـًٔا وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ (۱۹۱)
Do they ascribe [to Him] partners that create nothing and have been created themselves, (191)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- The child belongs to both the father and the mother. «آتاهما»

2- Allah grants a worthy and righteous child; it is we who cause the deviation of the children. «آتاهما صالحاً»

3- Humans become committed during hardships, «لنكوننّ من الشاكرین», but they are unfaithful when ease comes. «جعلا له شركاء»

4- A child is a divine gift. Parents should not attribute an independent role to themselves, the doctor, or others regarding the child's form, appearance, or health, as that is a type of polytheism (Shirk). «جعلا له شركاء»

5- The first condition for a deity (Ma'būd) is His power to create. In the world of science and industry, millions of people are still idolaters. «أیشركون ما لا یخلق»

Ayahs

وَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ لَهُمْ نَصْرًا وَلَآ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَنصُرُونَ (۱۹۲)
and can neither help them, nor help themselves? (192)
وَإِن تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ لَا يَتَّبِعُوكُمْ ۚ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْكُمْ أَدَعَوْتُمُوهُمْ أَمْ أَنتُمْ صَـٰمِتُونَ (۱۹۳)
If you call them to guidance, they will not follow you: it is the same to you whether you call them or whether you are silent. (193)

Key Points

The meaning of "تدعوهم الى الهدى" (if you call them to guidance) might be that if you ask them to guide you, they will not respond. In any case, although the discussion concerns idols that lack intellect and life, the verse uses the plural form for beings possessing intellect (جمع ذوى العقول) to expose the false notion of the polytheists who considered them intelligent, or even above intelligence, and thus worshipped them and sought help from them. The Quran has repeatedly, using various expressions such as: "لایستطیعون لهم نصراً" (they cannot grant them victory) and "لایملكون لانفسهم نفعاً" (they possess no power to benefit themselves) (388), prohibited us from attributing independent power and ability to things or individuals.

(388) Ra'd, 16

Messages

1- Things that have neither the ability to grant aid nor the ability to defend themselves are unworthy of worship. "و لا یستطیعون ..."

2- Even the worship of other human beings is unjustifiable, let alone the worship of objects and beings inferior to humans, which possess no ability for guidance or support. "اِن تدعوهم الى الهُدى لایتّبعوكم"

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ عِبَادٌ أَمْثَالُكُمْ ۖ فَٱدْعُوهُمْ فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لَكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱۹٤)
Indeed those whom you invoke besides Allah are creatures like you. So invoke them: they should answer you, if you are truthful. (194)

Key Points

It is possible that the intent of 'ibād (servants) refers to human beings who were presented as gods, such as Prophet Jesus عليه السلام, or angels. It is also possible that the intent refers to the idols themselves, which the idolaters consider to be ilāh (deities). If we take it to mean creatures, it includes anything that is worshipped instead of Allah.

Messages

1- Worship requires proof, and the worshipped (Ma'būd) must be superior and higher than the worshipper ('ābid). Worshipping creatures or humans like ourselves has neither proof nor distinction. «عباد أمثالكم» (servants like yourselves). Iqbal Lahori says regarding this:

Adam, due to lack of insight, served Adam He possessed a jewel, but offered it to Qubad and Jamshid

Meaning, due to the habit of servitude, he is lower than dogs I have never seen a dog bow its head before another dog

2- A deity is worthy of worship only if He answers His servant, fulfills his needs, and helps him grow, and if there is a reciprocal relationship between him and the worshipped. «فلیَستَجیبوا لكم»

3- The failure of the worshipped deities to respond to your needs is a sign of their weakness, incompetence, and falsehood. «فلیَستَجیبوا لكم ان كنتم صادقین»

Ayah

أَلَهُمْ أَرْجُلٌ يَمْشُونَ بِهَآ ۖ أَمْ لَهُمْ أَيْدٍ يَبْطِشُونَ بِهَآ ۖ أَمْ لَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ يُبْصِرُونَ بِهَآ ۖ أَمْ لَهُمْ ءَاذَانٌ يَسْمَعُونَ بِهَا ۗ قُلِ ٱدْعُوا۟ شُرَكَآءَكُمْ ثُمَّ كِيدُونِ فَلَا تُنظِرُونِ (۱۹٥)
Do they have any feet to walk with? Do they have any hands to grasp with? Do they have any eyes to see with? Do they have any ears to hear with? Say, ‘Invoke your partners [that you ascribe to Allah] and try out your stratagems against me without granting me any respite. (195)

Key Points

"یَبطِشون" (yabṭishūn) is derived from "بَطش" (baṭsh), meaning to seize with power and might. This Ayah is a reprimand to those who set up partners for Allah who are more helpless than they themselves are, because they themselves have the power to walk, see, hear, and act, but those lifeless statues do not even possess this power. And if those deities were asked to undertake any task, they would have no power and would be incapable. So why do they continue to worship them?

Messages

1- The Divine Leader must possess the power and courage to challenge (taḥaddī) and invite to mutual imprecation (mubāhalah), and assert that the forces of falsehood should implement whatever dangerous schemes they have, so that their helplessness may be proven. «قل ادعوا ...»

2- Those who have hands and feet do not have the power to help the polytheists, let alone the partners who are handless and footless and are not even up to your own standard. «ألهم ارجل یمشون بها...»

3- The polytheists refuse to obey the Prophet because they say: He is a human like us. But they submit to the worship of idols that are not even like them, but are inferior. «قل ادعوا شركائكم...»

4- The method of argumentation (iḥtijāj) and posing questions accompanied by criticism, reprimand, challenge (taḥaddī), and demanding confrontation is among the best methods of propagation and education. (Considering this Ayah and the preceding Ayahs)

Ayah

إِنَّ وَلِـِّۧىَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى نَزَّلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۖ وَهُوَ يَتَوَلَّى ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱۹٦)
My guardian is indeed Allah who sent down the Book, and He takes care of the righteous. (196)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the weakness of false deities was explained. This verse proceeds to introduce Allah.

Being righteous (Sālih) holds a high status with Allah. All the Prophets were among the righteous, «كلّ من الصالحین» (389), and they wished to join them, just as Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) said: «توفّنى مسلماً و الحقنى بالصالحین» (390). We also send salutations upon them at the end of every prayer: «السلام علینا و على عباداللّه الصالحین».

389) An'am, 85

390) Yusuf, 101

Messages

1- Allah supported and safeguarded the Prophet through the Qur'an. «انّ ولىّ اللّه الّذى نزّل الكتاب»

2- The righteous' attention to Allah's guardianship is the cause of their steadfastness and fearlessness. «ثمّ كیدون فلاتنظرون انّ ولىّ اللّه...»

3- The relationship between Allah and the believing person is very close. «ولىّ» (The word walī (ولىّ) originally means being continuous and following one after the other.)

4- The revelation of the Divine Book is a manifestation of Divine Guardianship (Wilāyah). «ولىّ... نزّل»

5- The true object of worship is He Who both provides a specific program, «نزّل الكتاب», and supervises the followers in its implementation. «یتولّى الصالحین» (The issuance of law must be accompanied by support for righteous and worthy implementers.)

6- Do not fear loneliness, for Allah is the Guardian (Walī) of the righteous and has promised His help. «انّ ولىّ اللّه... یتولّى الصالحین»

7- Human problems stem either from lacking a program or lacking a guardian, and the believer lacks neither of these two problems. «نزّل الكتاب... یتولّى الصالحین»

8- Allah's Guardianship (Wilāyah) over His righteous friends is perpetual and universal. «یتولّى الصالحین» And the disbeliever is outside the scope of Divine support and aid. «و انّ الكافرین لا مولى لهم» (391)

9- Righteous and virtuous believers do not reach a dead end. «یتولّى الصالحین» It is also stated in another verse: «اللّه ولىّ الّذین آمنوا یخرجهم من الظلمات الى النّور» (392). Allah is the Guardian of the believers and delivers them from darkness into light.

10- Meritocracy is a method approved by the Qur'an. «یتولّى الصالحین»

391) Muhammad, 11

392) Baqarah, 257

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَكُمْ وَلَآ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَنصُرُونَ (۱۹۷)
Those whom you invoke besides Him can neither help you, nor help themselves. (197)
وَإِن تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ لَا يَسْمَعُوا۟ ۖ وَتَرَىٰهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ (۱۹۸)
If you call them to guidance, they will not hear. You see them facing you, but they do not see.’ (198)

Key Points

It is inferred from the totality of the previous verses that the object of worship (Ma'būd) and the Lord (Rabb) must:

A: Be the Creator and Owner. «أیشركون ما لا یخلق شیئاً و هم یخلقون» B: Be a Helper and Supporter. «لا یستطیعون نصركم» C: Respond to requests and supplications. «سواء علیكم أدعوتموهم أم ...» D: Be capable and powerful. «أم لهم أید یبطشون بها ...» E: Be Hearing and Seeing. «أم لهم آذان یسمعون بها، أم لهم أعین یبصرون بها» F: Have the power to neutralize the enemy's plot. «ادعوا شركاءكم ثمّ كیدون» G: Present a Book and a Law. «نزل الكتاب» H: Support the good and the righteous. «یتولّى الصالحین»

Messages

1- The goal of the idolaters is to seek help from the idols, which the Quran rejects. «و الّذین تدعون من دونه لایستطیعون نصركم»

2- Idols and non-divine objects of worship should at least be immune from calamities, but they are not. «و لا انفسهم ینصرون»

3- Objects of worship lacking consciousness, will, and power are not worthy of adoration. «لایستطیعون، لا یسمعوا، لا یبصرون»

Ayah

خُذِ ٱلْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِٱلْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ (۱۹۹)
Adopt [a policy of] excusing [the faults of people], bid what is right, and turn away from the ignorant. (199)

Key Points

Although several meanings have been stated for the word al-'Afw (عفو): the middle ground and moderation, accepting the excuse of the wrongdoer and forgiving him, and making things easy; apparently, the first meaning is intended in this Ayah. "خُذ العفو" (Take the 'Afw) means having 'Afw under one's control and using it appropriately. Therefore, in some cases, one must deal decisively, as in "و لاتأخذكم بهما رأفة فى دین اللّه" (and let no pity for them detain you in the religion of Allah) [393].

Just as "اعرض عن الجاهلین" (turn away from the ignorant) does not mean leaving the ignorant and enemies free and unrestrained, but rather means not being with them and not inclining toward them. However, sometimes admonition, reminder, or confrontation with them is necessary. "فاعرض عنهم و عظهم" (So turn away from them and admonish them) [394].

This Ayah, despite its simplicity and conciseness, encompasses all ethical principles: both individual ethics ("عفو" - 'Afw), and social ethics ("وأمر بالعُرف" - and enjoin the good); dealing with a friend ('Afw), and dealing with an enemy ("أعرض" - turn away); verbal action ("وأمر" - and enjoin), and practical action ("أعرض" - turn away); positive action ("خُذ" - take), and negative action ("أعرض" - turn away); for the leader, and for the community; for that time, and for this time.

As Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: There is no Ayah in the Quran more comprehensive than this Ayah regarding noble morals (Makārim al-Akhlāq). [395]

Of course, there is no doubt that 'Afw (forgiveness) applies to personal matters, not to the rights of the people (Ḥaqq an-Nās) or public treasury (Bayt al-Māl).

When this Ayah was revealed, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله asked Jibril (Gabriel) for an explanation and the method of acting upon this Ayah. Jibril brought the message: "تَعفُو عمّن ظلمك، تُعطى مَن حَرمك، تَصِل مَن قطعك" [396]. Forgive the one who wronged you, bestow upon the one who deprived you, and establish connection with the one who cut ties with you.

This entire Surah is an invitation to moderation, using various expressions: moderation in rights (Ayah 29), in consumption (Ayah 31), in adornment (Ayah 32), in worship (Ayah 56), in house construction (Ayah 74), in economics (Ayah 85). Furthermore, valuable points regarding the method of implementing truth and justice in the community of Prophet Musa عليه السلام and the community of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله are mentioned in Ayahs 157 and 181.

393) An-Noor, 2 394) An-Nisa, 63 395) Tafsir Furqan 396) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan; Bihar, Vol. 75, p. 114

Messages

  1. One must always adopt the path of moderation and the middle way. "خذ العفو"
  2. Obligation must be commensurate with one's capacity, not more. "خذ العفو" (If al-'Afw means the middle ground).
  3. Merely being good is not enough; goodness must be promoted and enjoined in society. "وأمر بالعرف"
  4. The addressee of the Ayah is not only the Prophet; every Muslim, every preacher, and every social reformer must deal with stubborn and ignorant babblers by turning away, and must overlook and be patient in the face of insults and accusations, rather than becoming entangled and wrestling with them. "و اعرض..."
  5. One must both enjoin the good (Ma'rūf), and the method of enjoining must itself be good and praiseworthy. "و أمر بالعُرف"
  6. The intent of al-Jāhilīn (the ignorant) is the unwise, not the uneducated. "واعرض عن الجاهلین" In the culture of the Quran and narrations, ignorance (Jahl) is usually contrasted with intellect ('Aql), just as we have the title "العقل و الجهل" (Intellect and Ignorance) in narrations.
  7. In the method of turning away, 'Afw, and forgiveness, one must not pay attention to the demands and statements of the ignorant that are contrary to the public interest; rather, one must stand firm and steadfast. "اعرض عن الجاهلین"

Ayah

وَإِمَّا يَنزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ نَزْغٌ فَٱسْتَعِذْ بِٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۲٠٠)
Should a temptation from Satan disturb you, invoke the protection of Allah; indeed He is all-hearing, all-knowing. (200)

Key Points

"Nazgh" (نَزغ) means entering into an affair with the intention of corruption and incitement. In this Surah, the story of Satan's temptation of Prophet Adam (عليه السلام) is mentioned from Ayah 16 to 27, and warnings about Satan's temptations are also given towards the end of the Surah. In the previous Ayah, there was an invitation to disregard the ignorant. The Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) asked Gabriel: How can one endure [such provocation] despite the anger? This Ayah was revealed. [397] Of course, seeking refuge is not merely by uttering the phrase "A'udhu billah" (I seek refuge in Allah), but rather by establishing a spiritual connection, relying [on Him], and submitting oneself to God. Although all Prophets are infallible (ma'sum), the devils do not refrain from interfering and tempting even them. As this Ayah states: «و امّا ینزغنّك من الشیطان نزغ» (And if a temptation from Satan should tempt you), or the Ayah: «وكذلك جعلنا لكلّ نبىّ عَدواً شیاطین الانس والجن» [398] (And thus We have made for every prophet an enemy - devils from mankind and jinn), which indicates the opponents of the Prophets from among the jinn and mankind. But the value of the Prophets' work lies precisely in the fact that, despite human instincts and satanic temptations, they remain righteous, pious, and far removed from sin. In reality, Satan intends to mislead all human beings through his temptations, but he is defeated by the sincere ones (al-mukhlaseen). «قال فبعزّتك لاغوینّهم اجمعین الاّ عبادك منهم المخلصین» [399]

397) Tafasir Namūneh and Al-Manār 398) Al-An'ām, 112 399) Sād, 82 to 83

Messages

1- The supposition of sin and temptation does not imply its occurrence; it is merely a warning. ("Imma" (اِمّا) is in the form of a condition, not a realization. Similar to the Ayah: «لئن اشركت لیحبطنّ عملك» [400] (If you associate [others with Allah], your work will surely become worthless), and a condition is not proof of occurrence.)

2- Satan's temptation is certain. «ینزغنّك» (It uses the emphatic nūn).

3- Satan's temptation is continuous. «ینزغنّك» (The imperfect verb indicates continuity).

4- One must not be heedless even of the smallest temptation from Satan; one must seek refuge in Allah and place oneself under His protection. «نزغٌ»

5- Prophets are infallible, and one of the ways they maintain their infallibility is by seeking aid, attention, and refuge in Allah. «فاستعذ»

6- Seeking refuge (isti'ādhah) and aid from Allah is the best remedy for satanic temptations. «فاستعذ»

7- Even the Prophets need isti'ādhah and to seek refuge in Allah. «فاستعذ»

8- Special attention and vigilance are necessary in times of danger. «فاستعذ»

9- Since satanic temptations vary, seeking refuge must be directed to "Allah," Who encompasses all good attributes and perfections. (He said: "billah" (باللّه), and did not say: "bil-Ghani" (بالغنى), or "bil-'Alīm" (بالعلیم), or any other attribute of God).

10- One must seek refuge in a God Who is Hearing and Knowing, and Who is aware of every secret and mystery, not in idols or superstitions, etc. «انه سمیع علیم»

400) Az-Zumar, 65

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ إِذَا مَسَّهُمْ طَـٰٓئِفٌ مِّنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ تَذَكَّرُوا۟ فَإِذَا هُم مُّبْصِرُونَ (۲٠۱)
When those who are Godwary are touched by a visitation of Satan, they remember [Allah] and, behold, they perceive. (201)
وَإِخْوَٰنُهُمْ يَمُدُّونَهُمْ فِى ٱلْغَىِّ ثُمَّ لَا يُقْصِرُونَ (۲٠۲)
But their brethren, they draw them into error, and then they do not spare [any harm]. (202)

Key Points

The word mass (مس) means striking and colliding accompanied by touching. Tā’if (طائف) means one who circumambulates (performs tawāf). It is as if satanic whispers constantly revolve around the human mind and soul, like one performing tawāf, seeking a way to penetrate. [401] Perhaps the intent of tadhakkur (remembrance/attention) in this Ayah is the recollection of Allah's Hearing and Seeing, which was mentioned at the end of the previous Ayah. That is, they realize that God sees their actions and hears their words, and this attention to the presence of Allah causes them to abandon sin. As Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The temptation to sin comes to the servant, he remembers Allah, becomes mindful, and does not commit it. [402] In some narrations, the dhikr (remembrance) of "لااله الاّ اللّه" (There is no god but Allah) is recommended for repelling satanic whispers. [403] Satan's whispering is sometimes from afar, "فوسوس الیه" [404], sometimes through penetration into the soul and spirit, "فى صدور النّاس" [405], sometimes through companionship, "فهو له قرین" [406], and sometimes through connection and contact. "مسّهم"

401) Tafsir-e Nemooneh 402) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn 403) Tafsir Ithna Ashari 404) Taha, 120 405) Nas, 5 406) Zukhruf, 36

Messages

1- Satan approaches even pious and faithful people. "انّ الّذین اتّقوا اذا مسّهم"

2- Satans are constantly patrolling and circumambulating (performing tawāf) seeking deviation. "طائف" (Egoistic and satanic whispers exist everywhere like microbes and seek weak faith to penetrate.)

3- Sometimes scholars, educators, and reformers may be subjected to suspicious contacts intended to instill a line of deviation. They must be vigilant, avoid falling into the path of the enemy's desires, and seek refuge in God. "اذا مسّهم... تذكّروا"

4- The remembrance of God grants insight to man and protects him from whispers. "تذكّروا، مبصرون"

5- The pious person pays attention to God, is aware, and recognizes Satan. "اتّقوا... تذكّروا"

6- Sinners and those trapped by Satan are blind, while those rescued from the snares of Iblis are insightful. "مبصرون"

7- If a society is pure and pious morally, politically, economically, and militarily, interactions and contacts with satanic individuals will not be effective in it. "تذكّروا، مبصرون"

8- If piety and remembrance are absent, Satans become brothers to humans, their contact becomes easy and effective, and they drag man into the depths of deviation. "اخوانهم یمدّونهف فى الغىّ"

9- The path of deviation has no end or limit. "یمدّونهم فى الغىّ"

10- Allah places the righteous and pious under His guardianship, as we read in the previous Ayahs, "انّ ولىّ اللّه... و هو یتولّى الصالحین" (Indeed, My Protector is Allah... and He takes charge of the righteous). But the impious become afflicted by satanic brotherhood. "اخوانهم یمدّونهم فى الغىّ"

11- Satans, after leading astray the humans who have become their brothers, do not give up and continue to plunge them into the mire of deviation. "فى الغىّ لایقصرون"

12- Satanic individuals show no mercy to anyone on the path of deviation and do not abandon anyone. "لا یقصرون"

Ayah

وَإِذَا لَمْ تَأْتِهِم بِـَٔايَةٍ قَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا ٱجْتَبَيْتَهَا ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَتَّبِعُ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ مِن رَّبِّى ۚ هَـٰذَا بَصَآئِرُ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۲٠۳)
When you do not bring them a sign, they say, ‘Why do you not improvise one?’ Say, ‘I only follow what is revealed to me from my Lord; these are insights from your Lord, and a guidance and mercy for a people who have faith.’ (203)

Key Points

Ijtabā’ (اجتباء) is derived from jibāyah (جبایت), meaning the collection of water in a basin (hawḍ). A basin is also called a jābiyah (جابیه). The collection of land tax (kharāj) and taxes is also called jibāyah. Subsequently, the collection of selected things has been termed ijtabā’. And perhaps the meaning of the Ayah is: Why did you not select the miracle that we requested from you, but instead brought another miracle and did not act according to our preference?

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «اذا أردتُم عیش السعداء و موت الشهداء و النّجاة یوم الحشر و الظلّ یوم الحرور و الهدى یوم الضّلالة فادرسوا القرآن فانّه كلام الرّحمن و حرز من الشیطان و رجحان فى المیزان» [407] If you desire a prosperous life, a martyr-like death, salvation and deliverance on the hot and scorching Day of Resurrection, then learn the Quran, for it is the Word of Allah, the Most Compassionate (al-Raḥmān), a safeguard against Satan, and the weight that tips the scale on the Day of Resurrection.

In another narration, He said: The Quran is the perfection of your religion; whoever turns away from the Quran, his abode is the Fire. «فیه كمال دینكم ما عَدل احدٌ عن القرآن الاّ الى النّار». [408]

407) Biḥār, Vol. 89, p. 17 408) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 600

Messages

1- The verses (Ayat) of the Quran are the miracle and sign of the truthfulness of the mission of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. «لم تأتهم بآیة»

2- The verses of the Quran were sometimes revealed with long time intervals. «و اذا لم تأتهم بآیة»

3- It is not necessary for the preacher (or messenger) to present new discourse every day; sometimes silence may be necessary. «لم تأتهم بآیة»

4- The disbelievers imagined the verses of the Quran to be selected materials gathered by the Prophet from various sources and presented to the people, rather than being Divine Revelation. «لولااجتبیتها»

5- The fault-finding disbelievers reproached the Prophet for the intervals in the descent of the Revelation. «قالوا لولا اجتبیتها»

6- A heavenly leader should not be influenced by baseless excuses and undue demands, but must state the answer to these excuses clearly and decisively. «قل انّما اتّبع...»

7- The Prophet receives commands only from the source of Revelation, and does not select and present materials from his own accord. «انّما اتّبع ما یوحى الىّ»

8- Revelation is the means of educating the Prophet and the Ummah (community) and is a necessity of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربىّ، ربّكم»

9- The guidance of the Quran is pure and not mixed with the slightest deviation. (The use of the word hudā (هُدى) instead of hādin (هاد) indicates that the Quran is entirely guidance.)

10- Guidance and instruction must be based on insight. «بصائر، هُدىً»

11- The Quran is both a book of knowledge and intellectual insight, «بصائر», and a guide for instruction and practical movement, «هُدى». The result of following it is mercy and blessing in both worlds. «و رحمةٌ»

12- Although the Quran is a book of guidance for the general public, only the people of faith benefit from this guidance. The blind-hearted, who are far from Divine insight and guidance, deprive themselves of this mercy. «هدى و رحمةٌ لقومٍ یؤمنون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قُرِئَ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ فَٱسْتَمِعُوا۟ لَهُۥ وَأَنصِتُوا۟ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (۲٠٤)
When the Quran is recited, listen to it and be silent, maybe you will receive [Allah’s] mercy. (204)

Key Points

The word "inṣāt" means becoming silent in order to listen attentively. This Ayah states: When the Quran is recited, observe silence out of respect and listen to its verses. Of course, obligatory silence is only required when the Imam of the congregation (Imam al-Jama'ah) is reciting the Surah al-Hamd and the other Surah during the prayer, in which case the followers (ma'mūmīn) must be silent. In all other instances, it is recommended (mustaḥabb) and a sign of respect. Even if there were no command for silence, wisdom and respect would necessitate that we remain silent before the Word of the Creator. Imam Ali عليه السلام was performing prayer when a hypocrite recited the Quran loudly several times. Each time, the Imam observed silence and then continued His prayer. (409) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Reciters of the Quran are of three groups: A group who consider the Quran a means of earning a living and impose themselves upon people; a group who only have the Quran on their tongues but do not observe its rulings in practice; and a group who recite the Quran, consider it the cure for their ailments, and are constantly intimate with the Quran. It is through these individuals that Allah repels punishment and sends down rain because of them, and these people are rare. "A'azz min al-kibrīt al-aḥmar" (Rarer than red sulfur/the philosopher's stone). (410)

(409) Tafsir Namūneh (410) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 627

Messages

1- In the face of the recitation of the Quran, by whomever it may be, we must observe silence and listen attentively. "Wa idhā quri'a al-Qur'ānu fastami'ū lahu" (The verb "quri'a" is in the passive voice).

2- Silence and listening attentively to the Quran is the basis for benefiting from Divine Mercy. "La'allakum turḥamūn"

Ayah

وَٱذْكُر رَّبَّكَ فِى نَفْسِكَ تَضَرُّعًا وَخِيفَةً وَدُونَ ٱلْجَهْرِ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ بِٱلْغُدُوِّ وَٱلْـَٔاصَالِ وَلَا تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْغَـٰفِلِينَ (۲٠٥)
And remember your Lord within your heart beseechingly and reverentially, without being loud, morning, and evening, and do not be among the heedless. (205)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the etiquette of reciting the Quran was explained, and this Ayah concerns the etiquette of dhikr (remembrance) and du'a (supplication), which must be accompanied by humility (taḍarru‘), reverence (khushū‘), fear, and hope. The word ‘āṣāl (آصال) is the plural of aṣīl (اصیل), meaning near sunset and evening, and the word ghudūw (غُدوّ), the plural of ghudwah (غدوة), refers to the time from the break of dawn until sunrise. Some have said that the intent of dhikr (remembrance) in this Ayah is the obligatory prayers throughout the day and night. It is narrated from Ibn Abbas that, based on this Ayah, the Imam leading the prayer should recite aloud during the Fajr (morning) and night prayers, loud enough for the followers to hear, but no louder than that.

Messages

1- Even the Prophets must constantly remember Allah, let alone others. «واذكر ربّك»

2- The Quran praises not only verbal remembrance (dhikr) but also internal remembrance and recollection of the heart. «فى نفسك»

3- The remembrance of Allah removes heedlessness only when it is without ostentation or noise; otherwise, it becomes a form of distraction and heedlessness itself. «تضرّعاً و خیفةً و دون الجهر»

4- Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) is perpetual, so it is fitting that His remembrance should also be continuous. «واذكر ربّك... بالغدوّ و الاصال»

5- The remembrance that safeguards a person is that which is passionate, heartfelt, continuous, and performed every morning and evening. «تضرّعاً... بالغدوّ و الاصال»

6- One must remember Allah at the beginning of every day, when starting work and making decisions, and at the end of every day, when summarizing and concluding. «بالغدوّ و الاصال»

7- Those who do not remember Allah every morning and evening are among the heedless. « واذكر... ولاتكن من الغافلین»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ عِندَ رَبِّكَ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِهِۦ وَيُسَبِّحُونَهُۥ وَلَهُۥ يَسْجُدُونَ ۩ (۲٠٦)
Indeed those who are [stationed] near your Lord do not disdain to worship Him. They glorify Him and prostrate to Him. (206)

Key Points

«الّذین عند ربّك» (Those who are with your Lord) refers to the close ones in Allah’s presence, including both the angels and the purified servants who see themselves in the presence of God. Prostration (Sajdah) is recommended (Mustahabb) when reciting or hearing the above Ayah. [411] Prostration is mentioned regarding fifteen verses of the Quran, four of which are obligatory (Wajib): Ayahs 15 of Sajdah, 37 of Fussilat, 62 of Najm, and 19 of ‘Alaq. In eleven Ayahs, prostration is recommended (Mustahabb): Ayahs 206 of A‘raf, 15 of Ra‘d, 48 of Nahl, 107 of Isra’, 58 of Maryam, 18 and 77 of Hajj, 60 of Furqan, 25 of Naml, 24 of Sad, and 21 of Inshiqaq.

411) Tafsir Namuneh

Messages

1- Through worship, we should make ourselves similar and harmonious with the angels. «واذكر ربّك...انّ الّذین عند ربّك...»

2- The arrogant person does not reach the station of divine proximity. «الّذین عند ربّك لایستكبرون». Those close to Allah are humble and modest, and they do not show arrogance.

3- The remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah is a clear example of worship. «واذكر ربّك... لایستكبرون عن عبادته»

4- Negligence toward Allah results from self-importance and arrogance. «لا تكن من الغافلین انّ الّذین عند ربّك لا یستكبرون»

5- The path to proximity to Allah is humility, worship, and sincere prostration. «عند ربّك لایستكبرون... و له یسجدون»

6- Prostration is exclusive to Allah, and those close to Him believe this. «له یسجدون» (The precedence of «له» over «یسجدون» is a sign of exclusivity).

7- We should not be proud of our worship, because Allah has angels who are constantly engaged in worship. «انّ الّذین عند ربّك.... یسبّحونه و له یسجدون»

8- First, the spirit of arrogance must be abandoned, then Allah must be glorified (Tasbih), and in the third stage, one must fall into prostration. «لایستكبرون، یسبّحونه، یسجدون»

«الحمدلِلّه رَبِ ّ العالمین» # Portrait of Surah Al-Anfal

This Surah has seventy-five Ayahs and was revealed in Medina.

This Surah is named "Al-Anfal" due to the word «انفال» (spoils/public wealth) appearing at its beginning, and also because it explains the rulings regarding Anfal and public assets.

The other name for this Surah is "Badr," because most of its Ayahs concern the Battle of Badr, which was the first battle between the Muslims and the polytheists, and in which the Muslims benefited from divine aid and the victory granted by Allah.

In this Surah, alongside the discussion of the Battle of Badr, the characteristics of the army of truth versus the army of falsehood are subtly explained. Furthermore, lessons from the history of the Prophet of Islam and how he treated the Muslims, as well as issues such as Anfal, Bayt al-Mal (public treasury), the rulings of Jihad, war spoils (Ghanā’im), captives, Khums (one-fifth tax) and how to divide it, the story of the Hijra (migration) and Laylat al-Mabīt (the night of sleeping over), the characteristics of true believers, preventing disagreement and division, and other topics are discussed.
